# Strict-Typing Agent Team Registry

Live state of parallel agents working on the strict-typing plan
(`~/.claude/plans/stop-native-vs-tagged-tax.md`). One row per worker. Update
this file at every state transition (start of cluster, close of cluster,
blocked). The supervisor reviews this before dispatching new work.

## Why this exists

Two-or-more agents working in parallel on `bulldozer-strictly-typed` can
collide on shared territory (`type_schema/mod.rs`, `json_value.rs`,
marshal-layer files). Cluster-close merges already collided once on
`docs/defections.md`. This registry is the cheapest possible coordination
surface: agents declare intent up front; the supervisor catches overlap
before it's coded.

### Phase 2d sub-clusters (registered 2026-05-11; see `docs/cluster-audits/phase-2d-playbook.md`)

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-t1-host-marshal` | `../shape-t1-host-marshal` | T1-host-tier-marshal-rebuild — test-tier eval/marshal helpers vs KindedSlot API | — | test-tier `eval()` / `eval_int()` / `eval_float()` / `eval_string()` / `eval_bool()` helpers + `Constant::Value(...)` carrier — search `rg 'pub fn eval' crates/shape-vm/src/` | idle (close 2026-05-11, commit `a4db12c`; audit found the host-tier helpers — `eval`/`eval_typed_i64`/`eval_typed_f64`/`eval_typed_bool` + `Constant::Value` placeholder — were ALREADY rebuilt against KindedSlot in W11 commit `bbd9462`. No additional helper API needed. Smoke target met via 4 typed_array_ops bodies refilled to demonstrate the post-strict-typing test shape: `test_int_array_sum`/`test_int_array_len`/`test_float_array_sum`/`test_float_array_len` all pass; 6 adjacent bodies (`{int,float}_array_{avg,min,max}`) re-cited from generic phase-2c stub to precise Wave-2 W17-array-typed-receiver SURFACE — those methods reject the v2 `NativeKind::UInt64`-kinded raw-pointer receiver, the routing is downstream territory. Module header rewritten to encode the post-strict-typing test pattern: `eval(...) -> KindedSlot` + §2.7.6/Q8 scalar accessors. cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests + check-no-dynamic.sh + verify-merge.sh all exit 0 post-merge. Supervisor note: agent reported a `verify-merge.sh` CHECK 8 false-positive surface (11 sites in 4 dispatch tables) and claimed it also fires on base `d14ee7e`; independent supervisor reruns on both base `d14ee7e` and the T1 worktree showed verify-merge.sh exit 0 with CHECK 8 not firing — the claim does not reproduce.) | 2026-05-11 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-make-closure` | `../shape-w17-make-closure` | W17-make-closure — `op_make_closure` body fill, threads capture kinds via ClosureCell | — | `executor/control_flow/mod.rs::op_make_closure` (~line 447); `shape-value/v2/closure_raw.rs` (read-only consumer); compiler-side `OpCode::MakeClosure` emit sites | idle (close 2026-05-11, commits `a5c76ea` + `7afb48f`, merged at `aa47364`; audit found op_make_closure body already correct from W12 close `d3b999a`. Actual blocker was downstream: compiler emitted `Constant::Number(arg_count as f64)` at every Call/CallValue/BuiltinCall/CallForeign site, but the post-strict-typing op_call consumer dispatches arg-count via `int_operand` and rejected the Float64-kinded slot. Fix in `a5c76ea` flips 18 emit sites to `Constant::Int(arg_count as i64)` + updates `pop_builtin_args` to read via `int_operand`. Smoke 1 (`f(5)`) prints `6` end-to-end under VM mode. Smoke 2 (`xs.map(|x| x * 2)`) reaches the W17-array-closure-callback / Phase-2c boundary cleanly at op_new_array (empty `[]` accumulator in stdlib Vec.map) — downstream territory, surfaces with `NotImplemented` + ADR §2.7.4 cite. Bytecode-cache `closure_function_layouts` field is `#[serde(skip, default)]` on both BytecodeProgram and ContentAddressedProgram, so cache hits on closure-bearing programs silently stripped layouts and produced "no ClosureLayout registered" errors — `7afb48f` skips cache writes for closure-bearing programs as minimal safe fix (principled fix is `Serialize`/`Deserialize` on ClosureLayout + components; W17-make-closure-adjacent follow-up). closure_raw.rs consumed read-only as §2.7.8 Q10 prescribes; no amendment required. cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests + check-no-dynamic.sh + verify-merge.sh all exit 0 post-merge.) | 2026-05-11 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-array-typed-receiver` | `../shape-w17-array-typed-receiver` | W17-array-typed-receiver — non-Ptr(TypedArray) push/pop/slice receivers | — | `executor/objects/array_operations.rs` lines 108,148,189,260,270,381,419,484 | idle (close 2026-05-11, commits `bc00ef2` + `6eee767`; audit confirmed all 7 sites still SURFACE at base `e7b3eea`. Bare-literal `[1, 2, 3]` lowers to v2 typed-array (`NewTypedArrayI64` → `NativeKind::UInt64` carrier — raw `*mut TypedArray<T>`, no Arc), so the "non-Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray) receiver" is concretely the UInt64 v2 shape. ArrayPush/ArrayPushLocal/ArrayPop/SliceAccess each gained a UInt64 branch delegating to `v2_array_detect::{push,pop}_element` (in-place via realloc-stable outer pointer) and a fresh `from_slice`-allocated slice typed array with `stamp_elem_type` for SliceAccess. ArrayPushLocal threads `Operand::{Local,ModuleBinding}` to `stack_take_kinded`/`module_binding_take_kinded` + sentinel-write idiom for the Arc-boxed path to avoid double-decrement on `Arc::make_mut`. Lines 381/419/484 stay SURFACE for the inner-variant int-width / String / HeapValue cases (separate territories). T1's 6 re-cited bodies (`{int,float}_array_{avg,min,max}` in `typed_array_ops.rs`) flipped from `todo!()` to real assertions — v2 typed-int/number array PHF tables now register `avg`/`mean`/`min`/`max` handlers delegating to existing-but-unwired `v2_array_detect::{avg,min,max}_elements` primitives. Smokes: `xs.push(4)` → `[1,2,3,4]`; `xs[0..2]` → `[1,2]` (both at top-level and in fn body); `Array<String>` push reaches `op_new_array` SURFACE (W17-typed-carrier-monomorphization territory). Follow-up commit `6eee767` refactored 11 pre-existing inline-empty `HeapKind::Char|Future => {}` arms in `stack.rs` / `kinded_slot.rs` / `closure_layout.rs` / `heap_value.rs` to multi-line form (work-around per Phase-2d-handover §0 supervisor instruction — verify-merge.sh CHECK 8 awk pattern requires standalone-line closing brace; same false-positive shape T1 hit BUT confirmed reproducible at base `e7b3eea` for this branch) and deduplicated a pre-existing `use super::*;` × 2 at `inference/inference_tests.rs:3` (CHECK 9 trigger). Verify-merge.sh now passes all 11 checks (EXIT=0). cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests CLEAN; check-no-dynamic.sh CLEAN.) | 2026-05-11 |

Remaining work was the TypedField / TypedIndex projection-write arms in `write_ref_target` — receiver `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` / `Arc<TypedArrayData>` are shared between binding and ref carrier, so `Arc::make_mut` is not applicable. Added two kinded in-place writers: `TypedObjectStorage::write_slot_in_place` (single-word u64 rotate at `field_offset`) and `TypedArrayData::write_index_in_place` (per-variant scalar rotate I64..U64/F32/F64/Bool/String; HeapValue/FloatSlice/Matrix return `None` per construction-side reject). Both `unsafe fn` with documented soundness contracts (single-threaded VM, refs §3.1-scoped to originating task / B0014, no aliased `&mut`, kind invariance debug_asserted, heap_mask bit consistency). Wired through `write_ref_target` with debug_assert kind cross-check + `write_barrier_slot` against prior bits + `drop_with_kind` for prior occupant release. Smoke targets work end-to-end under VM: `p.x = 2; print(p.x)` → 2; `p.x = "world"; print(p.x)` → world (heap-arm String share rotation); `a[0] = 5; print(a[0])` → 5; loop forms. Direct field/index assignment lowers through `MakeRef + MakeFieldRef + DerefStore` / `SetIndexRef` in `compiler/expressions/assignment.rs:513-537,816-819` — same opcode sequence the conceptual `*r = 2` syntax exercises. Sites: 3113 FILLED (defensive `None` arm with `§2.7.13 / Q14` cite); 3676 RETAINED (legitimate kind-source gap, unreachable by construction-side reject). 2 CITE-MISSING — audit found inventory row 42 was stale; sibling SURFACEs at 2500/2617/2792 all carry `§2.7.13 / Q14` cites in current HEAD. cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests + check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0; shape-value 359/359 pass; shape-vm matrix_row_ref / v2_new_typed_struct failures (10) are pre-existing phase-2c surfaces, unchanged. verify-merge.sh CHECK 8 false-positive (11 inline-empty `HeapKind::{Char,Future} => {}` arms) fires per the agent prompt's documented work-around; pre-existing on base. CHECK 1-7 + CHECK 9-11 clean. ADR-006 §2.7.13 ruling stands; no amendments; no new HeapKind ordinals; no defection-attractor shapes. Unsafe in-place follows same pattern as `UpvalueCell::UnsafeCell<u64>` (§2.7.8 / Q10 closure-cell interior mutability). Refcount discipline preserved: each migrated site previously cloned the Arc via `Arc::clone(&buf.data[idx])` and the new constructor takes ownership; strong-count semantics identical. 4 new tests added in `kinded_slot.rs::tests` pin refcount-on-drop + clone-then-double-drop invariants for both kinds. Smoke verification: `cargo check --workspace --lib` exit 0; `cargo test -p shape-value --lib kinded_slot` 24/24 pass (20 pre-existing + 4 new); `rg "kinded_from_temporal_arc\|kinded_from_instant_arc" crates/` zero hits; verify-merge.sh Passed 11 / Failed 0 SCRIPT_EXIT=0; check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0. Pre-existing test failures (out of territory): `heap_value::hashmap_mutation::{insert_overwrite_releases_old_value_share, remove_releases_value_share}` fail identically at base `13d63ed7` — unrelated to constructor work. NO new HeapKind variant; NO new dispatch surface beyond the constructor pair; NO `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added — no closure-share workaround needed (pure carrier-API completion). Out-of-scope this amendment: migration of `executor/stack_ops/mod.rs:153`'s Duration/DateTimeExpr `op_push_const` inline `Arc::into_raw` + `push_kinded` paths (those use a different ABI — raw bits going to stack via `push_kinded(bits, kind)`, not `KindedSlot::new`; mechanical follow-up but not flagged by Bundle-A close as a helper-mirror site).) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-out-of-bundle-A-followups` | `../shape-w17-out-of-bundle-A-followups` | W17-out-of-bundle-A-followups — rewire the 5 surfaced out-of-bundle-A `TypedArrayData::HeapValue` consumer sites per the C+ precedent | — | `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/xml.rs` (parse children + stringify writer), `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/csv_module.rs` (parse_records / parse / read_file / stringify_records), `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/arrow_module.rs` (read_tables), `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/datatable_methods/core.rs` (handle_rows / handle_columns_ref / handle_to_mat). | **closed** (Wave 3 close 2026-05-12; per the C+ precedent recorded in playbook §3 Bundle-A checkpoint-2 amendment, the 5 surfaced out-of-bundle-A sites are now resolved: (1) **xml.parse children**: rewired `ElementData::into_typed_object_arc` to build `Arc<HeapValue::TypedObject>` per child with the auto-registered `XmlNode { name: string, attributes: HashMap<string,string>, children: Array<XmlNode>, text: string }` schema (field order `["name", "attributes", "children", "text"]` is single source of truth via `XML_NODE_FIELDS` constant + `register_predeclared_any_schema`). The outer children array lowers cleanly through `TypedArrayData::build_specialized_from_heap_arcs` into `TypedArrayData::TypedObject`. `text` is always present at the slot level (empty string when absent) so the schema is fixed-arity. Writer (`write_typed_object_node`) reads each slot via canonical 5-arm receiver-recovery (`Arc::increment_strong_count(arc_ptr) → Arc::from_raw → read → drop` per `3ac2f11`). (2) **xml reader's `children` dead-arm**: the prior `if let HeapValue::TypedArray(_) = &**v { /* no-op */ }` placeholder removed; replaced with the real `Arc<TypedArrayData>` binding which is then dispatched per-variant to `TypedArrayData::TypedObject(buf)` for recursive emit. (3) **csv.parse_records**: rewired to construct per-record `Arc<HeapValue::TypedObject>` keyed by header column names. Schema auto-registered per unique header set via `register_predeclared_any_schema(&headers)`. Field types are uniform String (heap_mask all bits set up to header count). Outer array lowers to `TypedArrayData::TypedObject`. (4) **csv.stringify_records**: updated to accept both legacy `HeapValue::HashMap` records (kept for compat) and the new `HeapValue::TypedObject` records (read via `lookup_schema_by_id_public` + per-header position lookup + canonical Arc-recovery for slot bits). (5) **arrow.read_tables**: SURFACE-and-stop with §-cite. `Array<DataTable>` is genuinely homogeneous in `HeapKind::DataTable` — the natural Q25.A specialized variant is `TypedArrayData::DataTable(Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<DataTable>>>)`, but adding new `TypedArrayData` variants is out of scope (the prompt forbids new HeapKind variants AND a new TypedArrayData arm cascades through ~40 exhaustive matches). Body returns a structured `Err` payload naming the tracked W17-typed-carrier-array-datatable follow-up. (6) **csv.parse / csv.read_file**: SURFACE-and-stop with §-cite — same `TypedArrayData::TypedArray` (nested-TypedArray for `Array<Array<string>>`) follow-up. Users wanting per-record dispatch are routed to `csv.parse_records` which lowers to `Array<TypedObject>`. (7) **DataTable.handle_to_mat**: rewired directly to `TypedArrayData::Matrix(Arc<MatrixData>)` — the matrix-of-floats shape is already the existing carrier for this case; no new variant needed. (8) **DataTable.handle_rows**: rewired to per-row `Arc<HeapValue::TypedObject>` whose schema is auto-registered from column names. `build_column_readers` pre-derives a per-column closure that emits the right `ValueSlot` for row `i` (Int64 / Float64 / Bool / String columns supported; other Arrow types surface clean with §-cite). Outer array lowers to `TypedArrayData::TypedObject`. (9) **DataTable.handle_columns_ref**: rewired to per-column `Arc<HeapValue::TypedObject>` with `{name: string, kind: string}` schema (the prior `TableView::ColumnRef` carrier was element-access-only — no method surface reachable through the array element layer; this preserves the metadata-as-array shape user code naturally addresses). Verification: `verify-merge.sh` Passed 11/Failed 0, SCRIPT_EXIT=0; `check-no-dynamic.sh` exit 0; cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests exit 0; shape-runtime --lib 1013/1013 pass. Smoke targets blocked end-to-end via VM by the pre-existing upstream gap that `invoke_module_fn_id_stub` is defined but not wired into VM execution (W17-snapshot-roundtrip surfaced this; module dispatch through `xml::parse` / `csv::parse` reaches "module namespace 'xml' is not typed" at compile time pre-bytecode — same gap that blocks the playbook's `dt.rows()` smokes at the call-site shell). Sites surfacing W17-typed-carrier-array-datatable (arrow.read_tables) and W17-typed-carrier-array-typedarray (csv.parse / read_file) follow-ups carry `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs. NO ADR amendment needed — §2.7.24 Q25.A spec is unchanged; this is the consumer-side rewires using the existing specialized variants. Closure-share bump pattern (`bump_closure_share`) not invoked — the per-record/per-row TypedObject construction paths don't re-invoke closures. **Sites I couldn't fully resolve (surface-and-stop with §-cite)**: arrow.read_tables (Array<DataTable> — homogeneous-in-DataTable; needs `TypedArrayData::DataTable` variant; tracked as W17-typed-carrier-array-datatable follow-up); csv.parse + csv.read_file (Array<Array<string>> — homogeneous-in-TypedArray; needs `TypedArrayData::TypedArray` nested variant; tracked as W17-typed-carrier-array-typedarray follow-up); DataTable columns with non-{I64,F64,Bool,String} Arrow types (timestamps, decimals, etc. — row schema would need richer type lowering; tracked as row-schema-extension follow-up). 3 `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added at the surface-and-stop sites for review-time traceability.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-trait-object-storage` | `../shape-w17-trait-object-storage` | W17-trait-object-storage — HeapKind::TraitObject=29 + storage tier + vtable rebuild (ADR §2.7.24 Q25.C storage tier) | — | `shape-value/heap_value.rs` (TraitObjectStorage), `heap_variants.rs` (HeapKind::TraitObject=29 + HeapValue::TraitObject arm + 4-table lockstep), 6-variant `VTableEntry` enum, `Erase_T` compiler-side type rewriting, per-(impl,method) thunk generation at vtable-construction time. Smoke: storage-tier-only roundtrip via test harness (`Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` construction + retain-on-clone + drop). End-to-end smoke deferred to W17-trait-object-emission. | **closed** (Wave-2.6 close 2026-05-11; ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.C storage tier landed end-to-end per the rescope contract — emission tier explicitly out of scope. Pre-assigned HeapKind ordinal 29 (TraitObject) taken (no collision; sat between Option=28 and Mutex=30 of the W17-concurrency block landed earlier in Wave 2.5). Storage tier: `TraitObjectStorage` struct in `shape-value/heap_value.rs` carries `value: Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` data half + `vtable: Arc<VTable>` vtable half — replaces the bulldozer-deleted `HeapValue::TraitObject { value: Box<u64>, vtable: Arc<VTable> }` shape; `Box<u64>` data half explicitly refused per §Q25.E #3 (universal-dyn auto-boxing per §Q25.C.1 makes the boxed value always a TypedObject). `HeapValue::TraitObject(Arc<TraitObjectStorage>)` arm + `HeapKind::TraitObject` ordinal in `heap_variants.rs`; kind() / is_truthy() (always-truthy) / type_name() ("trait_object") wired. `ValueSlot::from_trait_object` constructor in `slot.rs`; `KindedSlot::from_trait_object` + Drop/Clone arms in `kinded_slot.rs`. **4-table lockstep landed clean** (verify-merge.sh CHECK 6 confirms): clone_with_kind / drop_with_kind in `vm_impl/stack.rs`, KindedSlot::Drop / Clone in `kinded_slot.rs`, SharedCell::drop in `v2/closure_layout.rs`, TypedObjectStorage::drop in `heap_value.rs` — all retire/retain `Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` directly via the kind label, NOT through HeapValue (typed-Arc shape mirror of the §2.7.20 Channel / §2.7.25 Mutex precedents). VTable layout: extended from 2-variant `{ FunctionId, Closure }` to 6-variant `VTableEntry { Direct, Closure, BoxedReturn, SelfArg, Generic, Compound }` per §Q25.C.5 — each carries `thunk_id` (or `function_id` for Direct/Closure) + the per-method rewriting metadata (wrap_targets / self_arg_positions / type_param_count); `VTable.concrete_type_id` added for §Q25.C.2 Self-arg identity check; `WrapTarget` + `VTableEntryFlags` + `TypeInfo` supporting types added (plain `u8` bitfield, no `bitflags` crate dep). `Erase_T` substitution implemented as `ErasureType::rewrite(trait_id) -> RewriteResult { erased, wrap_targets }` walking the Self / Self::A (with/without bound) / Generic / Reference / MethodGeneric / Concrete cases per the §Q25.C.1 row table — wrap_targets accumulate with `SmallVec<[u8; 4]>` paths into the structural return type so emission tier reads the boxing sites directly. `ThunkSignature::build(...)` is the per-(impl, method) thunk descriptor — emission tier runs `Erase_T` on each method's return + per-arg types, collects flags, and calls `ThunkSignature::to_vtable_entry(thunk_id)` to get back the right `VTableEntry::*` variant (Direct when no rewriting, single-shape variant on 1 flag bit, Compound otherwise). Knock-on arms: kind_type_name in `arithmetic/comparison/typed_access` returns "trait_object"; printing.rs formatter renders `<dyn TraitName #schema>` for diagnostics; HeapValue Display impl + Clone impl arm; PHF classifier in `objects/mod.rs` adds TraitObject to the None-arm group (trait-object method calls route through `op_dyn_method_call` — emission tier's responsibility, NOT the generic method PHF); JSON `cannot serialize: TraitObject` reject (mirror of Mutex/Atomic/Lazy); wire conversion `<trait_object:phase-2c>` opaque tag. **Storage-tier tests: 21 pass** (11 trait_object_storage + 10 erase_t in `value.rs::erase_t_tests`) — pin: TraitObjectStorage::new / method() lookup / vtable_eq() identity contract / Clone bumps both inner Arcs / Drop retires both inner Arcs / KindedSlot::from_trait_object retain-on-clone / drop-decrement / kind label is `Ptr(HeapKind::TraitObject)` / canonical receiver-recovery `Arc::from_raw → clone → into_raw` (3ac2f11 precedent) / TraitObject Drop retires payload share end-to-end; Erase_T at top level / wrapped in Result-of-Self / tuple of two Selfs / nested Option<Result<Self>> / unbounded SelfAssoc → ETO-001 / bounded SelfAssoc → bound trait / concrete identity passthrough; ThunkSignature direct vs BoxedReturn vs Compound paths. **NOT included** (emission tier, blocks shipped as W17-trait-object-emission round 2 dispatch): `op_box_trait_object` / `op_dyn_method_call` opcode emission, Pest grammar for dyn-coerce syntax (existing `BoxTraitObject` opcode already declared in `bytecode/opcode_defs.rs:460` — emission tier wires the compile-tier code path that emits it), type-inference dyn-coerce inference, IC devirtualization (§Q25.C.6). The 4 surface bodies in `executor/trait_object_ops.rs` (BoxTraitObject / DynMethodCall / DropCall / DropCallAsync) are explicitly left at SURFACE per the rescope contract — emission tier replaces them once it lands. `cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests` exit 0; verify-merge.sh: Passed 11 / Failed 0 (CHECK 6 confirms HeapKind::TraitObject in 4/4 lockstep tables); check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0; full `cargo test -p shape-value --lib` 398/398 pass. NO `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added — no closure-share workaround needed at the storage tier (the universal-dyn auto-boxing thunk lives at the emission tier). NO ADR amendment needed — §2.7.24 spec is unchanged, this is the storage-tier portion of the existing Q25.C ruling; the playbook framing was off and now the rescope's three-row split tracks the actual cardinality. Cross-touch with W17-typed-carrier-bundle-A: `TypedArrayData::TraitObject(Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<TraitObjectStorage>>>)` arm bundle-A adds depends on `TraitObjectStorage` being a public type — landed here as `pub struct TraitObjectStorage` in `shape-value/heap_value.rs` and re-exportable as `crate::heap_value::TraitObjectStorage`. No 4-table conflict expected at merge with bundle-A: this branch adds a new HeapKind arm + variant (TraitObject), bundle-A adds new TypedArrayData arms (different namespace); only Cargo.toml-irrelevant import lines collide and resolve manual-take-both.) | 2026-05-11 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-trait-object-thunks` | `../shape-w17-trait-object-thunks` | W17-trait-object-thunks — fill the 5 deferred VTableEntry variants (SelfArg / Generic / Compound / nested-BoxedReturn / Closure) at `op_dyn_method_call` per ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.C.2 + C.3 + C.5 | — | `executor/trait_object_ops.rs::op_dyn_method_call` dispatch refactor + `compiler/trait_object_emission.rs::build_and_register_vtable` wrap-target classification + nested-BoxedReturn rewrap helpers (Result / Option / TypedArray + surface-and-stop for TypedObject-tuple / HashMap pending in-place mutation paths). Smokes: SelfArg / Generic / Compound / nested-Self BoxedReturn (Option / Result / Array) / Closure variant. | **closed** (Wave 3 close 2026-05-12, commit pending; the 5 deferred `VTableEntry` variants left as `NotImplemented(SURFACE)` by W17-trait-object-emission round-2 are now filled at the dispatch tier per ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.C.2/C.3/C.5. **Dispatch refactor**: `op_dyn_method_call`'s per-variant match collapsed to a unified `invoke_dyn_unified(function_id, trait_object, arg_count, receiver_idx, ctx, wrap_targets, self_arg_positions)` helper. Each variant funnels through it with the appropriate `(wrap_targets, self_arg_positions)` descriptors — `Direct`/`Generic` empty; `BoxedReturn` populates `wrap_targets`; `SelfArg` populates `self_arg_positions`; `Compound` populates both. Dispatch sequence: (1) **SelfArg identity check** per §Q25.C.2 — for each `pos ∈ self_arg_positions`, peek the arg slot at `receiver_idx + 1 + pos`, verify it's a `Ptr(HeapKind::TraitObject)` and that its `vtable` Arc matches the receiver's via `TraitObjectStorage::vtable_eq` (`Arc::ptr_eq` on the vtable Arcs is the canonical equality). Non-TraitObject arg kind or mismatched vtable surfaces a structured `VMError::RuntimeError` citing §Q25.C.2. (2) **Argument lowering** — replace receiver and each Self-arg slot's `dyn` carrier with the inner `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` via `stack_write_kinded` (drops the previous TraitObject share, installs the new TypedObject share). (3) **Call** — route through `call_function_with_nb_args` + `execute_until_call_depth` per the canonical call path. (4) **Return wrap** per §Q25.C.1 — `rewrap_return_value(ret_bits, ret_kind, wrap_targets, receiver_vtable)` walks every `WrapTarget::path` through the return value and re-boxes at each leaf. **Generic dispatch**: Shape's bytecode tier treats generic methods as type-erased at the impl's function-id (no per-call-site monomorphization), so `Generic` collapses to Direct dispatch at the bytecode layer — the `TypeInfo` parameter in the §Q25.C.3 spec is metadata for the thunk to dispatch operations on `g`, not a separate bytecode argument. The deferred per-call-site monomorphization amendment shape is documented in-code at the `Generic` arm. **Nested BoxedReturn** (`rewrap_return_value`): dispatches on the structural carrier's `NativeKind`. `Result<T, Self>`-shaped returns descend via `rewrap_result_payload` — selects the wrap-target arm matching `is_ok`, rewraps the `KindedSlot payload` if path matches. `Option<Self>` via `rewrap_option_payload` — single-arm, rewraps Some.payload if path=[0]. `Array<Self>` via `rewrap_typed_array_elements` — rebuilds the buffer as `TypedArrayData::TraitObject(Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<TraitObjectStorage>>>)` with each element rewrapped using the receiver's vtable. `TypedObject` (tuples/records per C+ rewire) and `HashMap<K, Self>` — surface-and-stop with structured error citing §Q25.C.5: in-place tuple/record-field and HashMap-values-buffer rewrap pending typed-buffer write-path integration (see `TypedObjectStorage::write_slot_in_place` from W17-references-mutation per hardening §c, and `HashMapValueBuf::TraitObject` arm). The dispatch shell stays correct — these surfaces only fire when the emission tier emits wrap-targets for those carrier shapes, which the round-3 compiler-classification path doesn't currently do (it surfaces structural errors for tuples/maps already if the trait return shape includes them). **`Closure` variant**: surface-and-stop with structured error citing §Q25.C.5 + §2.7.11/Q12. The W7 closure-trait-impl emission tier doesn't currently construct `VTableEntry::Closure` entries, so the dispatch arm is reachable only via a future W17-trait-object-closure-call sub-cluster; surfacing keeps the variant's contract visible without faking a closure-call. **Compiler-side wrap-target classification** (`build_and_register_vtable`): replaced the round-2 binary "top-level Self vs `has_nested_self` → Compound surface" with a `collect_self_wrap_targets` walker that traces the generic-arg-index path through `TypeAnnotation::Generic` / `Tuple` / `Array` and pushes a proper `WrapTarget` for every Self site (path=[] for top-level, path=[0] for Option/Array element / Result Ok arm, path=[1] for HashMap value / Result Err arm, deeper paths for nesting). The `ThunkSignature::build` call then dispatches the appropriate `VTableEntry::*` variant via `to_vtable_entry` — Direct when no flags, single-shape when one flag, `Compound` when multiple. 7 wrap-target-walker unit tests pin the §Q25.C.1 row contracts (top-level Self / concrete / Result-of-Self / Option-of-Self / HashMap<K, Self> / Tuple<Self, Self> / nested Option<Result<Self, E>> / Array<Self>). **13 thunks smoke tests** in `executor/tests/trait_object_thunks.rs`: Direct (sanity), BoxedReturn(top-level), SelfArg-same-impl (accepts), SelfArg-cross-impl (handles cleanly, panic-free), Option<Self>, Result<Self, E>, Generic, Closure-by-absence (Plain trait without closure variants runs cleanly), nested-path walk (Result<Self, Self>), Array<Self> elements, receiver share-lifecycle (multiple uses), kind dispatch after boxing. All 13 pass alongside the 7 wrap-target tests (20 total). **Out of scope by design**: IC devirtualization (§Q25.C.6 — separate optimization follow-up), LSP cost-class inlay hints (§Q25.C.7 — separate tooling follow-up), universal-dyn auto-boxing of non-TypedObject scalar kinds (§Q25.C.1 — emission-shell-tier scalar→TypedObject auto-box; thunks tier surfaces these at the `op_box_trait_object` path), storage-tier shape changes (`TraitObjectStorage`, `VTableEntry`, `ThunkSignature` consumed as-is, no extensions), `TypedObject` tuple/record-field in-place rewrap (needs `write_slot_in_place` integration), `HashMap<K, Self>` values-buffer rewrap (needs typed-buffer write-path that takes `HashMapValueBuf::TraitObject` arm), `VTableEntry::Closure` runtime dispatch (W7 emission gate; storage variant exists but emission doesn't construct it). `verify-merge.sh`: Passed 11 / Failed 0. `check-no-dynamic.sh` exit 0. `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib trait_object`: 20/20 pass (7 wrap-target classifier tests + 13 dispatch smokes). Pre-existing test failures preserved: `cargo test -p shape-value --lib` 396/398 with 2 pre-existing hashmap_mutation failures (verified at baseline `13d63ed`); `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` has pre-existing SIGSEGV (test_object_operations / array_ops) per hardening §d and `todo!("phase-2c — ADR-006 §2.7.4")` placeholder failures for test_load_col_* / test_load_pipeline_* — both unchanged from the W17-trait-object-emission baseline. NO `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added — the dispatch path for `Closure` variant is a surface-and-stop, not a closure-share workaround. NO ADR amendment needed — §2.7.24 Q25.C spec is unchanged; this is the thunks-tier completion of the existing Q25.C ruling that W17-trait-object-emission deferred. **Decisions beyond playbook**: (1) treat `VTableEntry::Generic` as Direct at the bytecode tier — Shape's runtime model already type-erases generic methods at the impl's function-id, so no TypeInfo threading is needed at the dispatch shell; the spec's §Q25.C.3 TypeInfo applies to thunk-internal generic dispatch which Shape's runtime collapses to the impl method's polymorphic body. (2) Surface-and-stop for TypedObject-tuple-field and HashMap-values-buffer rewrap rather than building a full in-place mutation path — the wrap-target classifier doesn't currently produce these wrap-target shapes (Shape's tuples lower to TypedObject records per the C+ amendment, which means trait return types of `(Self, Self)` lower to `TypedObject { 0: Self, 1: Self }`; the rewrap would need a numbered-field reverse lookup that's a separate emission-tier rebuild). The dispatch shell surfaces with a structured error citing the in-place-write follow-up. (3) `VTableEntry::Closure` arm reserved at the dispatch tier with a structured-error surface — the W7 closure-trait-impl emission gate hasn't landed, so no construction site exists; the dispatch arm makes the contract visible.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-trait-object-emission` | `../shape-w17-trait-object-emission` | W17-trait-object-emission — compiler-side dyn-coerce grammar + emit + DynMethodCall (the BoxTraitObject upstream gap) | — | Pest grammar for `dyn`-coerce syntax (verify or extend), type inference for dyn coercion, bytecode emit for `OpCode::BoxTraitObject` + `OpCode::DynMethodCall`, compiler-side vtable lookup at coerce-to-dyn time. End-to-end §2.7.24 Q25.C smoke: `let a: dyn Animal = box(Dog{...}); print(a.name())`. | **closed** (Wave-2.6 round-2 close 2026-05-11; emission-tier compiler+runtime half of ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.C landed end-to-end per the rescope contract — the 4 SURFACE bodies in `executor/trait_object_ops.rs` (BoxTraitObject / DynMethodCall / DropCall / DropCallAsync) replaced with real implementations, dyn-coerce sites in `let a: dyn T = ...` emit `OpCode::BoxTraitObject` with trait-name `Operand::Name`, dyn-method-call sites in `a.method()` (where `a` is dyn-tracked) emit `OpCode::DynMethodCall` with `TypedMethodCall` operand. Grammar: `TypeAnnotation::Dyn(traits)` already parses via `dyn_type = "dyn" ~ qualified_ident ~ ("+" ~ qualified_ident)*` in `crates/shape-ast/src/shape.pest:864`; no grammar extension needed. Type inference: dyn-typed locals tracked in two new `HashMap<u16, String>` maps on `BytecodeCompiler` (`dyn_locals` / `dyn_module_bindings`) keyed by slot index → trait name, populated at `Statement::VariableDecl` for both function-local and module-binding paths. Bytecode emit: `compile_expr_method_call` (`expressions/function_calls.rs`) checks `Expr::Identifier` receivers against these maps BEFORE compiling the receiver (since `compile_expr` overwrites `last_expr_*` state); when the receiver is dyn-tracked, emits `DynMethodCall` instead of `CallMethod`. Compile-tier vtable construction: new `compiler/trait_object_emission.rs` exposes `build_and_register_vtable` called from `register_item_functions` at `Item::Impl` entry; walks impl methods, classifies each via `Erase_T` (top-level `Self` in return → `BoxedReturn` w/ `wrap_targets=[path=[]]`; no Self / no generics → `Direct`; nested Self or Self-arg or method-generics → `Compound` with flags set, surface-and-stop at dispatch). Vtables stored in new `program.trait_vtables: HashMap<String, Arc<VTable>>` keyed by `"Trait::ConcreteType"`. Threaded through Program→ContentAddressedProgram→LinkedProgram→BytecodeProgram via per-step `.clone()` + propagated through `load_linked_program` and `linked_to_bytecode_program`. Bytecode-cache integration: `BytecodeCache::put` skips caching for trait-bearing programs (same pattern as the closure-bearing skip — `Arc<VTable>` is `#[serde(skip)]`). Runtime: `op_box_trait_object` recovers `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` via canonical `Arc::from_raw` (3ac2f11 pattern), looks up `Arc<VTable>` from program registry, allocates `Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` and pushes via `push_kinded(bits, Ptr(HeapKind::TraitObject))`. `op_dyn_method_call` recovers `Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` via transient borrow, resolves the impl function name via runtime `trait_method_symbols` lookup (`Trait::Type::__default__::method` → function name) + `function_name_index` → runtime function id (post-link), then dispatches per `VTableEntry` variant. `op_call_method` was extended with a TraitObject receiver fast-path that re-routes to the dyn dispatch shell — handles the `let b = a.clone_me()` case where the compiler doesn't yet propagate `dyn T` through method-call result types (compile-tier follow-up; deferred). `op_drop_call_sync` / `op_drop_call_async` look up `Drop::<TypeName>::__default__::drop` / `drop_async` from `trait_method_symbols`; silent no-op if no Drop impl is registered (the kinded pop already retired the share). End-to-end smoke on `--mode vm` (after `rm -rf ~/.shape/cache/bytecode` to invalidate stale cache): `let a: dyn Animal = Dog { name: "Rex" }; print(a.name()); let b = a.clone_me(); print(b.name())` → prints `Rex\nRex`. Direct dispatch (`name()`) and BoxedReturn dispatch (`clone_me()`) both verified. **Deferred** (in-scope but surfaced-and-stopped): `VTableEntry::SelfArg` (§Q25.C.2 Self-in-arg runtime vtable-identity check), `Generic` (§Q25.C.3 method-generic TypeInfo threading), `Compound` (§Q25.C.5 multi-flag thunk), `BoxedReturn` with nested wrap-targets (`Result<Self,E>`, `Option<Self>`, `(Self,Self)`, etc.) — each surfaces as `VMError::NotImplemented(SURFACE: ... per ADR-006 §Q25.C.5 ...)` at `op_dyn_method_call` dispatch time. `Closure` variant (W7 closure-trait-impl dispatch) deferred. IC devirtualization per §Q25.C.6 deferred (round-2 budget). LSP cost-class inlay hints per §Q25.C.7 deferred. Compile-tier dyn-type propagation through method-call result types deferred (runtime `op_call_method` TraitObject fast-path covers correctness). `verify-merge.sh`: Passed 11 / Failed 0. `check-no-dynamic.sh` exit 0. `cargo test -p shape-value --lib`: 398/398 pass; shape-runtime --lib 1013/1013 pass; shape-vm compiler:: 363/363 pass. NO `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added. NO ADR amendment needed — §2.7.24 Q25.C spec unchanged.) | 2026-05-11 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-snapshot-roundtrip` | `../shape-w17-snapshot-roundtrip` | W17-snapshot-roundtrip — actual snapshot/resume round-trip rebuild + wire-format extension (rescoped from W17-snapshot-resume) | — | (a) Fill `invoke_module_fn_id_stub` at `vm_impl/modules.rs:75` (upstream blocker out of W17-snapshot scope); (b) ADR-006 §2.7.5.1 `SerializableVMValue` extension for post-W14/W15/W16 HeapKinds (HashSet, Iterator, Result, Option, Deque, Channel, PriorityQueue, Reference, FilterExpr, SharedCell — needs amendment landed in branch's own commit); (c) kind-threaded `slot_to_serializable` / `serializable_to_slot` API alignment; (d) restore comptime introspection (`type_info`, `implements`, etc.). End-to-end smoke: `let s = snapshot(); ... mutate ...; resume(s); ...` produces deterministic state-restoration. | **closed** (Wave-2.6 close; commit pending; rescoped from W17-snapshot-resume 2026-05-11 — surface-cleanup shipped as W17-snapshot-surfaces in `0db5920`; this commit is the actual rebuild phase. **Wire-format extension (ADR-006 §2.7.5.1.A amendment landed in this commit per W17-concurrency §2.7.25 precedent):** added 15 new `SerializableVMValue` arms (HashSet/Iterator/Result-typed-Arc/Option-typed-Arc/Deque/Channel/PriorityQueue/Reference/FilterExpr/SharedCell/Mutex/Atomic/Lazy/Char/BigInt) covering all 13 playbook-listed kinds + Wave 2.5 Mutex/Atomic/Lazy + scalar Char/BigInt. Arm coverage policy: full payload round-trip where storage is trivially serializable (HashSet keys, PriorityQueue heap, AtomicI64 value, Char codepoint, BigInt i64); discriminator-only opaque arms where deep walks re-introduce the Arc<HeapValue> generic-serializer shape §2.7.5.1 forbids (Iterator graph / Deque heterogeneous elements / Channel queue / Reference identity / FilterExpr AST / SharedCell binding-storage / Mutex inner KindedSlot / Lazy initializer-closure). **Kind-threaded API (§2.7.5):** `slot_to_serializable(bits, kind, store) -> Result<SerializableVMValue, String>` and `serializable_to_slot(sv, expected_kind, store) -> Result<(u64, NativeKind), String>` landed in `shape-runtime/src/snapshot.rs`. Scalar kinds project from raw u64 bits (Int64/Int32/Int16/Int8/UInt64/UInt32/UInt16/UInt8/IntSize/UIntSize/Float64/Bool/String). Heap kinds use the canonical 5-arm receiver-recovery soundness pattern (`Arc::<XData>::from_raw(bits) → clone/read → Arc::into_raw(arc)` to restore the original share) per CLAUDE.md "The 5-arm receiver-recovery soundness rule" — casting bits to `*const HeapValue` (the early-draft approach) was wrong-type recovery (the bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<XData>)`, not `*const HeapValue`) and segfaults. Nullable-scalar kinds surface clean (sentinel-rule amendment is the W17-snapshot-nullable follow-up). **`invoke_module_fn_id_stub` resolution:** filled per §2.7.4/§2.7.5 — dispatches through the existing `module_fn_table` (sum-typed `Typed`/`TypedAsync` per Phase 4c.3) with `(args: &[KindedSlot]) -> Result<KindedSlot, VMError>` shape. Sync arm calls `TypedModuleFunction.invoke` with raw `&[u64]` bits + ModuleContext, then projects the `TypedReturn` to a KindedSlot via `project_typed_return`. Async arm uses `tokio::task::block_in_place(handle.block_on(fut))` on the ambient runtime; surfaces clean if no runtime. `project_typed_return` covers `ConcreteReturn::{I64, F64, Bool, Unit, String, OpaqueTypedObject}` end-to-end; container/wrapper arms (Ok/Err/Some/None/ArrayHeapValue/etc.) surface to W17-marshal-return-arms follow-up. **VM snapshot/resume (`crates/shape-vm/src/executor/snapshot.rs`):** `VirtualMachine::snapshot()` projects the live `(stack, kinds)` pair through `slot_to_serializable`, similarly for `(module_bindings, module_binding_kinds)`. Loop/timeframe/exception_handlers are structural-only (no slot payload). Call-stack frames preserve structural identity (return_ip / locals_base / locals_count / function_id) but per-frame upvalues are None at landing — non-empty call_stack on resume surfaces W17-snapshot-callstack-upvalues follow-up. `VirtualMachine::from_snapshot` is the symmetric inverse — uses `serializable_to_slot` per slot, pads module_binding tracks via `module_binding_pad_to_kinded` per §2.7.8/Q10. **Comptime introspection (build_config / implements / warning / error):** wired via `register_typed_function` in `compiler/comptime_builtins.rs`; bodies are end-to-end at the TypedModuleFunction layer. End-to-end VM dispatch for `comptime { build_config() }` is BLOCKED by the upstream `populate_module_objects` no-op stub (vm_impl/modules.rs:94 — needs a substantial separate workstream to build TypedObject module-bindings storing function-id field references that compiled bytecode's `LoadModuleBinding + GetFieldTyped + CallValue` chain dispatches through). `type_info` was intentionally removed by C2-comptime-rebuild and surfaces a structured "type_info has been removed" error per the existing `test_comptime_only_builtins_rejected_outside_comptime` gate. **6 W17 unit tests (executor/snapshot.rs::tests):** `test_w17_vm_snapshot_empty_ok` (empty VM snapshots cleanly), `test_w17_snapshot_roundtrip_scalar_state` (Int/Float/Bool stack round-trip end-to-end), `test_w17_snapshot_result_option_roundtrip` (Ok(42)/Some("hello")/None round-trip end-to-end), `test_w17_snapshot_hashset_roundtrip` (HashSet keys), `test_w17_snapshot_resume_incompatible_surfaces_error` (IteratorOpaque resume surfaces structured error, not panic), `test_w17_state_bodies_return_structured_errors` (state.* bodies still surface Err per W17-snapshot-surfaces preservation). All 6/6 pass. **27-surface-site disposition:** 0 of 27 production sites converted to happy-path (the state.* / VmStateSnapshot trait bodies remain surface per the W17-snapshot-surfaces close shape; the snapshot capability now works at the VM-direct `VirtualMachine::snapshot()` / `from_snapshot()` level — which is what end-to-end snapshot/resume actually drives; the state.serialize / state.diff / state.patch / state.capture_* / state.resume bodies are richer surfaces requiring follow-up `state.serialize → slot_to_serializable` wire-up plus the `state_diff` 1486-LoC module rebuild). **Pre-existing test failures (out of territory):** `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` SIGSEGVs at array_ops::first_int_array et al. (verified to fail identically at baseline `235256e` without my changes — this is the pre-existing `test_object_operations` SIGSEGV class per hardening §d, broader v2-raw-heap-audit territory). My 6 W17 tests + shape-vm executor::snapshot subset (19 tests) + shape-runtime --lib (1013 tests) all pass cleanly. **No `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added** — the closure-share bump pattern from `06d0d66` is not needed in snapshot/resume territory (the kind-threaded serialize/deserialize paths don't re-invoke closures). **Decisions beyond rescope note:** (1) used the canonical 5-arm receiver-recovery pattern (Arc::from_raw → read → Arc::into_raw) for typed-Arc heap arms after early-draft as_heap_value() approach segfaulted on Arc<HashSetData> bits — the §2.7.6/Q8 docstring's "slot.as_heap_value() + HeapValue::* match" path is for Box<HeapValue> slots only, NOT typed-Arc bits; (2) chose opaque-stub round-trip over deep-recursive serialization for kinds with cross-value graphs (Iterator/Deque/Channel/FilterExpr/SharedCell/Mutex/Lazy/Reference) to honor §2.7.5.1 forbid on Arc<HeapValue> generic serializer — discriminator round-trips cleanly, deep payload restoration is per-arm follow-up; (3) the `populate_module_objects` no-op stub (Phase-2c since `54883b2`) is the upstream blocker for comptime introspection end-to-end at the VM dispatch level — its rebuild requires substantial TypedObject construction across the module registry + per-function function-id encoding, separate workstream beyond W17-snapshot-roundtrip's effort budget. **Sites I couldn't fix (surface-and-stop with §-cite):** non-empty call_stack snapshot (W17-snapshot-callstack-upvalues, §2.7.5.1), Deque element-payload deep walk (W17-snapshot-deque, §2.7.5.1), Channel queue contents (W17-snapshot-channel-queue, §2.7.5.1), Iterator graph walker (W17-snapshot-iterator, §2.7.5.1), FilterExpr AST tree (W17-snapshot-filter-expr, §2.7.5.1), SharedCell per-kind payload (W17-snapshot-sharedcell, §2.7.5.1), Mutex inner KindedSlot (W17-snapshot-mutex-payload, §2.7.5.1), Lazy initializer-closure (W17-snapshot-lazy-closure, §2.7.5.1), Reference target identity (W17-snapshot-references, §2.7.5.1), nullable-scalar sentinel rule (W17-snapshot-nullable, §2.7.5.1), populate_module_objects → end-to-end comptime introspection (separate workstream, §2.7.4). verify-merge.sh: Passed 11/Failed 0. check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0 (baseline lowered 17→15 for `synthesize_value_word_from_raw` from the W17 surface-text deletion). cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests exit 0.) | 2026-05-11 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-state-tier-roundtrip` | `../shape-w17-state-tier-roundtrip` | W17-state-tier-roundtrip — wires state.* trait bodies + VmStateSnapshot on top of W17-snapshot-roundtrip's slot_to_serializable / serializable_to_slot API | — | `executor/state_builtins/{introspection,core}.rs` (9+8) + `executor/vm_state_snapshot.rs` ×8 + `executor/snapshot.rs` (per-frame upvalues + non-empty call_stack restore) + `executor/vm_impl/modules.rs` (vm_state wired into ModuleContext) + `executor/mod.rs:476` (pending_resume doc) | **closed** (Wave-3 close 2026-05-12; built on W17-snapshot-roundtrip foundation (commit `1e2bc69` / merge `fbfbfb6`). **VmStateSnapshot rebuilt** — pre-bulldozer `unreachable!()` panics replaced with kind-threaded data carrier: captures (frames, current_args, current_locals, module_bindings, instruction_count) at snapshot time, each KindedSlot owns its share via `clone_with_kind` dispatch. Per-frame upvalues recovered from `CallFrame.closure_heap_bits` / `closure_heap_kind` via `OwnedClosureBlock::read_capture_kinded` (§2.7.8/Q10 cell-storage parallel-kind track). `try_borrow_closure_block` looks up layout via `program.closure_function_layouts[fn_id]` (Vec<Option<Arc<ClosureLayout>>>, NOT keyed by type_id — that earlier reading was wrong), bumps refcount via `retain_typed_closure`, recovers OwnedClosureBlock via `from_raw`. **invoke_module_fn_id_stub wired** — `vm_state: Some(&vm_state_snap)` populated in ModuleContext via `self.capture_vm_state()` before each module-function dispatch. **state.* trait bodies (9+8)**: introspection.rs (9): state_capture/capture_all/capture_module/capture_call/caller/args/locals/resume/resume_frame — bodies now read `ctx.vm_state` and route slots through accessor API; container-typed returns (FrameState/VmState/ModuleState/CallPayload/FunctionRef?/Array<any>/Map<string,any>) surface clean at the marshal boundary per `project_typed_return`'s current scalar-only coverage (W17-marshal-return-arms follow-up). state.resume wires `set_pending_resume` when ctx callback is present. core.rs (8 of which 7 body): state_hash + state_schema_hash wired end-to-end (return `ConcreteReturn::String`); state_fn_hash wired via `ctx.function_hashes[fn_id]` lookup (surfaces clean when closure recovery / FunctionRef heap-kind paths aren't fully threaded); state_serialize body computes bytes but Array<int>/Bytes return needs marshal-return-arms; state_deserialize/diff/patch surface clean (diff/patch depend on deleted 1486-LoC state_diff module rebuild). **Non-empty call_stack roundtrip**: `from_snapshot` removed its blanket non-empty guard; `restore_call_stack` walks `SerializableCallFrame` and (a) for closures with registered ClosureLayout, allocates a fresh OwnedClosureBlock + writes each capture via `serializable_to_slot(sv, layout.capture_native_kind(i), store)` + writes the resulting bits via `write_capture_raw_u64` → installs as `closure_heap_bits` / `closure_heap_kind=Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)`; (b) for non-closure or no-layout frames, falls back to `Vec<u64>` upvalue carrier (legacy raw-bits path). `snapshot_call_stack_for_export` projects each frame's upvalues via the closure block walk + `slot_to_serializable` for each capture (full payload per supported scalar/heap-light kinds; opaque arms tag as `IteratorOpaque` sentinel and surface clean on resume). **Per-kind deep payload restoration**: kept opaque-stub for Deque/Channel/Iterator/FilterExpr/SharedCell/Mutex/Lazy/Reference per §2.7.5.1 forbid on Arc<HeapValue> generic serializer — discriminator round-trips clean, deep payloads need their own per-arm follow-up. Full payload kinds (HashSet, PriorityQueueHeap, AtomicI64, Char, BigInt, Decimal, String, Result-scalar-inner, Option-scalar-inner) round-trip end-to-end via W17-snapshot-roundtrip's existing wire-format arms. **Sites that stayed surface (cite-refined)**: nullable-scalar sentinel rule (W17-snapshot-nullable, §2.7.5.1); Deque/Channel/Iterator/Reference/FilterExpr/SharedCell/Mutex/Lazy deep payloads (per-kind follow-ups); state.serialize/deserialize Bytes return arm (W17-marshal-return-arms); state.diff/patch (1486-LoC state_diff module rebuild — out of state-tier scope); state.capture/capture_all/capture_module/caller/args/locals typed-object/Array/Map returns (W17-marshal-return-arms); state.resume/resume_frame body completion depends on set_pending_resume callback wiring in invoke_module_fn_id_stub (today vm_state is wired but set_pending_resume is None — requires Cell-based interior mutability shape, follow-up); state_resume_frame typed-object field-decode for ip_offset/locals (W17-marshal-return-arms); state.serialize need for `SnapshotStore` on `ModuleContext` (tempdir-backed ephemeral_store used in body — acceptable for content-hashing, follow-up for high-rate callers). data/cache.rs (DataCache::snapshot/restore_from_snapshot) and context/mod.rs (ExecutionContext::snapshot/restore_from_snapshot) stay surface-and-stop per existing W17 message — deep DataFrame and scope-binding serialization is its own substantial workstream beyond the state.* tier. **9 W17 tests pass** (4 new added — `test_w17_vm_state_snapshot_empty_accessor`, `test_w17_vm_state_snapshot_kind_threaded_locals`, `test_w17_snapshot_non_closure_callstack_roundtrip`, plus existing 6 from W17-snapshot-roundtrip; gate test `test_w17_state_bodies_return_structured_errors` still passes — every state.* body surfaces structured Err containing "W17-snapshot-resume surface" + "§2.7.4" cite). Schema test + module-export test pass. Pre-existing test failures observed: `executor::tests::test_type_method_to_string_returns_canonical_name` etc. are `todo!()` macros at `executor/tests/mod.rs:1110+` (last touched commit `bbd9462a` W11-vm-tests close — pre-existing, not territory); array_ops SIGSEGV (per CLAUDE.md "v2-raw-heap aliasing" pre-existing class). **No `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs** — kind-threaded read paths don't re-invoke closures, no share-bump workaround needed. **No ADR amendments** — §2.7.5.1.A foundation from W17-snapshot-roundtrip is consumed verbatim. verify-merge.sh: Passed 11 / Failed 0. check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0. cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests exit 0. | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-comptime-vm-dispatch` | `../shape-w17-comptime-vm-dispatch` | W17-comptime-vm-dispatch — rebuild `populate_module_objects` so the compiled bytecode chain `LoadModuleBinding + GetFieldTyped + CallValue` dispatches through TypedObject module-bindings storing function-id field references | — | `executor/vm_impl/modules.rs::populate_module_objects` (no-op stub → kinded TypedObject construction) + `executor/typed_object_ops.rs::op_get_field_typed` (canonical 5-arm receiver-recovery soundness fix + FIELD_TAG_ANY → field_kinds resolver) + `executor/call_convention.rs::call_value_immediate_nb` (new `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::ModuleFn)` dispatch arm) + new `HeapKind::ModuleFn=33` pure-discriminator HeapKind with 4-table lockstep | **closed** (Wave-3 close 2026-05-12; ADR-006 §2.7.26 amendment landed — module-function-id inline-scalar HeapKind ordinal 33 added to fill the upstream dispatch blocker flagged by W17-snapshot-roundtrip (`fbfbfb6`). **`populate_module_objects` rebuild shape**: iterates `module_registry.modules()` (33 native modules at populate time), for each: collects sync `TypedModuleFunction` + async `TypedModuleAsyncFunction` exports, registers each into `module_fn_table` via `register_module_fn_entry` to obtain a `module_fn_id`, looks up the predeclared `__mod_<name>` schema (registered by the compiler's `ensure_module_object_schema`), builds slots in schema field order — each callable field gets `ValueSlot::from_raw(fn_id as u64)` with `field_kinds[i] = NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::ModuleFn)` and heap_mask bit set; unmatched fields get the `(0, NativeKind::Bool)` sentinel pair. Constructs `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` via the typed `TypedObjectStorage::new` per ADR-006 §2.4 + writes through `module_binding_write_kinded(binding_idx, bits, Ptr(TypedObject))` per §2.7.8/Q10 lockstep. Binding-name resolution: prefers the hidden `__imported_module__::<name>` binding (compiler-injected by `ensure_hidden_native_module_binding`) and falls back to the plain name. Modules with no `__mod_<name>` schema are silently skipped — the binding stays at the no-op sentinel and any reference will surface clean at GetFieldTyped. **Dispatch chain end-to-end**: (1) `LoadModuleBinding(idx)` reads the populated TypedObject via `module_binding_read_kinded_raw` and pushes via `clone_with_kind` retain-on-read (§2.7.7); (2) `GetFieldTyped { type_id, field_idx, field_type_tag=FIELD_TAG_ANY }` recovers `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` via canonical `Arc::<X>::from_raw` (3ac2f11 precedent — the pre-W17 `as_heap_value()` cast was wrong-type recovery on `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` bits and segfaulted on schema_id alignment with HeapKind discriminator ordinals), then routes the FIELD_TAG_ANY operand to new helper `push_field_value_with_kind(slot, kind)` which sources the kind from `storage.field_kinds[i]` rather than the schema (resolves hardening item (f) — "`Any → Dynamic` readback bounded by `KindedSlot.kind`"); pushes `(module_fn_id as u64, NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::ModuleFn))`; (3) `CallValue` pops args + callee, dispatches via `call_value_immediate_nb`'s new `Ptr(HeapKind::ModuleFn)` arm which routes `bits as usize` to `invoke_module_fn_id_stub(fn_id, args)` (the upstream stub W17-snapshot-roundtrip wired at `fbfbfb6`). **Smoke results (6 W17 unit tests, all pass)**: `w17_comptime_arithmetic_sanity` (regression — comptime arithmetic still works after populate_module_objects rebuild); `w17_comptime_build_config_dispatches_end_to_end` (dispatch reaches body cleanly — body itself crashes on a pre-existing `kinded.slot().as_heap_value()` wrong-type recovery on `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` bits in `comptime_builtins.rs:282`, fixed in this commit using canonical `Arc::<TypedObjectStorage>::from_raw` pattern — same 5-arm soundness rule); `w17_comptime_implements_dispatches_end_to_end` (returns Bool false for unregistered trait); `w17_comptime_warning_dispatches_end_to_end` (returns Unit, body emits stderr); `w17_comptime_error_dispatches_end_to_end` (returns Err with `[comptime error]` prefix — arg-kind marshalling shim arg-as-Bool is pre-existing `register_typed_function` variadic-Bool issue out of W17 territory); `w17_type_info_removal_contract_preserved` (`type_info("Point")` outside comptime surfaces `"type_info has been removed"` — C2-comptime-rebuild removal contract preserved). **4-table lockstep ord 33 landed clean** (verify-merge.sh CHECK 6 confirms): `clone_with_kind` / `drop_with_kind` in `vm_impl/stack.rs` (inline-scalar no-op, mirror of `HeapKind::Future` / `HeapKind::Char`); `KindedSlot::Drop` / `Clone` in `kinded_slot.rs`; `SharedCell::drop` in `v2/closure_layout.rs`; `TypedObjectStorage::drop` in `heap_value.rs`. Knock-on arms: kind_type_name in arithmetic/comparison/typed_access returns "module_fn"; printing.rs renders `<module_fn:id>`; PHF classifier in `objects/mod.rs:696` routes ModuleFn to None group (callable via `op_call_value`, not method PHF); JSON `cannot serialize: ModuleFn` reject; wire `<module_fn:id>` opaque tag; HeapValue Clone + Display + kind() + is_truthy() + type_name() arms. **Decisions beyond playbook**: (1) chose pure-discriminator inline-scalar HeapKind (mirror of Future/Char) over typed-Arc carrier — `module_fn_id` is a `usize` index into `module_fn_table`, no heap state, no refcount work needed; clone/drop are no-op arms. (2) `op_get_field_typed` receiver recovery was changed from `slot.as_heap_value()` to canonical `Arc::<TypedObjectStorage>::from_raw` — the prior `as_heap_value()` cast was wrong-type recovery (the bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedObjectStorage>)` per `ValueSlot::from_typed_object`, NOT `Arc::into_raw(Arc<HeapValue>)`) and would segfault whenever schema_id happened to align with a small HeapKind ordinal — the pre-W17 path "happened to work" for some receivers but was unsound. (3) `comptime_builtins.rs::build_config` body also had the same wrong-type `as_heap_value()` recovery; fixed in this commit using the same canonical pattern. Both are 5-arm receiver-recovery soundness rule applications per CLAUDE.md. (4) `push_field_value_with_kind(slot, kind)` is the new FIELD_TAG_ANY → field_kinds resolver — bounded by `NativeKind` arity, NO Bool-default fallback for unhandled tags (§2.7.7 #9). **Sites I couldn't fix (surface-and-stop with §-cite)**: The 4 wired comptime forms (build_config / implements / warning / error) are end-to-end via VM dispatch in this commit, no remaining W17-comptime-vm-dispatch surfaces. The pre-existing `register_typed_function` variadic arg_kinds-as-Bool placeholder (marshal.rs:2031 — `arg_kinds: Vec<NativeKind> = params.iter().map(|_| NativeKind::Bool).collect()`) is upstream of this sub-cluster — when bodies inspect `nb_args.first().and_then(|nb| nb.as_str())` with an arg whose actual kind is String, the wrapper's NativeKind::Bool wrapping yields `as_str() → None` and the body falls through to a kind-name diagnostic (`<Bool>`). Tracked as W17-variadic-arg-kinds follow-up per ADR-006 §2.7.5/Q-amendment-TBD. **`type_info` is intentionally NOT restored** per C2-comptime-rebuild removal contract — `test_comptime_only_builtins_rejected_outside_comptime` preserves the "type_info has been removed" structured error. **No `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added** — no closure-share workarounds needed in this territory. **verify-merge.sh**: Passed 11 / Failed 0, exit 0 (CHECK 6 confirms HeapKind::ModuleFn in 4/4 lockstep tables). **`check-no-dynamic.sh`**: exit 0. `cargo check --workspace --lib + --tests` exit 0. `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib compiler::comptime` 31 passed / 0 failed / 4 ignored (4 ignored are pre-existing phase-2c deferrals — `test_target_captures_vmvalue_included`, `test_target_to_vmvalue`, `test_target_to_vmvalue_with_field_annotations`, `placeholder_phase_2c_comptime_tests` — all `#[ignore = "phase-2c — comptime rebuild against typed-Arc HeapValue layout — see ADR-006 §2.7.4"]`). `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib compiler::` 369 passed. `cargo test -p shape-runtime --lib` 1013 passed. **Pre-existing test failures (out of territory, verified at baseline `13d63ed7`)**: `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` shows ~329 failures in `executor::builtins::array_ops::*` / `executor::tests::channel_ops::*` / `executor::object_ops::*` etc. — same `array_ops::first_int_array` SIGSEGV class documented by W17-snapshot-roundtrip's close. `cargo test -p shape-value --lib` shows 2 failures (`hashmap_mutation::insert_overwrite_releases_old_value_share` / `remove_releases_value_share` Arc-refcount-2 expectation) — also pre-existing at baseline `13d63ed7`. Both clusters belong to the broader v2-raw-heap-audit territory (hardening §d).) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-concurrency` | `../shape-w17-concurrency` | W17-concurrency — Mutex / Atomic / Lazy new HeapKinds | — | `shape-value/heap_value.rs` (MutexData/AtomicData/LazyData) + `heap_variants.rs` (HeapKind::Mutex=30, Atomic=31, Lazy=32) + 4 lockstep tables ×3 ord + `executor/objects/concurrency_methods.rs:40,52,64,78,90,102,114,126,144,156` + `executor/vm_impl/builtins.rs:678` (ctors) | idle (close 2026-05-11, commit `6ba7e59`; three new typed-Arc HeapKinds landed end-to-end per ADR-006 §2.7.25 amendment, mirror of §2.7.20 Channel structure. Pre-assigned ordinals 30/31/32 held (no collision with parallel W17-typed-carrier-monomorphization=29). Storage tier: MutexData (Mutex<Option<KindedSlot>> for interior mutability — shared-cell-with-exclusion shape), AtomicData (std::sync::atomic::AtomicI64, SeqCst, i64-only at landing per W15-priority-queue precedent), LazyData (Mutex<initializer+cached> for OnceCell-style serialization). All three full HeapValue arms (NOT pure-discriminator) — receiver classification flows through slot.as_heap_value() per ADR-005 §1. 4-table lockstep ×3 ords landed clean (verify-merge.sh CHECK 6 confirms). Handler tier: 10 SURFACE'd handlers flipped to real bodies + added v2_mutex_get (m.value property-access form out of scope for W17-concurrency; method form is the equivalent — MUTEX_METHODS PHF gained "get" entry alongside playbook-listed lock/try_lock/set). v2_lazy_get routes through vm.call_value_immediate_nb per §2.7.11/Q12 (W17-make-closure partial-gate `aa47364` unlocked the re-entry shape). Each handler uses canonical 5-arm receiver-recovery soundness pattern (Arc::from_raw → clone → into_raw per `3ac2f11`). Knock-on arms: kind_type_name in arithmetic/comparison/typed_access; printing.rs formatters (`<mutex>`, `<atomic:N>`, `<lazy:initialized/pending>`); PHF classifier in objects/mod.rs routes new HeapKinds to MUTEX/ATOMIC/LAZY_METHODS; JSON reject + wire opaque-tag in shape-runtime. Ctors: Mutex(initial:any), Atomic(initial:i64), Lazy(closure) — kind-validated at the ctor; non-int Atomic / non-closure Lazy error cleanly. 18 storage-tier tests pin contracts + refcount-on-drop invariants — 18/18 pass. Smoke end-to-end on `--mode vm`: `let m=Mutex(0); m.lock(); m.set(5); print(m.get())` → 5; `let a=Atomic(0); print(a.fetch_add(1)); print(a.load())` → 0/1; `let l=Lazy(|| 42); print(l.get()); print(l.is_initialized())` → 42/true. Lazy idempotency verified (l.get() returns same cached value 3×). ADR-006 §2.7.25 amendment landed. verify-merge.sh: Passed 11 / Failed 0. cargo check --workspace --lib + cargo test -p shape-value --lib concurrency_storage all exit 0. Pre-existing channel_ops test failures (14 cases) at baseline f36bf1e — unrelated to W17-concurrency territory. NO phase-2d-hardening:(f) breadcrumbs added — the closure-share bump pattern from `06d0d66` precedent is not needed here since neither Mutex/Atomic nor Lazy.get invokes its closure repeatedly per receiver; the single-invocation Lazy.get path handles share discipline cleanly through call_value_immediate_nb.) | 2026-05-11 |
