| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-iterator-reference-rebuild` | `../shape-w17-iterator-reference-rebuild` | W17-iterator-reference-rebuild — `op_iter_done` / `op_iter_next` for Reference-shape iterators (`TypedArrayData::TypedObject` carrier) | — | `executor/loops/mod.rs::op_iter_done` + `::op_iter_next` (heap-side dispatch arms) | **closed** (Wave-3, 2026-05-12; commit pending. Surface root cause: `op_iter_done` / `op_iter_next` were dispatching every heap-bearing `iter_kind` through a single `slot.as_heap_value()` cast and matching the returned `HeapValue::*` variant. The cast is wrong-type recovery per the 5-arm soundness rule — slot bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<T>)` for the kind-specific `T`, not a `Box<HeapValue>` allocation. It worked coincidentally when the inner `T`'s discriminator at offset 0 happened to coincide with a `HeapValue::*` arm of the right shape. The killer case is `TypedArrayData::TypedObject` (post-bundle-A's `Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<TypedObjectStorage>>>` carrier produced by `HashMap.entries()` / `Array.zip()` / `IteratorTransform::Enumerate`): `TypedArrayData::TypedObject` ordinal 20 aliases `HeapValue::Reference` ordinal 20, so `as_heap_value()` reads back as `HeapValue::Reference(_)`, surfacing "iter_kind=Ptr(TypedArray) but heap arm is Reference" at every `for entry in m.entries()`. **Fix**: rewrote both `op_iter_done` and `op_iter_next` to dispatch per-kind via `Arc::from_raw::<T>(bits)` + borrowed read + `Arc::into_raw(arc)` restore (the canonical receiver-recovery sound-pattern from `iterator_methods::clone_typed_array_arc` / `range_methods::clone_range_arc`). Each `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::*)` arm now recovers the typed `T` directly: `TypedArrayData` for `Ptr(TypedArray)` (len from new centralized `typed_array_data_len` helper covering all 23 variants), `String` for `Ptr(String)` / `NativeKind::String`, `DataTable` for `Ptr(DataTable)`, `TableViewData` for `Ptr(TableView)`, `HashMapData` for `Ptr(HashMap)`. `op_iter_next`'s `TypedObject` element push (and the other 22 `TypedArrayData::*` arms) was already correct in the pre-existing `push_typed_array_element` helper — only the dispatcher needed the typed-Arc projection fix. **Smoke target end-to-end** under `--mode vm`: `m.set("a",1).set("b",2).set("c",3); for entry in m.entries() { print(entry.key, entry.value) }` → `a 1 / b 2 / c 3`. Pure-int array iteration (`for x in [1,2,3]`) and string iteration (`for c in "abc"`) still work (v2 raw-pointer fast path + Ptr(String) arm respectively). 3 new regression unit tests in `executor::loops::tests` pin the `TypedArrayData::TypedObject` carrier's done/next behavior so the wrong-type `as_heap_value()` cast can't creep back. **Out of territory**: `xs.zip(ys)` and `xs.enumerate()` smokes hit upstream surfaces — `handle_zip_v2` itself is `NotImplemented(SURFACE)` and `enumerate` isn't registered for v2 raw-pointer (`UInt64`-kinded) arrays; the global `builtin_zip` does construct the post-bundle-A Pair shape correctly but isn't reachable via the method-dispatch path for v2-carrier receivers. Those are W17-array-typed-receiver / W17-array-closure-callback cross-territories, not the Reference-shape iterator surface. `op_iter_next` for `DataTable` / `TableView` row materialization (RowView packed-tag carrier) and direct HashMap iteration remain surface-and-stop with §2.7.4 cites — same pre-existing disposition as the `executor/tests/table_iteration.rs` `todo!("phase-2c …")` markers. Test impact: 17 → 14 failures in `executor::tests::table_iteration` (3 new passes: `test_iter_done_datatable_false_when_in_bounds`, `test_iter_done_datatable_true_at_end`, `test_iter_done_negative_index` — these stopped surfacing on the unfixed `as_heap_value()` arm and now hit the correct `DataTable` row count check). cargo check --workspace --lib clean; verify-merge.sh: Passed 11 / Failed 0 (SCRIPT_EXIT=0); check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0. NO `phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added — the fix is the architected receiver-recovery pattern; no defection-attractor workaround.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-item-6-gate` | `../shape-item-6-gate` | Phase 2d Wave 4 Item 6 — `--all-targets` gating + justfile recipe hygiene | — | `justfile` + `CLAUDE.md` (Commands section) + `scripts/verify-merge.sh` (CHECK 2 sync) | closed (Wave-4, 2026-05-12; commit pending. Audit findings: (1) `shape-app` / `shape-server` are NOT workspace members in `shape/` — they live in a separate workspace at `../shape-app/`. The supervisor-prompt's "v8-blocked" exclusion list was misdirected. (2) Real `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` blockage is two `crates/shape-vm/benches/*.rs` files referencing deleted post-strict-typing shapes: `vm_benchmarks.rs` references `OpCode::Lt` (deleted generic opcode), `typed_access_bench.rs` references `ValueWord` / `ValueWordExt` / `Constant::Value` (deleted W-series shapes). Surface-and-stop: these are Item 5's bench-rebuild territory; do NOT fix in this item. (3) Decision: Option A — exclude `--benches` from the build gate via target selection `--lib --bins --tests --examples`. Widens coverage from the prior `--workspace --lib --tests` baseline (now also covers `--bins` + `--examples`) without touching the broken benches. Option B (fix benches) rejected as multi-day yak-shave belonging to Item 5. (4) Pre-fix state: `just test-check` was broken (used `--workspace --tests --all-targets`, exit 101 on the two benches); verify-merge.sh CHECK 2 used the narrower `--lib --tests` so passed; CHECK 1 (`--lib` only) passed. Changes: (a) `check-clean` recipe added — canonical `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples`; (b) `test-check` recipe rewritten to delegate to `check-clean`; (c) `ci-test` recipe's `--all-targets` swapped to the same target tuple for consistency; (d) `verify-merge.sh` CHECK 2 widened to the canonical command + doc-comment updated; (e) `CLAUDE.md` Commands section gets a Canonical Build Gate paragraph documenting coverage + exclusion rationale + the `just check-clean` invocation. NOT touched: `test-fast` / `test` / `test-all` / `test-deep` / `test-integration` / `test-crate` (these are `cargo test --lib` recipes, narrower scope intentional for iteration speed); `clippy` recipe (pre-existing -D-warnings issues unrelated to the gate); CI workflow files (no CI-side gate references the broken bench targets); any crate code. Final gate state: `just check-clean` exit 0, `just test-check` exit 0, `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 11/11 (SCRIPT_EXIT=0). Surface-and-stop note for Item 5: the two broken bench files reference deleted W-series carriers (`Constant::Value(ValueWord)`, `OpCode::Lt`, `ValueWordExt`); the bench-rebuild should target the kinded-slot / typed-opcode ABI per ADR-006 §2.7. No phase-2d-hardening:(f) breadcrumbs added — gate work doesn't touch closure-share / receiver-recovery code paths.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-mutation-writeback` | `../shape-w17-mutation-writeback` | W17-mutation-writeback — Phase 2d Item 4 ruling (`&mut self` opt-in + Arc-COW write-back + widening lattice + stdlib/builtin sweep) | — | `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/method_registry.rs` (per-kind `MUT_SELF_*` `phf::Set` constants) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mutation_writeback.rs` (new module — `ContainerKind` / `MutSelfWriteBackTarget`) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/{mod,statements,expressions/function_calls}.rs` (compile-time `Dup; Store{Local,ModuleBinding}` writeback emit + ctor-side kind tracking + bespoke push-fast-path gate) + `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/tests/mutation_writeback.rs` (27 source-level smoke tests) + `docs/adr/006-value-and-memory-model.md` (§2.7.27 amendment) | **closed** (Wave-4, 2026-05-12; commits Commit-1 `b5b101fe` (mutation core + operator sugar) + Commit-2 pending (widening lattice + sweep). **Item 4 ruling — `&mut self` opt-in for COW container methods + compile-time Arc write-back.** Pre-ruling `let s = HashSet(); s.add("a"); s.size()` returned 0 (handler returned new Arc after `Arc::make_mut`, no consumer, dropped). Post-ruling the compiler emits `Dup; StoreLocal recv` (or `StoreModuleBinding recv`) after `CallMethod` when the call site matches a `&mut self` opt-in pattern: receiver is `Identifier`, binding is a tracked COW container kind (`HashSet`/`HashMap`/`Deque`/`PriorityQueue`/`Array` via `mut_self_container_locals` populated by ctor-call recognition), method name is in the kind's `MUT_SELF_*` set in `method_registry`. **No `MethodFnV2` ABI change** — the IC fast-path fn-pointer transmute stays unchanged. Write-back is compile-time only — the dispatch shell pops the receiver before invoking the handler, so the source-binding location is lost at runtime; only the compiler knows where to write back. Mirror of the existing `ArrayPushLocal` opcode pattern. **Interior-mutability primitives** (Mutex / Atomic / Lazy / Channel) explicitly NOT registered — their mutating methods preserve receiver Arc identity (mutation through `Cell` / `AtomicI64` / `OnceCell` / channel-buffer), no write-back needed, `let m = Mutex(0); m.set(5)` stays valid (mirror of Rust). **`pop`-shape methods deliberately omitted** — `Array.pop`, `Deque.popBack`/`popFront`, `PriorityQueue.pop` return the popped element (not the receiver Arc); generic write-back would corrupt the binding slot. Listed in MUT_SELF docstrings as the next ABI milestone (tuple-return ABI). **`let` immutability check**: `resolve_mut_self_writeback_target` invokes existing `check_named_binding_write_allowed`; `let s = HashSet(); s.add("x")` fails compile with "Cannot reassign immutable variable 's'. Use `let mut` or `var` for mutable bindings". **R-value receiver — silent drop**: `compute_set().add(x)` — receiver isn't `Identifier`; resolver returns `None`; no writeback emitted. Matches dispatch text decision-call. **Bespoke push fast-path gate**: existing `arr.push(x)` fast paths in `function_calls.rs` and `statements.rs` emit `ArrayPushLocal` (runtime rejects non-Array slot kinds). Both fast paths gated to skip when receiver is a tracked non-Array container; fall through to standard `CallMethod` + §2.7.27 writeback. **Compound assignment for primitives** (`s += x`): existing parser desugaring (`s = s + x`) + compound-assignment lowering already emits `LoadLocal s; <load x>; AddInt; StoreLocal s` for primitive receivers — typed opcode + writeback. No changes to operator lowering required; tests verify behaviour. **Widening lattice (Commit 2)**: tests pin `i8 → i32` via `+=`, `i16 → i64` in binding, `u8 → u16` / `u8 → u32` in binding. Existing `can_numeric_widen` in `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/constraints.rs:298` covers integer-family ↔ integer-family widening; typed-opcode lowering in `binary_ops.rs` emits target-typed opcode when the producing context proves a widening source. Compile-time only — NO `IntToNumber` / `NumberToInt` coercion opcodes (CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" #5 stands verbatim). **Per-method-handler sweep audit (Commit 2)**: HashSet `v2_add` / `v2_delete` ✓; HashMap `v2_set` / `v2_delete` / `v2_merge` ✓; Deque `v2_push_back` / `v2_push_front` ✓ (pop variants excluded); PriorityQueue `v2_push` ✓ (pop excluded); Array `handle_push_v2` ✓ (pop excluded, reverse stays functional per pre-Wave-6.5 contract); TypedArray push/set ✓ (pop excluded); Mutex / Atomic / Lazy / Channel NOT registered (interior mutability); DataTable builders NOT registered (functional). **Decisions called**: (1) R-value receiver = silent drop, not error (composition compatibility); (2) `&mut self` returns Self by default so chaining works, but the compile-time writeback is still effective; (3) Multi-share = clone-on-write (Swift COW) — preserves Shape's existing Arc-share discipline; (4) Mutex/Atomic/Lazy/Channel = NOT `is_mut_self` (interior mutability — Arc identity unchanged). **Smoke results — 5/5 from dispatch text pass under `--mode vm`**: `let mut s = HashSet(); s.add("a"); s.add("b"); print(s.size())` → 2; `let mut xs = [1,2,3]; xs.push(4); print(xs)` → [1,2,3,4]; `let s = HashSet(); s.add("x")` → compile error "Cannot reassign immutable variable 's'"; `let mut n: i32 = 0; let x: i8 = 5; n += x; print(n)` → 5; `let mut s = "hi"; s += " there"; print(s)` → "hi there". **Mutation-method sweep table** (method × old-flag × new-flag × test-updates): HashSet.add/delete: functional → &mut self ✓; HashMap.set/delete/merge: functional → &mut self ✓; Deque.pushBack/pushFront: functional → &mut self ✓; PriorityQueue.push: functional → &mut self ✓; Array.push: special-cased via ArrayPushLocal (functional ABI but writeback via local-slot opcode) → unchanged + generic-dispatch fallback registered; Mutex/Atomic/Lazy/Channel methods: unchanged (NOT registered). Tests: 27 new tests pinning the new contracts; no existing tests broken or rewritten (the bespoke push fast-path gate preserves identical bytecode for `arr.push(x)`). **Builder methods that stayed `&self`**: `DataTable.with_column`, `groupBy`, `aggregate`, `filter`, `orderBy`, `head`, `tail`, `select`, `where`, `take`, `drop`, `skip`, `concat`, `flatten`, `unique`, `distinct`, `distinctBy`, `union`, `intersect`, `except`, `union`/`intersection`/`difference` on HashSet, `getOrDefault`, `toArray`, `keys`, `values`, `entries`, `len`, `size`, `isEmpty`, `peek*`, `to_unix_*`, `format`, `iso8601`, `rfc2822`, `add_*`, `sub`, `diff` on DateTime, `sort` / `reverse` on Array (functional per pre-Wave-6.5 contract — "produces a fresh array"). All justified by either (a) builder pattern returning a NEW value of receiver type, (b) read-only accessor, (c) explicit pre-existing functional-semantics contract (sort/reverse), or (d) interior-mutability dispatch through a constant Arc identity. **Sites couldn't fix (surface-and-stop with §-cite)**: zero — all five smoke targets pass; ADR §2.7.27 amendment complete; verify-merge.sh 11/11 green at both commit tips. **`phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added**: zero — write-back emission is the architected design (compile-time `Dup; Store*`); no closure-share workaround needed. **LSP naming-convention warning hook**: deferred to hardening backlog per dispatch text instruction; documented in §2.7.27 "Out-of-scope". **verify-merge.sh**: Passed 11 / Failed 0 (SCRIPT_EXIT=0) at both Commit-1 and Commit-2 tips. **27/27 mutation_writeback tests pass.**) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-item-3-jit-verify` | `../shape-item-3-jit-verify` | Phase 2d Item 3 — JIT path end-to-end verification (read-only audit) | — | `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/{value_ffi,jit_kinds,object/conversion}.rs` (audit-only) + `AGENTS.md` (closure row) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-2d-hardening.md` (item (i) appended) | **closed** (Wave-4, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Verification-only sub-cluster per handover §1 Item 3 / dispatch-text scope ("you may NOT add new JIT FFI arms unless an unhandled HeapKind is found AND fixing it is a one-line `match` arm addition"). Coverage matrix built for HeapKind ordinals 0..33 at the source-of-truth `crates/shape-value/src/heap_variants.rs`. JIT FFI files audited at `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/{value_ffi,jit_kinds,object/conversion}.rs` + producer/consumer call sites across `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/{call_method,iterator,object,async_ops,control,result}.rs` + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/{ffi_builder,places}.rs` + `crates/shape-jit/src/context.rs`. **CRITICAL FINDING (surface-and-stop)**: the JIT execution path is end-to-end **broken at compile time** at HEAD `2706f254`, not at any specific HeapKind boundary. `shape run --mode jit <any-program>` (even a pure-numeric `while i < n { sum += i }` with no array literal) fails with `phase-2c §2.7.14 / W10 jit-playbook §5: JitArray rebuild required for JIT execution path — FFI symbol jit_new_array is not registered.` The blocker is in `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/ffi_builder.rs:73` — `r!("jit_new_array")` is called unconditionally for the `FFIFuncRefs::new_array` slot at JIT compile time; the symbol is no-op'd in `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_symbols/array_symbols.rs` per its own module-doc SURFACE comment ("Until the rebuild lands (W11 / deeper Phase-2c), both `register_array_symbols` and `declare_array_functions` are no-ops"). So every program that reaches the JIT pipeline aborts before any HeapKind-specific path is exercised. Confirmed end-to-end on a representative smoke (`smoke_simple_loop.shape` — pure-numeric, no array literal, no HashSet/Result/Iterator/Range method): VM = 4999950000 (correct); JIT = `Error: Runtime error: JIT compilation failed: phase-2c §2.7.14 / W10 jit-playbook §5: JitArray rebuild required for JIT execution path — FFI symbol jit_new_array is not registered.` Same on the W17-mutation-writeback smoke (`smoke_writeback.shape`). `cargo test -p shape-jit --features deep-tests --lib --release -- mir_compiler::integration_tests` reproduces the same failure on every test (0 passed / 127 failed / 387 filtered out). **Per-smoke results**: VM mode 5/5 PASS; JIT mode 5/5 SURFACE (same blocker — `jit_new_array` unresolved at JIT compile). **HeapKind coverage matrix (ordinals 0..33, against the source-of-truth enum in `heap_variants.rs`)**: ALL kinds are **structurally BLOCKED at the JIT-execute-path level** by the `jit_new_array` SURFACE (W10 jit-playbook §5 / ADR-006 §2.7.14 Q15 — kinded `TypedArray<T>` FFI rebuild). At the JIT-internal FFI **producer/consumer-arm** level (the `HK_*` u16-prefix-based dispatch in `value_ffi.rs` / `jit_kinds.rs` against `JitAlloc<T>` / `UnifiedValue<T>` allocations, INDEPENDENT of the runtime-tier `KindedSlot` JitFfiCarrier surface which is also SURFACE'd in `ffi/object/conversion.rs::{jit_bits_to_nanboxed,nanboxed_to_jit_bits}`), the matrix is: **0 String** COVERED-FFI (HK_STRING = HeapKind::String as u16; produced via `box_string`/`box_str`; consumed in `call_string_method`); **1 TypedObject** COVERED-FFI (HK_TYPED_OBJECT; produced via `box_typed_object`; consumed in `call_object_method` / `receiver_type_name` schema-id lookup); **2 Closure** COVERED-FFI (HK_CLOSURE; produced in `context.rs:959,979` via `jit_box`; consumed in `simulation/mod.rs:43` via `is_heap_kind`); **3 Decimal** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_DECIMAL constant defined but no producer / no consumer arm); **4 BigInt** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_BIG_INT defined, no producer / consumer); **5 DataTable** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_DATATABLE defined, no JIT-side producer / consumer); **6 Future** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_FUTURE defined; consumer SURFACE at `async_ops.rs:209` `jit_cancel_task`); **7 TaskGroup** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_TASK_GROUP defined; consumer SURFACE at `async_ops.rs:145`); **8 TypedArray** SURFACE — `jit_array.rs` deleted (the JIT TypedArray rebuild itself is W10 jit-playbook §5 / ADR-006 §2.7.14 territory; current JIT-emitted code references the legacy `HK_ARRAY=1` "legacy ordinal" — note: `HK_ARRAY=1` collides with `HK_TYPED_OBJECT=1` because they both reference HeapKind::TypedObject's ordinal, which is a defect of the legacy-ordinal naming scheme; the arms in `call_method/mod.rs:357` / `iterator.rs:39,102` / `object_ops.rs:80` / `conversion.rs:37` / `property_access.rs:300` use HK_ARRAY symbolically but the constant value would tag-collide with HK_TYPED_OBJECT at runtime — this is a latent bug that the JitArray rebuild needs to disambiguate); **9 Temporal** RUNTIME-ONLY (no HK_TEMPORAL constant, no JIT-side production / consumption — JIT-emitted DateTime values use HK_TIME=22 "legacy ordinal" via `unified_box(HK_TIME, base.as_ref().clone())` in `ffi_symbols/helpers/mod.rs:60,92,125,177`); **10 TableView** RUNTIME-ONLY (no HK_TABLEVIEW const, JIT does not produce/consume); **11 Content** RUNTIME-ONLY; **12 Instant** RUNTIME-ONLY (Instant value goes through HK_TIME 'time' label); **13 IoHandle** RUNTIME-ONLY; **14 NativeScalar** RUNTIME-ONLY; **15 NativeView** RUNTIME-ONLY; **16 Char** RUNTIME-ONLY (no HK_CHAR const; JIT-side Char production at `iterator.rs:115` `box_string(ch.to_string())` re-encodes as HK_STRING per the Rust `char` `to_string` round-trip — this is the §2.7.4 follow-up pattern, not a defection-attractor); **17 HashMap** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_HASHMAP constant defined; no JIT producer / consumer arm — note: the `property_access.rs::jit_hashmap_*` family also surfaces at runtime per its `///` doc-comments referencing `HeapKind::HashMap` only as a *requirement* on the input slot kind, not as a JIT-produced kind); **18 FilterExpr** PRODUCER-GAP (HK_FILTER_EXPR defined; no JIT-side producer / consumer); **19 Reference** RUNTIME-ONLY (`ffi/references.rs::jit_make_ref` is whole-function SURFACE per `references.rs:33`); **20 SharedCell** RUNTIME-ONLY (the JIT side has per-kind `jit_alloc_shared_cell_*` FFI for typed cells, but those are storage-tier cell-data accessors keyed on FieldKind, not on the `HeapKind::SharedCell` discriminator label — different abstractions per ADR-006 §2.7.12); **21 HashSet** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W13; HK_SET constant absent; no JIT producer / consumer; if JIT-emitted code receives a HashSet-labeled slot from a VM trampoline call, the receiver-classification in `call_method/mod.rs:64-79` falls through to `_ => None` → `try_call_user_method` returns None → `dispatch_method_via_trampoline` which is itself SURFACE at `call_method/mod.rs:185` `todo!("phase-2c §2.7.10/Q11")`. So in principle the JIT can RECEIVE HashSet receivers but cannot dispatch them); **22 Iterator** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W13; same dispatch-via-trampoline gating as HashSet); **23 Deque** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W15-deque; same); **24 Channel** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W15-channel; same); **25 PriorityQueue** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W15-priority-queue; same); **26 Range** PRODUCER-PRESENT-VIA-LEGACY-ORDINAL (post-W15-range; `context.rs:427` does `jit_box(HK_RANGE, *range)` where HK_RANGE=12 is a "legacy ordinal" that overlaps with `HeapKind::Instant` ord 12 in the strict-typing enum; consumer arms in `iterator.rs:68,139` / `conversion.rs:40` / `call_method/mod.rs:362` use HK_RANGE symbolically; same latent legacy-ordinal collision as HK_ARRAY); **27 Result** PRODUCER-PRESENT-VIA-LEGACY-ORDINAL (post-W14-variant-codegen; JIT produces via `box_ok` / `box_err` using HK_OK=15 / HK_ERR=16 legacy ordinals; consumer arms in `call_method/mod.rs:349,350` `is_ok_tag` / `is_err_tag` + `result.rs:26,31` `make_ok`/`make_err`; latent collision with HeapKind=15 NativeView / HeapKind=16 Char); **28 Option** PRODUCER-PRESENT-VIA-LEGACY-ORDINAL (post-W14; `box_some` uses HK_SOME=14 legacy ordinal — collides with HeapKind=14 NativeScalar; `box_none` re-uses TAG_NULL); **29 TraitObject** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W17-trait-object-storage; HK_TRAIT_OBJECT=19 legacy ordinal would collide with HeapKind=19 Reference if a JIT-produced TraitObject value ever crossed the boundary; no current JIT producer/consumer; per Wave 2.6 W17-trait-object-emission is BLOCKED, so OpCode::BoxTraitObject isn't emitted yet and this isn't reachable); **30 Mutex** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W17-concurrency); **31 Atomic** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W17-concurrency); **32 Lazy** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W17-concurrency); **33 ModuleFn** RUNTIME-ONLY (post-W17-comptime-vm-dispatch; pure-discriminator label per ADR-006 §2.7.26 — inline-scalar payload, no Arc; JIT-side has no producer or consumer arm). **Mode `RUNTIME-ONLY` is correct disposition** for ords 9-25, 29-33: the JIT-execute path produces ONLY scalar return values per `executor.rs::execute_with_jit` (return-tag set is RETURN_TAG_F64 / I64 / I32 / BOOL; anything else triggers SURFACE-and-stop per `executor.rs:280-304`). Heap-valued returns from the JIT to the host are not currently a supported shape — `_ => { return Err(... RETURN_TAG_NANBOXED reached the host boundary without a stamped NativeKind ...)` per the post-strict-typing executor design. **Deopt-loop detection**: N/A — no JIT-compiled program runs end-to-end, so deopt counters cannot be observed under any smoke; surface preempts. **`cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` health**: pre-existing baseline issue — `test_cancel_task_null_trampoline` calls `jit_cancel_task` which is `extern "C"` + `todo!()` (cannot unwind from extern C; panics SIGABRT the test process). Same shape kills `native_fixed_arity_helpers_surface_pending_kinded_abi`. Test count: 354 tests discoverable. Tests pre-existing-broken in shape-jit lib (NOT introduced by this Item 3 work, which is read-only): the `should_panic(expected = "phase-2c")` tests on `extern "C"` `todo!()` functions. **Out-of-scope per dispatch-text scope rules**: the `jit_new_array` rebuild + the `nanboxed_to_jit_bits` / `jit_bits_to_nanboxed` carrier rebuild are each well beyond a one-line `match` arm fix; the dispatch text says "If the fix is larger than a one-arm addition, surface-and-stop and let the supervisor dispatch a follow-up sub-cluster" — done. Recommended follow-ups: (i) **W11-jit-new-array** (or equivalent name) — implement `jit_new_array` + `jit_array_push_elem` + the surrounding `FFIFuncRefs` slots in `compiler/ffi_builder.rs:73-74` against the kinded `Arc<TypedArrayData>` shape per ADR-006 §2.7.14 Q15. Unblocks `shape run --mode jit`. (ii) **W11-jit-carrier-conversion** — fill `jit_bits_to_nanboxed` / `nanboxed_to_jit_bits` per-arm re-encoding for the §2.7.5 JitFfiCarrier (`(u64, NativeKind)`) direction; unblocks heap-shape JIT return values. (iii) **W17-jit-legacy-ordinal-disambiguation** — audit the `HK_*` legacy-ordinal table in `value_ffi.rs:153-201` against current HeapKind ordinals; HK_ARRAY=1 (collides HeapKind::TypedObject=1), HK_RANGE=12 (collides HeapKind::Instant=12), HK_SOME=14 (collides HeapKind::NativeScalar=14), HK_OK=15 (collides HeapKind::NativeView=15), HK_ERR=16 (collides HeapKind::Char=16), HK_TRAIT_OBJECT=19 (collides HeapKind::Reference=19). The collisions don't currently cause bugs because the runtime-tier `HeapKind` discriminator never crosses the JIT FFI boundary at HEAD (the carrier-conversion functions are SURFACE), but a JIT-runtime heap dispatch that DOES cross will land on the wrong arm without disambiguation. (iv) **shape-jit lib test runner** — convert the `#[should_panic(expected = "phase-2c")]` tests on `extern "C"` `todo!()` SURFACEs to `#[ignore]` with a §-cite + supervisor-tracked follow-up, since `extern "C"` cannot unwind so SIGABRT is the only achievable failure mode and that aborts the whole process. **Verification artifacts**: `/tmp/jit-verify/smoke_simple_loop.shape` + `/tmp/jit-verify/smoke_writeback.shape` (smoke programs); `/tmp/vm-item-3-initial.out` (verify-merge.sh 11/11 PASS at branch HEAD before any edits); `/tmp/jit-tests-skip.out` + `/tmp/deep-tests2.out` (test-runner SIGABRT reproduction). **Decisions called**: (1) Smoke-harness shape = ad-hoc shell + the `shape` binary (rationale: in-test harness via `cargo test -p shape-vm` `jit_vm_parity` module would itself depend on the unblocked JIT execute path; since that path is structurally blocked, the harness would also fail to even compile a smoke through `--mode jit`. Ad-hoc shell smokes reproduce the structural blocker more directly with less infrastructure to maintain). (2) Unhandled HeapKind action = surface-and-stop and dispatch follow-up (rationale: the gaps surfaced — `jit_new_array` rebuild + carrier-conversion rebuild + legacy-ordinal disambiguation — are each well beyond a one-line match-arm fix; per scope rules in dispatch text "If the fix is larger than a one-arm addition, surface-and-stop and let the supervisor dispatch a follow-up sub-cluster"). (3) Deopt-counter access = N/A (rationale: per (1), no smoke reaches a JIT-compiled hot loop because compile-time fails first; deopt detection cannot be exercised on the current branch). **`phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added**: zero — read-only verification work does not touch closure-share / receiver-recovery code paths. **AGENTS.md row appended**: this row. **CHECK 8 inline-empty-arm**: N/A — no new HeapKind match arms added. cargo check --workspace --lib clean; verify-merge.sh: Passed 11 / Failed 0 (SCRIPT_EXIT=0); check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0. **Closure of Phase 2d Item 3 per handover §1 Item 3 close gate**, with surface-and-stop for the underlying JIT execute path SURFACE — the verification was the deliverable.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-pop-mutation` | `../shape-w17-pop-mutation` | W17-pop-mutation — Phase 2d Item 4 follow-on (`&mut self` tuple-return ABI for pop-shaped methods) | — | `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/method_registry.rs` (per-kind `MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_*` `phf::Set` constants + new `HASHMAP_METHODS["remove"]` entry) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mutation_writeback.rs` (`ContainerKind::is_mut_self_tuple_return_method` + `MutSelfWriteBackMode` enum) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/function_calls.rs` (compile-time `Swap; Store{Local,ModuleBinding}` and `Swap; Pop` post-CallMethod emit + `resolve_mut_self_tuple_return_target` resolver + immutability guard) + `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/{array_basic,deque_methods,priority_queue_methods,hashmap_methods}.rs` (pop handlers side-channel-publish new container Arc via `vm.push_kinded` before returning popped element; new `v2_remove` on HashMap) + `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/tests/pop_mutation.rs` (17 source-level smoke tests) + `docs/adr/006-value-and-memory-model.md` (§2.7.27 tuple-return amendment) | **closed** (Wave-4, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Item 4 follow-on amendment — tuple-return ABI variant for canonical pop-shape methods.** Generalizes W17-mutation-writeback's compile-time codegen pattern (post-`CallMethod` writeback to receiver binding) to handle methods that satisfy BOTH (a) canonical-extraction-from-collection AND (b) structural-mutation-of-collection. The conceptual dispatch signature is `(&mut self) -> (Option<T>, Self)`: user-facing return is the popped element; the new container Arc is published as a side effect on the VM stack and the compiler emits `Swap; Store*(receiver)` to write it back (or `Swap; Pop` for r-value receivers — silent drop, mirror of §2.7.27 self-return r-value rule). **No `MethodFnV2` ABI change** — runtime ABI stays unchanged; the new `MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_*` flag is a compile-time marker only. Convention: tuple-return handlers `vm.push_kinded(new_container_bits, kind)` before returning the popped element; dispatch shell pushes the returned popped element on top, so post-call stack is `[..., NewContainer, popped_element]`. **Canonical pop-method sweep (5 methods)**: Array.pop (`array_basic::handle_pop_v2`, `Ptr(TypedArray)` slow path — v2 raw-pointer fast path on UInt64 unchanged) → MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_ARRAY_METHODS; Deque.popBack (`deque_methods::v2_pop_back`), Deque.popFront (`deque_methods::v2_pop_front`) → MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_DEQUE_METHODS; PriorityQueue.pop (`priority_queue_methods::v2_pop`, empty-case = 0 per landing) → MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_PRIORITY_QUEUE_METHODS; HashMap.remove (`hashmap_methods::v2_remove`, NEW method — value extracted BEFORE Arc::make_mut to avoid post-clone strand; preserves stdlib `set.shape::remove` chain by keeping `delete` self-returning) → MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_HASHMAP_METHODS. **Audit-and-include disposition**: `Array.remove(i)` / `Array.swap_remove(i)` / `HashSet.take(x)` / `HashSet.pop_first` / `Channel.recv` — none exist in current stdlib. Queued as natural follow-up sites if/when introduced; would slot into the same ABI variant with no further machinery. **TypedArray<i64> / TypedArray<f64> v2 fast path** (`typed_int_array_methods::pop` / `typed_number_array_methods::pop`): stays in-place on raw `*mut TypedArray<T>` pointer — no Arc identity to write back, buffer mutation visible through stable pointer. NOT registered. **HashSet pop-shape**: no canonical pop method (`take(x)` / `pop_first` don't exist); `HashSet.remove(x) -> bool` is NOT pop-shape (returns "was present", not the value); stays self-returning. `ContainerKind::is_mut_self_tuple_return_method` returns `false` for HashSet. **Decisions called**: (1) `MUT_SELF_TUPLE_RETURN_*` shape = separate phf::Set per container kind, mirroring `MUT_SELF_*` (consistent registration shape, single source of truth shared with compile-time classifier); (2) No `UnpackTuple2` opcode in Shape's bytecode — emit pattern `Swap; Store*` / `Swap; Pop` (fewest opcodes, matches existing `Swap` / `Dup` / `Pop` primitives); (3) HashMap.remove added as NEW method alongside existing self-returning `delete` (preserves stdlib `set.shape` wrapper, exposes canonical pop-shape ABI for callers wanting `Option<V>`); (4) Audit-and-include candidates → none included (none exist in stdlib). **R-value receiver — silent drop**: r-value tuple-return pop emits `Swap; Pop` to drop the new container Arc cleanly via `drop_with_kind` (refcount balanced), leaves popped element as expression value. Mirror of §2.7.27 self-return silent-drop rule. **Let-immutability**: `let d = Deque(); d.popBack()` fails compile via existing `check_named_binding_write_allowed` — same diagnostic flow as self-return mutating methods. **Smoke results — 5/5 from dispatch text pass under `--mode vm`**: `let mut d = Deque(); d.pushBack("first"); d.pushBack("second"); let popped = d.popBack()` → "second", size 1; `popFront` returns "alpha", size 1 (parallel); `m.remove("a")` returns "ten", `m.len()` → 0; `let mut q = PriorityQueue(); q.push(3); q.push(1); q.push(2); q.pop()` → 1, size 2; `let d = Deque(); ...; d.popBack()` → compile error "Cannot reassign immutable variable 'd'"; `make_deque().popBack()` r-value → returns "three". **Pop-method sweep table** (method × old-return-shape × new-tuple-shape × test-updates): Array.pop: `Arc<TypedArrayData>` (lost) + popped → side-channel-published NewSelf + Option<T>; Deque.popBack: `Arc<DequeData>` (lost) + popped → side-channel-published + Option<T>; Deque.popFront: same; PriorityQueue.pop: `Arc<PriorityQueueData>` (lost) + popped int → side-channel-published + int (empty=0); HashMap.remove: NEW method, side-channel-published `Arc<HashMapData>` + Option<value>. Tests: 17 new tests pinning the new contracts in `pop_mutation.rs`; existing W17-mutation-writeback's 27 tests still pass; no regressions in stdlib `set.shape::remove` wrapper. **Sites couldn't fix (surface-and-stop with §-cite)**: zero — all five smoke targets pass; ADR §2.7.27 tuple-return amendment complete; verify-merge.sh 11/11 green. **`phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added**: zero — tuple-return emission is the architected design (compile-time `Swap; Store*` / `Swap; Pop`); no closure-share workaround surface arose. **CHECK 8 style rule**: not triggered — no new HeapKind match arms added (the amendment is pure codegen + handler convention; no new HeapKind ordinals or runtime variants). **Pre-existing SIGABRT in `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` full-suite (`test_object_operations`)**: documented in phase-2d-hardening:(d), unaffected by this work. **verify-merge.sh**: Passed 11 / Failed 0 (SCRIPT_EXIT=0). **check-no-dynamic.sh**: exit 0. **17/17 pop_mutation tests pass; 27/27 mutation_writeback tests pass.**) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-jit-test-runner` | `../shape-jit-test-runner` | Phase 3 cluster-0 / shape-jit-test-runner — convert `#[should_panic]` tests on `extern "C" todo!()` bodies to `#[ignore]` so `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` stops SIGABRT-aborting | — | `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/control/mod.rs` (`native_fixed_arity_helpers_surface_pending_kinded_abi`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/async_ops.rs` (`test_cancel_task_null_trampoline`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_symbols/simulation/mod.rs` (`test_simulation_with_function_handler`) + this AGENTS.md row | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Inventory of `#[should_panic]` in shape-jit/src**: 1 site at HEAD (`ffi/control/mod.rs:458` — `native_fixed_arity_helpers_surface_pending_kinded_abi`), matches the kickoff dispatch text. Two additional sites identified by binary-aborts-with-signal-6 observation under `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib`: (1) `ffi/async_ops.rs::test_cancel_task_null_trampoline` — a plain `#[test]` that calls `jit_cancel_task` (extern "C" + `todo!()` at `async_ops.rs:197`) and expects `result == -1`. The author's intent was the null-trampoline branch (`f.is_null() => -1`), but the unconditional `todo!()` at the top of the function makes that branch unreachable in the current strict-typing-migration state, and SIGABRTs the test process. Converted to `#[ignore = "SURFACE: ..."]` with the same comment shape. Same SIGABRT class; the test's "no function table → TAG_NULL fall-through" branch is unreachable because the unconditional todo!() at the top of jit_call_value aborts on the first per-row invocation. The three sibling test_simulation_* tests (null ctx / null config / zero rows / non-callable config) exit the loop before reaching jit_call_value and stay green. Converted to `#[ignore]` with the same comment shape. **Pre-existing failures uncovered by the SIGABRT removal** (NOT introduced by this cluster, surfaced as a finding because the Item 3 audit row 114 stated `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` test counts but could not measure pass/fail because the SIGABRT preempted the summary line): 17 tests fail with regular assertion errors, all caused by the upstream JIT-execute path SURFACE that Item 3 row 114 already documented: `core::tests::test_jit_arithmetic` (5.0 vs 15.0), `core::tests::test_jit_comparison` (5.0 vs 1.0), `context::tests::test_closure_jit_box_roundtrip`, `ffi::async_ops::tests::test_join_init_empty`, `ffi::result::tests::{test_result_inner, test_result_ok_roundtrip, test_option_some_roundtrip, test_result_err_roundtrip, test_unwrap_or_with_ok}` (5 tests), `ffi::typed_object::field_access::tests::test_jit_typed_object_ffi`, `ffi::typed_object::ffi_exports::tests::test_jit_field_access_both_paths`, `ffi::value_ffi::tests::test_typed_object_encoding`, `mir_compiler::closure_dispatch_regression_tests::{closure_simple_dispatch_returns_six, closure_non_jit_compiled_dispatches_through_trampoline_vm, closure_int_literal_on_rhs_propagates_param_kind, option_return_conditional_number_some, option_return_conditional_number_none}` (5 tests). Spot-checked: `closure_simple_dispatch_returns_six` panics with "JIT compilation failed: phase-2c §2.7.14 / W10 jit-playbook §5: JitArray rebuild required for JIT execution path — FFI symbol `jit_new_array` is not registered" — exactly the structural blocker Item 3 row 114 identified. `test_jit_arithmetic` / `test_jit_comparison` panic with mismatched arithmetic results, consistent with the carrier-conversion SURFACE that row 114's follow-up (ii) "W11-jit-carrier-conversion" was meant to close. `test_typed_object_encoding` and `test_jit_field_access_both_paths` panic with `is_number(boxed)` shape assertions, consistent with the carrier-conversion SURFACE. **These 17 failures are out-of-scope** for this Phase 3 cluster-0 (test-runner conversion only); they belong to row 114's recommended follow-ups (i) **W11-jit-new-array**, (ii) **W11-jit-carrier-conversion**, (iii) **W17-jit-legacy-ordinal-disambiguation**. **Test-count delta**: before conversions: 354 discoverable (Item 3 audit count) / 23 ignored / unknown pass/fail (SIGABRT pre-empted summary). | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-jit-legacy-ord` | `../shape-w17-jit-legacy-ord` | Phase 3 cluster-0 / W17-jit-legacy-ordinal-disambiguation — phase-2d-hardening item (i) close | — | `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs` (HK_* constant table — Tier 1 canonical-aliased + Tier 2 JIT-private contiguous block at `JIT_LEGACY_HK_BASE = 256`) + `scripts/verify-merge.sh` (new CHECK 12 — JIT-private HK_* ordinal-collision guard) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-2d-hardening.md` (item (i) RESOLVED status) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | **closed** (Phase 3 Wave-1, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Resolution per phase-2d-hardening item (i) close.** Picked **option (1)** — bump JIT-internal `HK_*` constants out of the `HeapKind as u16` ordinal range entirely — rather than option (2) (canonical-HeapKind alignment) because option (2) would require either inventing new HeapKind variants for JIT-internal-only labels (`HK_HOST_CLOSURE`, `HK_TYPED_TABLE`, `HK_ENUM`, `HK_EXPR_PROXY`, `HK_TIMESPAN`, `HK_TYPE_ANNOTATION`, `HK_PRINT_RESULT`, `HK_SIMULATION_CALL`, `HK_DATA_REFERENCE`, `HK_INT_ARRAY`..`HK_F32_ARRAY`) — a Q8-cardinality amendment per ADR-006 §2.7.6 — or coercing a dozen JIT-side semantic labels onto the existing canonical kinds (loses JIT-side dispatch granularity). Option (1) is mechanical and self-contained: change the constants, verify producers/consumers still type-check, add a CHECK 12 grep guard to verify-merge.sh that asserts every `HK_*` either aliases `HeapKind::X as u16` (Tier 1) or sits at `JIT_LEGACY_HK_BASE [+ N]` or `>= 256` (Tier 2). **Two-tier layout adopted** in `value_ffi.rs:137-251`: Tier 1 — `HK_STRING`, `HK_TYPED_OBJECT`, `HK_CLOSURE`, `HK_DECIMAL`, `HK_BIG_INT`, `HK_DATATABLE`, `HK_HASHMAP`, `HK_FUTURE`, `HK_TASK_GROUP`, `HK_FILTER_EXPR` (10 canonical-aliased — these labels DO mean runtime `HeapKind`). Tier 2 — every other `HK_*` (39 JIT-private — these labels DO NOT mean runtime `HeapKind`; they tag `JitAlloc<T>` / `UnifiedValue<T>` prefixes for JIT-internal values consumed by sibling JIT arms). **JIT_LEGACY_HK_BASE = 256** chosen so the JIT-private block sits comfortably above (a) the canonical `HeapKind as u16` range (currently 0..33, ample headroom to 255) and (b) the pre-existing JIT-private blocks in `jit_kinds.rs` (`HK_JIT_FUNCTION = 128`, `HK_JIT_TABLE_REF = 130`, `HK_JIT_OBJECT = 131`) and `v2_struct.rs` (`HK_V2_TYPED_STRUCT = 132`). Compile-time assert pins `JIT_LEGACY_HK_BASE >= 192` so a future agent can't silently lower the boundary. **Bumped-ordinal table at close** (39 entries, contiguous starting at 256): HK_ARRAY=256 (was 1; collided HeapKind::TypedObject); HK_HOST_CLOSURE=257 (was 6); HK_TYPED_TABLE=258 (was 8; collided HeapKind::TypedArray); HK_ROW_VIEW=259 (was 9; collided HeapKind::Temporal); HK_COLUMN_REF=260 (was 10; collided HeapKind::TableView); HK_INDEXED_TABLE=261 (was 11; collided HeapKind::Content); HK_RANGE=262 (was 12; collided HeapKind::Instant); HK_ENUM=263 (was 13; collided HeapKind::IoHandle); HK_SOME=264 (was 14; collided HeapKind::NativeScalar); HK_OK=265 (was 15; collided HeapKind::NativeView); HK_ERR=266 (was 16; collided HeapKind::Char); HK_TRAIT_OBJECT=267 (was 19; collided HeapKind::Reference); HK_EXPR_PROXY=268 (was 20; collided HeapKind::SharedCell); HK_TIME=269 (was 22; collided HeapKind::Iterator); HK_DURATION=270 (was 23; collided HeapKind::Deque); HK_TIMESPAN=271 (was 24; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Channel** per Phase 2d Wave 15 W15-channel-rebuild); HK_TIMEFRAME=272 (was 25; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::PriorityQueue** per W15-priority-queue); HK_TIME_REFERENCE=273 (was 26; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Range** per W15-range); HK_DATETIME_EXPR=274 (was 27; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Result** per W14-variant-codegen); HK_DATA_DATETIME_REF=275 (was 28; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Option** per W14-variant-codegen); HK_TYPE_ANNOTATION=276 (was 29; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::TraitObject** per W17-trait-object-storage); HK_TYPE_ANNOTATED_VALUE=277 (was 30; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Mutex** per W17-concurrency); HK_PRINT_RESULT=278 (was 31; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Atomic** per W17-concurrency); HK_SIMULATION_CALL=279 (was 32; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::Lazy** per W17-concurrency); HK_FUNCTION_REF=280 (was 33; **NEW finding — collides HeapKind::ModuleFn** per W17-comptime-vm-dispatch); HK_DATA_REFERENCE=281 (was 34 — one above current HeapKind tail; bumped pre-emptively); HK_INT_ARRAY=282 (was 48); HK_FLOAT_ARRAY=283 (was 49); HK_BOOL_ARRAY=284 (was 50); HK_MATRIX=285 (was 51); HK_I8_ARRAY=286 (was 57); HK_I16_ARRAY=287 (was 58); HK_I32_ARRAY=288 (was 59); HK_U8_ARRAY=289 (was 60); HK_U16_ARRAY=290 (was 61); HK_U32_ARRAY=291 (was 62); HK_U64_ARRAY=292 (was 63); HK_F32_ARRAY=293 (was 64); HK_FLOAT_ARRAY_SLICE=294 (was 71). **NEW findings beyond the hardening item (i) table** (10 additional collisions): the hardening table listed 15 collisions in ords 1..23 plus a stale `HK_FUTURE_..` entry referencing constants (17/18) that don't exist in the file; the table claimed `HK_TIMESPAN..HK_DATA_REFERENCE 24-34` and `HK_FLOAT_ARRAY..HK_F32_ARRAY 48-64,71` were "non-colliding". After Phase 2d's Wave 14/15/17 added HeapKind ords 23..33 (Deque/Channel/PriorityQueue/Range/Result/Option/TraitObject/Mutex/Atomic/Lazy/ModuleFn), every entry in 24-33 collides too. Bumping ALL legacy ordinals (not just the originally-listed 15) is the right disposition because (a) the new collisions are real today; (b) the array-family 48-71 entries are fragile to future HeapKind growth past 47; (c) a single contiguous JIT-private block + a single guard rule is simpler than maintaining a per-entry justification. **Touched producer/consumer sites** (none required changes — all sites reference the constants by symbolic name): producers `context.rs:407` `jit_box(HK_DURATION, ...)`, `context.rs:427` `jit_box(HK_RANGE, ...)`, `context.rs:447` `jit_box(HK_DATA_REFERENCE, ...)`, `ffi/value_ffi.rs:339,344` `unified_box(HK_OK/HK_ERR, ...)`, `ffi/value_ffi.rs:385` `unified_box(HK_SOME, ...)`, `ffi/value_ffi.rs:422` `unified_box(HK_COLUMN_REF, ...)`, `ffi/data.rs:180` `unified_box(HK_TIME, ...)`, `ffi/object/property_access.rs:167,191` `unified_box(HK_TIME/HK_TIMEFRAME, ...)`, `ffi_symbols/helpers/mod.rs:60,92,125,177` `unified_box(HK_TIME, ...)`, `ffi_symbols/intrinsics/mod.rs:361,371` `unified_box(HK_TIME, ...)`, `ffi/call_method/duration.rs:115` `unified_box(HK_DURATION, ...)`. Consumers `ffi/iterator.rs:39,68,102,139` (HK_ARRAY / HK_RANGE arms), `ffi/conversion.rs:37-194` (typeof + type-check arms), `ffi/object/object_ops.rs:80` (HK_ARRAY arm), `ffi/object/property_access.rs:60,120,128,163,300,321` (HK_ARRAY/HK_DURATION/HK_TIME/HK_DATA_REFERENCE/HK_COLUMN_REF arms), `ffi/object/format.rs:110` (HK_ARRAY arm), `ffi/object/closure.rs:46` (HK_COLUMN_REF arm), `ffi/call_method/{mod,duration,time}.rs` (HK_ARRAY/HK_DURATION/HK_TIME/HK_COLUMN_REF arms), `ffi/value_ffi.rs:324,329,370,432` (is_heap_kind probes). All 97 grep hits across `crates/shape-jit/src/` continue to compile + behave identically because the constant changes are value-only — the symbolic name → value mapping is preserved at every call site. **CHECK 12 added to scripts/verify-merge.sh**: awk-based guard scans `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs` for `pub const HK_*: u16 = ...;` lines and rejects any RHS that's not (a) `HeapKind::X as u16`, (b) `JIT_LEGACY_HK_BASE [+ N]`, or (c) a numeric literal `>= 256`. Self-tested against synthetic `HK_BAD_LOW = 12` input — fires correctly. **Verification artifacts**: `/tmp/w17.vm.baseline.out` (verify-merge.sh 11/11 PASS pre-edit); `/tmp/w17.vm.out` (verify-merge.sh **12/12 PASS** post-edit including new CHECK 12); `/tmp/w17.check.out` (`cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` exit 0); `/tmp/w17.test.skip.out` vs `/tmp/w17.test.baseline2.out` (`cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` — both exit 101 SIGABRT from pre-existing `extern "C" todo!()` SURFACEs `test_cancel_task_null_trampoline` + `native_fixed_arity_helpers_surface_pending_kinded_abi`, documented in W17-jit-verify row above; failing test set is 8 of 9 baseline failures — no regressions introduced by this change). **Decisions called**: (1) **Option (1) over Option (2)** — bump out-of-range, not align-with-canonical (rationale above: option 2 would require Q8 amendments for ~10 JIT-only labels). (2) **Bulk renumber including 24-34 and 48-71** — the prompt allowed "UNLESS your chosen scheme requires bulk renumbering — flag this in your decisions"; bulk renumber is required because the new findings show 10 additional collisions in 24-33 already, and the array-family 48-71 will collide on next HeapKind growth. Single contiguous block + single guard rule is the cleanest invariant. (3) **`JIT_LEGACY_HK_BASE = 256` not 128** — pre-existing JIT-private blocks already sit at 128-132; choosing 256 leaves a generous gap for future jit_kinds.rs growth and makes the "above HeapKind" intent unambiguous (HeapKind range is `u8`-representable today, so 256 is the first guaranteed-out-of-range value). **`phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added**: zero — value_ffi.rs is JIT-internal-only, no closure-share / receiver-recovery interaction. **CHECK 8 inline-empty-arm**: N/A — no new HeapKind match arms added (this work is purely constant renumbering + producer/consumer constants are symbolic). cargo check --workspace --lib --tests exit 0; verify-merge.sh: **Passed 12 / Failed 0** (SCRIPT_EXIT=0); check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0. **Closure of phase-2d-hardening item (i) per Phase 3 cluster-0 dispatch contract.**) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-item-5-benches` | `../shape-item-5-benches` | Item 5 — bench compilation migration (`crates/shape-vm/benches/{vm_benchmarks,typed_access_bench}.rs`) | — | `crates/shape-vm/benches/vm_benchmarks.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/benches/typed_access_bench.rs` | **closed** (Phase 2d post-Wave-3, 2026-05-12; commit pending. Two bench files migrated per handover §1 Item 5 / playbook §0 / CLAUDE.md "Benchmark Integrity" (API migration only — workload semantics preserved). **Audit findings (pre → post API mapping)**: (1) `OpCode::Lt` → `OpCode::LtInt` — generic comparison opcode deleted alongside ValueWord; `LtInt` is the post-strict-typing `i64 × i64 → bool` typed sibling, matches the loop's int operand kind. Single mechanical rename in `vm_benchmarks.rs::build_loop_program` (line 270). The `loop_1k_generic` criterion bench function name is preserved verbatim for TUI-name stability per playbook diff-scope discipline; the "generic-vs-typed" semantic distinction the bench originally measured no longer exists at the VM dispatch layer (both variants now route through `LtInt` / `AddInt` typed opcodes), documented in the build-fn doc-comment. (2) `shape_value::ValueWord::from_row_view(...)` + `shape_value::ValueWordExt` + `Constant::Value(ValueWord)` — DELETED (CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns": ValueWord at runtime is deleted; tagged-word dynamic-fallback is gone). `Constant::Value` survives as a unit-shape placeholder; `op_push_const` in `executor/stack_ops/mod.rs:185` returns `RuntimeError("unsupported constant variant in PushConst")` for any constant whose carrier was not migrated to the per-kind heap-Arc surface. `VirtualMachine::push_value(KindedSlot)` (the host-tier helper) is itself `todo!("phase-2c — see ADR-006 §2.7.4")`. `VirtualMachine::push_kinded(bits, kind)` exists but is `pub(crate)` — benches use shape-vm as an external crate, so the in-crate kinded direct-write path is unreachable. **Per-bench disposition**: `vm_benchmarks.rs::bench_typed_arithmetic` (`add_int_guarded`, `add_int_direct`, `add_number_guarded`, `add_number_direct`, `loop_1k_typed`, `loop_1k_generic`) — MIGRATED (all five typed-arithmetic benches plus the loop pair compile + register against criterion); `vm_benchmarks.rs::bench_dispatch_loop` (`while_loop_1k`, `while_loop_100k`, `while_loop_1m`) — MIGRATED (depends on `build_loop_program`'s `LtInt` migration); `vm_benchmarks.rs::bench_jit_dispatch` (`interpreter_baseline`, `tier_manager_overhead`, `tier_manager_osr_check`) — UNCHANGED (uses only public `TierManager` API + already-typed `AddInt` opcode); `vm_benchmarks.rs::bench_gc_alloc` + `bench_gc_young_pause` (gated on `feature = "gc"`) — UNCHANGED. `typed_access_bench.rs::benchmark_typed_vs_dynamic` (4 sub-benches: `load_col_f64`, `get_prop_f64`, `load_col_str`, `get_prop_str`) — SURFACE-AND-STOP (ADR-006 §2.7.4 host-tier eval/marshal API rebuild — deleted `Constant::Value(ValueWord)` carrier). Workload requires injecting an `Arc<TableViewData::RowView>` into the constant pool, which is workload-semantic: the bench was specifically measuring `op_push_const` (RowView arm) + `LoadColF64` / `GetProp` dispatch as a unit. The bench file is preserved as a stub that registers an empty criterion group + documents re-enablement criteria in the module-level doc-comment, satisfying the `cargo bench --no-run -p shape-vm` exit-0 gate without introducing a new `Constant::*` variant or flipping `push_kinded`'s visibility to `pub` (both out-of-scope per "Do NOT change crate-level setup"). **Decisions beyond playbook**: kept `loop_1k_generic` bench function name unchanged despite the typed/generic semantic distinction collapsing (criterion TUI-stability per diff-scope); chose surface-and-stop over scope-creep on `typed_access_bench.rs` (alternatives considered: (a) make `push_kinded` pub → crate-level setup change; (b) add `Constant::RowView(Arc<TableViewData>)` arm → new Constant variant + downstream serde/PushConst dispatch changes; (c) re-engineer the workload to construct the RowView via a different opcode sequence → would change what the hot path measures, violating Benchmark Integrity). **Sites couldn't fix**: typed_access_bench.rs's 4 RowView injection sites — gated on the same host-tier eval/marshal rebuild that gates `executor/tests/typed_array_ops.rs` / `executor/tests/matrix_ops.rs` (`todo!("phase-2c — see ADR-006 §2.7.4 (host-tier eval/marshal API rebuild — deleted ValueWord/Constant::Value(ValueWord) carrier)")`). **`phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added**: zero — bench code has no closure-share workaround sites; no HeapKind match arms were added (CHECK 8 inline-empty-arm rule did not arise). cargo check --workspace --lib --tests exit 0; cargo bench --no-run -p shape-vm exit 0 (both bench binaries built: `vm_benchmarks-*` + `typed_access_bench-*`); verify-merge.sh: Passed 11 / Failed 0 (SCRIPT_EXIT=0); check-no-dynamic.sh exit 0; bench binaries pass `--test` smoke (criterion `--test` mode runs each bench function once at minimum iter count and exits 0).) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w11-jit-carrier-conversion` | `../shape-w11-jit-carrier-conversion` | W11-jit-carrier-conversion — Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 2 (`JitFfiCarrier` conversion FFI + `jit_call_value` kinded value-call ABI per ADR-006 §2.7.5 / §2.7.11/Q12) | — | `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/object/conversion.rs` (rewrite — `jit_bits_to_nanboxed(bits, kind) -> JitFfiCarrier` + `jit_bits_to_nanboxed_with_ctx(bits, kind, ctx) -> JitFfiCarrier` take `NativeKind` as a new parameter per §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time; body is identity pack `(bits, kind)`; `nanboxed_to_jit_bits(&carrier) -> u64` is identity unpack `carrier.0` — per §2.7.5 the JIT bits ARE the raw bits, no decode/re-encode step; `drain_unified_heap_refs()` no-op preserved per §2.7.5/§2.7.7 — post-call retain/release dispatched per-slot via parallel-kind track at producing site, not via global accumulator; 5 round-trip / kind-preservation unit tests added) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/control/mod.rs` (`jit_call_value` real body — extern-C `fn(*mut JITContext) -> u64`; pops `arg_count: u64` (raw i64), pops args, pops callee_bits; classifies callee via JIT-internal NaN-box predicates `is_inline_function` / `is_heap_kind(_, HK_CLOSURE)` — these are the JIT's own value representation per `value_ffi.rs`, NOT the deleted runtime-tier tag_bits dispatch; case 1 inline function: try function table fast path `call_jit_fn_with_args` first, fall back to trampoline VM; case 2 deprecated `unified_box(HK_CLOSURE, JITClosure)` callee: extract captures via `unified_unbox::<JITClosure>` and route through trampoline VM; case 3 unrecognized — surface-and-stop returning TAG_NULL with `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1` diagnostic citing §2.7.5 kind-source gap, no SIGABRT, no Bool-default fallback; `dispatch_call_via_trampoline_vm` real body — stamps `NativeKind::UInt64` (the §2.7.11 callee-classification kind for function-id-shaped slots, also the §2.7.5 stable-FFI I64-wide-raw-bits carrier kind) for args/captures, routes to `VirtualMachine::jit_trampoline_call_closure` for closure callees or `VirtualMachine::call_value_immediate_nb` for bare function callees, `mem::forget` on result/kinded slots to transfer shares cleanly per §2.7.11/Q12 dispatch-shell pattern) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 2, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Carrier-conversion shape (the headline)**: `JitFfiCarrier` is the type alias `(u64, NativeKind)` per `jit_kinds.rs:43` and ADR-006 §2.7.5. The conversion functions are identity packing/unpacking — per §2.7.5 the JIT bits are the raw `u64` slot bits and the kind is the caller's static stamp; there is NO decode step on either side. Pre-existing signatures took `(bits: u64) -> JitFfiCarrier` and `(carrier: &JitFfiCarrier) -> u64` with `todo!()` bodies; new signatures take `(bits: u64, kind: NativeKind) -> JitFfiCarrier` (kind added as new parameter) and unchanged `(carrier: &JitFfiCarrier) -> u64`. The forbidden alternatives are: (i) decoding kind from bits via tag-bit probe (§2.7.7 #4 / #7 / CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" #4 — deleted ValueWord-shape); (ii) Bool-default fallback when kind unsupplied (§2.7.7 #9 / "Forbidden rationalizations" — the W11 round-1 walk-back precedent); (iii) per-arm re-encoding via `tag_bits::TAG_HEAP` / `make_tagged` on `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::*)` arms (the deleted ValueWord pipeline). No production caller calls these functions yet (only an `#[allow(unused_imports)]` placeholder in `data.rs:378`); they're a public API surface for the §2.7.5 stable-FFI handoff once downstream call sites (`async_ops`, `control/mod.rs`, `generic_builtin`, `data_access/mod.rs`, `executor.rs`, `property_access::jit_hashmap_*`) thread their kind through their respective JIT call signatures in their own waves. **`jit_call_value` resolution (the harder problem)**: pre-existing body was `extern "C" todo!()` — extern-C can't unwind, the body SIGABRTed the test process at the first call (which is why `closure_dispatch_regression_tests` are gated behind `deep-tests` per round-1 W11-jit-new-array). The signature is `extern "C" fn(*mut JITContext) -> u64` — reads callee/args from `ctx.stack`. The kind track on `JITContext.stack` does NOT exist (would be an ADR-level shape change; supervisor decision per task §"If the conversion needs an ADR-level shape change"). New body classifies callee via JIT-internal NaN-box predicates (which ARE preserved — JIT's own value representation, NOT the deleted runtime-tier tag_bits dispatch CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" #4 enumerates) for the two callee shapes that DO have classification: (a) inline function via `is_inline_function` / `unbox_function_id`; (b) deprecated `unified_box(HK_CLOSURE, JITClosure)` callee via `is_heap_kind(_, HK_CLOSURE)`. For the third shape — the `jit_finalize_heap_closure` return `Arc::into_raw(Arc::new(HeapValue::ClosureRaw(...))) as u64` which is a RAW Arc pointer with no NaN-box tag — the body surfaces-and-stops returning TAG_NULL (matches the W11-round-1 `jit_join_init` graceful-surface pattern, NOT a silent leak: the Arc share remains owned by the stack slot per §2.7.7 retain-on-read discipline; the surface is audible via `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1`). The §2.7.5 follow-up is to thread the callee's `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)` through the JIT call signature via a parallel-kind track on `JITContext.stack` — that's the ADR-level extension and is out of this sub-cluster's scope. **Test recovery delta**: 311 → 316 passed (+5 new tests in `conversion.rs::tests`: identity packing/unpacking + kind preservation + round-trip + drain_unified_heap_refs no-op). 0 → 0 failed. 38 → 38 ignored (unchanged — the previously-ignored tests assert deleted ValueWord-shape API per W11 round-1 close report's "9 individual tests `#[ignore]`'d that assert the deleted ValueWord-shape API"; those tests need test rewrites against the new strict-typed API, NOT carrier conversion work. Confirmed by un-ignoring `test_typed_object_encoding` and `test_jit_typed_object_ffi` and observing they still fail on `is_typed_object(bits)` returning false against raw Arc pointer bits — `box_typed_object` returns raw `Box::into_raw` per §2.7.5, not a NaN-boxed pointer, so `is_typed_object` which checks `is_heap(bits) && heap_kind == HK_TYPED_OBJECT` returns false. The deleted invariant was that the boxed value retained NaN-box TAG_HEAP tagging; under strict typing that tagging is gone.) **Decisions called beyond playbook / ADR**: (1) For `jit_call_value`'s case 3 (raw Arc closure callee, the `jit_finalize_heap_closure` return shape), the principled response is surface-and-stop returning TAG_NULL with §-cited diagnostic — NOT extern-C `todo!()` which SIGABRTs. The TAG_NULL return is graceful: the caller observes "value-call returned null" and propagates; it does NOT leak (the stack slot still owns the Arc share). This mirrors `jit_join_init` in W11 round-1 close. (2) Stamping `NativeKind::UInt64` as the kind for I64-wide raw bits in `dispatch_call_via_trampoline_vm` is the documented §2.7.11/Q12 function-id-callee classification kind (per `call_convention.rs:853-877` UInt64 arm) — NOT a Bool-default fallback. The pair-carrier is consumed by `jit_trampoline_call_closure` whose body uses kind only at the JIT→VM boundary (the VM-side per-slot kind track is established by the callee's own FrameDescriptor on entry per §2.7.7); the §2.7.5 stable-FFI handoff doesn't need per-arg semantic kind, only slot-size discipline (I64). (3) Did NOT add a parallel-kind track to `JITContext.stack` (would be an ADR-level shape change; surfaced as follow-up). **Sites surfaced (NOT silently skipped)**: (a) **§2.7.5 JIT-side parallel-kind track on `JITContext.stack`** — the raw Arc closure callee (case 3 in `jit_call_value`) cannot be classified without kind metadata; the JIT MIR emitter widens args to I64 at `terminators.rs:651-671` without kind info. Resolution requires either (i) extending `JITContext` with a `kinds: [NativeKind; 512]` parallel track + having `terminators.rs::TerminatorKind::Call` lowering push kinds in lockstep with bits, or (ii) per-callee kind side-table threaded through the MIR emitter into a separate FFI signature. Both are ADR-level extensions. Cluster-1 (hardening) territory. (b) **§2.7.5 `jit_v2_map_*` typed-HashMap FFI rebuild** — referenced as W11-jit-carrier-conversion follow-up in `ffi_refs.rs:209` / `compiler/ffi_builder.rs:208` / `mir_compiler/v2_typed_map.rs:71-99`. These are the kinded `Arc<HashMapData>` + `KindedSlot` map FFI bodies (e.g., `jit_v2_map_get_str_i64` / `get_str_f64` / `has_str` / `set_str_i64` / `len`); the deleted ValueWord-shape bodies decoded map handle + key as raw u64 bits via tag_bits. Implementing them is a full new FFI surface with kind-aware HashMap entry-point bodies — separate concern from the carrier-conversion identity functions. Surfaced for a future W12-jit-typed-map-ffi sub-cluster. (c) **§2.7.11/Q12 raw-Arc closure callee in `jit_call_value`** — the actual surface fires on smoke 1 (`fn divide(...) -> Result<int> { ... } match divide(10,2) { ... }`); the `match` translates to MIR that emits an indirect call with a raw Arc closure callee; my code surfaces gracefully. The §2.7.5 follow-up (item (a)) is the principled resolution. **Pre-existing JIT downstream behavior**: with my changes, smoke 1 in `--mode jit` continues past my graceful surface-and-stop (returning TAG_NULL) and downstream code segfaults (exit 139 / SIGSEGV) trying to interpret a TAG_NULL result. Pre-my-change: extern-C `todo!()` aborted at the first call (exit 134 / SIGABRT). Both are crashes; mine is downstream of my surface, the other was AT my surface. Smoke 3 in `--mode jit` fails earlier with a `Route A surface-and-stop` from `Rvalue::Aggregate` (top-level `concrete_types: Vec::new()` gap surfaced by W11-jit-new-array — out of W11-jit-carrier-conversion territory). Smoke 2 in `--mode jit` fails at JIT compile time with `can't resolve symbol main_f...` — JIT linker issue (orthogonal to carrier conversion). **`closure_dispatch_regression_tests`** stay gated behind `deep-tests` per W11 round-1 close — `jit_call_value`'s case 3 (raw Arc closure) still surfaces, which is what those tests exercise. **Refcount audit (strconcat smoke)**: `SHAPE_JIT_ARC_COUNTERS=1` on `let s = "hello"; print(s + " world")` measures `retain_calls=2 release_calls=0 release_frees=0` — same baseline as `phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` documented pre-existing leak (compile-time-boxed string constants leak by design via `box_string` in `MirConstant::Str` lowering; pre-W11 pattern, separate workstream). My work did NOT introduce new retain/release calls — the conversion functions are identity functions that don't touch refcounts. **Smoke target results — under `--mode vm` and `--mode jit` separately**: (1) Smoke 1 (`fn divide(a: int, b: int) -> Result<int> { ... } let r = divide(10, 2) match r { Ok(v) => print(v), Err(e) => print(e) }`): VM `5` exit 0; JIT segfaults at downstream-of-graceful-surface (exit 139) — does NOT match. The JIT segfault is the §2.7.5 raw-Arc closure callee gap surfacing as TAG_NULL then downstream code failing on the null result; my surface-and-stop is correct, the downstream gap is the §2.7.5 follow-up. (2) Smoke 2 (`fn first_positive(...) -> Option<int> { ... }`): VM `Some(3)` exit 0; JIT exit 1 with `can't resolve symbol main_f...` — JIT linker issue orthogonal to carrier conversion. (3) Smoke 3 (`type Point { x, y } let p = Point{...}; print(p.x + p.y)`): VM `7` exit 0; JIT exit 1 with `Route A surface-and-stop: Rvalue::Aggregate` (top-level `concrete_types` empty per W11-jit-new-array surface — out of W11-jit-carrier-conversion territory). The W11-round-1 smoke (`let mut sum = 0; for i in 0..100 { sum += i }; print(sum)`) continues to work: VM `4950` exit 0; JIT `4950` exit 0. **ADR-006 §2.7.5 amendment**: NOT needed — the carrier shape `(u64, NativeKind)` is unchanged; the conversion functions adopt the documented §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time discipline directly. A §2.7.5 follow-up paragraph on the JIT-side parallel-kind track on `JITContext.stack` is a candidate amendment for cluster-1 (hardening) territory when that work lands. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (316 passed, 0 failed, 38 ignored); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-stack-kind-track` | `../shape-w12-jit-stack-kind-track` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 3 — JIT-side parallel-kind track on `JITContext.stack` (`stack_kinds: [u8; 512]` byte-encoded `NativeKind`) mirroring ADR-006 §2.7.7 / Q9 stack parallel-kind invariant from the VM side. `JITContext` grows `stack_kinds: [u8; 512]` in lockstep with `stack: [u64; 512]`; offsets shifted accordingly (STACK_KINDS_OFFSET = 6208, STACK_PTR_OFFSET 6208 → 6720, GC_SAFEPOINT_FLAG_PTR_OFFSET 6328 → 6840). New `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/stack_kind_code.rs` defines the 1-byte encoding (codes 0..=23 scalar arms, 128..=156 `Ptr(HeapKind::*)` arms, 255 `SENTINEL` for uninitialized slots). `TerminatorKind::Call` indirect-call lowering, method-call lowering, and legacy ClosureCapture lowering all push kinds in lockstep with data writes via new `emit_kind_track_write` helper on `MirToIR`. `jit_call_value` reads the parallel track and dispatches on `callee_kind` per §2.7.11/Q12: `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)` → raw-Arc `Arc<HeapValue::ClosureRaw>` recovery via `typed_closure_function_id` + `read_capture_kinded` (the prior round's TAG_NULL graceful-surface for case 3 replaced by real closure-block extraction); `UInt64`/`Int64`-class → preserved JIT-internal NaN-box bit-shape dispatch (cases 1+2 — inline function refs `is_inline_function` and legacy unified-heap `HK_CLOSURE` callees, with the documented §2.7.5 "I64-wide raw bits without further classification" carrier kind). `infer_slot_kinds` extended with ClosureCapture arm seeding `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)` + `concrete_types` projection through new `native_kind_from_concrete_type` (Array → `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`, HashMap → `Ptr(HeapKind::HashMap)`, Struct/Enum/Tuple → `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)`, Result/Option → matching `Ptr(HeapKind::*)`, Closure/Function → `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)`, etc.). Producing-site `operand_slot_kind_or_carrier` falls back to documented §2.7.5 carrier kind `NativeKind::UInt64` (NOT Bool-default, NOT a "soft-fail counter" — `UInt64` is the documented JIT-FFI carrier kind for I64-wide raw bits per `dispatch_call_via_trampoline_vm` already-existing convention) when MIR inference is opaque (e.g. opaque-source field/index reads). The `MirConstant::Function(_)` constants now infer to `UInt64`, `MirConstant::Method(_)` and `Str(_)`/`StringId(_)` to `String`, `MirConstant::ClosurePlaceholder` to `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)`. **Smoke 1.5 still segfaults** — root cause is NOT the case 3 closure dispatch (case 3 is now closed) but the enum-constructor-pseudo-call shape: `MirConstant::Function("Ok")` not found in `function_indices` → `compile_constant` returns `iconst(I64, 0)` → indirect call dispatches on `callee_bits = 0` → my dispatch returns TAG_NULL → downstream code dereferences the null result and segfaults. The pre-W12 baseline crashed at the same point (extern-C `todo!()` SIGABRT, then W11 round-2 TAG_NULL graceful-surface, then this segfault); my parallel-kind track changes the surface message but not the segfault location. The actual smoke failure is an enum-constructor-lowering bug (not a case 3 closure bug) — surfaced for a separate cluster (likely enum-constructor-MIR-lowering or W12-jit-linker-resolve neighborhood). | cluster-0 Round 3 | `crates/shape-jit/src/context.rs` (JITContext field + offsets + Default init) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/stack_kind_code.rs` (new: 1-byte NativeKind encoding) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/mod.rs` (module registration) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/terminators.rs` (indirect-call + method-call kind-track writes) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs` (legacy ClosureCapture kind-track) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/conversions.rs` (`emit_kind_track_write` helper on `MirToIR`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs` (ClosureCapture seed + `native_kind_from_concrete_type` projection + Function/Method/ClosurePlaceholder constant kinds) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs` (`operand_slot_kind_or_carrier` + enriched constant kinds for Function/Method/Str/ClosurePlaceholder) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/mod.rs` (`concrete_types`-seed wiring through `infer_slot_kinds`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/control/mod.rs` (`jit_call_value` kind-aware dispatch, case 3 closed) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 3, close commit `1a4d1156`, 2026-05-12. **Parallel-kind track design + where it lives**: `JITContext.stack_kinds: [u8; 512]` next to `stack: [u64; 512]`, 1-byte-per-slot byte-encoded `NativeKind` per the new `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/stack_kind_code.rs` module. Cranelift codegen writes kind bytes via `MirToIR::emit_kind_track_write(slot_idx, kind)` — `iconst(I8, code) + store` against `JITContext + STACK_KINDS_OFFSET + slot_idx` (the kind track's byte offset equals the slot index since each entry is 1 byte; no `<< 3` shift like the data side). Init via `JITContext::default()` is the `SENTINEL = 255` byte across the whole array; live pops reset slots back to `SENTINEL` for hygiene (mirror of VM `pop_kinded`'s `write Bool sentinel on dead slot` discipline at `vm_impl/stack.rs:706`). **TerminatorKind::Call lowering changes**: indirect-call path writes 3 kind sets in lockstep with the existing data writes: (a) callee kind from `operand_slot_kind_or_carrier(func)`; (b) per-arg kinds from `operand_slot_kind_or_carrier(arg)`; (c) arg_count sentinel slot stamped `NativeKind::UInt64` per ADR-006 §2.7.5/§2.7.11 carrier-kind convention. Method-call path mirrors: per-arg kinds + method-name slot stamped `NativeKind::String` (heap String pointer) + arg_count sentinel stamped `UInt64`. Legacy ClosureCapture path (statements.rs, fires only when no `ClosureLayout` is registered) writes per-capture kinds via the same fallback. **jit_call_value case 3 resolution path**: pops callee/args/arg_count with their kinds from the parallel track; decodes via `stack_kind_code::decode`; classifies the callee on `NativeKind` directly (§2.7.11/Q12 dispatch) — `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)` arm performs `Arc::<HeapValue>::from_raw(callee_bits)` + `HeapValue::ClosureRaw(block)` pattern + `typed_closure_function_id(block.as_ptr())` + per-capture `block.read_capture_kinded(idx)` reads, then `Arc::into_raw` to restore the slot's share (the call shell borrows; the share stays with the producer per §2.7.11 borrow contract). `UInt64`-family arm preserves the legacy bit-shape dispatch for inline function refs (`is_inline_function` / `unbox_function_id`) and legacy `HK_CLOSURE` callees (`is_heap_kind(_, HK_CLOSURE)` + `unified_unbox::<JITClosure>`). The third (unrecognized kind) arm surfaces with a kind-named diagnostic citing §2.7.11/Q12 — no SIGABRT, no silent leak. **Smoke 1.5 result delta** (`fn divide(a: int, b: int) -> Result<int> { ... } let r = divide(10, 2); match r { Ok(v) => print(v), Err(e) => print(e) }`): VM `5` exit 0. JIT segfaults exit 139 (same exit code + same segfault location as W11-round-2 baseline) — the case 3 dispatch is now closed but the *root cause* of the smoke failure was misdiagnosed in the kickoff prompt. The actual surface is: bytecode compiler emits `MirConstant::Function("Ok")` (or some enum-related function name) for the enum constructor in match-arm or constructor lowering; `Ok` is not in JIT's `function_indices` (it's an enum-variant constructor, not a JIT-compiled function); JIT `compile_constant` returns `iconst(I64, 0)` per the fallback; indirect-call's `callee_bits = 0`; my code stamps `UInt64` kind from `operand_slot_kind_or_carrier`, decodes UInt64 at the entry, falls into the legacy bit-shape dispatch which classifies bits=0 as "neither inline function nor HK_CLOSURE", surfaces TAG_NULL; downstream code dereferences the null result and segfaults. The `--mode jit` end-to-end fix for Smoke 1.5 requires either (i) enum constructors to short-circuit through a different MIR shape (no indirect call), or (ii) the W12-jit-linker-resolve sub-cluster to fix the `function_indices` registration so enum constructors resolve. Out of W12-jit-stack-kind-track scope per the supervisor's task description. **Smoke 1 (`for i in 0..100 { sum += i } print(sum)`)** continues to print `4950` under both VM and JIT (no regression). **Refcount audit (SHAPE_JIT_ARC_COUNTERS=1)**: Smoke 1 unchanged at `retain=0 release=0 frees=0` — our parallel-kind track does not touch refcounts (the kind bytes are pure metadata, not Arc shares). The simple-closure smoke (`let n = 3; let f = |x| x + n; print(f(39))`) measures `retain=1 release=0 frees=0` — pre-existing leak from compile-time-boxed closure path (carries through ClosureCapture); independent of our parallel-kind track work. **Sites surfaced**: (i) **Enum-constructor function-id lookup gap** — `MirConstant::Function("Ok")` / `Err` / enum-variant names not in `function_indices` lower to `iconst(I64, 0)` → `callee_bits = 0` in `jit_call_value`. §-cite: ADR-006 §2.7.5 producing-site classification — the bytecode compiler should emit a different MIR shape for enum constructors (e.g. `EnumStore` or a dedicated `MakeEnumVariant` opcode), not pseudo-call a non-existent function. Disposition: surface to a separate enum-constructor-lowering cluster (likely overlap with W12-jit-linker-resolve or `op_new_array` heterogeneous-element surface item #4 in phase-3-cluster-0-status.md). (ii) **Top-level concrete_types empty (status item #1)** — `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/strategy.rs:205` still has `concrete_types: Vec::new()` for the top-level frame. This means closure-bearing slots returned from function calls at top level (e.g. `let add3 = make_adder(3)`) don't get their `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)` kind seeded via the new `native_kind_from_concrete_type` projection. Function bodies have non-empty `concrete_types` so their internal closure slots ARE seeded correctly. §-cite: ADR-006 §2.7.5 conduit gap. Disposition: W12-top-level-concrete-types sub-cluster (already in Round 3). (iii) **`MirConstant::None` carrier kind** — left as `None` in `operand_slot_kind` since "null/unit" doesn't have a single defined carrier kind; the call-site `_or_carrier` fallback covers it as `UInt64`. §-cite: ADR-006 §2.7.5.1 nullable-arm classification. Disposition: out of W12 scope; revisit when a `None`-carrying slot needs precise kind threading. **Decisions beyond playbook**: (1) Used `[u8; 512]` instead of `[NativeKind; 512]` directly because `NativeKind` is a Rust enum without `#[repr(...)]` (so cross-Cranelift-boundary layout would be unstable) and Cranelift codegen needs a known byte width per slot for the lockstep `iconst(I8, code) + store` pattern. The 1-byte encoding is the §2.7.5 stable-FFI raw-byte shape — same discipline as the existing `RETURN_TAG_*` byte tags. (2) `operand_slot_kind_or_carrier` falls back to `NativeKind::UInt64` instead of surface-and-stop. `UInt64` is the documented §2.7.5 carrier kind for "I64-wide raw bits without further classification" per `dispatch_call_via_trampoline_vm:106-125`'s pre-existing convention; using it as a fallback when MIR inference is opaque is structurally correct (the value IS I64-wide raw bits by construction — every operand widens to I64 before push per terminators.rs R4.2E discipline). NOT a Bool-default (which forces no-op Drop/Clone masking refcount bugs). Surface-and-stop would crash JIT compilation on existing programs (~50+ stdlib functions where MIR inference doesn't reach every slot). The parallel-kind track still provides precise kinds when available (closure slots seeded via ClosureCapture; typed slots via `concrete_types` projection); the `UInt64` fallback only applies to opaque-source operands that go through the existing `is_inline_function` / `is_heap_kind` bit-shape dispatch downstream — same behavior as pre-W12 minus the case 3 raw-Arc path which is now closed by the precise-kind path. (3) Extended `infer_slot_kinds` to handle `ClosureCapture` (seeding `Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)`) and seeded `slot_kinds` from `concrete_types` via the new `native_kind_from_concrete_type` projection. **Whether ADR-006 §2.7.7 or §2.7.11 needs amendment**: a small JIT-side analog amendment under §2.7.7 would be useful to formally document the JIT-tier parallel-kind track shape (1-byte encoded `[u8; 512]` companion to `[u64; 512]`, vs the VM's `Vec<u64> + Vec<NativeKind>` companion vectors). The semantic invariant is the same — lockstep `data + kinds`, no tag-bit decode, no Bool-default — but the storage shape differs because Cranelift codegen wants byte offsets. Tagged as a candidate §2.7.7 follow-up; not load-bearing for cluster-0 close. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (319 passed, 0 failed, 38 ignored — +3 new tests in `stack_kind_code::tests` for scalar/Ptr roundtrip + SENTINEL); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.) | 2026-05-12 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-vw-tests-rewrite` | `../shape-w12-vw-tests-rewrite` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 3 — rewrite 17 pre-existing tests asserting deleted ValueWord-shape API (`is_typed_object(bits)` on raw `Box::into_raw` pointers, `is_heap_kind(bits, HK_CLOSURE)` for raw `Arc::into_raw` closures, etc.) against the new strict-typed API (`NativeKind::is_refcounted()`, `HeapValue::kind()`, `KindedSlot`-shaped predicates). Same semantics, kinded expression.
