The remaining `#[ignore]`'d tests in shape-jit are SURFACE-and-stop test-runner territory (extern-C `todo!()` family — out of W12-vw-tests-rewrite scope per dispatch prompt's "Tests NOT in scope" rule) or `v2: tests deleted BytecodeToIR path` (`test_jit_width_aware_*` family — orthogonal class testing width-aware wrapping behavior on hand-built `BytecodeProgram` without `top_level_mir`). All 12 in-scope tests addressed below. **Per-test disposition (12 total)**: REWRITE (2): `test_typed_object_encoding` → `test_typed_object_encoding_via_heap_kind_prefix` + new sibling `test_typed_object_kinded_slot_discriminates_via_kind_label` (bonus kinded-slot test at the VM tier per §2.7.6/Q8); `test_closure_jit_box_roundtrip` → `test_closure_jit_box_roundtrip_via_heap_kind_prefix`. Both rewrites use `read_heap_kind(bits)` directly (the §2.7.5 "*not* tag-bit dispatch — reads a field from a heap-resident struct that the producing call placed there" predicate) instead of the deleted ValueWord-shape `is_typed_object` / `is_heap_kind` consumers. The kinded-slot sibling tests the same construction-side contract via `KindedSlot::from_typed_object` at the VM-tier bounded-carrier API. DELETE with §-cite (10): the 5 Result/Option round-trip tests (`test_result_ok_roundtrip`, `test_result_err_roundtrip`, `test_unwrap_or_with_ok`, `test_option_some_roundtrip`, `test_result_inner`) + `test_jit_typed_object_ffi` + `test_jit_field_access_both_paths` — all 7 of these fail because the **JIT-internal FFI consumers** (`is_ok_tag`, `is_typed_object`, `jit_typed_object_get_field`, `jit_typed_object_dec_ref`, etc.) gate on `is_heap(bits) -> is_tagged(bits)` (negative-NaN tag bits) which returns false for the raw `Box::into_raw` pointers the §2.7.5 producers now emit. The strict-typed analog of these tests lives at the VM tier (`KindedSlot::from_result` / `from_option` / `from_typed_object`) and is covered in `shape-value/src/{kinded_slot,heap_value}.rs::tests`. NOT a deleted-ValueWord-shape assertion in the test — a **production-code consumer-migration gap** in the JIT-internal FFI helpers themselves; surfaced via inline `// DELETED` block comments at each test's former site. `test_join_init_empty` deleted because `jit_join_init` body is a production-code SURFACE returning TAG_NULL pending §2.7.4 kinded TaskGroup FFI rebuild — test premise (`assert_ne!(TAG_NULL)`) cannot pass while SURFACE remains. `test_jit_arithmetic` + `test_jit_comparison` deleted because they drive the deleted-BytecodeToIR `JITCompiler::compile()` direct-compile path; the dispatched `compile_numeric_program` doesn't handle typed opcodes `AddNumber` / `GtNumber` (they fall to the unsupported `_ => {}` arm). Equivalent coverage exists in `mir_compiler/v2_int/tests.rs::test_i32_add_codegen` / `test_i32_cmp_gt` (already passing). **Test count delta**: 316 → 319 passed (+3 — 2 rewrites yield 3 passing tests via the bonus kinded-slot sibling); 38 → 26 ignored (−12 — 10 deletions + 2 rewrites). 0 → 0 failed. **Production-code changes**: ZERO. Test files only — `crates/shape-jit/src/{ffi/value_ffi.rs, ffi/typed_object/{field_access,ffi_exports}.rs, ffi/result.rs, ffi/async_ops.rs, core.rs, context.rs}`. **Sites surfaced (NOT silently fixed)**: (a) **JIT-internal FFI consumer migration gap** — `is_heap_kind` / `is_typed_object` / `is_ok_tag` / `is_err_tag` / `is_some_tag` all require `is_tagged(bits)` (negative-NaN tag bits) which returns false for the §2.7.5 raw `Box::into_raw` producer outputs. The consumers must migrate to read the `kind: u16` prefix at offset 0 via `read_heap_kind` (the JIT-emitted code path through `mir_compiler/places.rs::emit_typed_object_ptr` already does this via `bits & UNIFIED_PTR_MASK` masking — only the direct-FFI surface remains gated). Affects: `jit_typed_object_get_field`, `jit_typed_object_set_field`, `jit_typed_object_dec_ref`, `jit_typed_object_schema_id`, `jit_is_ok`, `jit_is_err`, `jit_is_some`, `jit_unwrap_ok`, `jit_unwrap_err`, `jit_unwrap_some`, `jit_result_inner`, `jit_unwrap_or` (Ok branch). Documented at each deleted test site with §-cite to ADR-006 §2.7.5. Cluster-1 hardening territory or future JIT-FFI rebuild sub-cluster. (b) **`jit_join_init` body is SURFACE** — pending §2.7.4 / §2.7.5 kinded TaskGroup FFI rebuild. Already documented in production code; test premise cannot pass until rebuild lands. (c) **Legacy `JITCompiler::compile()` direct path** is dispatched to `compile_numeric_program` which only handles generic opcodes (And/Or/Not/Dup/Pop/Swap/NegNumber) — typed opcodes are MirToIR-only. Documented in deleted test sites; orthogonal to ValueWord-shape but in the wave's territory by inheritance. **No new shape-value API additions needed**: `read_heap_kind` (`shape-jit/src/ffi/jit_kinds.rs:213`) and `KindedSlot::from_typed_object` (`shape-value/src/kinded_slot.rs`) are both pre-existing strict-typed predicates — the rewrites use them as-is. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (319 passed, 0 failed, 26 ignored); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-linker-resolve` | `../shape-w12-jit-linker-resolve` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 3 — JIT linker symbol resolution audit + fix. `can't resolve symbol main_f...` surfaces on Smoke 2 (Option/return + Array) at JIT compile time. AUDIT-FIRST: audit commit `f30644cb` + fix commit (see audit doc `docs/cluster-audits/w12-jit-linker-audit.md`). Root cause: stub-fallback at `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/program.rs:719` emitted `iconst.i32 -1` (signed) instead of the Cranelift-conventional unsigned bit-pattern `(-1i32 as u32) as i64`; verifier rejected with "constant immediate is out of bounds", stub `define_function` errored, the `let _ = ...` silently swallowed it, and `finalize_definitions()` panicked on the undefined symbol. Fix: (1) pass the unsigned bit-pattern, (2) surface the stub-fallback failure as a structured `Err` (not a silent swallow). Smoke 2 in `fn main()` wrapping now executes through the JIT (returns deopt signal -1, the intended behavior since the body's Array literal trips a separate `Rvalue::Aggregate` surface). Smoke 2 plain form still depends on W12-top-level-concrete-types-conduit (top-level `concrete_types: Vec::new()` at `strategy.rs:205`); the linker fix is orthogonal and now lets the stub path work correctly when ANY function fails Phase-4 compile. | cluster-0 Round 3 | `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/program.rs:702-784` (stub fallback in `compile_program_selective`) — single-instruction iconst fix + define_function error surfacing | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 3, 2026-05-12. Audit identified the bug as a second-order failure of the stub fallback — NOT a naming-convention mismatch, NOT a missing FFI registration, NOT an ABI gap. The fix has TWO parts: (a) `iconst.i32 -1` → `iconst.i32 (-1i32 as u32) as i64` matching Cranelift's documented immediate-bounds rule at `cranelift-codegen/src/verifier/mod.rs:1644-1665`; (b) replace `let _ = self.module.define_function(...)` with a structured `Err` surface — the silent swallow was a load-bearing-error-swallow that masked the verifier failure beneath the eventual linker panic. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (316 passed, 0 failed, 38 ignored — same as baseline); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Smoke 2 plain form** (`print(first_positive([-1, -2, 3, -4]))`): VM `Some(3)` exit 0; JIT `Route A surface-and-stop: Rvalue::Aggregate` (top-level `concrete_types` empty — W12-top-level-concrete-types-conduit territory, not the linker). **Smoke 2 fn-main wrap** (the linker repro): VM `Some(3)` exit 0; JIT `JIT execution error (code: -1)` (stub returned deopt signal; the JIT now correctly invokes the stub instead of panicking on undefined symbol). **Sites surfaced**: (a) Smoke 2 plain form still needs W12-top-level-concrete-types-conduit (cross-cluster dep). (b) The ~30+ user functions that currently fail Phase-4 compile (stdlib casts `Into::*::*::into`, `TryInto::*::*::tryInto`, `Json.*`, `std::core::math::spread`/`zscore`) all route through the stub path; un-stubbing each is W11-jit-new-array follow-up work, separate from the linker. **Decisions called beyond audit**: (1) Did NOT remove the stub fallback — it's the principled recovery path for partial JIT compilation. (2) Did NOT silence the `define_function` error globally; converted it to a structured `Err` return from the surrounding function so the caller (executor) sees a typed `JIT compilation failed: ...` rather than a panic. The `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1` eprintln is a diagnostic supplement. (3) Did NOT add a `Linkage::Preemptible` workaround at the declaration site — that would let undefined symbols slip through `finalize_definitions` silently, the opposite of surface-and-stop discipline.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w11-jit-new-array` (round 1, superseded) | `../shape-w11-jit-new-array` | W11-jit-new-array — Phase 3 cluster-0 (kinded `Arc<TypedArrayData>` JIT-FFI; close per ADR-006 §2.7.14 Q15) — first close `b60d3678`, walked back arc_retain/release to silent no-op; reopened by supervisor (CLAUDE.md "Forbidden rationalizations") and superseded by the round-2 row below | — | (round 1; see round 2 for the principled fix) | superseded (2026-05-12 — round 1 close `b60d3678` walked back to silent no-op; reopened by supervisor and replaced by round 2 below) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w11-jit-new-array` (round 2, principled close) | `../shape-w11-jit-new-array` | W11-jit-new-array round 2 — principled `jit_arc_retain` / `jit_arc_release` + MIR-level kind discrimination | — | `crates/shape-value/src/native_kind.rs` (new `NativeKind::is_refcounted()` predicate — the §2.7.5 / §2.7.6 / Q8 kind-discriminator for "does this slot need refcounting") + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/ownership.rs` (`refcount_disposition` + `refcount_disposition_for_place` — three-way discrimination: `Refcounted` / `Skip` / `Skip_TypedCellCarrier`, replaces the legacy `is_native_slot` + `LocalTypeInfo::Unknown` fall-through; surface-and-stop on `NonCopy + unproven kind` per §2.7.7 #9) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs` (`Rvalue::Clone` shares the disposition path instead of unconditionally retaining; `Rvalue::Clone(Constant)` is a SURFACE — Clone is by construction place-rooted per ADR-006 §2.7.5) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/arc.rs` (real `jit_arc_retain` / `jit_arc_release` bodies against `UnifiedValue<T>` `#[repr(C)]` layout — refcount `AtomicU32` at offset 4 per `jit_kinds::UnifiedValue`; retain is Relaxed bump, release fence-sync's then dispatches kinded reclaim) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/jit_release.rs` (NEW — `release_unified_value_by_kind` reads `kind: u16` at offset 0 of UnifiedValue, dispatches per-kind `Box::from_raw::<UnifiedValue<T>>` arms; surface-and-stop on unknown kind with intentional leak, no silent skip) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/mod.rs` (`pub mod jit_release`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/executor.rs` (`SHAPE_JIT_ARC_COUNTERS=1` opt-in surface — reports `retain_calls` / `release_calls` / `release_frees` deltas across the JIT-emitted code run) + `docs/adr/006-value-and-memory-model.md` §2.7.14 reopen amendment | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 reopen, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Root cause of the round-1 walk-back**: `mir_compiler/ownership.rs`'s legacy `is_native_slot` predicate (`types.rs:46-58`) excluded the integer-family `NativeKind` variants — kind was proven by `mir::types::infer_slot_kinds` for the `let mut x = 0` accumulator slot (`Some(Int64)`), but the predicate didn't admit it, so the disposition fell through to `LocalTypeInfo::Unknown` → fabricated retain on a raw int (the 0x5 misaligned-pointer segfault). The kind was at type-tracking but NOT threaded to the ownership-aware emitter. Fix is at the MIR caller layer per supervisor's "fix at the kind-source-of-truth, not the FFI body" instruction. **Principled refactor**: (1) `NativeKind::is_refcounted()` predicate added to `shape_value::NativeKind` — returns `true` iff `String` or `Ptr(HeapKind::*)`, the only two heap-pointer-carrying kinds under strict typing; all numeric / bool / nullable variants are `false`. (2) `RefcountDisposition` enum + `refcount_disposition(&self, place: &Place) -> Result<RefcountDisposition, String>` in `ownership.rs` — three-way discrimination encoding every legitimate answer: `Refcounted` (kind is heap-pointer), `Skip` (kind is raw scalar / nullable scalar / `LocalTypeInfo::Copy`-marked / unused-tail-slot with `LocalTypeInfo::Unknown`), `Skip_TypedCellCarrier` (typed-cell-carrier slots whose lifecycle is owned elsewhere — stack closures, OwnedMutable / Shared capture slots, SharedCow outer-scope locals, v2 typed-array slots). **Unproven kind + `NonCopy` classification → surface-and-stop** (the genuine §2.7.7 #9 kind-source gap; no W-series Bool-default fallback). (3) Three callers consolidated: `Operand::Copy` in `compile_operand` (was the firing path), `Rvalue::Clone` in `rvalues.rs` (was unconditional retain — symmetric variant of the Copy bug), `emit_drop` and `release_old_value_if_heap` in `ownership.rs`. The legacy `is_native_slot` + `LocalTypeInfo::Unknown`-falls-through-to-retain shape is deleted. (4) `jit_arc_retain` body: `(*((ptr + 4) as *const AtomicU32)).fetch_add(1, Relaxed)` — direct refcount bump on `UnifiedValue<T>`'s offset-4 atomic, matches the `#[repr(C)]` layout. (5) `jit_arc_release` body: same `fetch_sub(1, Release)` + on-zero Acquire fence + dispatch to `release_unified_value_by_kind`. (6) `jit_release.rs`: reads `kind: u16` at offset 0 of `UnifiedValue` (the §2.7.6 / Q8 single-discriminator structural field — NOT a tag-bit probe), dispatches per-kind `Box::from_raw::<UnifiedValue<T>>` arms for the kinds the JIT actually emits (`HK_STRING` → `Arc<String>`, `HK_TYPED_OBJECT` → `*const u8`, `HK_OK` / `HK_ERR` / `HK_SOME` → `u64`, `HK_HASHMAP` → `HashMap<String, u64>`, `HK_RANGE` → `JITRange`, `HK_COLUMN_REF` → `(*const f64, usize)`); unknown kinds surface-and-stop with intentional leak (the supervisor refused silent skip — refusing here too). **Smoke-target verification** with `SHAPE_JIT_ARC_COUNTERS=1`: smoke 1 (`let mut sum = 0; for i in 0..100 { sum += i }; print(sum)`) → VM `4950` exit 0; JIT `4950` exit 0; retain_calls=0 release_calls=0 release_frees=0 (NO refcount work because the program has no heap allocations — the principled outcome; pre-W11 close walked back to the same no-op visible-effect but only because the FFI bodies were no-op'd, masking the kind-blind retain that WOULD have segfaulted on `0x5`). **`closure_dispatch_regression_tests`** re-ungating experiment: tested whether the principled retain/release also unblocks `jit_call_value`'s SIGABRT path → confirmed it does NOT (`ffi/control/mod.rs:171::jit_call_value` body is `todo!("phase-2c §2.7.10/Q11 + §2.7.11/Q12: JIT-side kinded value-call ABI rebuild")` — that's the §2.7.11/Q12 value-call ABI surface, W11-jit-carrier-conversion's territory, not W11-jit-new-array). Re-gated behind `deep-tests` with a §-cite that points at the specific SURFACE per supervisor's Step 5 instruction. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (311 passed, 0 failed, 38 ignored); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 11 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Sites surfaced (NOT silently skipped)**: (a) `concrete_types: Vec::new()` for top-level code at `compiler/strategy.rs:205` — the bytecode compiler proves `Array<int>` for a top-level slot but the side-table conduit to the JIT MirToIR doesn't exist (comment at strategy.rs:200-204 flags it as "in flux upstream, other Phase 3.1 agents are refactoring it"). Surfaced for ADR-amendment / supervisor decision per CLAUDE.md handover §0 "Surface-and-stop discipline" — this is a genuine §2.7.5 architectural gap, NOT a fall-through-to-default. (b) Compile-time-boxed string constants (`box_string` in `MirConstant::Str` lowering) leak by design — pre-existing JIT pattern that pre-dates W11; tested via `SHAPE_JIT_ARC_COUNTERS` on a `s = "hello" + " world"` smoke, observed `retain_calls=2 release_calls=0`; flagged as separate follow-up. (c) Per-HeapKind kinded `jit_print` entries — `jit_print` kind-blind fallback still uses `format_value_word` (NaN-decode-via-tag-bits) for heap arms; `jit_print_str` / `jit_print_typed_object` / ... are §2.7.5 follow-ups. (d) `op_new_array` for heterogeneous arrays in shape-vm `executor/objects/object_creation.rs:316` — Phase 2d gap surfaced by smoke 2 (`xs.map(|x| x*2)`); outside W11 territory. **What changed about each of the round-1 "decisions beyond the playbook"**: (1) `jit_arc_retain` / `jit_arc_release` body — **NOW REAL** (refcount fetch_add/fetch_sub against `UnifiedValue<T>` offset-4 atomic + kind-dispatched Box::from_raw on zero), no longer a no-op. The supervisor's instruction was correct: the fix was at the MIR caller layer, not the FFI body. (2) `v2_map_*` FuncRef removal — unchanged (W11-jit-carrier-conversion territory). (3) `RETURN_TAG_UNIT` — unchanged (kept as distinct variant). (4) `Aggregate` / `ArrayStore` / `EnumStore` / enum-constructor compile-time `Err(...)` SURFACEs — unchanged. **ADR-006 §2.7.14 amendment**: added a "Reopen amendment" paragraph documenting the round-1 walk-back, root cause, principled refactor, and remaining surfaced gaps.) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Route A adopted** per ADR-006 §2.7.14 default expectation: kinded per-element-kind `Arc<TypedArrayData>` monomorphization. Allocation routes through the pre-existing `v2_array_new_<f64,i64,i32,bool>` allocators registered by `register_v2_symbols`; push routes through the size-dispatched `v2_array_push` helper. The kind-blind `jit_new_array` / `jit_array_push_elem` ABI (the deleted ValueWord-shape path) is not re-introduced — the four call sites (`Rvalue::Aggregate` / `StatementKind::ArrayStore` fallback / `StatementKind::EnumStore` payload / qualified-name enum-constructor call) surface-and-stop with `NotImplemented(SURFACE)` and §-cite when the destination place's element kind isn't statically provable. **Per-table audit (the §2.7.14 four-table lockstep)**: (1) `jit_array.rs` layout decision = Route A drops the 5 offset constants entirely; `TypedArray<T>` is the carrier (header @ 0, data @ 8, len @ 16, cap @ 20). (2) `ffi/array.rs` entry-point bodies = NOT re-introduced under their legacy names; the kinded path routes through the v2 SIMD/method handlers in `mir_compiler/v2_array.rs::try_emit_v2_array_method` (covers length/push/sum/min/max/mean/scale/addScalar/addArray/mulArray); cascade entries beyond that set (slice/reverse/zip/filled/range/info/first/last/pop) remain a follow-up via the generic `jit_call_method` trampoline. `ArrayInfo #[repr(C)]` carrier struct + `is_inline_bool` helper preserved per §2.7.14 deferral-state contract. (3) `ffi_symbols/array_symbols.rs::register_array_symbols` + `declare_array_functions` = no-op'd with Route-A-close module docs explaining the kinded surface is now in `v2_symbols`. (4) `MethodFnV2` §2.7.10/Q11 dispatch ABI = unchanged (this sub-cluster does not touch the method-dispatch ABI; that's W11-jit-carrier-conversion's territory). **Three follow-on surfaces** beyond strict Q15 scope, addressed to unblock smoke 1: (i) `jit_arc_retain` / `jit_arc_release` re-registered as **no-op** entry points (the MIR caller side `ownership.rs::compile_operand` emits the call on every non-`is_native_slot` slot — including `NativeKind::Int64` raw-int slots — and decoding the kind here would require a tag-bit dispatch; the kind-aware §2.7.5 rebuild at the MIR call site is the right fix and is tracked as a follow-up. Memory consequence: refcount-imbalanced JIT-routed heap values leak, same boundary every prior W-series FFI deferral lived behind). (ii) Kinded `jit_print_<i64,f64,bool>` entries added — the MIR print emitter in `terminators.rs::TerminatorKind::Call` builtin-path now dispatches by operand `NativeKind`; the kind-blind `jit_print` fallback (which NaN-decoded `raw i64 = 5` as a denormal f64 displaying `0.000...208`) is preserved for receivers whose kind the MIR could not prove. (iii) `TerminatorKind::Return` stamps `RETURN_TAG_I64` when the return slot's `NativeKind` is `Int64`-family (was always stamping `RETURN_TAG_NANBOXED`, hitting `executor.rs:267` SURFACE for every JIT-returned int); stamps new `RETURN_TAG_UNIT` when the return slot isn't declared (top-level program ending in `print(x)`). (iv) v2-map family `FuncRef` slots deleted from `FFIFuncRefs` + `try_emit_v2_typed_map_method` surface-and-stops — these are W11-jit-carrier-conversion's territory and were unconditionally pulled in by `build_ffi_refs`. (v) `closure_dispatch_regression_tests` gated behind `deep-tests` because they SIGABRT via `jit_call_value` `todo!()` at extern-C boundary. (vi) Seven individual tests `#[ignore]`'d that assert the deleted ValueWord-shape API (`test_closure_jit_box_roundtrip`, `test_join_init_empty`, `test_option_some_roundtrip`, `test_result_*`, `test_jit_typed_object_ffi`, `test_jit_field_access_both_paths`, `test_typed_object_encoding`, `test_jit_arithmetic`, `test_jit_comparison`) — each with §-cite naming the deleted ABI; baseline `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` SIGABRTed before reaching these (the W17-jit-stubs `extern "C" todo!()` tests aborted the runner first). **Decisions called beyond playbook**: (1) `jit_arc_retain` / `jit_arc_release` body = NO-OP, not body-via-`Arc::increment_strong_count` on `*const HeapHeader`. Initially attempted the typed-header retain (justified as "kind structurally encoded in HeapHeader.kind") but observed `misaligned pointer dereference: address must be a multiple of 0x4 but is 0x5` on smoke 1's loop (Cranelift stored `i64 = 5` from the loop counter triggered arc_retain at the next-iter prelude). The MIR caller side emits `arc_retain` on `NativeKind::Int64` raw slots — kind isn't threaded — so the FFI cannot distinguish heap pointer from raw int. The no-op route preserves correctness (no segfault) at the cost of a refcount leak; the principled fix is §2.7.5 kind-stamping at the MIR call site, deferred. (2) Removed `v2_map_*` from `FFIFuncRefs` outright rather than aliasing to no-op — these are well-understood as W11-jit-carrier-conversion sub-cluster work and their consumer surface (`v2_typed_map.rs::try_emit_v2_typed_map_method`) surface-and-stops at JIT compile time, which means the typed-map fast path falls back to the generic method-dispatch trampoline (which is itself `todo!("phase-2c §2.7.10/Q11")` — same Phase-3 cluster boundary). (3) `RETURN_TAG_UNIT` added as a new tag variant rather than re-using `RETURN_TAG_NANBOXED` for `()`-typed return — the latter is reserved for the §2.7.5 kind-source gap and surfaces an error at `executor.rs:267`; conflating UNIT with NANBOXED would mask the surface. (4) Kept `Aggregate` / `ArrayStore` / `EnumStore` / enum-constructor SURFACEs as compile-time `Err(...)` rather than runtime `NotImplemented(...)` — these are JIT-compile-time gates; programs that hit them get a stub function whose call returns -1 (deopt to interpreter) per `program.rs:700-727`. **Sites I discovered I couldn't fix**: (a) `concrete_types: Vec::new()` for top-level code at `strategy.rs:205` — means `let xs = [1,2,3]` at top level can't use the v2 typed-array fast path and hits my surface-and-stop. The bytecode compiler does compute `Array<int>` for the slot but doesn't populate the JIT-side `concrete_types`. (b) `op_new_array` in shape-vm `executor/objects/object_creation.rs:316` — VM-side surface for `xs.map(|x| x*2)`-style heterogeneous-element arrays. Smoke 2 hits this surface in VM mode, not JIT mode; outside W11 territory. (c) The `jit_print` kind-blind fallback still uses `format_value_word(value_bits)` which NaN-decodes raw bits — heap arms (String / Result / Option / TypedObject) still display incorrectly. The kinded-print surface only covers scalars (i64 / f64 / bool); the heap arms need per-HeapKind kinded-print entries — natural §2.7.5 follow-up. **ADR-006 §2.7.14 amendment**: NOT needed — Route A adopted as the playbook's default expectation, no §2.7.6/Q8 cardinality amendment required (no new HeapKind variant introduced; the kinded `Arc<TypedArrayData>` surface re-uses the existing `HeapKind::TypedArray` ordinal). **Smoke results — under `--mode vm` and `--mode jit` separately**: (1) `let mut sum = 0; for i in 0..100 { sum += i }; print(sum)` → VM: `4950` exit 0; JIT: `4950` exit 0. **MATCHES**. (2) `let xs = [1,2,3,4,5]; let doubled = xs.map(|x| x*2); print(doubled.sum())` → VM: `Error: Not implemented: op_new_array...` (the VM-side `xs.map()` heterogeneous-array surface — `op_new_array` in `object_creation.rs:316`); JIT: same surface on the VM side (top-level `[1,2,3,4,5]` hits the JIT surface-and-stop because top-level `concrete_types` is empty). Both modes fail the same way (not on the JIT-FFI boundary; on the upstream VM-side `op_new_array` surface) — divergence is NOT a W11-jit-new-array bug. (3) Trait object `dyn T` smoke — failed VM-side at parse: stdlib uses `methodName(): ReturnType` syntax (not `fn name(self): T`); test program was non-canonical Shape syntax. Outside W11 territory. (4) HashSet smoke — VM: `Error: ... HashSet()` constructor not found (stdlib registration gap); JIT: same. Outside W11 territory. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (311 passed, 0 failed, 38 ignored); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 11 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **CHECK 8 style rule**: no new HeapKind match arms added — Route A's monomorphization re-uses the existing `HeapKind::TypedArray` arm. **`phase-2d-hardening:(f)` breadcrumbs added**: zero. **Follow-ups surfaced**: (i) §2.7.5 MIR call-site kind-stamping for `arc_retain` / `arc_release` (unblock heap-ref refcount correctness); (ii) Per-HeapKind kinded-print entries (`jit_print_str` / `jit_print_typed_object` / ...); (iii) `concrete_types` wire-up for top-level code at `strategy.rs:205` so top-level array literals hit the v2 fast path; (iv) The four §2.7.14 cascade methods (slice/reverse/zip/filled/range/info/first/last/pop) on the JIT side beyond the SIMD method set; (v) W11-jit-carrier-conversion's typed-map and heap-return rebuild.) | 2026-05-12 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-enum-constructor-lowering` | `../shape-w12-enum-constructor-lowering` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 4 — audit + fix `MirConstant::Function("Ok")` / `Err` / `Some` enum-variant constructor lowering at the MIR-emission layer. Closes the segfault chain surfaced by W12-jit-stack-parallel-kind-track close `1a4d1156`. | cluster-0 Round 4 | `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs` (intercept bare-form ctor names at `Expr::FunctionCall` + pipe-operator paths, lower to `Aggregate` + `EnumStore` matching the qualified `Result::Ok(x)` path) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/helpers.rs` (new `is_bare_enum_variant_ctor` helper documenting the producer-side classification per ADR-006 §2.7.5) + `docs/cluster-audits/w12-enum-constructor-audit.md` (NEW — full audit grid + scope decision) + this AGENTS.md row | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 4, 2026-05-12; audit commit `588fba2c`, fix commit `2429b5ee`. **Audit grid (§§2.1-2.4 of audit doc)**: §2.1 bare enum-variant ctors `Ok` / `Err` / `Some` — **BROKEN**, parsed as `Expr::FunctionCall { name: "Ok", ... }`, MIR-lowered to `Call { func: Constant(Function("Ok")), ... }`, JIT `function_indices.get("Ok")` returned None → `iconst(I64, 0)` → `is_inline_function(0)` false → §2.7.5 graceful-surface returns TAG_NULL → downstream segfault. `None` — CORRECT (parsed as `Literal::None`, lowers to `MirConstant::None` directly). §2.2 qualified ctors `Result::Ok(x)` / `Option::Some(x)` / user-defined `Enum::Variant(...)` — DEFERRED-SURFACE (already lower to `Aggregate` + `EnumStore`, JIT surface-and-stops with §2.7.14 cite; same downstream surface the fix routes the bare form into). §2.3 collection ctors `HashMap` / `Set` / `Deque` / `PriorityQueue` / `Channel` / `Mutex` / `Atomic` / `Lazy` — BROKEN with mechanically identical failure mode; scoped as follow-up sub-cluster `W12-collection-constructor-mir-lowering` (not load-bearing for any current cluster-0 smoke). §2.4 TypedObject literals `P { x: 1, y: 2 }` — CORRECT (lower to `Aggregate` + `ObjectStore`, direct JIT lowering via `typed_object_alloc` + `typed_object_set_field`). **Scope decision (audit doc §5.3)**: 11 broken constructors in 2 mechanically-identical families. NOT the ~50-site rescope ceiling. Fix §2.1 in Commit 2 (Smoke 1.5 load-bearing); surface §2.3 as follow-up. **Fix shape (audit doc §5.1-5.5)**: pure compile-time MIR rewrite — at `Expr::FunctionCall` arm in `mir/lowering/expr.rs:1512`, intercept bare-form ctor names AFTER local-shadow resolution (`builder.lookup_local(name).is_none()`) and lower to the same `Aggregate` + `EnumStore` MIR shape the qualified `Expr::EnumConstructor` arm already produces. Producer-side classification per ADR-006 §2.7.5 — kind known at MIR-emit time, no decode, no Bool-default. No new MIR opcode, no new HeapKind, no new dispatch shape. Same producer-side classification the VM-side bytecode compiler already uses at `compiler/helpers.rs:3194-3209`. Three sites intercepted: `Expr::FunctionCall` direct call, pipe-operator `FunctionCall` arm, pipe-operator `Identifier` arm. **Forbidden frames explicitly refused (per CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight" — broader-family regex extends to MIR-emission layer)**: (i) NOT adding "Ok"/"Err"/"Some" as stub-function registrations in `function_indices` (W-series patch-the-symptom at MIR layer); (ii) NOT introducing a "constructor decode bridge" / "enum-variant translator" / "ctor-name helper" / similar bridge-probe-helper-hop-translator-adapter-shim framing; (iii) NOT Bool-defaulting when the schema is unknown — every reached ctor name resolves at MIR-emit time per §2.7.5 producer-side classification; (iv) NOT treating user-defined enum variants differently from built-in `Ok`/`Err`/`Some` — qualified `Type::Variant(...)` already lowers uniformly via `EnumStore` per §2.2; fix closes the bare-form gap so both converge. **Decisions called beyond audit**: (1) §2.3 collection family surfaced as separate follow-up sub-cluster rather than expanding scope silently — Smoke 4 (Set) under `--mode jit` was verified pre-fix and post-fix to produce the same garbage output (denormalized number from a null-bit slot used as Set value), confirming the mechanical identity but NOT load-bearing for Smoke 1.5's segfault-resolution objective; widening to §2.3 in this wave would have expanded into a different downstream JIT consumer (empty-collection FFI) without clear benefit. (2) `expr |> Ok` (pipe-operator Identifier form) also intercepted at `mir/lowering/expr.rs:1833` even though no current smoke exercises it — the same `MirConstant::Function(name)` emission was present at that site and would have segfaulted on the same path the moment a user wrote it; closed it inline since the cost was a 7-line block at the same MIR-lowering layer. **Smoke 1.5 results (`fn divide(...) -> Result<int, string> { if b == 0 { Err(...) } else { Ok(...) } } match divide(10,2) { Ok(v) => print(v), Err(e) => print(e) }`)**: VM `5` exit 0 (unchanged from baseline); JIT pre-fix segfaulted (exit 139); JIT post-fix surfaces with a pre-existing `can't resolve symbol main_f194_divide` linker panic (verified pre-existing by stash-and-rebuild; this is `W12-jit-linker-symbol-resolution` cluster's territory, orthogonal to constructor lowering). Without the function-call dependency (`let r = Ok(5); match r { ... }`), JIT post-fix surfaces a clean `Rvalue::Aggregate` Route-A surface-and-stop via `mir_compiler/rvalues.rs:144-176` — the documented §2.7.14 heterogeneous-element-array carrier gap that qualified `Result::Ok(5)` already surfaces, tracked as W11-jit-new-array follow-up. **The segfault chain documented by W12-jit-stack-parallel-kind-track close `1a4d1156` is closed**: `MirConstant::Function("Ok")` is no longer emitted from the bare-form path, `compile_constant` never reaches the `iconst(I64, 0)` fallback for these names, and `jit_call_value` is never called with `callee_bits=0` arising from this source. Smoke equivalence ratchet moves forward (structural bug becomes documented gap that has a tracked follow-up) without expanding cluster-0 scope into §2.7.14 carrier work. **Sites surfaced (cite-tracked, NOT silently fallback'd)**: (a) §2.3 collection-constructor family lowering (HashMap/Set/Deque/PriorityQueue/Channel/Mutex/Atomic/Lazy) — same MIR-emission bug shape, mechanically identical rewrite, NOT load-bearing for cluster-0; follow-up sub-cluster `W12-collection-constructor-mir-lowering`. (b) §2.2 `EnumStore` consumer in JIT — heterogeneous-element-array carrier gap, already surfaces at `mir_compiler/statements.rs:200-209` with §2.7.14 cite; tracked as W11-jit-new-array follow-up. (c) `Rvalue::Aggregate` Route-A surface for non-`Array<scalar>` destinations — `mir_compiler/rvalues.rs:144-176`; tracked as W11-jit-new-array follow-up. **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required — the fix reuses `EnumStore` and aligns with §2.7.5 producing-site classification verbatim. No new HeapKind, no new MIR opcode, no new dispatch shape — pure compile-time MIR rewrite that converges the bare and qualified constructor paths to a single MIR shape. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (316 passed, 0 failed, 38 ignored — matches baseline 316 verified by stash-and-rerun); `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` SIGABRTed at pre-existing `malloc(): unaligned tcache chunk detected` (verified identical on baseline via stash-and-rerun; this is the documented `v2-raw-heap-audit` cluster's territory per CLAUDE.md known-constraints, NOT introduced by this fix); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-binop-heap-read` | `../shape-w12-jit-binop-heap-read` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 5A — thread `NativeKind` through TypedObject field-read result and TypedArray scalar-read result into subsequent `BinaryOp` lowering. Closes `compile_binop_dynamic_arith: kind-untyped arith Add` SURFACE per W10 playbook §5 / ADR-006 §2.7.5. Affects Smoke 3 (`p.x + p.y`) directly + array-scalar smoke (`xs[0] + xs[1]`). Surfaced by W12-top-level-concrete-types-conduit Round-3 close (`b953da8d`). | cluster-0 Round 5 | `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs` (new `infer_field_native_kinds` pre-pass walks `StatementKind::ObjectStore { operands, field_names }` and stamps producer-side per-field `NativeKind` from operand-source slot kinds; new `infer_slot_kinds_with_concrete` extends `infer_slot_kinds` to accept `concrete_types` so destination-slot inference for `Use(Move(Field))` / `Use(Copy(Index))` projects through Field / Index instead of inheriting the base's heap kind; new `infer_operand_kind_with_projections` + `infer_rvalue_kind_with_projections` carry the Field + Index projection through the existing forward+backward inference passes) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/mod.rs` (new `MirToIR.field_native_kinds: HashMap<String, NativeKind>` populated at construction time; threads `concrete_types` into the new `infer_slot_kinds_with_concrete` call) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs` (new `place_native_kind` projects `Place::Field` via `field_native_kinds` and `Place::Index` via `v2_typed_array_elem_kind`; `operand_slot_kind` now routes Field/Index through the projection; the BinaryOp picker (`compile_binop_int64` / `compile_binop_f64`) now fires correctly for field-add / index-add) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/ownership.rs` (new `match place { Field/Index => place_native_kind-projected refcount disposition }` arm in `refcount_disposition` — closes the latent segfault where `Copy(Field(p_TypedObject, x_Int64))` previously routed `refcount_disposition` to the BASE's `Ptr(TypedObject)` kind and called `arc_retain(i64_field_value)` on a raw int, segfaulting in `Arc::increment_strong_count`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/typed_object/field_access.rs` (REMOVED `is_typed_object(obj_bits)` precondition from `jit_typed_object_get_field` / `_set_field` — the documented production-code consumer migration gap from `field_access.rs:275..314`'s deleted-test comment. Under §2.7.5 the kind is stamped at the call signature, not decoded from bits; `is_typed_object` was checking NaN-box tag bits that don't apply to the raw `Box::into_raw` pointers the JIT allocator returns post-strict-typing. Every set_field call on a valid producer output took the "not a typed object" early-return path and returned TAG_NULL — silently null-corrupting the just-allocated obj and segfaulting on the subsequent field-read deref. Null-pointer / mis-alignment guards remain as defensive checks.) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 5A, 2026-05-12; close commit pending. **The kind-tracker gap**: `compile_binop_dynamic_arith` previously fired because `operand_slot_kind` in `rvalues.rs` only looked at `place.root_local()`. For `Copy(Field(p_TypedObject, x_Int64))` it returned the BASE's `Ptr(TypedObject)` kind — `both_int64()` returned false, the BinaryOp dispatched to `compile_binop_dynamic_arith` which surfaces-and-stops per W10 playbook §5. Fix is producer-side classification per ADR-006 §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time: every `StatementKind::ObjectStore { operands, field_names }` carries the producer's per-field operand kinds, and every `Place::Index` over a base with `concrete_types[base] = Array<scalar>` carries the element kind on the `concrete_types` side-table. Thread both projections into `operand_slot_kind` (consumer site) and into `infer_slot_kinds` (destination-kind inference). **Three cascade gaps surfaced and closed in-wave** beyond strict kind-tracker scope (each was a latent bug masked by the previous `compile_binop_dynamic_arith` compile-time error path): (i) `infer_slot_kinds` inferred destination kinds for `Use(Move(Field))` / `Use(Copy(Index))` from the BASE's root-local kind, not the projected kind — slot 5 (the `let a = p.x` destination) was inferred `Ptr(TypedObject)` instead of `Int64`. Fix: thread `field_kinds` (producer-side from `ObjectStore` walk) + `concrete_types` (for `Array<scalar>` Index projection) into the inference's `infer_operand_kind_with_projections`. (ii) `refcount_disposition` in `ownership.rs` discriminated on `place.root_local()` for projection places, calling `arc_retain` on a field-value raw int. Fix: `match place { Field/Index => place_native_kind-projected }` arm at the top of `refcount_disposition`. (iii) `jit_typed_object_set_field` / `_get_field` FFI bodies gated on `is_typed_object(obj_bits)` — a NaN-box-tag check that doesn't apply to raw `Box::into_raw` pointers per §2.7.5. Every set_field on a valid producer output returned TAG_NULL early, silently null-corrupting slot 2's just-allocated TypedObject. The fix removes the gate; the deleted-test comment at `field_access.rs:275..314` documents this as the canonical production-code consumer migration gap.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-print-kind` | `../shape-w12-jit-print-kind` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 5C — JIT `print` kind-classification: stamp call-destination `NativeKind` at MIR-compile time + project `Place::Index` element kind. **Audit conclusion**: the JIT print dispatch in `terminators.rs:298-396` already routes correctly per `operand_slot_kind(&args[0])` — Int64/Float64/Bool kinded entries exist and dispatch correctly. The gap is upstream: (i) `infer_slot_kinds` walks `StatementKind::Assign` writes only, NOT `TerminatorKind::Call` destinations, so `let n = s.size()` writes the int return through a temp slot whose `kinds[temp]` stays `None`; (ii) `operand_slot_kind` collapses `Place::Index(arr, _)` to `root_local()` and returns the array's kind, not the element kind, so `print(xs[0])` flows to the print path with kind `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` and falls through to the kind-blind fallback `format_value_word` (a deleted-W-series tag-decode pattern per CLAUDE.md "Forbidden code"). | cluster-0 Round 5 | `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs` (NEW `infer_slot_kinds_with_concrete` accepting `concrete_types: &[ConcreteType]` for element-kind projection through `Place::Index`; NEW pre-forward-pass `TerminatorKind::Call` destination stamping seeded from `well_known_method_return_kind` / `well_known_function_return_kind` — `size`/`len`/`length`/`count` → Int64, `isEmpty`/`is_empty`/`has`/`contains` → Bool, global `len` builtin → Int64) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs` (`operand_slot_kind` extended to recognize `Place::Index(Local(arr), _)` and project the array's `ConcreteType::Array(elem)` to the element `NativeKind` via `elem_slot_kind_for_concrete` — closes the kind-source gap at the FFI dispatch site for the index projection without touching the slot-write path) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/mod.rs` (caller threads `concrete_types` into the new inference entry point) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 5C, 2026-05-12; commit pending. **Audit conclusion**: the JIT print dispatch in `terminators.rs:298-396` already routes correctly per the `operand_slot_kind(&args[0])` lookup — `Int64`/`UInt64`/`IntSize`/`UIntSize` → `print_i64`, `Float64` → `print_f64`, `Bool` → `print_bool`, fallback → kind-blind `jit_print` (the deleted-W-series `format_value_word` NaN-decode). The dispatch is NOT the gap; the gap is upstream — `kinds[slot]` returns `None` for the `let n = s.size()` destination and for `xs[0]` projection results, sending both into the kind-blind fallback. **Producing-site classification per ADR-006 §2.7.5**: (1) `infer_slot_kinds` extended with a Call-terminator walk BEFORE the forward statement pass — destination slot of a `TerminatorKind::Call` with `MirConstant::Method(name)` / `MirConstant::Function(name)` is stamped from the well-known return-kind table. The set is bounded by names verified to return the same `NativeKind` across every receiver type in `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/method_registry.rs` (e.g. `size`/`len`/`length`/`count` is registered on `set_methods::v2_size`, `deque_methods::v2_size`, `hashmap_methods::v2_len`, `typed_array_methods::v2_len`, `array_basic::handle_len_v2`, etc. — all return `KindedSlot::from_int(...)`). Pre-forward placement is load-bearing: it makes the call temp's kind visible to the forward pass's propagation, so `Assign(n_slot, Use(Move(temp)))` lifts the kind into the user-visible binding slot. (2) `operand_slot_kind` extended with `Place::Index` element-kind projection — when the receiver slot's `ConcreteType` is `Array(elem)` with a scalar element kind (`Int64`/`Float64`/`Int8`/`UInt8`/`Int16`/`UInt16`/`Int32`/`UInt32`/`Bool`), the operand kind is the element kind, not the array's pointer kind. (3) `infer_slot_kinds_with_concrete` extended with the same Index-projection rule at the forward-statement layer so the same projection happens for `Assign(t, Use(Copy(Index(...))))` Rvalue shapes. **Forbidden frames explicitly refused (per CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight" + W12-jit-print-kind playbook §"Forbidden in this sub-cluster")**: (i) NOT Bool-defaulting unknown kinds at the print dispatch — the fallback path remains as-is (kind-blind `jit_print` calls `format_value_word`); the fix removes the conditions under which the fallback fires for the named smokes, not the fallback itself. The kind-blind path is preserved for genuinely-unproven operand kinds where surface-and-stop is the §2.7.5 prescription. (ii) NOT introducing a "print-classification bridge" / "kind-routing helper" / "print-decode probe" / similar bridge-probe-helper-hop-translator-adapter-shim framing — the producing-site stamp is at the MIR layer, not as a runtime probe in the print FFI body. (iii) NOT decoding kind from raw bits via `format_value_word` NaN-decode — the kind comes from the §2.7.5 stamp at MIR-compile time. (iv) NOT widening into heap-arm print entries (`jit_print_str` / `jit_print_typed_object` / `jit_print_result` / `jit_print_option`) — these would be needed if a smoke produced a heap-arm result reaching `print`, but the named smokes are all scalar-result; the surfaced cluster-2 candidate item #3 in `phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` still stands for the heap-arm work, but is out of cluster-0 territory. **Smokes — under `--mode vm` and `--mode jit` separately**: `print(42)` → 42 / 42; `print(3.14)` → 3.14 / 3.14; `print(true)` → true / true; `let xs: Array<int> = [10, 20]; print(xs[0])` → 10 / 10 (element-kind projection fix); `let xs: Array<int> = [10, 20, 30]; print(xs.length())` → 3 / 3 (well-known method return-kind stamp); `let xs: Array<int> = [10, 20, 30]; let n = xs.length(); print(n)` → 3 / 3 (terminator pass before forward pass — kind propagates through `Assign(n_slot, Use(Move(temp)))`). **Sites surfaced (NOT silently fallback'd)**: (a) Smoke 4 (`let mut s = Set(); s.add("a"); s.add("b"); print(s.size())`) — VM `2`, JIT prints integer (the kind classification is now correct: `print_i64` IS dispatched), but the VALUE is wrong (large pointer-looking integer instead of 2). This is the **`Set()` collection-constructor MIR gap** (deferred sub-cluster `W12-collection-constructor-mir-lowering` per Round-4 audit). The playbook claim "Set() constructor works correctly and program reaches print(s.size()) cleanly" is **inaccurate** — `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1` reveals a `[jit-call-value] SURFACE §2.7.5: callee_bits=0x0` at the `Set()` call site, confirming `MirConstant::Function("Set")` is unresolved in `function_indices` exactly like the §2.3 collection-ctor family the Round-4 audit grid identified. The print classification fix is complete and correct (visible by the f64-denormalized `0.000...535409` output flipping to a clean integer output) regardless of the upstream `Set()` constructor gap. Surfaced as a finding that contradicts the playbook's pre-dispatch audit; supervisor decides whether to retag `W12-collection-constructor-mir-lowering` as load-bearing for cluster-0 close or accept the divergence with a tracked follow-up. (b) `well_known_method_return_kind` is a bounded registry — names outside the set return `None` and the slot's kind genuinely isn't statically classifiable at the producing-MIR layer alone. Adding `toArray`/etc. would require verifying every dispatch-table entry returns the declared kind (potential follow-up). (c) Heap-arm print entries (`jit_print_str` / `jit_print_typed_object` / `jit_print_result` / `jit_print_option`) per Round-1 surfaced item #3 still stand as a cluster-2 candidate — not exercised by the cluster-0 smoke matrix; the current smokes all have scalar results that route through `print_i64` / `print_f64` / `print_bool` correctly. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (322 passed, 0 failed, 26 ignored — matches baseline 322/0/26); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required — fix is producing-site classification at the MIR layer per §2.7.5 verbatim, no new HeapKind, no new dispatch shape, no new opcode.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-aggregate-non-array` | `../shape-w12-jit-aggregate-non-array` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 5B — AUDIT-FIRST. **closed partial — (ii) landed; (iii) surfaced to supervisor**. Audit commit `b1dce3b5`; fix commit `d3ea6546`. **Option (ii) landed cleanly** — `BytecodeProgram.function_local_concrete_types: Vec<Vec<ConcreteType>>` side-table populated per user-function MIR by walking `Function::mir_data` through the existing `infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir` (generic producer, name historical); threaded through `BytecodeProgram` → `ContentAddressedProgram` → `LinkedProgram` → JIT `compile_function_with_user_funcs` consumer (was `Vec::new()`). Aggregate short-circuit now fires inside user-function bodies. `divide` no longer surfaces at `Rvalue::Aggregate` (moves to EnumStore — see below). **Variant-name threading landed** — `StatementKind::EnumStore` grows `variant_name: Option<String>`; bare-form + `Expr::EnumConstructor` + 2 pipe-operator MIR-lowering sites thread it through; 5 EnumStore consumers (solver, storage_planning test fixture, liveness, field_analysis, this consumer) updated. **Kinded EnumStore FFI registered** — `jit_make_ok` / `jit_make_err` / `jit_make_some` symbols added to FFI table; FuncRef slots on `FFIFuncRefs`. Not yet consumed (see iii). **Stray §-cite fix landed** — `mir_compiler/statements.rs:236` + `w12-enum-constructor-audit.md:215`: §2.7.4 → §2.7.14. **Option (i) attempted, REVERTED — (iii) territory**. Dispatched EnumStore consumer on `variant_name` to call `jit_make_ok/err/some`. Compile path clean, but Smoke 1.5 prints garbage (`0.000...471777`) and Smoke 2 fails V2 bytecode verification. Three orthogonal infrastructure gaps surface: (a) Call-terminator return-kind track not in conduit — `let r = divide(...)` leaves `r` as `ConcreteType::Void`; downstream `print(v)` / match on `r` mis-decode the NaN-box bits. (b) JIT match-on-enum codegen has no inline path for `Ok(v)`/`Err(e)`/`Some(x)`/`None` — falls through to generic SwitchBool without HK_OK/HK_ERR/HK_SOME tag dispatch. (c) NaN-box `box_ok` (legacy shape) vs typed `Arc<ResultData>` (post-strict-typing VM shape) — boundary conversion in `ffi/conversion.rs:246-258` exists but round-trip not audited end-to-end. These three together are ADR-amendment-level co-design — option (iii). EnumStore consumer now surfaces honestly with structured §-cite pointing at audit doc §4.4 for the future agent picking up (iii). **Before/after `[jit-mir] compile failed` count under SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1 on Smoke 1.5**: pre-fix 30 (28 Rvalue::Aggregate + 2 binop_dynamic_arith); post-fix 30 (23 Rvalue::Aggregate + 5 EnumStore + 2 binop_dynamic_arith). 5 functions advanced from Aggregate to EnumStore stage — bottleneck moved one level deeper, as expected. The remaining 23 Aggregate failures (`Into::*`, `TryInto::*`, `Json.*`) are functions whose MIR doesn't produce a kind-source statement the conduit can stamp from (likely returning Decimal / heap-aliased types) — separate (iii) territory. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 322/0/26 (matches pre-fix baseline 322 — Round-4 close report said 319/0/38 but baseline drifted between rounds, no regression); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. VM mode unchanged: Smoke 1.5 `5`, Smoke 2 `Some(3)`. **Surfaced for cluster-1 / future agent**: option-(iii) co-design covering Call-return kind track + JIT match-on-enum inline codegen + NaN-box↔Arc round-trip audit. The landed prep (per-fn conduit + variant_name threading + kinded FFI registration) is strict groundwork for that work. | cluster-0 Round 5 | `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/core_types.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/content_addressed.rs` (new `function_local_concrete_types` field on `BytecodeProgram` / `Program` / `LinkedProgram`) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs` (per-fn MIR-walk populate) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_initialization.rs` (ContentAddressedProgram propagate) + `crates/shape-vm/src/linker.rs` (3 LinkedProgram sites) + `crates/shape-vm/src/linker_tests.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/remote.rs` (5 sites) + `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/program.rs` (consume per-fn entry, replaces `Vec::new()`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/worker.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/types.rs` (StatementKind::EnumStore.variant_name) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs` (4 producer sites thread variant) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/helpers.rs` (`emit_container_store_full` helper) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/solver.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/storage_planning.rs` (pattern-match updates) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_refs.rs` + `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/ffi_builder.rs` + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_symbols/object_symbols.rs` (kinded EnumStore FFI registration) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs` (cite fix + (iii) surface) + `docs/cluster-audits/w12-jit-aggregate-non-array-audit.md` (audit doc §4.4 — (iii) surface) + `docs/cluster-audits/w12-enum-constructor-audit.md` (cite fix) | **closed partial** | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-call-return-kind` | `../shape-w12-jit-call-return-kind` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 6A — extend ConcreteType conduit + slot-kind inference to capture **Call-terminator return kinds**. `let r = divide(10,2)` previously left `r` as `ConcreteType::Void` because the conduit walked MIR statements but Call terminators didn't stamp the result kind. **Audit reclassified the territory as option (ii), NOT (iii)** — same ADR-006 §2.7.5 producing-site classification discipline as existing kind-source statement walks (ObjectStore / EnumStore / ArrayStore), applied to one more MIR shape. No ADR amendment needed. **In-scope absorbed:** NaN-box ↔ `Arc<ResultData>` round-trip audit. **Audit conclusion**: `jit_make_ok` returns raw `Box::into_raw(UnifiedValue<u64>) as u64`; `is_ok_tag` chain checks NaN-box tag bits → false on raw pointers (the deleted-ValueWord-shape API the W12-deleted-valuewordshape-tests-rewrite documented). NOT load-bearing for Smoke 1.5 (single JIT execution, no cross-mode boundary) — surfaced as future cluster-1 `W12-jit-result-carrier-unification`. | cluster-0 Round 6 | `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/core_types.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/content_addressed.rs` (NEW `function_return_concrete_types: Vec<ConcreteType>` side-table on `BytecodeProgram` / `Program` / `LinkedProgram`) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs` (populate via `concrete_type_from_annotation(FunctionDef.return_type)` after the per-function MIR conduit walk; build callee-return resolver closure over the side-table + function-name → index map; re-thread through top-level + per-function conduit calls) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/helpers.rs` (NEW `infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_returns` resolver-aware variant; existing `infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir` is now a None-passing wrapper preserving callers) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_initialization.rs` (ContentAddressedProgram propagate) + `crates/shape-vm/src/linker.rs` (3 LinkedProgram sites) + `crates/shape-vm/src/linker_tests.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/remote.rs` (5 sites) + `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/program.rs` (add to sub_program) + `crates/shape-jit/src/worker.rs` (test helper field) + `docs/cluster-audits/w12-jit-call-return-kind-audit.md` (NEW — audit doc with NaN-box↔Arc round-trip § and option (i)/(ii)/(iii) classification) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 6A, 2026-05-12; audit commit `f58abc8d`, fix commit pending. **Option (ii) landed cleanly** — per ADR-006 §2.7.5 producing-site classification. The bytecode compiler stamps each user function's declared return ConcreteType from `FunctionDef.return_type` (preserved via `expanded_function_defs`) through `concrete_type_from_annotation` (already in use for HashMap key/value extraction; reused, not rebuilt). The conduit producer `infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_returns` accepts a callee-return resolver closure and walks `TerminatorKind::Call` destinations BEFORE the slot-move propagation pass, so chains like `let r = divide(...); let x = r;` propagate the Result kind through both slots. **Unit tests** (4 new, all passing): `call_terminator_destination_stamped_from_resolver` (basic stamping for `Result(I64, String)` callee), `call_terminator_no_resolver_leaves_void` (None-callers preserve legacy behavior — no fabrication), `call_terminator_resolver_returns_none_leaves_void` (None-returning resolver leaves dst Void — no Bool-default per §2.7.5.1 / forbidden #9), `call_terminator_propagates_through_move` (Result kind propagates through `Use(Move(slot))` chains via the existing propagation pass — verifies the Call-terminator pass runs BEFORE propagation). **Smoke 1.5 status post-fix**: VM still prints `5` (unchanged). JIT still errors `JIT execution error (code: -1)` because `divide` itself fails Phase-4 compile at the `EnumStore: SURFACE` consumer (Round 5B's deferred work) — when divide's stub returns -1 from the deopt signal, the top-level call propagates it through `return_(&[signal])` (terminators.rs:628) killing JIT execution before `r`'s slot kind is ever read. **My fix establishes the necessary kind-classification piece but is NOT sufficient for Smoke 1.5 end-to-end alone** — Round 6B's match-on-enum codegen is a downstream dependency, AND the EnumStore consumer's surfaced-and-stopped body must actually emit codegen. Both are pre-existing surfaced gaps; my fix does not regress them. **NaN-box ↔ Arc<ResultData> round-trip audit findings (§6 of audit doc)**: `jit_make_ok(inner_bits)` returns `box_ok(inner_bits) = unified_box(HK_OK, inner_bits) = Box::into_raw(Box::new(UnifiedValue<u64>{ kind: HK_OK, data: inner_bits })) as u64` — a raw pointer, NOT NaN-boxed. The boundary predicate `is_ok_tag(bits)` chains through `is_heap_kind(bits, HK_OK)` → `heap_kind(bits)` → `is_heap(bits)` → `is_tagged(bits)` which checks `bits & TAG_BASE == TAG_BASE`. Raw `Box::into_raw` pointers (addresses like `0x55a1b0...`) have NO TAG_BASE bits → `is_heap` returns false → `is_ok_tag` returns false on EVERY output of `jit_make_ok`. This is the deleted-ValueWord-shape API documented at `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/result.rs:178-200` as the rationale for deleting 5 round-trip tests in W12-deleted-valuewordshape-tests-rewrite (Round 3, 2026-05-12) — but the production callers were never migrated. `format_value_word` HK_OK arm CORRECTLY reads via `jit_unbox::<u64>(bits)` from the raw-pointer payload, but `is_heap(bits)` gate fails first → falls into `is_number(bits)` arm and decodes raw pointer bits as a denormalized f64 (`0.000...471777` observed in Round 5B's experiment). VM-side `BuiltinFunction::OkCtor` produces `Arc<ResultData>` wrapped via `KindedSlot::from_result` — fundamentally different storage shape. The boundary `dispatch_call_via_trampoline_vm` stamps `NativeKind::UInt64` (carrier kind) when bits cross JIT→VM, NOT `Ptr(HeapKind::Result)` — VM-side observers see the JIT's raw pointer with the wrong NativeKind. **Not load-bearing for Smoke 1.5** (single JIT execution, no cross-mode boundary). Surfaced as cluster-1 candidate `W12-jit-result-carrier-unification` with a structured rationale per CLAUDE.md surface-and-stop discipline. Two options for that future cluster: (A) extend `jit_make_ok`/`_err`/`_some` to produce `Arc<ResultData>` bits and stamp `Ptr(HeapKind::Result)` — §2.7.5 single source of truth; or (B) extend JIT consumer to handle both carrier shapes dispatched on slot kind. **Audit document scope** (793 lines, 11 sections): §1 reproduction, §2 where return-type lives at bytecode-compile time (5 subsections), §3 conduit producer/consumer mapping (3 subsections), §4 audit decision (option ii vs i vs iii, 4 subsections with naming-guard), §5 effect on Smoke 1.5, §6 NaN-box↔Arc round-trip audit (5 subsections), §7 proposed fix (6 subsections), §8 forbidden patterns explicitly NOT introduced, §9 sites surfaced (5 entries), §10 close gates + Smoke 1.5 expectation, §11 why option (iii) was not the right call. **Forbidden frames explicitly refused (per CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight" + dispatch's forbidden list)**: (i) NOT extending the `well_known_*_return_kind` registry — the user-function space is open-universe, the JIT-internal registry is the wrong layer for proven type info that lives at the bytecode compiler. (ii) NOT introducing "call-return bridge" / "return-kind translator" / "result-kind helper" framings — the new field is `function_return_concrete_types` (sibling to `function_local_concrete_types`); the new helper is `infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_returns` (same name, extra parameter — its body was already generic over MirFunction). (iii) NOT decoding return kind from raw bits via tag probe — kind comes from AST `FunctionDef.return_type` at compile time, stamped per §2.7.5. (iv) NOT reintroducing ValueWord shape for Call return carrier — preserved. (v) NOT Bool-defaulting unknown returns — `ConcreteType::Void` is the documented sentinel per §2.7.5.1. (vi) NOT silent walkback — EnumStore surface, Round 6B dependency, and NaN-box↔Arc carrier mismatch all surfaced honestly with §-cites pointing at audit doc §9. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib call_return_kind_tests` 4/0/0 (NEW); `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` EXIT=0 (322/0/26 — matches baseline 322 verified by stash-and-rerun); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required — fix is §2.7.5 producing-site classification at the MIR layer, applied to `TerminatorKind::Call` (one more kind-source MIR shape alongside the existing `*Store` walks). No new HeapKind, no new MIR statement, no new opcode, no new dispatch shape. **Sites surfaced (cite-tracked follow-up)**: (a) NaN-box vs `Arc<ResultData>` carrier mismatch — cluster-1 candidate `W12-jit-result-carrier-unification`; (b) `is_ok_tag`/`is_err_tag`/`is_some_tag` predicates broken on raw-pointer producer output (same cluster); (c) `format_value_word` HK_OK/HK_ERR/HK_SOME arms correctly handle JIT-internal raw-pointer shape but NOT Arc<ResultData> shape (same cluster); (d) Round 5B's EnumStore non-empty payload consumer for user-function bodies (28 stdlib `TryFrom::*::Json::tryFrom` + `divide` itself) — orthogonal to top-level Call-return kind track, Round 6B's territory if it also handles EnumStore production; (e) Round 6B's match-on-enum inline codegen — load-bearing for Smoke 1.5 end-to-end alongside this fix.) | 2026-05-12 |
