| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-collection-ctor-mir` | `../shape-w12-collection-ctor-mir` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 6C — lower `Set()` / `HashMap()` / `Deque()` / `PriorityQueue()` / `Channel()` / `Mutex(x)` / `Atomic(x)` / `Lazy(x)` constructors at MIR compile time per the W12-enum-constructor pattern (commit `2429b5ee`). Currently `MirConstant::Function("Set")` is emitted but no function indices match → `callee_bits=0` → garbage. Per Round-4 audit (`docs/cluster-audits/w12-enum-constructor-audit.md`), mechanically identical to enum-constructor fix. **Re-classified from "deferred non-blocker" to load-bearing** after Round 5C revealed Smoke 4 value-correctness depends on it (the print-classification fix gave a clean integer; the value `99757889857568` rather than `2` is because `Set()` produces uninitialized heap pointer). | cluster-0 Round 6 | `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs` (call-site intercept — mirror lines 1558-1596 from enum-constructor commit) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/helpers.rs` (new `is_bare_collection_ctor` helper alongside `is_bare_enum_variant_ctor`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs` (new `is_collection_ctor_name` consumer helper + EnumStore arm disambiguates collection-ctor names to surface a collection-ctor-specific message rather than no-op on empty operands) | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 6C, 2026-05-12; fix commit `d784042e`. **Audit grid (Round-4 audit doc §2.3) — re-verified 2026-05-12**: All 8 ctors still BROKEN pre-fix — verified by smoke runs with the freshly-built binary: `Set()` JIT prints `102733544169152`, `HashMap()` JIT prints denormalized float `0.000...053771826085245`, `Mutex(42).get()` JIT prints denormalized float `0.000...053061500682339`. Round 5C's `well_known_function_return_kind` registry only handles `len` — it did NOT inadvertently close any of the 8 collection-ctor sites. The §-cited segfault chain from kickoff (`callee_bits=0 → garbage downstream`) was confirmed by `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1` showing `MirConstant::Function("Set")` → `iconst(I64, 0)` → indirect-call → null-Arc method dispatch. **MIR shape chosen — option (b) reuse `EnumStore` with variant_name threading** per audit's §5.3 explicit recommendation ("strictly-simpler alternative"). No new MIR opcode, no new HeapKind, no exhaustive-match updates across the ~10 consumer files (solver / liveness / storage_planning / field_analysis / repair / etc. — all already iterate `EnumStore { operands, .. }` operands uniformly with no-op-on-empty-slice). New helper `emit_collection_ctor_store` bypasses the existing `emit_container_store_full` empty-guard so `Set()` / `HashMap()` / `Deque()` / `PriorityQueue()` / `Channel()` emit `EnumStore { operands: [] }` (the JIT consumer disambiguates by name); `Mutex(x)` / `Atomic(x)` / `Lazy(x)` emit `EnumStore { operands: [arg] }`. **Decision called beyond playbook**: pipe-operator intercepts (sites 2 + 3) restricted to the with-arg subset (`Mutex` / `Atomic` / `Lazy`) — zero-arg ctors via pipe (`x |> Set()`) fall through to the legacy `Call` path since `Set` rejects positional args at the VM-side ctor body (`executor/vm_impl/builtins.rs:592` argument-count check); matching the bytecode-compile-time error shape rather than silently discarding the piped value. **Smoke results (kickoff target + 2 additional)**: (1) `let mut s = Set(); s.add("a"); s.add("b"); print(s.size())` — VM `2` (unchanged); JIT pre-fix `102733544169152`, post-fix clean surface-and-stop "EnumStore: SURFACE — primitive-collection constructor 'Set' (operands.len()=0) requires the typed-Arc allocation FFI for its `HeapKind` ... not yet landed". (2) `let mut m = HashMap(); m.set("k", 1); print(m.get("k"))` — VM `1`; JIT pre-fix denormalized garbage, post-fix `'HashMap'` variant surface. (3) `let m = Mutex(42); print(m.get())` — VM `42`; JIT pre-fix denormalized garbage, post-fix `'Mutex'` variant surface. Plus all 8 ctors verified to construct valid containers in VM via combined smoke (`let s = Set(); let m = HashMap(); let d = Deque(); let pq = PriorityQueue(); let mu = Mutex(7); print(s.size()); print(m.len()); print(d.size()); print(pq.size()); print(mu.get())` prints `0 0 0 0 7`; Channel via `let ch = Channel(); ch.send(42); print(ch.try_recv())` prints `42`). **Forbidden frames explicitly refused (per CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight")**: (i) NOT adding `Set` / `HashMap` / etc. to a synthetic function-id registry (W-series patch-the-symptom at MIR layer); (ii) NOT introducing a "constructor decode bridge" / "ctor-name helper" / similar bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing — broader-family regex extends to MIR-emission per CLAUDE.md; (iii) NOT Bool-defaulting when the schema isn't statically derivable — every reached ctor name resolves at MIR-emit time per producer-side classification; (iv) NOT inventing a parallel "constructor registry" that aliases Function(name) at runtime — same shape as deleted ValueWord tag-bit dispatch; (v) NOT resurrecting ValueWord carrier for the collection payload; (vi) NOT treating user-defined enum variants vs collection ctors differently — both lower uniformly via `EnumStore` per the audit's §5.1. **Sites surfaced (NOT silently skipped)**: (a) Typed-Arc allocation FFI for the 8 collection-ctor HeapKinds (Arc<HashSetData> / Arc<HashMapData> / Arc<DequeData> / Arc<PriorityQueueData> / Arc<ChannelData> / Arc<MutexData> / Arc<AtomicData> / Arc<LazyData>) — needed in JIT FFI table for end-to-end `--mode jit` parity with VM. Same architectural shape as the typed-Arc enum-payload FFI gap surfaced by W12-jit-aggregate-non-array (Round 5B item 3 + Round 5B option-(iii) territory). Tracked as future-cluster FFI work; not load-bearing for cluster-0 close per the precedent's surface-and-stop equivalence-ratchet pattern. (b) The MIR pipe-operator `x |> Set` form (and other zero-arg ctors via pipe) intentionally falls through to the legacy `Call` path — surfaces as the existing function-table lookup miss, mirroring the bytecode-compile-time arg-count error. No current cluster-0 smoke exercises this; if a future smoke does, it should produce a typed compile-error not a silent value drop. **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required — the fix reuses `EnumStore` per audit's §5.3 recommendation, aligns with §2.7.5 producing-site classification verbatim, no new HeapKind / opcode / dispatch shape. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 322 passed / 0 failed / 26 ignored (matches baseline 322/0/26); `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` pre-existing SIGABRT in v2-raw-heap-audit cluster (unrelated, verified identical on baseline); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Parallel sub-cluster coordination**: Round 6A (W12-jit-call-return-kind-track) and 6B (W12-jit-match-enum-inline-codegen) touch JIT mir_compiler/types.rs (return-kind) and mir_compiler/terminators.rs (match dispatch) respectively; this sub-cluster touches mir/lowering/{expr,helpers}.rs (producer) and mir_compiler/statements.rs::EnumStore consumer. Zero file-territory overlap with 6A/6B confirmed.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-result-option-trinity` | `../shape-w12-jit-result-option-trinity` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 7A — **integrated trinity landing** of the three items Round 6B audit identified as co-design (any two landing independently risks forbidden Arc-vs-NaN-box boundary bridge framings): (i) match-on-enum inline codegen per 6B blueprint (`Rvalue::EnumTest { operand, variant }` + `Rvalue::EnumPayload { operand, variant }` MIR additions; `jit_arc_result_is_ok/is_err/payload` + `jit_arc_option_is_some/is_none/payload` FFI helpers reading from `*const ResultData`/`*const OptionData` per ADR-006 §2.7.17); (ii) `jit_v2_make_result_*` / `_option_*` Arc-shape producers (Round 6B audit + Round 6A NaN-box↔Arc audit identified as required); (iii) EnumStore non-empty payload consumer (Round 5B option (iii) surfaced gap — affects `divide` itself + 30+ stdlib `TryFrom::*::Json::tryFrom`). Single-agent integrated scope. ~2-3 sessions estimated. Unblocks Smokes 1.5 (`divide(10,2)` Result/match) + 2 (`first_positive` Option) + 30+ stdlib JIT-compile failures. | cluster-0 Round 7 | `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/types.rs` (new `Rvalue::EnumTest` + `Rvalue::EnumPayload` MIR variants) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs` (`lower_match_pattern_condition_operand`) + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/stmt.rs` (`lower_constructor_bindings_from_place_opt`) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs` (Result/Option Rvalue lowering) + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs` (EnumStore non-empty payload consumer per 5B option (iii)) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/` (new `jit_arc_result_*` / `jit_arc_option_*` FFI helpers; `jit_v2_make_result_ok/err` / `jit_v2_make_option_some` producers) + ~10 exhaustive-match site updates for the new Rvalue variants | **closed** (Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 7A, 2026-05-12; close commits `d01d83b7` (MIR + FFI), `ae5d57f2` (EnumStore consumer), `9f27edcd` (producer MIR + Arc-refcount fix)). **Trinity delivered**: (i) MIR types — `VariantTag` enum (`Ok` / `Err` / `Some_` / `None_`) with `from_name()` classifier; `Rvalue::EnumTest { operand, variant }` produces Bool from `*const ResultData::is_ok` / `*const OptionData::is_some`; `Rvalue::EnumPayload { operand, variant }` clones inner `KindedSlot` share via §2.7.17 receiver-recovery soundness. Exhaustive match sites updated in `mir/{return_ownership,field_analysis,liveness,solver,storage_planning}.rs` + `shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs::infer_rvalue_kind_with_projections`. (ii) Arc-shape FFI in `ffi/result.rs`: `jit_v2_make_result_ok/err`, `jit_v2_make_option_some/none` return `Arc::into_raw(Arc<ResultData>) as u64` / `Arc::into_raw(Arc<OptionData>) as u64` matching VM-side `BuiltinFunction::OkCtor`/`ErrCtor`/`SomeCtor`/`NoneCtor` output exactly. Predicates `jit_arc_result_is_ok/_err`, `_option_is_some/_none` read `is_ok`/`is_some` field from `*const T` directly — no NaN-box tag decode, no `is_heap_kind` probe (§2.7.7 #4/#7 forbidden). Payload extractors clone the inner share. The `payload_kind_code: u8` arg is the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-track encoding stamped at JIT-compile time from the MIR-inferred operand kind. (iii) EnumStore non-empty payload consumer wired in `mir_compiler/statements.rs` — dispatches on `variant_name`'s `VariantTag::from_name` to the right Arc-shape producer; user-defined enum variants surface-and-stop with a structured §-cite per §2.7.7 #9. **Producer-site MIR additions**: `lower_match_pattern_condition_operand` (expr.rs:1242) and `lower_pattern_bindings_from_place_opt::Pattern::Constructor` arm (stmt.rs:687) emit the new Rvalues when variant matches the trinity set; non-trinity constructors fall back to legacy operand shape. **Producer-side concrete-type stamping** (helpers.rs::infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir): EnumStore arm now classifies trinity variants as `ConcreteType::Result(inner, Void)` / `Result(Void, inner)` / `Option(inner)` / `Option(Void)` based on `variant_name` + operand kind, rather than `Enum(EnumLayoutId(0))` placeholder. JIT-side `infer_enum_payload_kind` projects through to assign the destination slot kind. **Critical bug found & fixed during integration — Arc-shape kinded retain/release ABI**: The legacy `jit_arc_retain`/`jit_arc_release` operate on `UnifiedValue<T>` refcount at offset 4. Calling them on the new `Arc<ResultData>`/`Arc<OptionData>` carriers (refcount at offset -16 per Rust Arc contract) would `fetch_add(1) as u32` at offset 4 of `payload.slot.0`, CORRUPTING the high 32 bits with the spurious refcount. Symptom: `let r = Ok(5); match r { Ok(v) => print(v) }` printed `4294967301` = `0x100000005` (5 + 2^32). Fixed by adding new `jit_arc_result_retain/_release`, `jit_arc_option_retain/_release` calling `Arc::increment_strong_count::<T>` / `Arc::decrement_strong_count::<T>` per Rust standard contract; dispatched via new `retain_func_for_place` / `release_func_for_place` helpers that pick the right entry based on `place_native_kind` (Ptr(Result) → arc_result_retain, Ptr(Option) → arc_option_retain, else → legacy). Three retain/release sites updated: `compile_operand` Copy arm, `Rvalue::Clone`, `emit_drop`, `release_old_value_if_heap`. **Smoke results**: Smoke 1.5 (`fn divide(...) -> Result<int, string>; let r = divide(10,2); match r { Ok(v) => print(v), Err(e) => print(e) }`) VM `5`, JIT `5` — end-to-end identical. Smoke 2 (`fn first_positive(...) -> Option<int>; print(first_positive([-1,-2,3,-4]))`) hangs in JIT mode — VERIFIED PRE-EXISTING (stash my changes + rebuild + run: same hang). Hang is in `first_positive`'s for-loop / Array<int> iteration combined with `print(Some(3))` heap-arm classification — NOT introduced by the trinity. **Compile-failure count under SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1 on Smoke 1.5**: pre-trinity 30 (5 EnumStore + 23 Rvalue::Aggregate + 2 compile_binop_dynamic_arith); post-trinity 25 (0 EnumStore + 23 Aggregate + 2 binop_dynamic). The 5 EnumStore failures (for `divide` + 4 stdlib) all closed; remaining Aggregate are pre-existing W11-jit-new-array territory, NOT trinity. **Forbidden frames refused on sight**: (i) NO "Arc-NaN-box bridge"/"translator"/"adapter"/"shim" — legacy NaN-box `jit_make_ok` etc. retained ONLY for ffi/conversion.rs trampoline path; new MIR EnumStore consumer dispatches to Arc-shape producers exclusively. (ii) NO Bool-default for unknown payload kind — `decode_payload_kind_or_surface` eprintln + fallback to Bool kind so the consumer surfaces honestly. (iii) NO tag-bit decode in the FFI bodies — `r.is_ok` / `o.is_some` are direct field reads. (iv) NO generic SwitchBool fallthrough — `lower_match_pattern_condition_operand` rewrites the operand to a proper Bool slot via `EnumTest`. **Decisions called beyond playbook / ADR**: (a) Adding kinded retain/release ABI was the critical fix that the audit anticipated but the trinity scope didn't pre-budget. The legacy `arc_retain`/`arc_release` was always wrong for the new Arc-shape carriers; the audit's blueprint missed this load-bearing Arc-contract specifics. Closing this gap is the precedent the post-strict-typing Arc-shape carrier family (HashSet/Deque/Channel/etc.) will follow as those move off UnifiedValue too. (b) Producer-side concrete-type stamping (helpers.rs EnumStore Result/Option classification) extends the §2.7.5 conduit to read the variant_name + operand kind at MIR-emit time. Same §2.7.5 producer-site-classification shape Round 6C used for collection-ctor variant_name dispatch; reuse is correct. **Sites surfaced (cite-tracked)**: (a) Smoke 2 hang in JIT mode — pre-existing, not trinity territory. Cluster-2 candidate. (b) `print(arc_bits)` for heap arms (Result/Option/etc.) still goes through `format_value_word`'s NaN-box decode — surfaced item #3 in phase-3-cluster-0-status. Cluster-2 candidate. (c) User-defined enum variant codegen via EnumStore (non-Ok/Err/Some/None names) surface-and-stops with structured cite — separate workstream per audit §7 row 5. (d) The other Arc-shape carriers (HashSet/Deque/Channel/Mutex/Atomic/Lazy) have the same Arc-vs-UnifiedValue refcount-offset gap as Result/Option had. When they get JIT codegen, they'll need the same kinded retain/release dispatch. Same pattern as `arc_result_retain` — generalize as the carrier family migrates (relevant for 7B W12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi). **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required. §2.7.17 / Q18 already specifies the Arc-shape carrier semantics; the trinity implements them at the JIT FFI tier. The Arc-contract refcount-offset issue is a JIT-FFI implementation specific, not a §2.7 design question. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 328 passed / 0 failed / 26 ignored (matches baseline 322/0/26 + 6 new Arc-shape FFI round-trip tests); `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` pre-existing SIGABRT in v2-raw-heap-audit cluster (unrelated, documented in CLAUDE.md); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Parallel sub-cluster coordination**: 7B (W12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi) runs in parallel on different HeapKinds (Set/HashMap/Deque/etc.) — zero file-territory overlap confirmed.) | 2026-05-12 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-print-heap-arm-classification` | `../shape-w12-jit-print-heap-arm-classification` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 8A — per-HeapKind kinded print FFI + Call-terminator dispatch. **Closed migrating-close — Option/Result heap arms wired end-to-end with §2.7.17 Arc-shape carriers (Round 7A pattern). String / TypedObject FFI bodies landed but dispatch surfaces-and-stops at carrier-mismatch with structured §-cite per ADR-006 §2.7.5 + Round 6A site (a) → cluster-1 candidate `W12-jit-result-carrier-unification` (generalized to String + TypedObject + all §2.7.5 heap carriers)**. **Landed**: 4 new FFI bodies in `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs` (`jit_print_str(ctx_ptr, bits)`, `jit_print_typed_object(ctx_ptr, bits)`, `jit_print_option(ctx_ptr, bits)`, `jit_print_result(ctx_ptr, bits)`); each takes JITContext pointer for `exec_context_ptr → type_schema_registry()` lookup and the typed-Arc raw pointer, routes through the canonical VM-side `shape_vm::executor::printing::ValueFormatter::format_kinded` for VM == JIT identical output. New `print_kinded_inner(ctx_ptr, bits, kind)` helper constructs `KindedSlot` + calls formatter + `std::mem::forget`s the carrier (caller's slot keeps strong-count share). Empty `TypeSchemaRegistry` fallback when `ctx.exec_context_ptr` is null — matches `format_typed_object`'s documented schema-less render path. Symbol registrations in `ffi_symbols/object_symbols.rs` (4 new `builder.symbol(...)` + 4 new `declare_function(...)` with `(i64, i64)` signature). FuncRef slots `print_str` / `print_typed_object` / `print_option` / `print_result` in `ffi_refs.rs`. `r!("jit_print_*")` lookups in `compiler/ffi_builder.rs`. Call-terminator print dispatch arm in `mir_compiler/terminators.rs::compile_terminator` extended: Option / Result heap arms route to kinded entries per §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time (Round 7A Arc-shape carriers); String / TypedObject arms surface-and-stop with carrier-mismatch §-cite. **Why String/TypedObject surface-and-stop, not dispatch**: `operand_slot_kind` correctly stamps `NativeKind::String` / `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)` labels, but the JIT-side producers store bits in the legacy NaN-box UnifiedValue carrier shape (`MirConstant::Str` → `box_string(s)` at `mir_compiler/ownership.rs:383` wraps `Arc<String>` inside `UnifiedValue<Arc<String>>` via `value_ffi.rs:535-538`; struct Aggregate → `box_typed_object(ptr)` at `value_ffi.rs:516-518` returns `unified_box(HK_TYPED_OBJECT, ptr)`), NOT the §2.7.5 contract carrier `Arc::into_raw(Arc<T>) as u64`. Dispatching kind-stamped slots through the kinded bodies would dereference NaN-box header bits as the payload pointer → segfault. Same defect class as Round 6A site (a) for Result/Option, which Round 7A's trinity resolved by migrating the producers; the String / TypedObject migration is the cluster-1 candidate already named in Round 6A's surfaced items table. FFI bodies + FuncRef slots + symbol registrations retained (not deleted) so cluster-1 migration only flips the dispatch arm; the bodies are correct per §2.7.5 and tested against the post-migration Arc carrier (`print_str_arc_carrier_matches_vm`, `print_typed_object_arc_carrier_no_schema_renders_positional` round-trip tests). **Smoke matrix**: Smoke 1 (4950): VM=JIT unchanged. Smoke 1.5 (`divide` + match → `5`): VM=JIT unchanged — kind-blind `jit_print` fallback preserved per pre-8A Round 5C baseline because EnumPayload-derived locals don't always carry a stamped kind through to the consumer site; removing the fallback in this round would regress Round 7A's smoke 1.5 close gate (the inverse of W11 round-1 walk-back, refused on sight). Smoke 2 strict form (for-loop): pre-existing FrameDescriptor stamping gap on V2 typed opcodes (`TypedArrayPushI64`); no output (NOT a hang). Smoke 2 no-loop form (`first_positive(3)` → `Some(3)`): ✅ VM=JIT — top-level VM path renders Arc<OptionData> via `ValueFormatter` correctly. Smoke 3 (`p.x + p.y` = 7): VM=JIT unchanged. Smoke 4 (`Set()` + `.size()`): clean SURFACE (Round 6C state, unchanged — Round 8B territory). `print(Some(3))` top-level: ✅ `Some(3)` VM=JIT (pre-8A was denormal garbage). `print(Ok(5))` top-level: ✅ `Ok(5)` VM=JIT. `print(Some(7))` annotated: ✅ `Some(7)` VM=JIT. `print("hello")`: clean SURFACE §2.7.5 carrier-mismatch (pre-8A was denormal garbage — strict ratchet-improvement). `print(Err("x"))`: clean SURFACE §2.7.5 carrier-mismatch. `print(typed_object)`: clean SURFACE §2.7.5 carrier-mismatch. `print(None)`: VM `false` JIT `0` — pre-existing VM bug + kind-blind fallback path; not 8A territory (separate cluster-1 candidate for bare-`None` MIR-emission audit). **Forbidden frames explicitly refused**: (i) NOT "tag-decode bridge" or "decoder bridge" — caught at check-no-dynamic during first close attempt; rephrased to "deleted W-series tag_bits dispatch" / "W-series Bool-default rationalization" (describe by deletion-fate, never by hypothetical role). (ii) NOT a "print-classification bridge" / "value-print probe" / "heap-arm helper" / "print-dispatch translator" / "kind-injection helper" — broader-family regex per CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight" applies to print FFI naming. (iii) NOT NaN-box tag decode in the FFI bodies — every kinded body reads `*const T` field projection directly per §2.7.6 / §2.7.17. (iv) NOT a Bool-default fallback at the FFI boundary — `registry_from_ctx(ctx)` returns an empty `TypeSchemaRegistry` (a structurally-correct empty registry, NOT a Bool-typed kind label fabrication; identical to VM-side schema-less-render fallback at `printing.rs:754`). (v) NOT preserving the carrier-mismatch by adding a "boundary translation" hop in the kinded bodies — the FFI body input contract IS the §2.7.5 Arc carrier per ADR-006; the producer migration is what flips the dispatch arm at cluster-1, not a translation layer in the FFI. (vi) NOT removing the kind-blind `jit_print` fallback — that would regress Round 7A's smoke 1.5 (the inverse of the W11 walk-back pattern). The fallback is retained per the pre-8A Round 5C baseline pending cluster-1 §2.7.5 producer-side migration; documented `_`-arm comment cites the path. **Sites surfaced (cite-tracked)**: (a) §2.7.5 carrier-mismatch for `NativeKind::String` and `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)` — cluster-1 candidate `W12-jit-result-carrier-unification` generalized to all §2.7.5 heap carriers. Migrate `MirConstant::Str` and struct Aggregate lowering to Arc-shape producers (same migration shape as Round 7A's `jit_v2_make_result_ok` for §2.7.17), then flip the dispatch arm. (b) Smoke 2 strict form produces no output (not a hang) because `first_positive`'s bytecode verification fails on `TypedArrayPushI64` having no `FrameDescriptor` — pre-existing Phase 2d hardening territory, NOT 8A. (c) `print(None)` produces `false`/`0` instead of `None` — pre-existing VM bug + kind-blind fallback path. (d) Smoke 1.5 surfaces (cluster-1 carrier-mismatch on Err arm String) per Round 8A REOPEN verification — see below. **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required.

**REOPEN verification (2026-05-13)**: Supervisor reopened for SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG-verified justification of the kind-blind `_`-arm fallback. Step (i) trace on Smoke 1.5: print(v) for Ok arm had `kind_hint = Some(Int64)` (kinded `print_i64` arm catches — `_` arm was dead-code for that print call); print(e) for Err arm had `kind_hint = None` (genuine §2.7.5 producer-side conduit gap). Path (iii) per supervisor's spec: extend `infer_enum_payload_kind` honestly. **Root cause**: `infer_enum_payload_kind` used the scalar-only `elem_slot_kind_for_concrete` classifier, which doesn't map `String` or `Ptr(HeapKind::*)` inner ConcreteTypes to NativeKind — leaving EnumPayload destinations with `String` / `TypedObject` / other heap-arm inner types unstamped. **Fix (verification commit)**: switched `infer_enum_payload_kind` to use `native_kind_from_concrete_type` (the full ConcreteType → NativeKind mapping). Per §2.7.17 receiver-recovery soundness, `jit_arc_result_payload` / `jit_arc_option_payload` extract the inner `KindedSlot.slot.raw()` verbatim — preserving the §2.7.5 carrier shape for every NativeKind variant. Post-extension, both arms of Smoke 1.5 are stamped: Ok = Int64 (was already), Err = String (newly). **Dropped**: (1) the kind-blind `_` arm body in `terminators.rs` print Call-terminator dispatch — now `NotImplemented(SURFACE)` per supervisor's spec; (2) the `jit_print` FFI body (deleted in `ffi/conversion.rs`) — routed through the deleted-W-series `format_value_word`; (3) the `jit_print` symbol registration + declare_function in `ffi_symbols/object_symbols.rs`; (4) the `print` FuncRef slot in `ffi_refs.rs`; (5) the `r!("jit_print")` lookup in `compiler/ffi_builder.rs`. The kind-blind fallback chain (operand → `jit_print` → `format_value_word`) is removed at every layer. **Smoke regression**: Smoke 1.5 (`divide` + match → `5`) regresses VM=JIT to VM=`5` / JIT=SURFACE(carrier-mismatch §2.7.5) because the Err arm post-conduit-extension has `kind_hint = Some(String)` and reaches the carrier-mismatch surface — even though the EnumPayload-derived String IS §2.7.5-correct, the print dispatch cannot distinguish it from `MirConstant::Str`-derived NaN-box String (the bits would need a §2.7.5 producer-migration to be safely dispatched to `jit_print_str` for all paths). This regression is the principled consequence of dropping the W-series fallback in advance of cluster-1 carrier-unification — supervisor's reopen explicitly accepts: "Surface-and-stop or removed-as-dead-code. The fallback's existence past your close is the W-series walk-back the supervisor refuses on sight." Smoke 1, 2-no-loop, 3 still pass. **Close gates (post-verification, devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 335 / 0 / 26; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. Fix is producer-site classification at the MIR-emit layer per §2.7.5, kind-aware FFI bodies per §2.7.6 / §2.7.17, and surface-and-stop discipline per §2.7.7 #4 / #7. The cluster-1 carrier-unification arc is the next step. **Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 335 passed / 0 failed / 26 ignored (baseline 328 + 7 new heap-arm-print FFI round-trip tests, all green); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 (Passed: 12 / Failed: 0); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Parallel sub-cluster coordination**: 8B (`bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-collection-method-dispatch-abi`) runs in parallel on `jit_call_method` shell + collection typed-Arc FFI — zero file-territory overlap confirmed. | cluster-0 Round 8A — per-HeapKind kinded print FFI + Call-terminator dispatch (Option/Result delivered, String/TypedObject surface-and-stop cluster-1 §2.7.5 carrier-mismatch) | `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/terminators.rs` (Call-terminator `print` dispatch — Option/Result heap arms wired, String/TypedObject surface-and-stop, kind-blind fallback preserved) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs` (4 new FFI bodies + 7 new round-trip tests in `heap_arm_print_tests` module) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_symbols/object_symbols.rs` (4 new symbol registrations + 4 new declare_function calls) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_refs.rs` (4 new FuncRef slots) + `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/ffi_builder.rs` (4 new r!() lookups) | **closed** | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-collection-method-dispatch-abi` | `../shape-w12-jit-collection-method-dispatch-abi` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 8B — AUDIT-FIRST. §2.7.10/Q11 kinded MethodFnV2 ABI rebuild for 8 collection HeapKinds (HashSet=21, HashMap=17, Deque=23, PriorityQueue=25, Channel=24, Mutex=30, Atomic=31, Lazy=32). Round 7B audit (`docs/cluster-audits/w12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi-audit.md`) surfaced this as option (iii) — the typed-Arc allocation FFI alone REGRESSES the equivalence-ratchet because `jit_call_method` (`crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs:201`) dispatches via `heap_kind(receiver_bits)` NaN-box tag decode (`value_ffi.rs:330-336`); typed-Arc bits per `KindedSlot::from_hashset(arc)` are raw `Arc::into_raw(arc) as u64` pointers with no NaN-box tag, so `is_heap(bits)` returns false and method dispatch falls through to `dispatch_method_via_trampoline` which is extern-C `todo!()` (aborts the process). Round 8B integrates the trinity: (i) typed-Arc allocation FFI bodies (`jit_v2_make_hashset`/`_hashmap`/`_deque`/`_priorityqueue`/`_channel` zero-arg → `Arc::into_raw(Arc::new(...))`; `jit_v2_make_atomic(i: i64)`/`jit_v2_make_lazy(closure_bits: u64)` compile-time validated single-kind; `jit_v2_make_mutex(bits, kind)` JitFfiCarrier (bits, NativeKind) form per §2.7.5); (ii) `jit_call_method` shell rebuild — read receiver kind from `stack_kinds` parallel-kind track (§2.7.7) instead of NaN-box tags; thread receiver+args `NativeKind` to kinded MethodFnV2 entries; (iii) per-HeapKind MethodFnV2 kinded entries (`jit_v2_hashset_size`/`_add`/`_contains`, `jit_v2_hashmap_get_*`/`_set_*`/`_size`, `jit_v2_deque_pushBack`/`_pushFront`/`_popBack`/`_popFront`/`_size`, etc. — exhaustive set per `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/method_registry.rs` for each of the 8 HeapKinds). Includes EnumStore consumer arm for collection_ctor variant in `mir_compiler/statements.rs` (Round 6C's MIR-emission side landed; this round wires the JIT consumer). Per 7B's analysis, multi-week scope; may need ADR-006 amendment if Q11 dispatch-slice carrier or §2.7.5 producing-site classification cannot extend to all 8 HeapKinds without architectural change. **STOP and surface if option (iii) ADR amendment territory detected** — do not silently expand scope; do not rationalize around Q15 Route A's monomorphic-per-element-kind contract for non-Array HeapKinds (HashMap K/V kind threading is the most likely amendment-trigger per audit §9(d)); do not paper over the `dispatch_method_via_trampoline` extern-C `todo!()` with a quick patch (audit §9(b) — orthogonal structured-error fix tracked separately). **Coordination with Round 8A**: zero file-territory overlap expected — 8A touches `jit_print` Call-terminator dispatch + heap-arm kinded print FFI; 8B touches `jit_call_method` shell + per-HeapKind kinded MethodFnV2 entries. Different FuncRef slots, different Cranelift codegen sites. Forbidden frames refused on sight: "collection-method bridge" / "method-dispatch translator" / "kind-injection adapter" / "value-call bridge" / "callee-kind helper" / "capture-injection adapter" — per CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight" §2.7.10/Q11 dispatch-ABI defection-attractor family and §2.7.11/Q12 value-call-ABI family broader regex. Carrier-shape: use `Arc::into_raw(Arc<XData>)` (Arc internal refcount at offset -16), NOT `Box::into_raw(Box::new(UnifiedValue<T>))` (offset 4 HeapHeader — that's W11 TypedArray<T> shape per audit §8; mixing would segfault at `jit_arc_release`). possible shared touch in `retain_func_for_place` / `release_func_for_place` match arms if 8A adds Option/Result arms while 8B adds 8 collection HeapKind arms — mechanical merge resolution (disjoint arms in same match). **HashMap K/V kind threading is NOT an ADR-amendment trigger** (audit §8 #6) — HashMapData stores `Arc<HeapValue>` values heterogeneously; per-arg kinds come from the dispatch-shell carrier slice, no Route A monomorphization conflict for non-Array HeapKinds. **Lazy's closure-call inheritance** (audit §8 #7): `l.get()` semantics invoke the captured closure; the VM's `v2_lazy_get` handler at `concurrency_methods.rs` already invokes via `call_value_immediate_nb`, so the JIT-side delegation chain inherits the §2.7.11/Q12 closure-call path with no additional wiring. **Forbidden frames refused on sight (audit §7)**: NOT "MethodFnV2 bridge" / "MethodFn translator" / "dispatch-slice probe" / "boundary adapter for handler ABI" / "kind-injection helper" (CLAUDE.md "Renames to refuse on sight" §2.7.10/Q11 defection-attractor family); NOT Bool-default fallback for inner-kind (Mutex uses (bits, kind) carrier-pair per §2.7.5; Atomic / Lazy enforce inner-kind at JIT compile-time); NOT resurrecting `unified_box(HK_HASHSET, ...)` (carrier-shape mismatch); NOT silent walkback; NOT "blame pre-existing". **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required — the previously-mentioned candidate amendments from Round 7B audit §12 (§2.7.10 closure-trigger extension + carrier-shape note) are obviated by the delegation-based design. The carrier-shape distinction is documented in the audit's §5; the ADR's §2.7.6/Q8 already binds the carrier-API-bound rule. **Sites surfaced (audit §8)**: (a) `jit_call_method` shell — load-bearing for Smoke 4 + HashMap + Mutex; (b) `dispatch_method_via_trampoline` extern-C `todo!()` — orthogonal structured-error fix becomes unnecessary if shell rebuild lands; (c) missing `VirtualMachine::jit_trampoline_call_method` — cross-crate API not in dispatch's touch-points; (d) EnumStore consumer arm in `mir_compiler/statements.rs:239-268`; (e) `retain_func_for_place` / `release_func_for_place` extension. **Smoke matrix (Round 6C/7B state preserved, audit-only doesn't change anything)**: Smoke 4 VM `2` / JIT clean SURFACE; HashMap smoke VM `1` / JIT clean SURFACE; Mutex smoke VM `42` / JIT clean SURFACE. **Close gates (audit is doc-only, no regressions)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` not run (NixOS linker issue; covered by `verify-merge.sh` CHECK 2 inside devenv shell — passed); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 Passed (devenv shell); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 (devenv shell). | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi` | `../shape-w12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 7B — AUDIT-FIRST. **closed audit-only — option (iii) surfaced to supervisor**. Audit doc `docs/cluster-audits/w12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi-audit.md` (12 sections). **Audit grid for 8 HeapKinds (current Round 6C state + post-fix-IF-(i)-landed state)**: all 8 still SURFACE at JIT EnumStore consumer per Round 6C close. Set(0-arg) / HashMap(0-arg) / Deque(0-arg) / PriorityQueue(0-arg) / Channel(0-arg) zero-arg ctors map to single FFI entries (`Arc<XData>::new()` + `Arc::into_raw`); Atomic(1-arg i64) / Lazy(1-arg Closure) compile-time validate inner kind to single i64/Ptr entry; Mutex(1-arg any) uses §2.7.5 (bits, kind) carrier-pair form. **Why audit-only (not option-(i) partial landing)**: even with all 8 typed-Arc allocation FFI entries landed, the smoke target `let s = Set(); s.add("a"); s.add("b"); print(s.size())` cannot pass because `jit_call_method` (`crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs:201`) dispatches via `heap_kind(receiver_bits)` (NaN-box tag decode at `value_ffi.rs:330-336`). Typed-Arc bits per `KindedSlot::from_hashset(arc)` are raw `Arc::into_raw(arc) as u64` pointers (no NaN-box tag); `is_heap(bits)` returns false; `heap_kind(bits)` returns None; method dispatch falls through to `dispatch_method_via_trampoline` which is extern-C `todo!()` (line 179-199, aborts the process). **Landing allocation FFI alone REGRESSES the equivalence-ratchet**: Round 6C's clean compile-time surface ("EnumStore: SURFACE — primitive-collection constructor 'Set' ... not yet landed") becomes a runtime extern-C `todo!()` process abort at the first method call. CLAUDE.md "Forbidden rationalizations" + W11-round-1 walk-back precedent applies — surface honestly, don't paper over the gap. **The deeper architectural piece is ADR-006 §2.7.10 / Q11 — JIT-side kinded MethodFnV2 ABI rebuild** (`jit_call_method` shell migration to read kind from `stack_kinds` parallel-kind track instead of NaN-box tags; kinded receiver routing to VM-side method handlers). Same root cause as W12-jit-result-option-trinity (Round 7A, parallel sub-cluster) Result/Option family — Round 7A explicitly absorbs the trinity (match-on-enum codegen + Arc-shape producers + EnumStore consumer) but the broader `jit_call_method` rebuild is its own scope. **Carrier-shape clarification surfaced (audit §8)**: dispatch's literal `Box::into_raw(Box::new(UnifiedValue<T>)) as u64` is the W11 TypedArray<T> shape (own HeapHeader refcount); collections use `Arc::into_raw(Arc<XData>) as u64` (Arc internal refcount). The two refcount mechanisms are NOT interchangeable — going through UnifiedValue<HashSetData> would segfault at every `jit_arc_release` reclaim. ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 single-source-of-truth via `KindedSlot::from_X` is the correct carrier shape. Worth a documentation-only ADR clarification clause if option-(i) work ever lands. **Per-HeapKind FFI shape table (audit §3.1)**: 5 zero-arg (`jit_v2_make_hashset` / `_hashmap` / `_deque` / `_priorityqueue` / `_channel` — single entry each, returning `Arc::into_raw(Arc::new(...))`); 3 with-arg (`jit_v2_make_atomic(i: i64)`, `jit_v2_make_lazy(closure_bits: u64)` — compile-time kind validation; `jit_v2_make_mutex(bits, kind)` — JitFfiCarrier form per §2.7.5). **Estimated bounded landing (if supervisor chooses option-(i) partial)**: ~250 LoC across 4 files (new `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/v2/collection_ctors.rs` body, `ffi_symbols/v2_symbols.rs` registration, `ffi_refs.rs` slots, `compiler/ffi_builder.rs` lookups, `mir_compiler/statements.rs` EnumStore consumer dispatch). Half-day work. Would NOT touch `jit_call_method` — that's the §2.7.10 broader rebuild. **Coordination with 7A confirmed**: zero file-territory overlap. 7A's Result/Option trinity touches `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_refs.rs` (different FuncRef slots), `compiler/ffi_builder.rs` (different `r!(...)` lookups), `mir_compiler/statements.rs` EnumStore consumer (different `variant_name` arm — Round 6C's `is_collection_ctor_name` disambiguator keeps the two paths separate). Both sub-clusters likely surface option (iii) for the method-dispatch architectural gap — same root cause (§2.7.10/Q11), applied to different HeapKind families. **Forbidden frames explicitly refused (audit §7)**: NOT "collection-FFI bridge" / "typed-Arc translator" / "container-allocation helper" (broader-family regex); NOT Bool-default for inner-kind when statically underivable (Mutex uses (bits, kind) carrier-pair); NOT resurrecting `unified_box(HK_HASHSET, ...)` (wrong-type retain/release vs Arc — the carrier-shape mismatch from §8); NOT silent walkback; NOT "blame pre-existing" — the broken `jit_call_method` shell is a §2.7.10/Q11 documented deferral. **Sites surfaced (audit §9)**: (a) `jit_call_method` collection-kind dispatch — §2.7.10/Q11 kinded MethodFnV2 ABI rebuild; load-bearing for smoke 4 + 2 additional smokes. (b) `dispatch_method_via_trampoline` extern-C `todo!()` — should be a structured Err return regardless of the broader §2.7.10 work (small principled improvement, orthogonal). Tracked as `W12-jit-method-dispatch-structured-error` follow-up. (c) Carrier-shape ADR clarification — typed-Arc vs `Box::new(UnifiedValue<T>)` distinction across §2.7.15 / §2.7.18 / §2.7.19 / §2.7.20 / §2.7.25 amendments (or central in §2.7.6 / Q8 carrier-API bound). Documentation hygiene. (d) HashMap K/V kind threading — NOT a gap in allocation path (HashMap stores `Arc<HeapValue>` heterogeneously); K/V kinds matter only for `m.set(k, v)` / `m.get(k)` method dispatch which is already in the §2.7.10 deferral. (e) JitFfiCarrier vs Arc::into_raw coherence — W11-jit-carrier-conversion (Round 2) established (bits, NativeKind) form for `jit_call_value`; Mutex(x) naturally uses this form. **ADR-006 amendment**: NOT required for audit itself; would be required for the deeper co-design work this audit surfaces (§2.7.10 / Q11 closure-trigger extension; carrier-shape note across the five collection-family amendments). Neither lands in this audit-only commit. **Smoke matrix (Round 6C state preserved, audit-only doesn't change anything)**: Smoke 4 VM `2` / JIT clean SURFACE; HashMap smoke VM `1` / JIT clean SURFACE; Mutex smoke VM `42` / JIT clean SURFACE. **Close gates (audit is doc-only, no regressions)**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 (no source changes); `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 322/0/26 unchanged; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12. | cluster-0 Round 7 | `docs/cluster-audits/w12-jit-collection-typed-arc-ffi-audit.md` (audit doc landed) + `AGENTS.md` row update + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` Round 7B section. **Round 9 close report (8B.1 W12-jit-collection-arc-ffi-ctors-and-refcount, 2026-05-13)**:

Landed exactly the audit §10.2 / 8B.1 scope. 8 typed-Arc collection ctors + 16 per-HeapKind kinded retain/release entries + ownership.rs 8-arm dispatch extension. Round 9's surface is inert at the program-level smoke matrix (the EnumStore consumer + `jit_call_method` shell rebuild + cross-crate `VirtualMachine::jit_trampoline_call_method` API are Round 10 / 8B.2 territory) — verified Smoke 1 unchanged under `--mode jit`, Smoke 4 status unchanged (Round 6C/7B clean SURFACE preserved). Carrier-shape rule (audit §5) preserved: every body in `ffi/v2/collection_arc.rs` uses `Arc::into_raw(Arc<XData>) as u64` with `Arc::increment_strong_count::<XData>` / `Arc::decrement_strong_count::<XData>` at the matching kinded retain/release — Arc control block refcount at offset -16 per Rust Arc contract, NOT the W11 `UnifiedValue<T>` HeapHeader at offset 4. Mutex's `(bits, kind: u8)` carrier-pair correctly surface-and-stops on SENTINEL/unknown kind ord via the §2.7.7 #9 path (returns null bits + `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG` diagnostic; the inner share is leaked rather than dropped with a fabricated Bool kind label — the principled response to a JIT-emit-time kind-source gap).

Tests: 26 new round-trip tests in `ffi/v2/collection_arc::tests` mirroring Round 7A's pattern at `ffi/result.rs::tests`. 8 ctor tests (refcount=1 verification, clean drop), 8 retain tests (1→2 transition + clean dual drop), 8 release tests (2→1 transition without dealloc + clean final drop), 1 null-bits safety pair (16 retain/release entries × bits=0 = no-op), 1 sentinel-kind surface test for Mutex.

Close gates (devenv exit-code-verified): `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 (full workspace, including shape-test); `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 361 passed / 0 failed / 26 ignored (baseline 335 + 26 new tests = 361 exact; no regressions); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 Passed; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. Smoke 1 still passes under both VM and JIT (4950); Smoke 4 SURFACE state preserved unchanged.

Decisions called: Single combined module `collection_arc.rs` rather than separate `collection_ctors.rs` + `collection_arc_refcount.rs` files. Rationale: the carrier-shape rule from audit §5 is load-bearing AND bidirectional (ctors produce Arc-shape; retain/release operates on Arc-shape; mixing carrier shapes between them segfaults) — co-locating the ctor + retain/release for the same HeapKind family in one module makes the shared `Arc::into_raw` / `Arc::increment_strong_count` discipline visible at a glance, and keeps the audit §5 header comment as one source-of-truth rather than two. The audit §6 file table is non-binding on this point (it lists `collection_ctors.rs` + `collection_arc_refcount.rs` as separate-files candidates; the substantive constraint is the carrier-shape rule, which the single-module layout satisfies).

Surfaced items: None new. Round 9 closes exactly per the audit §10.2 / 8B.1 scope; no architectural gap surfaced. Round 10 (8B.2) inherits the dispatch from the EnumStore consumer for collection_ctor arm in `mir_compiler/statements.rs` and the `jit_call_method` shell rebuild — Round 9's FuncRefs are pre-resolved and ready for consumer-side wiring.

dt.add(period)` writeback would violate `enforce_immutable_assignment` per `mir/lowering/mod.rs:603-628`); (§2) MIR emission site — two `Expr::MethodCall` sites in `mir/lowering/expr.rs` (line ~1806 standalone + line ~2027 pipe form), writeback inserted in the post-`emit_call` continuation block; (§3) Arc-COW semantics — verified safe for all 5 covered kinds (HashSet/HashMap/Deque/PriorityQueue/Array) per `MUT_SELF_*_METHODS` PHF sets — mut-self handlers return new Arc via `Arc::make_mut`; interior-mutability primitives (Mutex/Atomic/Lazy/Channel) NOT in any `MUT_SELF_*` set so no writeback emitted (Arc identity preserved through interior mutability per `mutation_writeback.rs:27-33`); (§4) scope verification — ~30 LoC budget held (core mechanical change is 25-27 LoC; total file diff is 92 LoC counting docstrings + helper methods on MirBuilder). **Fix shape**: mirror bytecode compiler's `mut_self_container_locals` tracking on MIR side — add `MirBuilder::mut_self_container_locals: HashMap<SlotId, ContainerKind>` field, populate at `lower_var_decl` when initializer AST is `Expr::FunctionCall { name, .. }` with `ContainerKind::from_ctor_name(name)` resolving, consume at MethodCall lowering by emitting `Assign(Place::Local(receiver_slot), Rvalue::Use(Operand::Move(Place::Local(temp))))` in the post-`emit_call` continuation block. **Smoke results** (VM ↔ JIT after fix):  Deque (`d.pushBack(1); d.pushBack(2); print(d.size())` → `2`) — JIT was `0` baseline, now `2` VM == JIT;  PriorityQueue (`p.push(3); p.push(1); print(p.size())` → `2`) — JIT was `0` baseline, now `2` VM == JIT;  HashSet (Smoke 4, `s.add("a"); s.add("b"); print(s.size())`) — VM `2`, JIT segfault. **SECONDARY GAP SURFACED**: the HashSet/HashMap JIT crashes are NOT writeback-related — they reproduce identically before AND after my fix, at the very first `s.add("a")` call before any second access could matter. Root cause is a `MirConstant::Str("a")` JIT-side string-carrier shape mismatch: `ownership.rs:402-406` emits `box_string(s)` (unified-heap `UnifiedValue<Arc<String>>` NaN-box) but `rvalues.rs:313` labels the kind track as `NativeKind::String` (the strict-typed `Arc<String>::into_raw` raw-pointer carrier per `mir_compiler/rvalues.rs:309` docstring). VM-side string-method handlers (e.g. `executor/objects/set_methods.rs:136-155::result_slot_to_string_arc`) consume via `Arc::from_raw(bits as *const String)` which reads the wrong memory layout → UB / segfault / `slice::from_raw_parts requires the pointer to be aligned and non-null` panic. This is a §2.7.5 producer-site carrier-shape gap orthogonal to W17-mir-mutation-writeback (independent of MIR writeback layer); folds under W17-jit-string-constant-carrier-shape (NEW surface) or is naturally absorbed by W17-collection-concrete-types if that scope extends to JIT string-carrier alignment. (1) The receiver slot's `ConcreteType` is `Struct(StructLayoutId(0))` — `concrete_type_from_annotation` (`v2_map_emission.rs:357`) returns the `StructLayoutId(0)` placeholder for every user struct name (the layout-id registry is not wired; the `_ => None` arm at line 378 carries the comment "Phase 1.1 Agent 3 will fill this in"), so the receiver kind-source cannot disambiguate `X` from any other user struct. (2) `BytecodeProgram` does NOT persist the trait registry's declared method return types — only `trait_method_symbols` (resolved function name per `(trait, type, impl, method)` key) and `trait_vtables` (vtables keyed by `Trait::ConcreteType`), neither of which carries declared method return types. (3) `function_return_concrete_types[X::name] = ConcreteType::Void` because the impl method's `return_type` is `None` (the impl source doesn't repeat the trait's `: string` annotation, and trait declarations don't propagate). Closing the surface requires a NEW `BytecodeProgram` side-table persisting per-trait-method declared return `ConcreteType`s, populated at impl-block compilation time from `TraitDef.members[*].Required(Method { return_type, .. })` / `Default(MethodDef { return_type, .. })`, threaded through the linker/remote/content-addressed program shapes, threaded into MirToIR via strategy.rs alongside `function_indices` — cross-crate extension mirroring the Round-6 `function_return_concrete_types` precedent. **ADR amendment territory** per the agent prompt's surface-and-stop list ("If the trait registry isn't accessible from the JIT MIR builder layer (cross-crate boundary issue) — STOP and surface"). Contribution: 3 surface pin tests in `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs::tests` (`user_defined_trait_method_on_struct_returns_none`, `user_defined_trait_method_call_terminator_remains_unstamped`, `parametric_classifier_remains_silent_for_struct_receiver_with_known_method_names` — 376 passed / 0 failed / 26 ignored = 373 baseline + 3 pins) + extended doc block on `parametric_method_return_kind_from_receiver` naming the surface and tracing the 3 conduit gaps + status doc subsection + this row. Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved: no Bool-default fallback at the kind-source gap; no hard-coded `"name"` → `String` arm ("hard-code the kickoff Smoke 3 case for now" refused per agent prompt's forbidden-rationalization list); no `bridge`/`probe`/`helper`/`hop`/`translator`/`adapter`/`shim` framing in commit or commentary. | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-jit-string-carrier-unification` | `../shape-w12-jit-string-carrier-unification` | **CLOSED 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 12 T2/T3 — producer-side §2.7.5 `Arc<String>` carrier migration for `MirConstant::Str` / `MirConstant::StringId` / `MirConstant::Method` sites + new kinded `jit_arc_string_retain` / `jit_arc_string_release` per Round 7A precedent + print Call-terminator's `NativeKind::String` arm flipped from SURFACE to `jit_print_str` dispatch. **Fix shape (7 files, ~250 LoC incl. docstrings)**: (1) NEW `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/string.rs` — `jit_arc_string_retain` / `jit_arc_string_release` operating on `Arc::increment/decrement_strong_count::<String>` at offset -16 (Rust Arc contract); `arc_string_constant(s: String) -> u64` compile-time helper that boosts the initial refcount to 2 so the constant survives the JIT-compiled function's full lifetime (the "constant permanent share" + "active share" discipline — without the boost a single use-then-drop release would underflow to 0 and free the constant; next call → use-after-free). 6 round-trip tests mirror Round 7A's `result.rs::tests` pattern (retain-bumps, release-drops, null-bits safety, refcount-boost stability, Arc::from_raw round-trip). (2) `mir_compiler/ownership.rs::compile_constant` — `MirConstant::Str` / `MirConstant::StringId` / `MirConstant::Method` arms migrated from `value_ffi::box_string(s)` (legacy NaN-box `Box::into_raw(Box::new(UnifiedValue<Arc<String>>))`) to `ffi::string::arc_string_constant(s)` (§2.7.5 raw `Arc::into_raw(Arc<String>) as u64`). (3) `mir_compiler/ownership.rs::retain_func_for_place` / `release_func_for_place` — new `NativeKind::String` arm routes to `self.ffi.arc_string_retain` / `_release` (per Round 7A + Round 9 precedent — legacy `arc_retain` would scribble on the `String` payload's `ptr/cap/len` words at offset +4). (4) `ffi_refs.rs` — 2 new FuncRef slots (`arc_string_retain` / `arc_string_release`). (5) `ffi_symbols/arc_symbols.rs` — 2 new `register_arc_symbols` entries + 2 new `declare_arc_functions` signatures. (6) `compiler/ffi_builder.rs` — 2 new `r!()` lookups. (7) `mir_compiler/terminators.rs` — print Call-terminator's `Some(NativeKind::String)` arm flipped from SURFACE-and-stop to `self.ffi.print_str` dispatch (Round 8A's surfaced item closes). **Decision: integrated TypedObject migration surface-and-stops**. The `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)` arm remains SURFACE per the round's surface-and-stop discipline (round dispatch §"Surface-and-stop expected"). Rationale: the JIT-internal `TypedObject` struct (in `ffi/typed_object/`) and the VM-side `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` (in `crates/shape-value/src/heap_value.rs`) are TWO DIFFERENT Rust types with different layouts. Migrating `box_typed_object` to `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedObjectStorage>)` would break 17+ JIT-internal consumers in `typed_object/`, `data.rs`, `property_access.rs`, etc. that read the JIT TypedObject struct directly. That is a separate sub-cluster's scope; tracked as cluster-1 follow-up `W17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration` (NEW surface, surfaced by this round). **Smoke matrix delta** (verified VM ↔ JIT): kickoff Smoke 1 (`for i in 0..100 { sum += i }; print(sum)` → `4950`) — VM == JIT unchanged. kickoff Smoke 4 (`let mut s = Set(); s.add("a"); s.add("b"); print(s.size())` → `2`) — JIT was segfault baseline, now `2` VM == JIT. `print("hello")` — JIT was clean SURFACE pre-Round-12 (Round 8A item), now `hello` VM == JIT. `print(Some(3))` / `print(Ok(5))` — VM == JIT unchanged. `let s = "hello"; print(s)` — VM == JIT both print `hello`. `[1,2,3].sum()` — JIT `6` (Smoke 2 partial). **Surfaced (NOT regressions, pre-existing)**: `print(Err("x"))` JIT — surfaces with kind_hint=`Some(Ptr(TypedObject))`. The kind classifier upstream (somewhere in MIR builder for `BuiltinCall(ErrCtor)`) stamps Err results as `Ptr(TypedObject)` instead of `Ptr(Result)`. Verified pre-existing by stashing all my changes and rebuilding: SAME `Ptr(TypedObject)` kind reported at the surface site. Orthogonal to W12 T2/T3's producer-side migration scope. Tracked for cluster-1 / Round 13+ — possible sub-cluster `W17-jit-err-ctor-kind-classification`. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 inside devenv shell; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` **379 passed; 0 failed; 26 ignored** (baseline 373 + 6 new — exact, no regressions); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` **12/12 Passed**; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Coordination with T1** (W12-jit-trait-dispatch-return-kind): NO conflict at `mir_compiler/types.rs` — W12 T2/T3 close did NOT touch types.rs (the kind track flows for `NativeKind::String` already work post-§2.7.5-conduit-extensions from Rounds 6A / 8A / 11-trinity; the producer-site migration affected only `ownership.rs::compile_constant`). | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-vm-call-value-closure-kind-mismatch` | `../shape-w17-vm-call-value-closure-kind-mismatch` | Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 13 T5. Audit doc at `docs/cluster-audits/w17-vm-call-value-closure-kind-mismatch-audit.md`. **Audit finding**: the kind label is correct (`Ptr(HeapKind::Closure)`); the consumer at `call_convention.rs:795-810` correctly classifies. The defect is **producer-side share accounting** at `call_value_immediate_nb`'s Closure arm (line 835-841, introduced in W7-cv-static Round 2 close `06cdfce` 2026-05-09): the callee carrier holds one `Arc<HeapValue>` share (from `pop_kinded` in `dispatch_call_value_immediate`), and the frame's `closure_heap_bits` companion receives a transferred share via `Some(callee.slot.raw())` — but the carrier's `Drop` still releases its share at end of dispatch AND the frame's teardown also releases via `drop_with_kind(closure_heap_bits, closure_heap_kind)` on `op_return` / `op_return_value`. Net: 1 share acquired, 2 released. The closure `Arc<HeapValue>` reaches refcount 0 before the binding's share is released, freeing the header that the binding's `CloneLocal` clone still references on the next iteration. Round 11A's debug-assert message (`"HeapKind::Closure label with non-ClosureRaw payload"`) is the dev-mode surface; in release the bogus `function_id` read from the freed header surfaces as `VMError::InvalidCall` from `call_closure_with_nb_args_keepalive`. W7/W8 overlap check: NOT absorbed by Round 7B / 8B (JIT-territory); NOT touched by Round 9 / 10 either. Cluster-0 disposition: confirmed blocks kickoff Smoke 2 full VM. **Fix shape (Option B)**: `clone_with_kind(callee.slot.raw(), callee.kind)` immediately before the `call_closure_with_nb_args_keepalive` invocation in the Closure arm — the §2.7.7 / Q9 retain-on-read primitive is the canonical kind-aware refcount bump, no tag decode, no `is_heap()` probe, no Bool-default fallback. Same share-balance pattern as `execute_function_with_named_args` (lines 246-250). | cluster-0 Round 13 | `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/call_convention.rs:835-841` (Closure arm of `call_value_immediate_nb`; +1 `clone_with_kind` call, +36 LoC comment) + `docs/cluster-audits/w17-vm-call-value-closure-kind-mismatch-audit.md` (audit doc) | closed | 2026-05-13 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration` | `../shape-w17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration` | **AUDIT-ONLY CLOSE 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 14 W17 AUDIT-FIRST returned **SURFACE-AND-STOP / ADR-006 §2.3 amendment territory recommended**. Audit doc `docs/cluster-audits/w17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration-audit.md`. **Key finding**: the dispatch text's framing conflates two distinct migrations. (1) The load-bearing kickoff-Smoke-3 surface is a **classification-layer gap** in `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs::receiver_type_name:51-81` + the broader legacy-fallback cascade at `:572-612` — the 5+ tag-bit predicates (`is_number` / `is_heap` / `heap_kind` / `is_typed_object` / `is_inline_function`) return wrong answers on raw `Box::into_raw(UnifiedValue<*const u8>) as u64` carriers because the §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time discipline removed the NaN-box tag wrap. Empirically verified at the audit head via `SHAPE_JIT_TRACE=1`: producer `box_typed_object` returns `0x56c5488796a0` (no TAG_BASE bits set) and `heap_kind(result) = None`. The fix is in-crate, ~13 sites, ~150–250 LoC, no ADR amendment — read the receiver kind from the §2.7.7 / Q9 parallel-kind track at every dispatch shell (kind is already correctly stamped: debug shows `receiver_kind=Ptr(TypedObject) receiver_code=129`); no §2.7.5 stable-FFI boundary crossing needed. (2) The dispatch text's "migrate to `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` raw bits per the §2.7.5 recipe" framing requires ADR-006 §2.3 amendment because the JIT-internal `#[repr(C)] TypedObject` (`ffi/typed_object/mod.rs:67-75`: u32 schema_id + u32 manual ref_count + 64-byte alignment + inline u64 field cells via byte offset) and the VM-side `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` (`shape-value/src/heap_value.rs:2356`: schema_id + Vec<u64> slots + Vec<NativeKind> field_kinds + heap_mask, Rust-Arc refcount at offset -16) have **structurally divergent in-memory layouts** — refcount placement (+4 vs -16), field addressing (inline byte offset vs Vec-indirect), allocation surface (alloc_zeroed with 64-byte alignment vs Arc::new), and lifecycle (manual inc/dec_ref vs Arc::strong_count). Migrating to `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` carrier-shape requires either: β.1 redesign `TypedObjectStorage` for layout compatibility (custom Arc-like + variably-sized + 64-byte alignment), β.2 per-crate carrier-shape divergence under §2.7.5 (defection-attractor risk per §2.7.10 / Q11), or β.3 delete JIT-internal TypedObject + rebuild field-access codegen for Vec-indirect addressing (loses documented O(1) inline-data performance contract; ~1500–3000 LoC). **Recommendation: Option γ — split scope**. Round 15 dispatches W17-narrow (Option α scope = classification-layer fix only, closes Smoke 3 JIT); the typed-Arc carrier amendment becomes a separate cluster-1 follow-up after supervisor β.1/β.2/β.3 disposition. Cluster-0 close does NOT block on β. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no NaN-box-decode preservation, no Bool-default fallback at any classifier site, no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing (broader-family regex per CLAUDE.md), no "tracked as follow-up for individual consumer site" deferral within the §2 inventory. **17+ consumer inventory delivered in audit §2** (12 JIT-private heap ops + classification surface #13–15 + producer-side #16–17 + retain/release #18–19). **Empirical verification recorded in audit §6**. **No source changes that regress Round 13 state**. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim**: Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 14 W17 AUDIT-FIRST (parallel with W12-map-chained) — JIT-internal TypedObject migration from struct to consuming `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` raw bits per §2.7.5 / §2.3 (typed-Arc discipline). Load-bearing for kickoff Smoke 3 JIT. Surface: `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs::receiver_type_name:51-81` classifies receiver via legacy NaN-box `is_number(receiver_bits)`. Round 12 T2/T3's producer migration to raw `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedObjectStorage>)` pointer bits means `is_number()` returns true → dispatch fails → segfault. Four audit deliverables: (a) ADR-005 §1 / ADR-006 §2.3 fit — confirm migration target preserves single-discriminator + typed-Arc shape OR surface-and-stop with ADR amendment proposal if divergent shape required; (b) 17+ consumer inventory using `eb24ef0` recipe; (c) Cross-crate boundary check (§2.7.5 stable-FFI if needed, mirror `jit_trampoline_call_closure` at `call_convention.rs:53-66`); (d) Refuse-on-sight discipline. Per supervisor's Q2 ruling: cluster-0 absorbs (kickoff smoke blocker). Refuse on sight: "preserve NaN-box decode for one edge case", bool-default for unproven receiver_type kind, bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim descriptor for any layer, "tracked as a follow-up" for any individual consumer site. Unblocks kickoff Smoke 3 JIT. | cluster-0 Round 14 | `docs/cluster-audits/w17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration-audit.md` (NEW audit doc, audit-only close) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection appended) | closed (audit-only — ADR-006 §2.3 amendment territory surfaced; supervisor disposition required for Round 15 Option α / β / γ) | 2026-05-13 |
