| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-narrow` | `../shape-w17-narrow` | **CLOSED 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 15 W17-narrow (Option γ from the W17-typed-object-arc Round 14 audit, supervisor-ratified). Classification-layer-only migration: receiver-type classification at the JIT `call_method` shell now dispatches on the `NativeKind` companion popped from the §2.7.7 / Q9 parallel-kind track at the dispatch entry — replacing the 5-arm NaN-box tag-bit cascade in `receiver_type_name` (`crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs:47-215`) and the 6-arm `is_ok_tag` / `is_err_tag` / `is_number` / `is_inline_function` / `heap_kind` cascade at the legacy-fallback dispatch (`call_method/mod.rs:572-624`). For `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)` the schema id is read via `jit_typed_object_schema_id`'s direct `(*ptr).schema_id` field load (§2.7.5 "*not* tag-bit dispatch — field-load from a heap-resident struct"); schema name resolves through the two-tier registry (global stdlib via `lookup_schema_by_id_public`, then trampoline VM's bytecode `program().type_schema_registry` for user-defined types — same pattern as `object/property_access.rs::HK_TYPED_OBJECT`). For `NativeKind::UInt64` carriers (legacy JIT-format HK_ARRAY / HK_JIT_OBJECT / HK_DURATION / HK_TIME / HK_MATRIX / HK_OK / HK_ERR / HK_STRING) the inner discriminator is read via `read_heap_kind` direct field load. `try_call_user_method` signature widened to thread `receiver_kind` through to `receiver_type_name` and threads `arg_pairs: &[(u64, NativeKind)]` so the JIT stack pushes both the data half and the §2.7.7 / Q9 parallel-kind half in lockstep before dispatching to the JIT-compiled UFCS function — pre-W17-narrow only the data half was written; under the prior broken classification the UFCS lookup always missed (`"number::name"` doesn't exist) so the body was unreachable and the missing kind writes were latent. **Tag-bit gates dropped** in 5 sites per audit §2 rows #3/#4/#7/#8/#9: `jit_typed_object_inc_ref`, `jit_typed_object_dec_ref` (`ffi/typed_object/allocation.rs:159-198`), `jit_typed_object_schema_id` (`ffi/typed_object/field_access.rs:209-220`), `jit_typed_merge_object`, `jit_typed_object_from_hashmap` (`ffi/typed_object/merge_ops.rs:29-141`). All five followed the same recipe as the W12-jit-binop-after-heap-read-kind-tracker close that already landed `_get_field` / `_set_field` (2026-05-12): under §2.7.5 raw `Box::into_raw` carriers `is_typed_object()` always returned false because it gates on `is_heap()` (the deleted NaN-box tag check), so every consumer silently no-op'd or returned TAG_NULL. `jit_typed_object_from_hashmap` discriminates via `read_heap_kind` instead — same §2.7.5-carved field-load path. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 Passed; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` **383 passed; 0 failed; 26 ignored** (baseline 383 + 0 new — exact, no regressions). **Smoke 3 status**: VM `x` ✓; JIT segfaults (pre-existing on baseline `3d42ba52` — verified by stashing all changes, rebuilding, and reproducing the identical segfault before the print). The classification-layer fix is sound and addresses the W17-narrow scope per the audit; Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end pass is gated by an upstream JIT MIR-lowering surface NOT in W17-narrow scope: `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs::StatementKind::ObjectStore:141-201` allocates typed objects via `register_predeclared_any_schema(&field_names)` (returning a `__predecl_*`-named schema id) instead of preserving the bytecode-side `OpCode::NewTypedObject`'s user-declared schema id (the X schema 53 in Smoke 3). With the classification-layer fix in place, `receiver_type_name` correctly resolves the JIT-allocated TypedObject's schema id (54, predeclared) but the predeclared name `__predecl_*` doesn't match the user-declared UFCS function `X::name` in `function_indices`, so `try_call_user_method` returns None correctly. The downstream segfault occurs when `jit_call_method`'s TAG_NULL fallthrough flows into `jit_print_str` (the kind-aware print dispatched per the W12 T1' String return-kind conduit) — `print_kinded_inner` at `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/printing.rs:154-163` treats `bits == 0` as "None" but TAG_NULL is a tagged sentinel (`make_tagged(TAG_NONE_BITS, 0)`), so the dereference-as-`*const String` segfaults. **Surfaces uncovered** (NOT regressions, pre-existing on `3d42ba52` baseline): (a) `W17-narrow-follow-up-A`: JIT MIR-lowering `StatementKind::ObjectStore` schema_id identity loss — fix is threading `Option<u32> schema_id` field on `ObjectStore` from the bytecode `OpCode::NewTypedObject` operand; (b) `W17-narrow-follow-up-B`: JIT print kind-aware null handling — `jit_print_str` / sibling kinded-print bodies must treat tagged-null sentinels (`TAG_NULL` / `TAG_NONE`) as "None" alongside `bits == 0`. Either follow-up unblocks Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end. **The W17-narrow audit's §5 Option α prediction ("Acceptance: kickoff Smoke 3 JIT `t.name()` → `x` matches VM") was overoptimistic** — the classification fix is necessary but not sufficient. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no NaN-box-decode preservation, no Bool-default fallback at any classifier site, no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing (broader-family regex per CLAUDE.md), no β typed-Arc carrier-shape migration (cluster-1 territory per audit §1.3), no expansion into the print-path carrier-shape mismatch (audit §2 row #17, separate scope), no widening into the JIT MIR-lowering schema-id surface (follow-up sub-cluster). **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim**: Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 15 W17-narrow (Option γ from the W17-typed-object-arc Round 14 audit). Replace tag-bit dispatch in receiver-type classification at the JIT `call_method` shell with kind-from-parallel-track dispatch. The `receiver_kind` is already popped from the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind track at the dispatch entry (audit §2 row #15 notes: "already popped from the parallel track at line 332-349") — use it instead of re-classifying via broken tag-bit predicates. Primary territory: `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs:51-81` (`receiver_type_name`) — signature changes from `(receiver_bits, exec_ctx) -> Option<String>` to `(receiver_bits, receiver_kind, exec_ctx) -> Option<String>`. For `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)` read `(*ptr).schema_id` via direct field load and resolve via `type_schema_registry`. `:111-167` (`try_call_user_method`) — threads `receiver_kind` through. `:572-592` (legacy-fallback builtin JIT-format method dispatch cascade) — replace each `is_*` tag-bit predicate with `matches!(receiver_kind, ...)` checks. Plus the ~13 consumer call-site updates enumerated in audit §2 inventory rows #3/#4/#7/#8/#9/#13/#14/#15. Smoke target: `trait T { name(): string } type X {} impl T for X { method name() { "x" } } let t = X {} print(t.name())` → `x` under both `--mode vm` and `--mode jit`. Refuse on sight: β typed-Arc carrier-shape question (cluster-1 territory); Bool-default for unproven receiver-type kind; "bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim" framing; "preserve NaN-box decode for one edge case"; ValueWord resurrection. | cluster-0 Round 15 | `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs` (~360 LoC net, classification-layer rewrite with full NativeKind / HeapKind kind-from-parallel-track dispatch + threading `receiver_kind` through `try_call_user_method` + arg_pairs lockstep stack-kind writes) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/typed_object/allocation.rs` (jit_typed_object_inc_ref + _dec_ref: dropped is_typed_object gate) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/typed_object/field_access.rs` (jit_typed_object_schema_id: dropped is_typed_object gate) + `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/typed_object/merge_ops.rs` (jit_typed_merge_object + jit_typed_object_from_hashmap: dropped is_typed_object gates, switched to read_heap_kind for HK_JIT_OBJECT discrimination); 4 files / +362 −66 LoC net | closed (classification-layer fix landed; Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end blocked by upstream W17-narrow-follow-up-A schema-id MIR-lowering + W17-narrow-follow-up-B print null-handling, surfaced for supervisor disposition) | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-option-b-reframed` | `../shape-w12-option-b-reframed` | **AUDIT-ONLY CLOSE 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 16 W12-Option-B-reframed (supervisor-ratified after Round 15 W12-Option-B audit-only close surfaced that the Round 14 prescription conflated `TypedArrayData<T>` Arc-wrapped Rust enum with `TypedArray<T>` 24-byte `#[repr(C)]` flat struct). Phase 1 surface-and-stop trigger fires — the kickoff's working hypothesis "Option B''' boundary conversion at JIT FFI dispatch shell per §2.7.14-A — find the existing conversion site that `[1,2,3].sum()` after Round 11A already uses" rests on an **incorrect premise**. Audit deliverable (a) finding: **no conversion site exists**. `[1,2,3].sum()` works **not via conversion** but because both ends of the literal-array→JIT-consumer pipeline are natively the flat-struct shape. The literal `[1,2,3]` lowers to `OpCode::NewTypedArrayI64` (`crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/collections.rs:214-228`) → VM executes `TypedArray::<i64>::with_capacity(...)` (`crates/shape-vm/src/executor/v2_handlers/array.rs:44-53`) → push_kinded with `NativeKind::UInt64` → JIT consumer at `v2_array.rs:367-387` reads as `*const TypedArray<i64>` directly. **Empirical confirmation**: `[1,2,3,4,5].sum()` produces `15` under `--mode jit` at this baseline. The stdlib `vec.shape::map`'s `let mut result = []` (empty unannotated literal) takes a **bytecode-emitter-disjoint** path: `OpCode::NewArray` (`collections.rs:229-264` else branch) → `op_new_array` (`crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/object_creation.rs:336-388`) → `Arc<TypedArrayData::I64(Arc<TypedBuffer<i64>>)>` with `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`. The two carriers are **runtime peers** selected at bytecode-emission time, not convertible variants. `op_array_push_local` (`crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/array_operations.rs:215-302`) supports both per-carrier-shape arms (Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray) Arc-path vs UInt64 v2-raw-path). The JIT consumer fast-path gate at `terminators.rs:116-118` reads `concrete_types[slot]` (compile-time ConcreteType stamp), not runtime NativeKind — both runtime carriers map to ConcreteType `Array(I64)`, but only the flat-struct carrier is sound to dispatch to the flat-struct FFI. Audit deliverable (b): `.map()` results route into the fast-path when the conduit extension (predecessor stash `4ddd1bfb`, currently NOT applied at baseline `a7b93def`) stamps `concrete_types[doubled] = Array(I64)`; the runtime carrier is the Arc shape but the gate doesn't discriminate, producing the SIGSEGV the predecessor audit `8354968a` §7.1 recorded. Audit deliverable (c): **no minimal "boundary conversion" fix shape exists** — a conversion would require allocating a fresh `TypedArray<T>` + O(n) byte copy of the `TypedBuffer<T>` payload + Arc-share release on the original, a synthesis (not conversion) of a new value with a different lifecycle. Audit deliverable (d): the supervisor-provided §2.7.14-A draft text **NOT committed** — it mis-describes the runtime reality in three load-bearing places: (1) "Heap carrier `Arc<TypedArrayData::T>`" treats `T` as a type parameter when it's an enum-variant tag; (2) the variant tag is NOT the JIT-FFI-boundary discriminator (NativeKind is); (3) "conversion site is the JIT FFI dispatch shell: per-variant unwrap-and-flatten" asserts a site that does not exist + describes the layouts as compatible representations when they are structurally distinct types. Committing the draft would lock in the Round 14 conflation in different vocabulary. **Recommendation (audit §3 / §8) for Round 17 / supervisor disposition** — four options: (1) **B'** (ADR-006 §2.3 amendment to unify carrier shapes, multi-week); (2) **B''** (producer migration to v2-raw + ADR amendment to acknowledge dual carrier shapes for scalar Array<T>); (3) **A-§2.7.7** new disposition (distinct from Round-15-refused Option A): change `v2_typed_array_elem_kind` from `concrete_types[slot]` lookup to runtime-NativeKind-track lookup at the dispatch shell — same shape as W17-narrow Round 15 (no ADR amendment, smallest scope, accepts performance gap that `.map().sum()` runs through method-dispatch trampoline instead of inline SIMD until B'/B'' land); (4) **Status quo + accept performance gap** (close Smoke 2 by extending `helpers.rs:494` conduit for the non-fast-path generic dispatch route's kind propagation through `print`). **4-instance defection-attractor class** now: W12-map-chained Round 14 + W17-typed-object-arc Round 14 + W12-Option-B Round 15 + W12-Option-B-reframed Round 16 — CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" amendment binding per supervisor's 2026-05-13 Round-14-close recurrent-pattern note. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: §2.7.14-A draft NOT committed (mis-describes reality + repeats Round 14 conflation); no Option A / Option C / bool-default / stashed-WIP resurrection / bridge-family framing / carrier unification via deletion / ValueWord resurrection. Option A-§2.7.7 explicitly distinct from Option A by mechanism (reads §2.7.7/Q9 authoritative track; no new producer_kind side-table). Zero source changes. Smoke 2 baseline state (audit §6 verification): VM `--mode vm` prints `30`; JIT `--mode jit` errors `Route A surface-and-stop: NotImplemented(SURFACE) — print Call-terminator operand NativeKind is None` (pre-conduit-extension surface; SIGSEGV NOT reachable on this baseline). Audit doc at `docs/cluster-audits/w12-option-b-reframed-audit.md`. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim** (audit §9): Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 16 W12-Option-B-reframed working hypothesis "Option B''' (boundary conversion per §2.7.14-A) — per-variant unwrap-and-flatten from `Arc<TypedArrayData::T>` to `*const TypedArray<T>` at the JIT FFI boundary. Other typed-array fast-paths already work for direct invocation; find that site." Audit's response: working hypothesis rests on incorrect premise; conversion site does not exist; `[1,2,3].sum()` works because literal-array bytecode-emission path natively produces flat-struct carrier shape; two carriers are runtime peers selected at bytecode-emission time with bytecode-emitter-disjoint paths to JIT consumer surfaces. | cluster-0 Round 16 | `docs/cluster-audits/w12-option-b-reframed-audit.md` (NEW audit doc) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection appended) | closed (audit-only — kickoff's "Option B''' boundary conversion site does not exist" premise refuted; §2.7.14-A draft mis-describes runtime reality and NOT committed; four supervisor disposition options surfaced for Round 17) | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-followup-b` | `../shape-w17-followup-b` | **SURFACE-AND-STOP 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 16 W17-narrow-follow-up-B (production-first, JIT kind-aware print null handling). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a7b93def`. **No source changes landed.** The supervisor's prescription has a structural blocker that meets the dispatch's named STOP condition: *"If TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE_BITS predicates aren't exposed cleanly from the existing tag layout (`crates/shape-value/src/value_word.rs` or the JIT FFI tag constants at `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs` / `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/jit_kinds.rs`), STOP and surface — this would suggest the fix shape needs revisiting, possibly more involved than the supervisor's bounded-mechanical-scope estimate. Do not synthesize predicates locally."* **Finding**: (1) The primary territory `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/printing.rs::format_kinded_inner` lines 154-163 + 189-190 lives in `shape-vm`. The two null-detection arms (`NativeKind::String` arm at 155 and `format_heap_kind` heap arm at 189) currently check `if bits == 0`. (2) The prescribed fix shape `bits == 0 || is_tag_null(bits) || is_tag_none(bits)` requires `TAG_NULL` / `TAG_NONE_BITS` predicates. (3) `crates/shape-value/src/value_word.rs` does NOT exist (file removed by the strict-typing bulldozer Phase 2 per `ffi/value_ffi.rs:9-16` module docstring); `grep -rn "TAG_NULL\|TAG_NONE\|TAG_BASE" crates/shape-value/src/` returns **zero hits**. (4) `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs:87-124` does define `pub const TAG_NULL: u64 = make_tagged(TAG_NONE_BITS, 0)`, `pub const TAG_NONE: u64 = TAG_NULL`, `TAG_NONE_BITS: u64 = 0b011` (private), and `pub fn is_none_tag(bits: u64) -> bool { bits == TAG_NULL }` — but `shape-vm` cannot import these. `crates/shape-vm/Cargo.toml` depends only on `shape-value` (not `shape-jit`); `crates/shape-jit/Cargo.toml:14` depends on `shape-vm` — making `shape-vm → shape-jit` a circular dependency. (5) The `ffi/value_ffi.rs:1-28` module docstring explicitly declares the constants `JIT-internal sentinel layout` and architecturally scoped to shape-jit: *"The constants and helpers in this module are JIT-internal: they encode the sentinel u64 layout that JIT-emitted Cranelift code uses for inline scalars (TAG_NULL, TAG_BOOL_*, TAG_UNIT, TAG_DATA_ROW)"*. **Smoke 3 reproduction at `a7b93def` HEAD** (no source changes): VM `x` ✓ exit 0; JIT `JIT_EXIT=139` SIGSEGV; `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1` last trace lines confirm the exact W17-narrow-close-documented surface: `[jit-call-method] method='name' receiver_kind=Ptr(TypedObject) receiver_code=129 receiver_bits=0x5b91b9039a20`, then segfault before "x" is printed (downstream when `jit_print_str` dereferences the TAG_NULL bits as `*const String`). The receiver-kind classification correctly flows the producing-call kind per W17-narrow; the segfault is exactly the prescribed surface. **Three plausible disposition shapes for supervisor's Round 16/17 ratification** (no recommendation between them — supervisor authority): **(α) Expose TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE / is_none_tag predicates from shape-value** — move the sentinel definitions (or a `pub fn is_jit_null(bits: u64) -> bool` shape-value-side predicate) into a `shape-value` module so shape-vm can consume them by importing shape-value. Risk: shape-value-side ownership of the JIT sentinel layout creates a forward-coupling — the JIT NaN-box layout is currently a JIT-internal stable-FFI sentinel per ADR-006 §2.7.5; ceding ownership to shape-value gives the runtime tier knowledge about the JIT producer's sentinel encoding (the "decode bridge at the wrong tier" defection-attractor class per the CLAUDE.md broader-family rule). May be acceptable IFF framed as *"shape-value defines the null-sentinel ABI; shape-jit and shape-vm both speak it"* — i.e. moving the sentinel up the dependency stack from JIT-internal to shape-value-canonical. Needs explicit ADR-006 §2.7.5 amendment with named justification. **(β) Filter TAG_NULL at the JIT-producer site, before the slot reaches shape-vm** — `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs::print_kinded_inner` (or each `jit_print_*` body) checks `is_none_tag(bits)` BEFORE constructing the `KindedSlot`, printing "None" directly and returning. This is in-crate to shape-jit; predicates are already available locally. Discipline-coherent: the §2.7.5 invariant says a `NativeKind::String`-kinded slot must carry `Arc::into_raw(Arc<String>)` bits, never a JIT sentinel. The proper §2.7.5-discipline response is for the JIT producer to NOT stamp a §2.7.5 carrier kind on bits that aren't actually a typed-Arc payload — and the JIT-side filter at the print FFI body is the bounded mechanical realization. Risk: this is band-aid territory — the real producer of the §2.7.5-violation is `jit_call_method`'s `TAG_NULL` fallthrough returning bits with a downstream-stamped `NativeKind::String` parallel-track kind. **(γ) Fix at the actual root cause: `jit_call_method`'s TAG_NULL fallthrough** — the dispatch shell in `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs` when method dispatch fails (e.g. follow-up-A's schema-id MIR-lowering gap leaves no `X::name` registered) returns `TAG_NULL` paired with a result kind stamped from the call's expected return kind on the parallel track (`NativeKind::String` for the `name(self): string` signature). This pairing violates §2.7.5 / §2.7.7 — the kind label says "this slot carries `Arc::into_raw(Arc<String>)`" but the bits carry a NaN-box sentinel. The principled fix is either: (γ.1) `jit_call_method` surface-and-stops with structured error when classification fails (refuse to manufacture an undefined slot); (γ.2) `jit_call_method` stamps a separate "missing dispatch" kind variant (a new `NativeKind`?) when the method dispatch fails, and `format_kinded_inner` adds an arm for it; (γ.3) follow-up-A's schema-id fix lands first, so the dispatch never fails for Smoke 3 in practice and the TAG_NULL fallthrough becomes unreachable for this case. Note (γ.3) is the **dispatch text's own coordination remark**: *"Combined with W17-narrow-follow-up-A (running in parallel), your fix unblocks Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end. Without follow-up-A's schema-id MIR-lowering fix, JIT may still produce wrong output (schema lookup miss → wrong method dispatch path → null/empty result), and your null-handling fix may render that null correctly as 'None' instead of segfaulting — that's the expected partial-unblock state from your sub-cluster alone."* (γ.3) suggests follow-up-A may be the principal load-bearing fix and follow-up-B may be a band-aid for an edge case that follow-up-A renders mostly-unreachable. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no synthesis of `TAG_NULL` / `TAG_NONE_BITS` predicates locally in shape-vm (the prescribed STOP condition); no per-call-site bit-pattern duplication (would also be DRY-violation); no kind-blind raw-bit-pattern probing (would be §2.7.7/Q9 violation framing); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing; no "preserve segfault for one edge case" framing; no ValueWord resurrection; no Bool-default fallback at any classifier site. **Close gates run at baseline `a7b93def` HEAD** (sanity, no source changes): `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 (1m 41s, devenv wrapper); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` NOT RUN (no source changes to verify-merge). Smoke 3 VM=`x` exit 0; Smoke 3 JIT=SIGSEGV (`JIT_EXIT=139`) with debug trace showing `[jit-call-method] method='name' receiver_kind=Ptr(TypedObject)` then crash before print — the exact prescribed surface. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim**: Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 16 sub-cluster W17-narrow-follow-up-B (production-first, JIT kind-aware print null handling). Handle tagged-null sentinels (`TAG_NULL` / `TAG_NONE_BITS`) as "None" alongside the existing `bits == 0` check in the kind-aware print path. Currently `jit_call_method`'s TAG_NULL fallthrough returns `make_tagged(TAG_NONE_BITS, 0)` — a tagged sentinel that is NOT equal to `0`. Downstream `format_kinded_inner` only checks `bits == 0` for the None case, so the tagged sentinel falls through to a `*const String` dereference and segfaults. Primary territory: `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/printing.rs::format_kinded_inner` (lines 154-163) — extend the null detection to handle tagged-null sentinels. Sibling kinded `jit_print_*` bodies — same null-detection extension. Fix shape: `bits == 0 \|\| is_tag_null(bits) \|\| is_tag_none(bits)` in each null-detection arm. Extract a helper (`is_null_kinded` or similar) and use it at every call site — DO NOT duplicate the bit-pattern check per call site (DRY discipline). If TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE_BITS predicates aren't exposed cleanly from the existing tag layout, STOP and surface. Refuse on sight: per-call-site bit-pattern duplication; kind-blind decode of the tagged sentinels; bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing; "preserve segfault for one edge case" framing; re-introducing ValueWord under any rename; synthesizing TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE_BITS predicates locally if they aren't already exposed. | cluster-0 Round 16 | (no source changes) `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (Round 16 follow-up-B subsection NOT appended — supervisor disposition needed first); `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/printing.rs` SURFACED as primary territory but UNTOUCHED; `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs:87-124` SURFACED as predicate source but architecturally inaccessible to shape-vm via dependency direction; `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs::print_kinded_inner` SURFACED as plausible β-disposition territory but out of W17-narrow-follow-up-B's named primary-territory scope | surface-and-stop (TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE_BITS predicates structurally unreachable from primary territory — dispatch's named STOP condition met; three disposition shapes α/β/γ surfaced for supervisor authority) | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit` | `../shape-w12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit` | **AUDIT-ONLY CLOSE 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 17 W12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit (supervisor-authorized aggressive deletion of `TypedArrayData` enum + `TypedBuffer<T>` wrapper layer; `TypedArray<T>` flat struct becomes the universal `Array<T>` carrier). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ aa5de4ab` (post-Round-16-A+C-merge HEAD). Zero source changes — audit-only deliverable per supervisor's dispatch. **Audit findings (deliverables a–h delivered in `docs/cluster-audits/w12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit.md`)**: (a) per-variant migration disposition for all 22 live `TypedArrayData` variants — **12 scalar variants CLEAN** (I64/F64/Bool/I8..F32/Char migrate to `TypedArray<T>` monomorphization; recipe: `compiler/expressions/collections.rs` + `executor/v2_handlers/array.rs` + `ffi/v2/mod.rs` + `ffi_symbols/v2_symbols.rs` + `v2_array_detect.rs::stamp_elem_type` lockstep per kind); **5 heap-element variants CLEAN with prereq** (String/Decimal/BigInt/DateTime/Timespan/Duration/Instant migrate to `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` once the v2-raw `<X>Obj` carrier structs land); **2 heap-element variants STRUCTURAL-OBSTACLE O-3/O-3a** (TypedObject/TraitObject — `TypedObjectStorage`/`TraitObjectStorage` are `Arc<>`-wrapped without `HeapHeader` at offset 0, so per-element retain/release would need to know per-T whether to use `v2_retain`/`v2_release` vs `Arc::increment_strong_count`/`decrement_strong_count` — breaks `TypedArray<T>` retain-uniformity); **2 variants CATEGORY-ERROR** (Matrix/FloatSlice: `TypedArrayData::Matrix(Arc<MatrixData>)` is a **single Matrix**, not a buffer-of-Matrix — the variant exists per ADR-006 §2.7.22 Q23 (W15-matrix) because Q23 refused to add `HeapKind::Matrix` parallel to `HeapKind::TypedArray` under ADR-005 §1 single-discriminator. Under Round 17 deletion authorization that ruling collapses: with `TypedArrayData::Matrix` deleted there's no longer a parallel HeapKind label issue, so Matrix exits the array-carrier hierarchy entirely via new `HeapKind::Matrix = 34` + `HeapValue::Matrix(Arc<MatrixData>)` arm; FloatSlice gets `HeapKind::MatrixSlice = 35`). Per supervisor's explicit framing: "keep TypedArrayData::X for one variant" disposition refused on sight — every variant either has clean migration or structural obstacle named. (d) **HashMapValueBuf parallel verdict**: same deletion principle applies; `HashMapData::values: HashMapValueBuf` migrates to `*mut TypedArray<V>` with per-V monomorphization; same O-1/O-3/O-3a obstacles apply; separate cluster-1 deletion target with parallel migration shape (NOT cluster-0 scope). (e) **Migration sub-cluster plan (S1-S5)**: S1 scalar width pass (12 variants, 1 session) + S2 heap-element migration (5 variants, ~2 sessions, requires v2-raw `<X>Obj` carrier structs to land in parallel) + S3 TypedObject/TraitObject element-kind (gated on O-3/O-3a resolution; defer until `TypedObjectStorage` migration as own cluster) + S4 Matrix/FloatSlice exit to own HeapKinds (1 session) + S5 `TypedArrayData` enum + `TypedBuffer<T>` actual deletion (0.5 session). (f) **Drafted §2.7.24 Q25.A amendment text** (NOT committed in this audit; commits in S5 close with enum deletion): retires "TypedArrayData with per-built-in-heap-type specialized variants" framing, replaces with "`TypedArray<T>` flat struct + per-T monomorphization; per-element-kind monomorphizations supported: f64/i64/i32/i16/i8/u8/u16/u32/u64/f32/char/`*const StringObj`/`*const DecimalObj`/`*const BigIntObj`/`*const TemporalObj`/`*const InstantObj`/(TypedObject + TraitObject deferred O-3.c)". Q25.B drafted in parallel (HashMapValueBuf deletion, cluster-1 work order). (g) **Session-count estimate**: **floor 4 sessions, ceiling 6 sessions** at Phase 2d post-audit production-first cadence (~3 sub-clusters/session). Ceiling triggered if O-3 resolution requires a multi-week `TypedObjectStorage` v2-raw migration as own cluster-level work order before S3 can land. (h) **Structural obstacles surfaced for supervisor disposition**: **O-1** (DateTime/Timespan/Duration share `Arc<TemporalData>` payload, distinguish at user-facing-type via enum variant tag; migrating to `TypedArray<*const TemporalObj>` loses the distinction — resolution options: O-1.a element-type-tag byte extension with ADR-006 §2.7.5 amendment / O-1.b separate carriers per semantic kind / O-1.c language-level merge); **O-2** (F64 SIMD alignment — `AlignedTypedBuffer` uses AlignedVec<f64>'s stricter alignment, `Layout::array::<f64>` only gives 8-byte; resolution: O-2.a per-T alignment parameter on `TypedArray<T>` / O-2.c accept unaligned-load trade-off; NOT O-2.b parallel `AlignedTypedArray<f64>` which would be the carrier-duality the deletion is solving); **O-3/O-3a** (TypedObject/TraitObject Arc-vs-HeapHeader carrier shape — recommend O-3.c defer until `TypedObjectStorage` migration lands as own cluster); **O-4** (TypedBuffer<T>'s null-validity bitmap has no `TypedArray<T>` equivalent — clean resolution O-4.a: bitmap-bearing path migrates to separate `ArrowBuffer<T>` v2-raw shape; TypedArray<T> stays at 24-byte v2-spec contract). **CLAUDE.md amendment landed**: "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" sub-section appended to "Forbidden Patterns" / "Renames to refuse on sight" per supervisor's verbatim ADDITIONAL DIRECTIVE — refuses "documented intentional duality" / "preserve both carriers" / "carrier unification via boundary deletion" applied as one-off patch / "per-variant unwrap-and-flatten" framing. Names 5-instance class history (W12-jit-string-carrier-unification R12, W17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration R14, W12-Option-B R15, W12-Option-B-reframed R16, this audit R17). Names deletion target: `TypedArrayData` enum + `TypedBuffer<T>` wrapper layer; keep `TypedArray<T>` flat struct. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no "keep one variant" disposition; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for migration boundaries (audit describes migration shape by name — per-variant producer migration — and by deletion-fate — the deleted TypedArrayData enum class); no Bool-default for unproven kind during migration; no "migration is intractable, accept status quo" without structural reason. Every variant has a named disposition or a named obstacle with named resolution shape. | cluster-0 Round 17 | `docs/cluster-audits/w12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit.md` (NEW audit doc, ~570 LoC) + `CLAUDE.md` (Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries sub-section appended to "Renames to refuse on sight") + `AGENTS.md` (this row) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection appended) | closed (audit-only — deletion path clean for 18 of 22 variants; 2 variants O-3/O-3a deferred for supervisor disposition on `TypedObjectStorage` v2-raw migration; 2 variants Matrix/FloatSlice surface category-error requiring §2.7.22 amendment with new HeapKind::Matrix/MatrixSlice ordinals; HashMapValueBuf parallel deletion confirmed as cluster-1 work order; floor 4 / ceiling 6 sessions for full migration arc) | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-followup-a` | `../shape-w17-followup-a` | **CLOSED 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 16 W17-narrow-follow-up-A (production-first, schema-id MIR-lowering threading). The Round 15 W17-narrow close uncovered that the JIT MIR consumer at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs::StatementKind::ObjectStore:141-201` allocated typed objects via `shape_runtime::type_schema::register_predeclared_any_schema(&real_field_names)` (returning a `__predecl_*`-named schema id) instead of preserving the bytecode-side `OpCode::NewTypedObject` operand's user-declared schema id (Smoke 3 trace: bytecode emits `TypedObjectAlloc { schema_id: 53, field_count: 0 }` for `let t = X {}`, but JIT-MIR allocated with `schema=54` — a fresh predeclared id). With the predeclared-named schema id stamped into `(*ptr).schema_id`, the W17-narrow `receiver_type_name` classification resolved through `vm.program().type_schema_registry.get_by_id(54)` to `__predecl_` — never matching the user-declared `X::name` UFCS function. **Fix**: thread `schema_id: Option<u32>` field on the MIR `StatementKind::ObjectStore` carrier from the bytecode-side `OpCode::NewTypedObject` operand. (1) Added `schema_id: Option<u32>` field on `StatementKind::ObjectStore` in `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/types.rs`. (2) MIR lowering at `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/helpers.rs::emit_container_store_full` emits `schema_id: None` initially — the MIR lowering pass operates on AST only and does not have access to the bytecode compiler's `type_tracker.schema_registry`. (3) New module `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mir_schema_threading.rs` defines `back_patch_schema_ids(mir, type_tracker)` which walks MIR statements and resolves each `ObjectStore` site via two paths: named-struct (uses `mir.local_struct_type_names[container_slot]` + `type_tracker.schema_registry().get(name)` — matches `Expr::StructLiteral { type_name, .. }` lowering, the canonical Smoke 3 site `let t = X {}`); anonymous-inline (uses `type_tracker.register_inline_object_schema(&field_refs)` — mirrors the bytecode-side `expressions/collections.rs:396` call, idempotent on repeated calls). Spread-bearing inline objects (empty-name field-name entries) are left `None` for surface-and-stop. (4) Bytecode-compiler call sites: `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/functions.rs` (post per-function MIR closure-id back-patch + pre `mir_data` cache) and `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs` (post top-level MIR closure-id back-patch + pre `top_level_mir` cache). Both sites have full access to the populated `self.type_tracker.schema_registry()` because bytecode compilation has run before the back-patch. (5) JIT consumer at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs::StatementKind::ObjectStore` updated to use `schema_id: Option<u32>` directly: if `Some(id)`, allocate with that schema id via `typed_object_alloc(id, data_size)`; if `None`, surface-and-stop with a structured `ObjectStore: SURFACE — schema_id not threaded ...` error citing ADR-006 §2.7.5. The `register_predeclared_any_schema` call site at the previous `statements.rs:152` is DELETED, not preserved as a "compatibility helper". **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no `register_predeclared_any_schema` fallback in JIT consumer; no Bool-default schema id; no `__predecl_*` shape preservation "for one edge case"; no `register_predeclared_any_schema` resurrection under a rename; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the threading (described as "schema-id identity preservation from bytecode operand" and "back-patch via type_tracker schema registry"); no cross-crate operand-threading question fabricated (the bytecode operand carried the schema id; the MIR struct is in `shape-vm` same as the bytecode opcode; the JIT consumer is in `shape-jit` — modifying the MIR struct is single-crate change, threading through to the JIT consumer is the existing single-crate boundary the `field_names` field already crosses). **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` SCRIPT_EXIT=0, 12/12 PASS; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Smoke 3 status** (per W17-narrow precedent stash-then-rebuild verification): under `--mode vm` prints `x`; under `--mode jit` still segfaults — verified via stash-then-rebuild at base `a7b93def` that segfault is identical pre/post-fix, AND `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1 SHAPE_JIT_TRACE=1` confirms the fix lands correctly (`[alloc] schema=53` post-fix matches the bytecode-side `OpCode::NewTypedObject schema_id: 53`, versus baseline `schema=54` predeclared; `receiver_kind=Ptr(TypedObject)` flows correctly through the W17-narrow classification). The downstream segfault is the W17-narrow-follow-up-B `print_kinded_inner` null-handling gap at `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/printing.rs:154-163` (parallel sub-cluster's territory, not in this scope). Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end pass is gated on follow-up-B merging. This is the supervisor-ratified gate-deferred-to-follow-up-B shape per Round 15 W17-narrow precedent. **Files touched**: `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/types.rs` (StatementKind::ObjectStore `schema_id` field); `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/helpers.rs` (emit `None`); `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mir_schema_threading.rs` (NEW module, ~70 LoC); `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mod.rs` (module registration); `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/functions.rs` (call site); `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs` (top-level call site); `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs` (consumer + delete `register_predeclared_any_schema` call); `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/storage_planning.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/helpers.rs` (4 test-site updates for new `schema_id` field). 8 files / +179 −19 LoC net. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim**: Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 16 W17-narrow-follow-up-A (production-first, schema-id MIR-lowering threading). Thread `Option<u32> schema_id` from the bytecode-side `OpCode::NewTypedObject` operand through the JIT MIR's `StatementKind::ObjectStore` into the JIT type-object allocation site. Currently the JIT lowering allocates via `register_predeclared_any_schema(&field_names)` returning a `__predecl_*`-named schema id; the fix preserves the user-declared schema id (e.g. `X` schema = 53 in Smoke 3). Refuse on sight: default-schema-id fallback when the bytecode operand is absent or unset; preserving the `__predecl_*` shape "for one edge case"; `register_predeclared_any_schema` resurrection under a rename; bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing; cross-crate coordination questions; ValueWord resurrection. | cluster-0 Round 16 | `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/types.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/helpers.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mir_schema_threading.rs` (NEW) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mod.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/functions.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs` + `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/storage_planning.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/helpers.rs` | closed (production fix landed; Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end gate-deferred to W17-narrow-follow-up-B null-handling fix per supervisor-ratified Round 15 precedent) | 2026-05-13 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-typed-array-data-scalar-migration` | `../shape-w12-typed-array-data-scalar-migration` | **CLOSED-WITH-REOPEN 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 18 W12-typed-array-data-scalar-migration S1 (production, sized-integer scalar producer migration). Initial close at `4bcae991` landed 6 mechanical scalar variants (I8/U8/I16/U16/U32/U64) + a defensive low-address-pointer guard at `v2_array_detect.rs::as_v2_typed_array` (`bits < 0x1_0000 -> None`). **Supervisor reopen (W11-round-1 reopen precedent)**: the defensive guard + the U64 variant migration that surfaced it are the 6th instance of the parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries defection-attractor class per CLAUDE.md §"Renames to refuse on sight" / §Parallel-implementation entry (landed at `e55b8e71` as part of the R17 audit close). The guard's "preserves the documented kind-only classification" framing was structurally overstated: `NativeKind::UInt64` at HEAD is ambiguous between "scalar u64" and "pointer-to-TypedArray<u64>"; the kind track CANNOT discriminate these without the §2.7.7/Q9 NativeKind extension that doesn't yet exist. The guard substituted a memory-region heuristic (`bits < 0x1_0000`) for the missing compile-time discriminator. ADR-006 §2.7.7 is explicit: "kind-aware clone/drop dispatch — no tag decode, no `is_heap()` probe." A memory-region heuristic IS an `is_heap()` probe in different framing. Reopen disposition: (1) defensive guard at `v2_array_detect.rs:148-167` removed; surface-and-stop comment block landed citing CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation + the deletion-fate of S1.5's discriminator extension as the structural fix; (2) U64 variant migration removed from S1 — `OpCode::{New,Get,Push,Set}TypedArrayU64` opcodes excised (byte values 0x11F..0x122 left unallocated for S1.5's re-mint), `TypedArrayKind::U64` excised, `V2ElemType::U64` excised, `ELEM_TYPE_U64` constant excised (tag byte 10 reserved for S1.5), all U64 dispatch arms (read/write/push/pop/clone/slice/iter-next/materialization/JIT-preflight/method-classifier) excised; `should_use_typed_array(ConcreteType::U64)` returns `None` (compile-time fall-through to legacy NaN-boxed `NewArray`); `should_use_typed_array_from_slot_kind(NativeKind::UInt64)` returns `None` (the slot kind is shared with v2-typed-array pointers — routing through a typed-array fast path here would conflate the two shapes); (3) U64 added to S1's surface-and-stop list alongside F32/Char/String as the 4th deferred variant, framing: "U64 surface-and-stop. `NativeKind::UInt64` at HEAD is ambiguous between scalar u64 and pointer-to-`TypedArray<u64>`; the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind-track invariant requires a discriminating variant before the U64 carrier migration can proceed without runtime `is_heap()` probes. Deferred to S1.5 sub-cluster (W12-nativekind-typed-array-ptr-extension or equivalent per team-lead's pre-dispatch audit). ADR-006 §2.7.7 / Q9 + the new CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation-across-producer/consumer-carrier-shape-boundaries entry." Post-reopen close: **5 mechanically migrated variants** (I8/U8/I16/U16/U32) + 4 unchanged-already-supported (I64/F64/Bool/I32) = 9 net scalar coverage; surface-and-stop list **4 deferred variants** (U64/F32/Char/String). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 52dbe312` (post-W17-narrow-follow-up-A merge HEAD). **S1 scope per Round 17 W12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit §3.1** (post-reopen): extend the v2 `TypedArray<T>` producer / consumer / refcount plumbing from 4 already-supported scalar element kinds (F64 / I64 / I32 / Bool) to 5 additional Copy-scalar kinds **I8 / U8 / I16 / U16 / U32**. **Scope-trimming disposition**: dispatch's variant list named **String / F32 / Char** alongside the mechanical Copy-scalars, plus U64 post-reopen — audit §2.2 + §2.1 placed Char in §3.1 scalar bucket and String in §2.2 heap-element bucket (out of §3.1 S1 scope), `NativeKind` / `ConcreteType` have **no F32 nor Char variants** at HEAD `52dbe312`, and U64 was reopen-removed because `NativeKind::UInt64` is overloaded between scalar u64 and v2-typed-array-pointer carrier (the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind-track invariant violation that the defensive guard attempted to paper over with a memory-region heuristic refused on sight per CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation). **Surface-and-stop discipline** (phase-2d-handover §0): the work shape "mechanical NewTypedArrayX recipe per kind" requires `NativeKind::<X>` + `ConcreteType::<X>` to already exist AND the kind to not be overloaded with another carrier shape; U64/F32/Char/String violate one or both prerequisites. F32/Char would each require an ADR-006 §2.7.5 amendment adding a new NativeKind variant + cascading the 4-table HeapKind-style lockstep across `decode_*`/`is_integer_family`/etc.; String would require per-element retain/release plumbing on `TypedArray<*const StringObj>`; U64 requires a §2.7.7/Q9 amendment adding a discriminating NativeKind variant for "pointer to TypedArray<T>" distinct from scalar UInt64. All four shapes are explicitly out of S1's stated mechanical-recipe boundary. Followed audit's verbatim "every variant either has clean migration OR surfaces a specific structural obstacle to the supervisor — no 'keep both' disposition" by **proceeding with the 5 mechanical variants and surfacing U64/F32/Char/String as structural obstacles in the close report** rather than fabricating Bool-default fallbacks, `NativeKind` synthesis, or memory-region heuristics. **O-2 (F64 SIMD alignment) disposition**: **N/A — not applicable to S1**. S1 is producer migration only; F64 is in the 4 already-supported kinds (not migrated). Producer for `Array<number>` emits `OpCode::NewTypedArrayF64` at HEAD which produces `*mut TypedArray<f64>` with `Layout::array::<f64>` 8-byte alignment — that path is already in production and not changed by this S1. The legacy `AlignedTypedBuffer` lives behind the `TypedArrayData::F64` enum consumer-side, untouched by producer migration. SIMD reductions at `v2_array_detect.rs::simd_sum_f64` use `f64x4::from([*data.add(i), ...])` (scalar loads + lane construction, not aligned-vector loads), so the existing F64 v2 path already runs without aligned-load semantics — no SIMD regression introduced by S1, no `AlignedTypedArray<f64>` parallel preservation (refused on sight per audit §4.2 O-2.b). **Per-variant migration summary** (audit §3.1 recipe, post-reopen 5 variants × ~15 sites each = ~75 mechanical extensions): each variant gets (1) `TypedArrayKind::<X>` enum + 4-opcode dispatch (`new_opcode`/`get_opcode`/`push_opcode`/`set_opcode`); (2) `should_use_typed_array(ConcreteType::<X>)` arm; (3) `should_use_typed_array_from_slot_kind(NativeKind::<X>)` arm; (4) `typed_array_kind_from_type_name("<x>")` arm; (5) 4 new `OpCode` variants `New/Get/Push/SetTypedArray<X>` (bytes from gaps `0x10-0x16`, `0x20-0x25`, `0x118-0x11E` reusing the deleted Dynamic-arithmetic / Dynamic-comparison slots + previously-unallocated 0x118+; **byte values 0x11F..0x122 left unallocated for S1.5's U64 re-mint**); (6) `ELEM_TYPE_<X>` byte constant + `V2ElemType::<X>` variant + `from_byte`/`elem_kind` arms (**tag byte 10 = `ELEM_TYPE_U64` reserved for S1.5; not produced by any current allocation path**); (7) `read_element`/`write_element`/`push_element`/`pop_element`/`clone_array` arms; (8) VM executor handler in `executor/v2_handlers/array.rs` (per-kind New/Get/Push/Set bodies); (9) dispatch routing in `executor/dispatch.rs::exec_v2_typed_array` cascade; (10) `is_v2_typed()` whitelist in `bytecode/opcode_defs.rs`; (11) JIT preflight whitelist in `shape-jit/src/compiler/accessors.rs::ALL_OPCODES`; (12) materialization arms in `executor/objects/array_transform.rs` (v2→legacy `Arc<TypedArrayData::<X>>` conversion via existing `TypedArrayData::<X>` enum arms that ALREADY EXIST at HEAD `52dbe312` — confirmed via grep `^\s+(I8\|U8\|I16\|U16\|U32\|U64)\(` in `crates/shape-value/src/heap_value.rs:2883-2889`; the `TypedArrayData::U64` enum arm itself stays in source untouched, the deletion is S5's commit — only the v2→legacy materialization arm is removed in S1); (13) slice arms in `executor/objects/array_operations.rs::slice_v2_typed_array`; (14) iter-next arms in `executor/loops/mod.rs::op_iter_next` UInt64-carrier branch; (15) method-dispatch classifier in `executor/objects/mod.rs::receiver_handler` UInt64 / V2ElemType cascade — flows U8/I8/U16/I16/U32 to `TYPED_INT_ARRAY_METHODS` (same PHF as I64/I32, methods sum/min/max/etc. operate on integer bit pattern regardless of width; narrower widths sign-/zero-extend at read time; **U64 omitted from the integer-family arm post-reopen** — `Array<u64>` lowers via the legacy NaN-boxed `OpCode::NewArray` path, dispatched through the existing `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` receiver handler, not the v2 fast path). U8 has a distinct `ELEM_TYPE_U8 = 6` byte from `ELEM_TYPE_BOOL = 4` (audit §2.1 explicit: "collides with `Bool` element-type-tag — Bool is `1` valid bit, U8 is full `0..=255`; the `stamp_elem_type` byte at v2_array_detect.rs ELEM_TYPE_BOOL distinguishes them at runtime") — `NewTypedArrayU8` stamps `ELEM_TYPE_U8`, distinct from Bool's path. **Defensive guard REMOVED at reopen**: the `as_v2_typed_array` low-address-pointer guard (`bits < 0x1_0000 -> None`) that the pre-reopen S1 commit `4bcae991` added is excised. The supervisor's reopen named the heuristic as the 6th instance of the CLAUDE.md §"Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" defection-attractor class — an `is_heap()` probe in different framing, refused on sight per ADR-006 §2.7.7's "no tag decode, no `is_heap()` probe" mandate. The site at `v2_array_detect.rs:148-167` now carries a surface-and-stop comment block citing CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation + the deletion-fate of S1.5's discriminator extension as the structural fix; the runtime SIGSEGV that motivated the guard is replaced by compile-time legacy-path dispatch (`should_use_typed_array(ConcreteType::U64) → None` at the bytecode-emitter routes to `OpCode::NewArray`, which never lights up the `as_v2_typed_array` probe path on U64 element reads). **Files touched (10 files, post-reopen net ~+460 LoC; pre-reopen S1 commit `4bcae991` was 12 files / +1308 / -19; the reopen WIP removes 24 U64 OpCode variants / 6 dispatch tables / U64 handler bodies / U64 dispatch routing / U64 iter-next arm / U64 slice arm / U64 materialization arm / U64 method-dispatch arm / U64 JIT preflight entries + the defensive guard at v2_array_detect.rs:148-167)**: `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/opcode_defs.rs` (+20 OpCode variants in `0x10-0x16`/`0x20-0x25`/`0x118-0x11E` + 20 `is_v2_typed()` arms;
