U64 entries REMOVED with comment block citing reopen). **Close gates run via devenv wrapper, post-reopen** (`devenv shell --quiet -- bash -c "cd <wt> && <cmd>"`): `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/vm.out 2>&1` SCRIPT_EXIT=0, **Passed: 12 / Failed: 0**; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib v2_typed_emission` 56/56 PASS (U64 tests `test_u64_falls_back_to_legacy` + `test_slot_kind_uint64_falls_back_to_legacy` now assert `None` — explicit regression guards against U64 re-entering the typed fast path before S1.5 lands); `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib v2_array_detect` 5/5 PASS. **Smoke S1 end-to-end post-reopen** (9-variant comprehensive smoke `let a: Array<i8> = [...]; print(a[0]); ...` for 5 newly-migrated + 4 already-supported, U64 omitted): under `--mode vm` prints `-5 / 100 / 255 / 30000 / 30000 / 300 / 2.5 / 30 / true`; under `--mode jit` prints identical sequence — VM ≡ JIT for all 9 variants. **U64 compile-time fall-through verification post-reopen**: `let d: Array<u64> = [1000, 2000, 3000]; print(d[0])` lowers via the legacy NaN-boxed `OpCode::NewArray` path (compiler's `should_use_typed_array(ConcreteType::U64) → None` routes to the legacy generic-array fallback in `compile_expr_array`), prints `1000` correctly under VM — the runtime SIGSEGV that motivated the (now-removed) defensive guard is replaced by compile-time legacy-path dispatch. Smoke 2 baseline (kickoff `print([1,2,3,4,5].sum())`) unchanged from baseline `52dbe312`: VM=`15` ✓, JIT=`15` ✓ — S1 doesn't touch the I64 producer paths Smoke 2 exercises. **Surfaced as structural obstacles for team-lead/supervisor disposition post-reopen** (phase-2d-handover §0 surface-and-stop discipline, not Bool-defaulted; 4 deferred variants): (i) **U64 element kind** [NEW, reopen-added]: `NativeKind::UInt64` at HEAD is overloaded between scalar u64 and v2-typed-array-pointer carrier (every `*mut TypedArray<T>` flows through this kind). Routing producer-emission and consumer-classification through the same overloaded discriminator violates the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind-track invariant. Defensive memory-region heuristics (`bits < 0x1_0000`) are `is_heap()` probes in different framing and are refused on sight per CLAUDE.md §"Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" entry (`e55b8e71`). Resolution shape: ADR-006 §2.7.7/Q9 amendment adding a discriminating `NativeKind` variant (e.g. `NativeKind::TypedArrayPtr`) that flows alongside `NativeKind::UInt64` on the §2.7.7 parallel-kind track, so producers and consumers can dispatch on kind without runtime bit-pattern probes. Cluster-equivalent scope. **Deferred to sub-cluster S1.5 (W12-nativekind-typed-array-ptr-extension or equivalent per team-lead's pre-dispatch audit)**. (ii) **F32 element kind**: dispatch named in scope but no `NativeKind::Float32` nor `ConcreteType::F32` at HEAD; mechanical recipe requires both. Resolution shape if pursued: ADR-006 §2.7.5 amendment adding `NativeKind::Float32` variant + cascading `decode_f64`/`is_integer`/`is_nullable_integer`/`is_floating`/etc. across `crates/shape-value/src/native_kind.rs` (~30 sites) + cascading through every `KindedSlot::from_<X>` constructor + adding `ConcreteType::F32` variant + monomorphisation cascade. Cluster-equivalent scope. (iii) **Char element kind**: same shape as F32 — no `NativeKind::Char` nor `ConcreteType::Char` at HEAD; audit §3.1 included Char among 12 scalar variants but the prerequisite NativeKind variant doesn't exist. Cluster-equivalent scope. (iv) **String element kind**: audit §2.2 explicit out-of-S1-scope (S2 territory: requires v2-raw `StringObj` carrier prerequisite to land + `TypedArray<T>` retain/release plumbing extension for per-element `v2_retain`/`v2_release` on heap-pointer elements — currently `TypedArray<T>::drop_array` does NOT release per-element refcounts because `T: Copy` semantics apply). The `StringObj` struct DOES exist at HEAD `52dbe312` (`crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs:19-26`), but the producer/consumer plumbing for `TypedArray<*const StringObj>` with per-element retain on push + release on pop + release-all on drop_array does NOT — this is the audit §2.2 S2 sub-cluster scope. Including String in S1 would either silently leak StringObj refcounts (defection-class fallback) or require S2 prerequisite work to land alongside (scope explosion). **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved post-reopen** (full list): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind (U64/F32/Char/String surfaced explicitly); no preservation of `TypedArrayData::<X>` arms (audit §2.1 / §6 forbidden — those arms still live in source but are unreachable from S1-migrated producer paths; their actual deletion is S5's commit); no `AlignedTypedBuffer` parallel preservation (audit §4.2 O-2.b explicit forbidden); no §2.7.24 Q25.A amendment text touched (S5's commit); no carrier-unification-via-boundary-deletion one-off patch framing (the audit §2.1 §3.1 recipe is systematic producer migration per kind); no NativeKind synthesis for F32/Char (forbidden — surface-and-stop the prerequisite gap); no String per-element silent-leak (forbidden — surface to S2 scope); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration shape (described by name: "S1 per-variant producer migration to `TypedArray<T>`"; by deletion-fate: "the deleted-class `TypedArrayData::<scalar>` arms become unreachable from production code paths"); no ValueWord resurrection; **no memory-region heuristic (`bits < 0x1_0000`) substituting for a missing compile-time discriminator — the reopen's load-bearing addition to the discipline list, an `is_heap()` probe in different framing per CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation entry; equivalently no defensive runtime is_heap() probes substituting for the §2.7.7/Q9 NativeKind extension that the U64 carrier requires before it can dispatch cleanly**. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim** (audit §9 / dispatch §Scope): "Migrate every site emitting `Arc<TypedArrayData::<scalar-variant>>` to direct `TypedArray<T>` per-T monomorphization. Variants in scope: I64, F64, Bool, U8, U16, U32, U64, I8, I16, I32, F32, String (12 total). Reference recipe (from audit §3.1): `compiler/expressions/collections.rs` + `executor/v2_handlers/array.rs` + `ffi/v2/mod.rs` + `ffi_symbols/v2_symbols.rs` + `v2_array_detect.rs::stamp_elem_type` lockstep per kind. The `TypedArrayData` enum itself stays alive. S1 is producer migration only — making `TypedArrayData::<scalar-variant>` arms unreachable from production code paths. The actual enum deletion is S5's commit." S1 close response (post-reopen): **5 of 12 named variants migrated mechanically** (I64/F64/Bool/I32 already in production at HEAD, no migration needed; I8/U8/I16/U16/U32 newly added); **4 of 12 named variants (U64/F32/Char/String) surfaced as structural obstacles** for supervisor disposition with named resolution shapes; 0 of 12 named variants Bool-defaulted, silently dropped, or papered over with runtime heuristics. **W11-round-1 reopen precedent preserved**: pre-reopen S1 commit `4bcae991` superseded by post-reopen close commit; substantive source changes attributed to the prior S1 work + the reopen disposition (U64 + defensive guard excised). | cluster-0 Round 18 (closed-with-reopen) | 11 files (post-reopen net ~+460 LoC over baseline `52dbe312`) — `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/opcode_defs.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/v2_typed_emission.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/v2_handlers/v2_array_detect.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/v2_handlers/array.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/dispatch.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/loops/mod.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/array_operations.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/array_transform.rs`, `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/mod.rs`, `crates/shape-jit/src/compiler/accessors.rs`, `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed-with-reopen (production — 5 of 12 named variants migrated mechanically; 4 variants U64/F32/Char/String surfaced as structural obstacles requiring §2.7.7/Q9 discriminator (U64), NativeKind/ConcreteType variant additions (F32/Char), or `StringObj` retain/release plumbing (String); supervisor reopen excised U64 + defensive low-address-pointer guard per CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation (`e55b8e71`); S5 enum deletion still gated on S2/S3/S4 + S1.5 prerequisites) | 2026-05-13 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration` | `../shape-w12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration` | **SURFACE-AND-STOP CLOSE 2026-05-14.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 19 S2 sub-cluster — W12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration (audit §2.2 / §3.2). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 9135a8a6`. **No source changes (zero LoC delta beyond this AGENTS.md row and the close subsection appended to `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md`); workspace `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 verified at baseline `9135a8a6`.** Dispatched scope (team-lead prompt verbatim): migrate the 7 heap-element `TypedArrayData` arms (String / Decimal / BigInt / DateTime / Timespan / Duration / Instant — Char excluded per R18 close supervisor disposition) per audit §2.2 to `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` carriers with per-element `v2_retain` / `v2_release` plumbing on `push` / `pop` / `drop_array`. Per-variant carrier prereqs at HEAD: `StringObj` exists at `crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs:19-26`; `DecimalObj` / `BigIntObj` / `DateTimeObj` / `TimespanObj` / `DurationObj` / `InstantObj` v2-raw structs need to be created (named in dispatch as "NEEDS TO BE CREATED if not at HEAD"). **Three blocking structural obstacles surfaced** for supervisor disposition; the team-lead's surface-and-stop discipline (phase-2d-handover §0, CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" / "Renames to refuse on sight") refuses Bool-default fallbacks, silent refcount-leak shims, and "preserve heap-element TypedArrayData arm for one edge case" framings — the right discipline call is to surface honestly rather than fabricate partial Bool-default work or silent-leak intermediate states. **Obstacle 1 — target-shape conflict between audit §2.2 and ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A.** Audit §2.2 prescribes `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` (v2-raw flat-struct carrier, exits the `TypedArrayData` enum); ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A (the formally-committed amendment text) prescribes `TypedArrayData::Decimal(Arc<TypedBuffer<Decimal>>)` / `DateTime(Arc<TypedBuffer<DateTimeData>>)` / etc. — specialized monomorphic variants **kept inside** `TypedArrayData`. These two prescriptions produce structurally different target shapes: §2.2 makes the heap-element arms unreachable (S2's stated goal "make heap-element arms unreachable from production code paths, not deleted"); Q25.A keeps the arms but reshapes them to elide the inner `Arc<X>` indirection. Team-lead's "Refuse on sight" list forbids touching §2.7.24 Q25.A amendment text (S5's commit) — so the agent cannot land the Q25.A path even if it were the correct one. Resolution shape: supervisor disposition required to (a) confirm §2.2 over Q25.A as the canonical S2 migration target with explicit Q25.A amendment-text supersession authorization, OR (b) flip S2 to the Q25.A path with the supersession committed as part of S2 (re-scoping S2 to overlap S5's amendment-text territory), OR (c) reframe S2 as a smaller "Obj-carrier prereq landing only" sub-cluster with the actual producer migration deferred to a Round-20+ S2-prime that gets the architectural ratification. **Obstacle 2 — `TemporalData` enum has 7 variants, not 3.** Per dispatch's O-1 newtype ruling (R17 disposition), DateTime / Timespan / Duration "get separate Obj carriers via newtype path (NOT a shared `Arc<TemporalData>` with semantic-kind tag)". The current `TemporalData` enum at `crates/shape-value/src/heap_value.rs:3549-3557` carries 7 variants: `DateTime(chrono::DateTime<FixedOffset>)`, `Duration(shape_ast::ast::Duration)`, `TimeSpan(chrono::Duration)`, `Timeframe(shape_ast::data::Timeframe)`, `TimeReference(Box<TimeReference>)`, `DateTimeExpr(Box<DateTimeExpr>)`, `DataDateTimeRef(Box<DataDateTimeRef>)`. Only 3 of the 7 are user-facing (DateTime / TimeSpan / Duration); the other 4 (Timeframe / TimeReference / DateTimeExpr / DataDateTimeRef) are AST/internal payloads that flow through `HeapValue::Temporal(Arc<TemporalData>)` but aren't separately exposed at the language type layer. The O-1.b newtype ruling implicitly assumes a 3-way split, but the underlying carrier has 7 payload shapes. Resolution shape: supervisor disposition required to (a) define `DateTimeObj` / `TimespanObj` / `DurationObj` as newtype wrappers around just the 3 user-facing variant payloads and treat the other 4 TemporalData variants as continuing to use the legacy `HeapValue::Temporal(Arc<TemporalData>)` carrier (so TemporalData stays alive, just isn't a `TypedArray` element type for user `Array<DateTime>` / `Array<Timespan>` / `Array<Duration>`), OR (b) refactor TemporalData to extract the 3 user-facing variants into dedicated structs (`DateTimeData` / `TimespanData` / `DurationData`) and leave the AST-internal 4 in a renamed enum (`TemporalAstData` or similar). **Obstacle 3 — `BigInt` type doesn't exist at HEAD.** Audit §2.2 names BigInt explicitly as in-scope but notes the payload is `Arc<i64>` placeholder per W14 stubs ("BigInt at landing is i64-only per the W14 / Wave 14 BigInt stubs"). Q25.A prescribes `BigInt(Arc<TypedBuffer<BigInt>>)` but no `BigInt` Rust type exists at HEAD `9135a8a6` — `grep -rn 'pub struct BigInt\b\|pub enum BigInt\b' crates/` returns zero hits. The dispatch directs creating `BigIntObj` but does not specify what payload `BigIntObj` should wrap (the i64 placeholder? a stub `bigint`/`malachite`/`num-bigint` external dependency? an arbitrary-precision integer carrier yet to be designed?). The audit §2.2 explicitly notes "the BigInt payload-width question is its own follow-up" and offers either (a) thin `TypedArray<i64>` wrapping while BigInt full-width remains unimplemented OR (b) the bigint full-width migration lands in the same sub-cluster. Resolution shape: supervisor disposition required to (a) approve the thin `Array<bigint>` = `TypedArray<*const BigIntObj>` where `BigIntObj` is just a `HeapHeader` + `i64` payload at landing (24-byte struct, deferring full-width to follow-up), OR (b) defer BigInt from S2 scope until the full-width payload design lands as its own sub-cluster, OR (c) take a specific external `bigint` crate dependency (`malachite-nz` / `num-bigint` / etc.) which would be a workspace-Cargo.toml-touching decision. **Producer-site migration scope** (the actual S2 mechanical work, currently blocked by Obstacles 1-3 above): `grep -rn 'TypedArrayData::(String\|Decimal\|BigInt\|DateTime\|Timespan\|Duration\|Instant)\(Arc::new' crates/ --include="*.rs"` returns 53 construction sites across 14 files in `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/{objects/{array_sets,array_transform,array_operations,builtins/array_ops,concat,iterator_methods,object_creation,string_methods},loops/mod.rs,window_join.rs,v2_handlers/typed_array_elem.rs}`, `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/{comptime,comptime_target}.rs`, `crates/shape-value/src/heap_value.rs`, plus 193+ consumer match-arm sites across ~30 files. Audit §3.2 estimates **~5-7k LoC across 2 sessions** for the full migration (Session-2 part 1: String+Decimal+BigInt; Session-2 part 2: DateTime+Timespan+Duration+Instant); each variant requires producer + consumer + JIT FFI + 4-table lockstep cascade per the S1 recipe (S1 row above documents the per-variant recipe at ~15 sites × variant); plus the per-element retain/release plumbing extension on `TypedArray<T>::push` / `pop` / `drop_array` (currently `T: Copy` semantics, no element refcount management — see `crates/shape-value/src/v2/typed_array.rs:46-249`). **Per-element retain/release ABI gap** (named in dispatch prompt's "Surface-and-stop if the existing `v2_retain` / `v2_release` ABI doesn't support per-element type-specific release" trigger): the v2 refcount ABI at `crates/shape-value/src/v2/refcount.rs:14-38` is `v2_retain(*const HeapHeader)` + `v2_release(*const HeapHeader) -> bool` operating on the universal HeapHeader; it manipulates the refcount but does NOT free the allocation on refcount==0 (caller-responsibility — `v2_release` returns true and the caller must invoke the per-T `<X>Obj::drop` body that knows about per-T sub-allocations: StringObj has a separate `data` buffer at `string_obj.rs:88-99::StringObj::drop`; a DecimalObj would have just the inline 16-byte Decimal payload; an InstantObj would have just the 16-byte Instant payload; DateTimeObj/TimespanObj/DurationObj would each have a payload variant-specific). For `TypedArray<*const StringObj>::drop_array` to release per-element refcounts at the right per-T deallocator, either (a) a new `unsafe trait HeapElement { unsafe fn release_elem(*const Self); }` per-T monomorphized dispatch needs to land (each `<X>Obj` impls it, calling `v2_release` then the per-T deallocator on return-true), OR (b) `TypedArray<T>` becomes specialized-per-element-T with per-T `drop_array` bodies, OR (c) `drop_array` is invoked with a runtime kind discriminator that selects the per-T deallocator. Path (a) is the audit §4.3 "O-3.b style" approach but was audit-flagged forbidden for TypedObject specifically (since for TypedObject it perpetuates Arc-vs-HeapHeader duality); for the heap-element Obj structs (all uniformly HeapHeader-equipped) the O-3.b objection does NOT apply — there's no second retain/release shape, and the `HeapElement` trait approach is the in-scope clean resolution. But path (a) is itself a non-trivial ABI extension that touches the canonical `TypedArray<T>` flat-struct API and would benefit from supervisor disposition before landing. **Surface-and-stop discipline preserved** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns"): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind; no silent per-element refcount leak (the dispatch prompt's "Do NOT silently leak refcounts" rule); no preservation of `TypedArrayData::<heap-element>` arms via "documented exception" framing; no Q25.A amendment text touched (S5's commit, refused on sight per dispatch); no shared `Arc<TemporalData>` carrier with semantic-kind tag (refused on sight per O-1 newtype ruling); no Char inclusion (S1.5's NativeKind extension territory per R18 supervisor disposition); no addition to S5's territory (enum deletion is S5's commit); no ValueWord resurrection; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration boundary. Migration boundary described by name (per-variant producer migration to v2-raw `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>`) and by deletion-fate (the deleted-class `TypedArrayData::<heap-element>` arms become unreachable from production code paths once the producer migration + Obj carrier prereqs + per-element retain/release plumbing land in some future S2-prime). **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim** (team-lead R19 dispatch §Scope): "Migrate heap-element `TypedArrayData` variants per audit §2.2 to `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` carriers with per-element retain/release plumbing. Variants in scope: String, Decimal, BigInt, DateTime, Timespan, Duration, Instant. Char excluded per R19 supervisor disposition — Char is a scalar bucket variant (Copy + fits in u32) and migrates via S1.5 NativeKind extension. Per-element retain/release plumbing requirement: push callsite invokes `v2_retain(elem_obj_ptr)` before storing the pointer; pop callsite invokes `v2_release(elem_obj_ptr)` after removing; `drop_array` walks all elements + invokes `v2_release` per element before freeing the buffer. Surface-and-stop if the existing `v2_retain` / `v2_release` ABI doesn't support per-element type-specific release. Do NOT silently leak refcounts. O-1 Temporal disambiguation per supervisor R17 disposition: DateTime/Timespan/Duration get separate Obj carriers via newtype path (NOT a shared `Arc<TemporalData>` with semantic-kind tag). Refuse on sight: preserve TypedArrayData arm framings, Bool-default, bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing, silent refcount leak, shared `Arc<TemporalData>` payload, addition to S5's territory, §2.7.24 Q25.A amendment text touching, ValueWord resurrection." Response: **0 of 7 named variants migrated** (every variant blocked by at least one of Obstacles 1-3 above); **3 structural obstacles surfaced** for supervisor disposition with named resolution shapes; **0 variants Bool-defaulted, silently dropped, papered over with runtime heuristics, or partially-migrated with silent refcount leaks**. The 7 variants' Obj carrier prereqs (DecimalObj / BigIntObj / DateTimeObj / TimespanObj / DurationObj / InstantObj) were NOT landed in this commit because doing so without supervisor disposition on Obstacles 1-3 (target-shape selection, TemporalData 7-variant split, BigInt payload-width) would commit the codebase to a specific architectural path before that path is ratified — exactly the defection-class pattern the team-lead's "Refuse on sight" list refuses (parallel-implementation pattern dressed as feature, premature commit before supervisor decision). **R19 parallel-sub-cluster coordination** (per dispatch §Coordination): S1.5 (W12-nativekind-typed-array-ptr-extension) touches `crates/shape-value/src/native_kind.rs` + `crates/shape-value/src/v2/concrete_type.rs` for F32 + Char additions — would have collided with S2's needed Timespan/Duration/Instant additions to `concrete_type.rs` (the `ConcreteType` enum at `crates/shape-value/src/v2/concrete_type.rs:36-90` already has `String` / `Decimal` / `BigInt` / `DateTime` arms but lacks `Timespan` / `Duration` / `Instant`; adding them while S1.5 adds F32/Char would have been a parallel-edit-file collision the dispatch said wouldn't happen — flagging for team-lead awareness as a pre-existing audit-doc detail gap, not an obstacle to S2 close); C (W17-narrow-follow-up-B-β) touches `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs` — no overlap with S2. AGENTS.md row collisions handled by take-both at merge per dispatch. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper, per `reference_phase2d_devenv.md`): (1) `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 — verified at baseline `9135a8a6` (no source changes, baseline state preserved). (2) `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/vm.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` — not run because source state is identical to baseline `9135a8a6`; the verify-merge.sh gate validates merge-state coherence, and a no-source-change commit on a branch identical to its parent has nothing for the gate to validate. (3) `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 — verified at baseline `9135a8a6`. (4) AGENTS.md row appended (this row). (5) NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. (6) NEVER blame "pre-existing" (the BigInt non-existence, the TemporalData 7-variant shape, and the §2.2-vs-Q25.A target-shape conflict are all owned as S2's surface-and-stop responsibility — not blamed on prior agents or W-series sessions; the dispatch frame "audit §2.2 specifies the migration target" was incomplete because §2.2 was drafted before Q25.A's amendment text was committed, and the agent's responsibility is to surface the conflict to the team-lead/supervisor rather than commit to either path unilaterally). **Recommendation for Round 20 / supervisor disposition**: re-dispatch S2-prime once supervisor rules on (1) §2.2-vs-Q25.A canonical target shape (and either authorizes Q25.A supersession or amends §2.2's prescription to match Q25.A), (2) TemporalData 7-variant disposition (newtype-wrappers-for-3 keeping legacy carrier for the other 4, vs full extraction-into-3-data-structs), (3) BigInt payload-width at landing (thin i64-wrapper, deferred-from-S2, or external crate dependency). Pre-dispatch audit-document amendments (analogous to the team-lead's pre-S1.5 audit pattern from R18 close) would land the supervisor's rulings as named §-citations on the audit doc + ADR-006 + AGENTS.md sub-cluster row before S2-prime dispatches mechanical work. Following that ratification the S2-prime scope (per-variant producer migration + Obj carrier creation + HeapElement trait or per-T `drop_array` specialization + 4-table lockstep cascade) is plausibly ~2 sessions of mechanical work as audit §3.2 originally estimated. | cluster-0 Round 19 S2 | `AGENTS.md` (this row) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection to be appended in same commit) — zero source changes | blocked (surface-and-stop — 3 structural obstacles surfaced for supervisor disposition: §2.2-vs-Q25.A target-shape conflict, TemporalData 7-variant disposition for O-1 newtype path, BigInt non-existence at HEAD; per-element retain/release ABI gap noted as in-scope-resolvable once §2.2-vs-Q25.A is decided; 0 of 7 named variants migrated, 0 Obj carriers landed, 0 silent refcount leaks, 0 Bool-default fallbacks) | 2026-05-14 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-map-chained-option-b` | `.claude/worktrees/agent-a05ae404d22c1d5b8` | **AUDIT-ONLY CLOSE 2026-05-13.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 15 W12-map-chained Option B (supervisor-ratified). Phase 1 audit-confirmation surfaced the dispatch prompt's third surface-and-stop trigger: "**the conduit extension's stamp doesn't align with the new producer's carrier**". Phase 2 production NOT performed. **Phase 1 finding (audit doc §1)**: the dispatch prompt's literal Option B prescription (`Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData<T>>) as u64` producer carrier) describes the VM-side `.map` family's current output shape — `int_array_result` at `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/typed_array_methods.rs:135-139` already returns `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData::I64>) as u64` with kind `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`, and `op_new_array` + `op_array_push_local` (the stdlib `vec.shape::map` body's bytecode lowering) produce the same shape. The JIT consumer fast-path at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/v2_array.rs::try_emit_v2_array_method:334-441` (specifically `jit_v2_array_sum_i64(arr: *const TypedArray<i64>)` at `ffi/v2/mod.rs:115-124`) reads the bits as **v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<i64>` flat struct** (`crates/shape-value/src/v2/typed_array.rs:28-44` — `#[repr(C)] { header: HeapHeader, data: *mut T, len: u32, cap: u32 }`), NOT `Arc<TypedArrayData>` (Rust enum with `Arc<TypedBuffer<T>>` variants, layout per `shape-value/src/heap_value.rs:2878-2923`). The two layouts are **structurally distinct**: refcount placement (HeapHeader-inline vs Rust-Arc-at-offset-minus-16), data addressing (inline `*mut T` field vs Arc-wrapped TypedBuffer indirection), enum discriminator placement. Reading `Arc<TypedArrayData::I64>` bits as `TypedArray<i64>` interprets the enum tag + Arc payload as header + data pointer, then dereferences invalid bits — the exact SIGSEGV the predecessor `8354968a` recorded at audit §7.1. **Reading-A (literal prompt) is a no-op on the producer**: the smoke cannot close because the producer already produces what the prompt asks for. **Reading-B (charitable, ADR-006 §2.7.14 Route A — producer migrates to v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<T>`)** is the structurally-coherent close path BUT requires either compiler-side specialization of `vec.shape::map`'s body (change `let mut result = []` to `let mut result: Array<U> = []` so the empty literal lowers to `NewTypedArrayI64`, then propagate the type annotation through monomorphization) OR a new Rust-side method handler that intercepts before the stdlib's `Call(FunctionId)` dispatch and emits a v2-raw carrier. Reading B contradicts ADR-006 §2.3 mandate "`HeapValue::TypedArray(Arc<TypedArrayData>)`" for typed-array HeapValue kinds, and would itself require an ADR-006 §2.3 / §2.7.14 amendment to acknowledge two carrier shapes for `Array<T>` where T is scalar (`*mut TypedArray<T>` UInt64-tagged v2-raw and `Arc<TypedArrayData>` Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)-tagged typed-Arc). **Scope-bounded check delivered (audit §§1.1-1.2)**: consumers bounded to `try_emit_v2_array_method` (one file), producers bounded to stdlib `vec.shape` methods + Rust-side `array_transform::handle_*_v2` (~10 sites). The third trigger (conduit-extension/producer alignment) is the load-bearing surface-and-stop. **Recommendation (audit §3) for Round 16 / supervisor disposition**: mirror the W17 audit `8ae56222` Option γ scope-split pattern — Round 16 dispatches either B' (ADR-006 §2.3 amendment to unify carrier shapes, mirror of W17 β.1) or B'' (producer migration to v2-raw + ADR amendment to acknowledge dual carrier shapes for scalar Array<T>). Producer/consumer fast-path mismatch is now a **3-instance class** (W12-map-chained + W12-jit-string-carrier-unification's TypedObject surface + W17-typed-object-arc) — CLAUDE.md amendment candidate per the supervisor's 2026-05-13 Round-14-close recurrent-pattern note. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no Option A (consumer-side narrowing) recommendation, no Option C (both) recommendation, no defensive fallback (Option A.1 producer_kind tag track), no bool-default for unproven `.map()` return-shape kind, no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for any layer, no "preserve mismatched carrier for one edge case" framing, no `ValueWord` resurrection, no stashed WIP (`stash@{0}` `4ddd1bfb`) resurrection (the predecessor branch's WIP stash stays stashed per dispatch). Zero source changes. Smoke 2 baseline state (audit §0 verification): VM prints `30`; JIT errors `Route A surface-and-stop: NotImplemented(SURFACE) — print Call-terminator operand NativeKind is None` (pre-conduit-extension surface; the conduit extension at `8354968a` was audit-only-closed with code stashed, so the SIGSEGV surface from `8354968a` §7 is not currently reachable on the baseline). Audit doc at `docs/cluster-audits/w12-map-chained-option-b-audit.md`. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim**: Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 15 W12-map-chained Option B (producer-side carrier alignment, supervisor-ratified). Migrate the VM-side `.map` and its parametric companions (`.filter`, `.flatMap`, `.sort`, `.reverse`, `.slice`, `.take`, `.skip`, `.concat`) to construct results directly as raw `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData<T>>) as u64` carriers when the input is a typed-array — matching the consumer-side JIT fast-path expectation in `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/v2_array.rs::try_emit_v2_array_method`. Phase 1: audit-confirmation-only (consumer enumeration, producer enumeration, no unexpected ripple to other typed-array variants, no unexpected ripple to non-typed-array consumers, conduit-extension alignment at `helpers.rs:494`). Phase 2: production. Refuse on sight: Option A / Option C / bool-default / stashed-WIP resurrection / bridge-family framing / preserve-mismatched-carrier framing / ValueWord resurrection. Stop and surface to team lead if scope unbounded. | cluster-0 Round 15 | `docs/cluster-audits/w12-map-chained-option-b-audit.md` (NEW audit doc) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection appended) | closed (audit-only — dispatch prompt's literal Option B prescription is a no-op on the producer; charitable Reading B requires ADR-006 §2.3 / §2.7.14 amendment) | 2026-05-13 |

Drop/Clone arms added in the inline-scalar group of `kinded_slot.rs` (both Drop and Clone), `vm_impl/stack.rs::clone_with_kind` + `::drop_with_kind`, and `v2/closure_layout.rs::SharedCell::drop`. **Cross-crate consumer cascade — 22 exhaustive-pattern sites updated** with parallel `NativeKind::Float32` / `NativeKind::Char` arms (Rust pattern-match exhaustivity demanded it; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/vm.out 2>&1` SCRIPT_EXIT=0, **Passed: 12 / Failed: 0**; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no U64 NativeKind extension (refused on sight per supervisor's Shape D out-of-scope disposition); no parametric `NativeKind::Float32(precision)` / `NativeKind::Char(encoding)` variants (refused per `Ptr(HeapKind)` docstring watchlist); no Bool-default for unproven kind during F32/Char cascade (consumer arms added exhaustively); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the NativeKind extension (described by name: "scalar NativeKind addition" / "F32 + Char scalar variants"; by deletion-fate: "the pre-amendment `Ptr(HeapKind::Char)` carrier label remains in source for direct-push call-sites with retirement scoped to a follow-up cluster-1 hardening sub-cluster"); no §2.7.24 Q25.A amendment text touched (S5's commit); no §2.7.22 Q23 amendment touched (R18 S3's commit already landed at `3eb53290`); no audit-doc clarification deferred to a separate edit (folded into this same close commit per dispatch); no `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer (user preference per MEMORY.md); no blame "pre-existing" (own all code quality). **Decisions beyond audit prescription**: (a) cross-tier compatibility for `KindedSlot::as_char` — accessor recognizes BOTH `NativeKind::Char` (new scalar label) AND `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::Char)` (pre-amendment carrier label) for the bounded migration scope; the alternative (migrate all 32 existing `Ptr(HeapKind::Char)` cross-crate consumer sites in this same dispatch) would exceed the ~100-site ceiling and exit the dispatch's "F32 + Char only" scope. (b) `to_field_kind(F32)` / `to_field_kind(Char)` map to `FieldKind::U32` (bit-equivalent 4-byte storage; struct-layout typing of F32/Char is a follow-up sub-cluster if it becomes load-bearing — FieldKind cardinality extension is OUT OF SCOPE for this dispatch). Cascade landed within the ~50-100 site estimate (~22 sites actual; the 32 unmigrated `Ptr(HeapKind::Char)` arms in cross-crate consumers are explicitly preserved per cross-tier compatibility decision (a) above, not silently migrated). | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w17-narrow-followup-b-beta` | `../shape-w17-narrow-followup-b-beta` | **CLOSED-WITH-SURFACE 2026-05-14.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 19 sub-cluster C — W17-narrow-follow-up-B-β (β disposition from R16 dispatch options α/β/γ; γ.3 disproven by R18 close — Smoke 3 JIT still segfaults via the null-handling gap after W17-narrow-follow-up-A landed). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 9135a8a6`. **Fix shape (1 file, +36 LoC, no deletions)**: filter TAG_NULL at the JIT-producer site BEFORE the slot reaches `shape-vm`'s `format_kinded_inner`. (1) New `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs::is_jit_null_sentinel(bits: u64) -> bool` — single DRY helper delegating to `super::value_ffi::is_none_tag` (the existing predicate at `value_ffi.rs:417` returning `bits == TAG_NULL` where `TAG_NULL = make_tagged(TAG_NONE_BITS, 0)`). Bounded mechanical realization of the §2.7.5 producer-site discipline at the kinded print FFI boundary: the kind label says what the slot SHOULD carry (`Arc<T>` typed-Arc carrier per §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time), `is_none_tag` says what the bits ACTUALLY are; the filter early-returns only when bits-don't-match-kind-expectation. (2) `print_kinded_inner` — checks `is_jit_null_sentinel(bits)` BEFORE constructing the `KindedSlot`; on hit, prints "None" and returns. Single call site by construction — all four `jit_print_<heap_arm>` bodies (`jit_print_str` / `jit_print_typed_object` / `jit_print_option` / `jit_print_result`) route through `print_kinded_inner`, so the DRY discipline is naturally satisfied by intercepting at the inner helper. No per-call-site bit-pattern duplication. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no synthesis of TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE_BITS predicates locally (used the existing `is_none_tag` at `value_ffi.rs:417`); no per-call-site bit-pattern duplication (single helper, single call site); no kind-blind decode of tagged sentinels (the filter respects the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind track — kind label says what the slot SHOULD carry, `is_none_tag` says what the bits ACTUALLY are, the filter only fires when these disagree); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing (described as "TAG_NULL early-return at JIT print path"); no α disposition (in-crate to shape-jit, not moving predicates to shape-value); no γ.1/γ.2 root-cause work at `jit_call_method` (out of β scope); no ValueWord resurrection; no Bool-default fallback. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` SCRIPT_EXIT=0, 12/12 PASS; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. **Smoke 3 status post-fix**: under `--mode vm` prints `x` ✓; under `--mode jit` prints `None` (NOT `x`) exit 0. **Cluster-0 close criterion NOT met** — the dispatch's named STOP condition reached: *"If Smoke 3 JIT prints 'None' instead of `x` post-fix (i.e., the TAG_NULL filter fires but the upstream dispatch is reaching the fallthrough when it shouldn't), STOP and surface — that's an upstream regression caught at the print path, NOT in β scope."* **Finding**: the filter is firing — meaning the upstream JIT method-dispatch shell (`jit_call_method`) is reaching the TAG_NULL fallthrough for `t.name()` on a `dyn T = X {}` receiver and returning `TAG_NULL` paired with the call's expected return kind (`NativeKind::String`) stamped on the §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind track. The filter correctly intercepts the §2.7.5 invariant violation at the print boundary (kind label says "Arc<String>", bits carry TAG_NULL — `Arc::from_raw` on this would segfault) and produces the documented `format_kinded_inner` null-handling fallback per `printing.rs:154-163`. But the upstream classification reach-of-fallthrough is the γ-disposition territory (R16 disposition shapes γ.1 surface-and-stop / γ.2 missing-dispatch kind variant / γ.3 follow-up-A pre-emptive fix — γ.3 disproven by R18 close, so γ.1 or γ.2 remains the principal load-bearing fix). **Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end (printing `x` matching VM) is now gated on γ-class root-cause fix at `jit_call_method`** — even with follow-up-A's schema-id MIR threading landed at `5fb42b1c`, the UFCS lookup for `X::name` is missing post-schema-resolve (likely a separate gap in `try_call_user_method`'s function_indices lookup, or in trait-method dispatch for `dyn T` receivers where the schema-resolved type name doesn't match the impl-method-registered name). Surface-and-stop disposition per the dispatch's explicit β-scope boundary. **Original dispatch text preserved verbatim**: Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 19 sub-cluster C — W17-narrow-follow-up-B-β. Filter TAG_NULL at the JIT-producer site, BEFORE the slot reaches shape-vm. Primary territory: `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs::print_kinded_inner` (or each `jit_print_*` body in that file) checks `is_none_tag(bits)` (predicate already at `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs:87-124`) BEFORE constructing the `KindedSlot`. If `is_none_tag(bits)` is true, print "None" directly and return; else proceed with the kinded-slot construction. DRY discipline: extract a single helper (e.g., `is_jit_null_sentinel(bits: u64) -> bool { is_none_tag(bits) }`) within shape-jit and use it at every JIT-producer-side print call site. Do NOT duplicate the bit-pattern check per call site. Refuse on sight: synthesizing TAG_NULL / TAG_NONE_BITS predicates locally if `is_none_tag` is missing; per-call-site bit-pattern duplication; kind-blind decode of the tagged sentinels; bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing; "preserve segfault for one edge case" framing; disposition α (move predicates to shape-value); disposition γ.1/γ.2 (root-cause fix at `jit_call_method` TAG_NULL fallthrough — out of β scope); ValueWord resurrection. | cluster-0 Round 19 | `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs` (+36 LoC: `is_jit_null_sentinel` helper + early-return in `print_kinded_inner`) | closed-with-surface (β-disposition fix landed; Smoke 3 JIT prints `None` instead of segfaulting — the dispatch's named STOP condition reached; γ-class root-cause fix at `jit_call_method`'s TAG_NULL fallthrough remains the principal load-bearing fix for Smoke 3 JIT end-to-end `x`-matching-VM, surfaced for supervisor disposition) | 2026-05-14 |

Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 3fe3ffa9` (post-R20 S2-prime audit-only merge + R20 status-doc close commit). **Smoke 3 JIT unblocked — cluster-0 close criterion met:** `--mode jit` now returns `x` matching VM (was `None` pre-γ; was SIGSEGV pre-R19-C-β). **Audit findings (file:line)**: (1) W17-narrow R15 classification IS correct — `SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1` trace confirms `receiver_kind=Ptr(TypedObject)` flows through `jit_call_method` at `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/call_method/mod.rs:507-525`; (2) `receiver_type_name` at `mod.rs:115-145` correctly resolves the TypedObject schema-id via `jit_typed_object_schema_id` then the global stdlib registry then the trampoline VM's per-program registry — returns `"X"` for the user-defined type; (3) UFCS name construction at `mod.rs:285` correctly assembles `"X::name"`; (4) function compilation IS correct — `[jit-mir] func[194]='X::name' jit_compat=true has_mir=true ... Compiled function 'X::name' via MirToIR` per trace; (5) **the gap is layer (c) — JITContext function-name table never linked**: `JITContext.function_names_ptr` is initialized to `std::ptr::null()` at `crates/shape-jit/src/context.rs:611` and **no producer site anywhere in the codebase populates it** (grep `function_names_ptr\s*=` across `crates/` + `bin/` returns only the one initializer site). The consumer at `find_function_by_name` (`call_method/mod.rs:229-242`) early-returns `None` on null `function_names_ptr` — every UFCS dispatch falls through. **Production fix** (1 file, +33 LoC): linked `function_names_ptr` + `function_names_len` in `crates/shape-jit/src/executor.rs:189-221` immediately after the existing `function_table` linking block. Built a stable `Vec<String> = bytecode.functions.iter().map(|f| f.name.clone()).collect()` in scope `function_names_storage` paralleling the `function_table` slot ordering (`compile_program_selective`'s loop at `compiler/program.rs:800-823` populates both by `program.functions` index 1:1 — `function_names[idx]` describes the same function as `function_table[idx]`). Same lifetime discipline as the function-table borrow + trampoline VM lifecycle: storage lives until `jit_fn` completes and is dropped after execution. No new ABI surface; no kind-aware dispatch changes; no Bool-default fallback; no §-cite gap; cross-tier symmetry preserved (VM-side method dispatch unchanged; JIT-side now resolves UFCS via the same `Function.name` strings the VM uses). No 4-table lockstep changes (no new HeapKind variant — TraitObject ord 29 is reserved per ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.C; this fix operates at the existing TypedObject path). The β filter at `print_kinded_inner` (R19 C close, commit `9bf2cf35`) preserved as defensive guard. **Refuse-on-sight discipline preserved**: no ValueWord resurrection; no Bool-default fallback (`receiver_kind` flows through from §2.7.7/Q9 parallel-kind track at `jit_call_method`'s dispatch entry per W17-narrow R15); no Convert<X>To<Y> opcode added; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the fix (described by name: "JIT function-name table linkage in executor.rs paralleling function-table linkage" / by deletion-fate: "the missing linkage between `JITContext.function_names_ptr` and `bytecode.functions[*].name`"); no tag-bit decode at FFI boundary (the linkage is a `Vec<String>::as_ptr()` analogous to the function_table linkage; no NaN-box / heap-prefix probing); no resurrection of deleted shape under renamed alias; no 5-arm receiver-recovery violation (no `Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` recovery needed — the live Smoke 3 path uses TypedObject, not the §2.7.24 Q25.C TraitObject rebuild territory). **Architectural note for supervisor visibility**: Smoke 3's `let t = X{}` is a plain TypedObject binding (NOT `let t: dyn T = box(X{})` as the dispatch text described); the JIT-side method dispatch for `t.name()` flows through the TypedObject schema-id path, NOT the §2.7.24 Q25.C `HeapKind::TraitObject` carrier. The §2.7.24 Q25.C TraitObject rebuild (W17-typed-carrier-monomorphization, ord 29 reserved) remains the future-work territory for true `dyn T` receivers with `Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` carriers; γ closes the cluster-0 close criterion via the existing TypedObject + UFCS-by-name path, which is the path the bytecode compiler emits today for this smoke shape. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper): `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` SCRIPT_EXIT=0, **12/12 PASS**; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib` 383 passed / 0 failed / 26 ignored. **Smoke matrix re-verification (release binary)**: Smoke 1 VM=4950/JIT=4950 ✓; Smoke 2 VM=30 ✓ / JIT pre-existing R14 conduit blocker (unchanged); **Smoke 3 VM=`x` / JIT=`x` ✓ (was None pre-γ — the load-bearing cluster-0 close criterion)**; Smoke 4 VM=2/JIT=2 ✓. **NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer.** | cluster-0 Round 20 γ | 2 files — `crates/shape-jit/src/executor.rs` (+33 LoC: function-name table linkage paralleling existing function_table linkage) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection appended in close commit) | closed (production fix landed; Smoke 3 JIT returns `x` matching VM; cluster-0 close criterion for Smoke 3 met) | 2026-05-14 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration-prime` | `../shape-w12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration-prime` | **AUDIT-FIRST CLOSE 2026-05-14.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 20 S2-prime sub-cluster — W12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration REOPEN with supervisor's R19 partial dispositions (Q25.A SUPERSEDED amendment scope; TemporalData 7-variant audit-first; BigInt defer). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 7e95069f` (post-R19-close handover-doc finalization). **Audit-first close — all 4 audit-first deliverables (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) landed; production migration NOT performed in this dispatch per the audit's surface-and-stop discipline.** **Deliverable (a) — TemporalData variant classification (commit `9d18c937`, audit doc §4.1.A inline)**: ground-truths supervisor's R19 prediction against actual source at HEAD `7e95069f`. **Refines the prediction in two structurally important ways**: (1) Only 2 of 7 TemporalData variants are user-facing (DateTime + TimeSpan), not 3. `TemporalData::Duration(shape_ast::ast::Duration)` is a dead enum variant with zero constructors anywhere in source — the user-facing Shape "duration" type lowers via `ast_duration_to_chrono` to `chrono::Duration` wrapped in `TemporalData::TimeSpan(chrono::Duration)`, NOT in `TemporalData::Duration`. The `Duration` enum variant is an architectural leftover from a pre-bulldozer design. (2) 4 of 6 R19-S2-named `TypedArrayData` heap-element variants (DateTime, Timespan, Duration, Instant) have **ZERO root constructors** — they are dead arms post-W17-typed-carrier-bundle-A monomorphization. `build_specialized_from_heap_arcs` at `crates/shape-value/src/heap_value.rs:3060-3093` has no Temporal / Instant arms; `Array<DateTime>` / `Array<Timespan>` / `Array<Duration>` / `Array<Instant>` are unreachable from user code today (zero source references). The genuinely live producer migrations are `String` (multiple root sites — `object_creation.rs:518/799`, `concat.rs:240`, derived sites in `array_transform.rs`) + `Decimal` (multiple root sites — `object_creation.rs:544`, `heap_value.rs:3044` build_specialized arm, `builtins/array_ops.rs:485` filled, derived sites in `array_transform.rs` / `concat.rs:255`). The S2-prime migration of `TypedArrayData::DateTime` / `Timespan` / `Instant` is **migrating dead arms, not load-bearing production work** — structural cleanliness for S5's enum deletion. **Deliverable (b) — Per-element retain/release ABI shape decision (commit `dd862ee8`, audit doc §4.1.B inline)**: **decided Option (a) — `unsafe trait HeapElement { unsafe fn release_elem(*const Self); }` per-T monomorphized dispatch.** Each `<X>Obj` implements the trait with its own release body calling `v2_release(self.header)` + the per-T deallocator on return-true. Option (b) (per-T `TypedArray<T>` impl specialization) refused due to Rust coherence collision with the blanket `impl<T: Copy> TypedArray<T>::drop_array`; workarounds (rename / newtype-per-kind) multiply API surface or break callers. Option (c) (runtime kind discriminator at `drop_array`) refused — re-introduces the W10-misc-deleted §2.7.7 #4 / #7 runtime-element-kind dispatch pattern. The architectural cleanliness of v2-raw `TypedArray<T>` is "T is monomorphized at compile time; no runtime dispatch on element kind." **Rationale**: discipline-coherent with ADR-006 §2.7.5 (stamp at compile time) + ADR-005 §1 (single-discriminator — `HeapElement` is structural trait, not a parallel sum type) + §2.7.6 / Q8 carrier-API bound (trait method takes `*const Self`, no NativeKind parameter); audit §4.3 O-3.b objection ("per-T retain/release dispatch perpetuates Arc-vs-HeapHeader duality") scoped correctly — that objection applies to TypedObject/TraitObject (Arc-wrapped, no HeapHeader); for uniformly HeapHeader-equipped `<X>Obj` carriers, every implementor has same HeapHeader-at-offset-0 contract. **Forbidden-pattern check passed**: `HeapElement` is structural (describes "this T lives on v2-raw heap"), not defection-attractor (no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing). Forbidden post-decision: HeapElement rename via defection-attractor framing; `release_elem` taking NativeKind parameter; Bool-default in release_elem body; HeapElement for non-HeapHeader-equipped types (compile error at field access). **Deliverable (c) — Q25.A SUPERSEDED amendment text landed (commit `71162daf`, ADR-006 §2.7.24 inline)**: refines the team-lead handover's draft amendment text shape against actual ADR-006 + Q25.A text + the R20 audit-first deliverables (a) and (b). Adds a SUPERSEDED preamble subsection at the top of Q25.A (line 4715+ pre-amendment) with: authority cite (strategic-owner authorization 2026-05-13 + supervisor R19 partial disposition 2026-05-14); canonical replacement target (§2.2 of W12 deletion audit doc); per-variant migration shape table reflecting audit findings AND R19 dispositions (Decimal: migrates per §2.2; BigInt: DEFERRED to cluster-1+; DateTime/Timespan/Instant: dead arms per §4.1.A.2 — migrate for forward-S5 cleanliness; Duration: NO MIGRATION — dead enum variant per §4.1.A.1; Char: out of S2-prime scope per R19 S1.5; TypedObject/TraitObject: S3 territory, gated on O-3/O-3a); 4 forbidden-post-supersession entries (resurrection / preserve-fallback / mixed-migration / Q25.A draft-text-payload-type-name regression); Q25.B / Q25.C explicitly NOT superseded; migration cadence (S2-prime + S5). Original Q25.A ratification body RENAMED to "Q25.A (Phase 2d original ratification, 2026-05-11, SUPERSEDED)" and PRESERVED for historical provenance. **Deliverable (d) — Per-variant `<X>Obj` carrier shape design (commit `9543431c`, audit doc §4.1.C cross-reference + §4.1.D inline)**: per-variant carrier shape designs for DecimalObj / DateTimeObj / TimespanObj / InstantObj (mirror StringObj precedent — 24-byte HeapHeader + 16-byte inline payload + repr(C) + new/value/drop + size assertion + `unsafe impl HeapElement`). DurationObj REFUSED per §4.1.A.1 dead-variant finding (Disposition D-1: skip entirely; the `TypedArrayData::Duration` arm stays in place; S5 deletes alongside enum; cluster-1+ language-design cleanup for the "duration" type's runtime backing). BigIntObj REFUSED per Obstacle 3 R19 defer (BigInt type design out of cluster-0 scope). New `HEAP_KIND_V2_*` constants for Decimal / DateTime / Timespan / Instant; these are HeapHeader.kind field values (u16), distinct from the HeapKind enum (which preserves ADR-005 §1 single-discriminator cardinality bound — no new HeapKind enum variants). Production migration scope: 5 new files (heap_element.rs + 4 Obj files) + extensions to mod.rs / heap_header.rs / typed_array.rs / concrete_type.rs / producer-side bytecode emission + VM/JIT handlers + 4-table lockstep. Estimated ~600-1200 LoC for new carrier infrastructure + ~5-7k LoC for producer-side migration per audit §3.2 ("2 sessions of mechanical work"). **Production migration NOT performed in S2-prime dispatch.** The audit-first deliverables surfaced a structurally significant finding (4 of 6 named variants are dead arms today) that changes the migration's load-bearing scope: from "6 user-facing variant producer migrations" (R19 prediction) to "2 live (String + Decimal) + 3 forward-S5 dead-arm carriers (DateTime + Timespan + Instant)". This affects: (i) S2-prime scope decision — minimal (live only) vs comprehensive (live + dead-but-create-forward) — per §4.1.A.5 surfaced for supervisor disposition; (ii) Duration disposition — §4.1.D.5 D-1 (skip entirely) recommended but affects cluster-1+ language-design cleanup; (iii) R20 sequencing — if S2-prime ships minimal-scope (String + Decimal only), S5 can dispatch sooner; if comprehensive, S5 waits longer. Per the dispatch prompt: "If your audit finds the classification differs from the prediction OR surfaces a deeper structural issue ... surface-and-stop with audit-only close. Same R19 S2 pattern. Don't paper over." **Surface-and-stop discipline preserved** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns"): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind; no silent per-element refcount leak; no preservation of `TypedArrayData::<heap-element>` arms via "documented exception" framing (the supersession amendment landed cleanly at deliverable (c)); no shared `Arc<TemporalData>` carrier with semantic-kind tag (refused on sight per O-1.b newtype ruling — §4.1.D.3 TimespanObj wraps `chrono::Duration` directly); no "preserve fallback for one period" framing (the supersession is binding for cluster-0 itself — see Q25.A SUPERSEDED forbidden entry #2); no Char inclusion (S1.5 scalar bucket territory); no addition to S5's territory beyond the supersession amendment (which is part of S2-prime's audit-first scope per dispatch); no ValueWord resurrection; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration boundary or for the `HeapElement` trait (named structurally as "trait describing v2-raw HeapHeader-equipped element storage"); no `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer (MEMORY.md user rule); no blame "pre-existing" — the dead-arm finding is owned as S2-prime's audit responsibility surfaced via grep-of-actual-source, not as inherited gap. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper): (1) `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 — verified post-deliverable-(c) ADR-006 edit (doc-only changes preserve workspace cleanliness at baseline `7e95069f`); (2) `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/vm.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` — to be run pre-close as final gate; (3) `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 — verified at baseline `7e95069f` (no source changes). (4) AGENTS.md row appended (this row). (5) NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **Recommendation for Round 20+ / supervisor disposition**: re-dispatch S2-prime-production with explicit scope ratification on (i) minimal-vs-comprehensive scope (per §4.1.A.5); (ii) Duration D-1 vs D-2 disposition (§4.1.D.5); (iii) whether the ConcreteType cascade for Timespan + Instant lands as part of S2-prime-production OR splits to an S2-prime-cascade follow-up (mirror of S1.5's ~22-site cascade pattern). The Q25.A SUPERSEDED amendment text + the `HeapElement` trait design + the per-`<X>Obj` carrier shape are all ratified by these audit deliverables; the next dispatch consumes them as the architectural foundation, not as further deliberation territory. | cluster-0 Round 20 S2-prime | 2 files — `docs/cluster-audits/w12-typed-array-data-deletion-audit.md` (§4.1.A / §4.1.B / §4.1.C / §4.1.D inline audit deliverables) + `docs/adr/006-value-and-memory-model.md` (§2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED amendment text); plus `AGENTS.md` (this row) + `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-cluster-0-status.md` (close subsection appended in close commit) — zero source changes | closed (audit-first — 4 deliverables (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) landed; dead-arm finding surfaced; production migration scope refined; surface-and-stop for supervisor disposition on minimal-vs-comprehensive S2-prime-production scope + Duration D-1/D-2 disposition + ConcreteType-cascade-fold decisions) | 2026-05-14 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w12-typed-array-data-s2-prime-production` | `../shape-w12-typed-array-data-s2-prime-production` | **INFRASTRUCTURE-LANDED + SURFACE-AND-STOP CLOSE 2026-05-14.** Phase 3 cluster-0 Round 20 S2-prime-production sub-cluster — W12-typed-array-data-heap-element-migration production migration consuming R20 supervisor's partial dispositions ((A) Minimal scope for §4.1.A.5; (D-1) Skip for §4.1.D.5 DurationObj; MOOT under (A) for §4.1.D.7 ConcreteType cascade). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 3fe3ffa9` (post-R20 S2-prime audit-first merge `64a61338` + status-doc R20 close subsection + Smoke 4 typo fix). **Infrastructure landed (audit deliverables (b) + (d) implementations); production producer-side migration NOT performed per surface-and-stop discipline.** **Infrastructure landed (5 file changes + 2 new files)**: (1) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_element.rs` — NEW module (~80 LoC including doc-comment forbidden-pattern recap). `unsafe trait HeapElement { unsafe fn release_elem(*const Self); }` per audit §4.1.B decision. Trait constrains `T: HeapElement` for `TypedArray<*const T>` heap-element instantiations; per-T release dispatch monomorphized at compile time via Rust type system — no runtime NativeKind probe, no `is_heap()` probe, no Bool-default fallback. (2) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/decimal_obj.rs` — NEW module (~200 LoC including 10 tests). `DecimalObj` is `#[repr(C)]` 24-byte struct (HeapHeader 8 + inline `rust_decimal::Decimal` 16) with `new` / `value` / `drop` + size assertion + `OFFSET_VALUE = 8` for JIT codegen + `unsafe impl HeapElement for DecimalObj` calling `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true. Compile-time size assertion: `size_of::<DecimalObj>() == 24` && `align_of::<DecimalObj>() == 4` (rust_decimal::Decimal is 16 bytes with 4-byte alignment — measured at HEAD via standalone repro under `/tmp/dec_check`; HeapHeader is 8 bytes with 4-byte alignment — `AtomicU32(4) + u16(2) + u8(1) + u8(1)`). 10 tests landed: `test_size_of_decimal_obj`, `test_create_and_read_decimal`, `test_create_zero_decimal`, `test_create_max_decimal`, `test_drop_does_not_leak`, `test_field_offsets`, `test_refcount_starts_at_one`, `test_refcount_retain_release`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_to_zero`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_held_share`. All pass. (3) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs` — `unsafe impl HeapElement for StringObj` added (calls `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true, mirroring DecimalObj). The 9 existing StringObj tests preserved untouched. (4) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/typed_array.rs` — `impl<T: HeapElement> TypedArray<*const T>` block added with `unsafe fn drop_array_heap(ptr: *mut Self)` per audit §4.1.B.4 migration recipe. Body walks per-element pointers, calls `T::release_elem(elem_ptr)`, then frees the data buffer (`Layout::array::<*const T>(cap)`) + the TypedArray struct (`Layout::new::<Self>()`). Original POD-element `drop_array` (`impl<T: Copy>`) untouched — callers pick at compile time. 4 new tests added: `test_drop_array_heap_string_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_decimal_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_empty`, `test_drop_array_heap_with_held_share`. All pass. (5) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_header.rs` — `HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL = 85` constant appended; provenance comment cites ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + audit §4.1.D.1. Added to `test_kind_constants_are_distinct` test. (6) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/mod.rs` — register `pub mod decimal_obj;` + `pub mod heap_element;`. **All 175 v2-tier tests pass** (existing 161 + 10 new DecimalObj + 4 new TypedArray drop_array_heap tests). **`cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0, Passed: 12 / Failed: 0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.** **Production producer migration NOT performed.** **Reason**: the actual producer migration scope is structurally larger than a single sub-cluster session can land cleanly. Concrete grep of HEAD source: `grep -rn 'TypedArrayData::(String|Decimal)' crates/ --include='*.rs' | wc -l` returns **147 references across 35 files**. Of these, **~25 sites are construction sites** (`TypedArrayData::String(Arc::new(...))` / `TypedArrayData::Decimal(Arc::new(...))`) and **~120 sites are consumer match arms** that today receive `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` + `Arc<TypedArrayData::String|Decimal>` shape from those producers. The dispatch directive named the canonical producer sites (`object_creation.rs:518/799/544`, `concat.rs:240/255`, `heap_value.rs:3044` `build_specialized_from_heap_arcs`, `builtins/array_ops.rs:485` filled, `array_transform.rs:567/732/983/1460`); but the actual grep finds **additional producer sites the dispatch list missed**: `string_methods.rs:414/604` (`split` returning `Array<string>`), `array_ops.rs:262/304/467/676/696` (filtered/mapped/filled/empty/clone), `array_sort.rs:120` (sorted), `array_transform.rs:544/552/717/721/834/969/971/1093` (multiple derived ops), `array_basic.rs:259/324/425` (multiple). Migrating only the named root sites without the additional derived sites produces **partial mixed-carrier reality at runtime** — some producers push v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<*const StringObj/DecimalObj>` shape (under `NativeKind::UInt64` per the existing F64/I64/I32/Bool v2-raw convention at `v2_array_detect.rs`); other producers push legacy `Arc<TypedArrayData::String/Decimal>` shape (under `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`). This is exactly the parallel-implementation-defection-attractor pattern CLAUDE.md "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" refuses and that ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED §4 #3 forbidden entry refuses ("Mixed-migration shape preserving some Q25.A specialized variants while migrating others to v2-raw `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` — the migration is single-target ... deviations require a separate ADR amendment"). **Additional architectural prerequisites surfaced**: (i) **No `NewTypedArrayString` / `NewTypedArrayDecimal` opcodes exist at HEAD** — the compiler at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/typed_emission.rs::TypedArrayKind` enumerates only `F64 / I64 / I32 / Bool`, and `should_use_typed_array` returns `None` for all other element types (including `ConcreteType::String` / `ConcreteType::Decimal`). For `Array<string>` and `Array<decimal>` literals the compiler emits the legacy `OpCode::NewArray` (which routes to `op_new_array` → `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal(Arc::new(TypedBuffer::from_vec(...)))`), NOT a v2-raw allocator opcode. Migrating the producer to v2-raw requires either (a) adding 8 new opcodes (`NewTypedArrayString`, `NewTypedArrayDecimal`, `TypedArrayGetString`, `TypedArrayGetDecimal`, `TypedArrayPushString`, `TypedArrayPushDecimal`, `TypedArraySetString`, `TypedArraySetDecimal`) + compiler emission via `TypedArrayKind::String` / `Decimal` variants + executor handlers + 4-table lockstep (`v2_handlers/array.rs` allocator handlers, `v2_handlers/v2_array_detect.rs` ElemType discriminants and read/write/push/pop dispatch arms), or (b) reworking `op_new_array`'s String/Decimal arms to push v2-raw shape (which breaks every existing consumer expecting `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` for that element type). Both routes have lockstep dependencies across the consumer side: `v2_array_detect.rs::V2TypedArrayView` + `read_element` / `write_element` / `push_element` / `pop_element` + `sum_elements` need `V2ElemType::String` / `V2ElemType::Decimal` variants AND the per-T retain/release plumbing at read time (each read of a `*const StringObj` element bumps the refcount before pushing the pointer onto the stack as `NativeKind::String` with `Arc<String>` bits — but the v2-raw element is NOT an `Arc<String>`, it's a `*const StringObj`; the cross-tier shape conversion at the array-element boundary needs ADR-level ratification because it's a new carrier-shape conversion not covered by existing ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 dispatch tables). (ii) **The existing `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` consumer dispatch tables have ~30 sites across `printing.rs`, `arithmetic/mod.rs`, `comparison/mod.rs`, `typed_access.rs`, `objects/mod.rs`, `array_aggregation.rs`, `control_flow/mod.rs`, JIT layer** — each consumer assumes the slot bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` and reconstructs the Arc to dispatch. Adding v2-raw String/Decimal arms requires every consumer to discriminate between the two carrier shapes at the slot-bits layer, which is itself the §2.7.14 `UnifiedArray`-on-heap kind-byte pattern in a different framing if done via header.kind probe at the consumer (instead of via the producing-call-signature stamp that the typed-opcode approach uses). The clean disposition is to add separate typed opcodes (route (a) above) and let the compiler stamp the carrier shape via the producing opcode — but that's the multi-opcode-addition route gated on adding 8 opcodes + cascading compiler emission + 4-table lockstep + the cross-tier shape-conversion ADR. (iii) **VM Decimal SIGSEGV at baseline** confirmed via baseline binary at `/home/dev/dev/shape-lang/shape/target/release/shape`: `let xs: Array<decimal> = [1.0d, 2.0d, 3.0d]; print(xs[0])` segfaults in VM mode (RC=139, "Segmentation fault (core dumped)") on baseline `3fe3ffa9` — independent of this sub-cluster's changes. The SIGSEGV is in the legacy `TypedArrayData::Decimal` consumer path (likely the v2-raw-heap aliasing class CLAUDE.md "v2-raw-heap aliasing class — 4 simulation tests deferred" describes). Migrating the producer to v2-raw with HeapElement retain/release at element-read time could either fix or worsen this depending on whether the consumer path's drop-glue accounts for v2-raw shape. Pre-existing SIGSEGV is owned as part of S2-prime-production's audit responsibility (per MEMORY.md "Don't blame pre-existing"); fixing it is non-trivial because it requires the full producer migration to land cleanly. **JIT String pre-existing W11-jit-new-array kind-source gap** also confirmed at baseline: `let xs: Array<string> = ["a","b","c"]; print(xs[0])` JIT-fails with "Route A surface-and-stop: SURFACE — Rvalue::Aggregate reached the kind-blind fallback ... Tracked as W11-jit-new-array per phase-3-kickoff-prompt.md. ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5." This is the parallel R20 γ sub-cluster's territory (W12-jit-trait-impl-method-registry per R20 dispatch shape), not S2-prime-production's. **`#[deprecated]` annotations NOT added** in this dispatch — adding `#[deprecated]` on `TypedArrayData::String` / `Decimal` enum arms at `heap_value.rs:2960/2967` per audit §3.6 deprecation cadence would surface ~147 deprecation warnings across 35 files (one per construction or match arm site). That is noise, not signal, until producer migration is complete enough that the remaining `#[deprecated]` warnings count as the deletion-track to S5. **Surface-and-stop discipline preserved** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" + "Renames to refuse on sight" + supervisor R20 partial dispositions): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind; no silent per-element refcount leak; no preservation of `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal` arms via "documented exception" framing (the supersession amendment landed cleanly at R20 (c)); no mixed-migration shape (refused per Q25.A SUPERSEDED forbidden #3); no DateTime/Timespan/Duration/Instant arm migration ((A) Minimal scope binding — "If you find yourself reasoning 'while I'm here I might as well migrate the dead arms for forward-S5 cleanliness', STOP — that's the (B) Comprehensive scope the supervisor refused"); no ValueWord resurrection; no `Convert<X>To<Y>` opcodes; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration boundary or for the `HeapElement` trait (named structurally as "trait describing v2-raw HeapHeader-equipped element storage" per audit §4.1.B.3 forbidden-pattern check). The infrastructure (HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + StringObj HeapElement impl + TypedArray::drop_array_heap + HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL) is **the ratified architectural foundation** for the production migration; no further deliberation territory on the carrier shape itself. Producer sites at the 29 enumerated locations: VERIFIED present at HEAD via per-file grep (object_creation.rs:518/544/799 [3], concat.rs:240/255 [2], string_methods.rs:414/599 [2], array_sets.rs:96/256 [2], array_transform.rs:599/618/746/757/959/970/1058/1408/1425 [9 — note slightly different line numbers vs kickoff prediction of 581/596/720/731/935/947/1032/1382/1399 due to A2 Round 2's drive-by edits], builtins/array_ops.rs:251/293/451/469/655/675 [6], iterator_methods.rs:933 [1], set_methods.rs:218 [1], datatable_methods/core.rs:82 [1], comptime_target.rs:254 [1], marshal.rs:552 [1], heap_value.rs:3135/3149 [2 — kickoff predicted 2985/2999, off by ~150 lines due to A2 Round 2's drive-by edits] = 31 total producer-construction-site locations under the 29-predicted threshold by exact count, but covering 29 expressions across the line set). A2 Round 2 architectural foundation verified present: 8 typed opcodes (NewTypedArrayString/Decimal + Get/Push/Set in opcode_defs.rs:689-704 + dispatch.rs:927-934), TypedArrayKind::String/Decimal variants in v2_typed_emission.rs:79-84/103-105/124-126/145-147/166-168, V2ElemType::String/Decimal arms in v2_array_detect.rs:114-115/136-137/372-382/487-501/607-619/685-691/762-763, A1 F32/Char drive-by routing fix preserved. Agent B's NativeKind::StringV2/DecimalV2 variants verified present (objects/mod.rs:549/552 method-registry routing maps StringV2→STRING_METHODS, DecimalV2→NUMBER_METHODS — per A2 Round 2 finding confirmed). R20 S2-prime-production infrastructure verified: HeapElement trait at `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_element.rs:69-79`, StringObj HeapElement impl at `crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs:116-122`, DecimalObj HeapElement impl at `crates/shape-value/src/v2/decimal_obj.rs:94-100`. array_transform.rs::build_specialized's String/Decimal arms confirmed NotImplemented(SURFACE) at `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/array_transform.rs:380-394` with §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED #3 + §4.1.B.3 cite. **STRUCTURAL FINDING (new at Round 3a — extends A2 Round 2's cascade scope diagnosis 1 layer outward)**: the migration's atomic unit is NOT just the 29 producers + ~129 consumer arms. The `should_use_typed_array` gate flip routes Array<string>/Array<decimal> literals to v2-raw — but **every ARRAY_METHODS handler (~48 functions across 6 array-method files) has an entry-point dispatch that materializes its receiver via `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` (in array_transform.rs:138, mirrored across array_aggregation.rs / array_query.rs / array_sets.rs / array_sort.rs / array_basic.rs / array_joins.rs)**. That helper is the canonical v2-raw → Arc<TypedArrayData> materialization path; for scalar element kinds it builds a fresh `Arc<TypedArrayData::I64/F64/Bool/I8/U8/I16/U16/U32/F32/Char>` snapshot of the v2-raw buffer; but for `V2ElemType::String | V2ElemType::Decimal` it already returns `VMError::NotImplemented(SURFACE)` per §4.1.B.3 materialize-on-read forbidden pattern (the explicit refusal landed in A2 Round 2). Post-gate-flip every method-call on an `Array<string>` / `Array<decimal>` literal arrives at `with_typed_array` (or `typed_array_arc_from_kinded`) expecting either a `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` receiver OR a materializable v2-raw view; receives a `NativeKind::UInt64` + ELEM_TYPE_STRING/DECIMAL stamp; the materialize path correctly refuses (per §4.1.B.3); but the result is `arr.slice()` / `arr.first()` / `arr.concat()` / `arr.filter()` / `arr.map()` / etc. **all surface SURFACE-AND-STOP at runtime** for v2-raw String/Decimal arrays. The single-atomic-commit "mechanical" framing in the kickoff under-models this by 1 architectural layer: the gate flip + producer migration + consumer arm cascade landing in lockstep is **necessary but not sufficient** — the cascade ceiling is the method-handler-family count, not the match-arm count. The full landable atomic unit is: (i) gate flip; (ii) 29 producer migrations; (iii) ~129 consumer arm deletions / migrations; (iv) **per-method v2-raw entry-point dispatch in every ARRAY_METHODS handler (~48 handlers × per-handler v2-raw String/Decimal arm) — at minimum a `(receiver_kind, elem_type)` shape-dispatch wrapper for each of the 48 handlers, OR a structural unification of `with_typed_array` / `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` entry points to dispatch the v2-raw String/Decimal element kinds through a non-materializing per-element-iteration path that builds the result in v2-raw shape directly**. Both options require per-method audit of mutation/aliasing semantics (e.g. `arr.slice` on String must produce a v2-raw clone with per-element retain; `arr.concat(other)` must additionally retain elements from `other`; `arr.first` must read+retain a single element and push as `NativeKind::StringV2`; `arr.map(|s| ...)` must allocate a new v2-raw array of unknown element kind based on the closure return shape — same problem class but per-handler-deep). **Scope-extension scale**: 48 handlers × ~30-50 LoC per v2-raw arm = ~1,500-2,500 LoC of new method-handler v2-raw code, ON TOP of the 29 producer + ~129 consumer cascade. Realistic atomic-commit size: **~3-5k LoC** confirms the kickoff estimate, but distributed across the 48-handler entry-point layer NOT the 158-cascade-site layer (the 158 number includes many redundant pattern-arm references that resolve to a handful of canonical helper functions per file; the 48-handler count is the irreducible structural unit). **Why STRUCTURED SURFACE-AND-STOP is the right outcome here (and NOT the W-series declare-victory pattern)**: the W-series pattern is "preserve fallback for one period" — a literal rename of deleted dispatch to a less suspicious name. This close is the opposite shape: it explicitly REFUSES to flip the gate without the per-method v2-raw entry points landed in lockstep, BECAUSE flipping the gate without those entry points produces a system where ALL Array<string>/Array<decimal> method calls SURFACE-AND-STOP at runtime — that's the §4.1.B.3 materialize-on-read forbidden pattern firing under user code, NOT a hidden defection. The architectural surface from A2 Round 2 is preserved; the close adds a 2nd-layer architectural finding (the 48-handler entry-point dispatch ceiling) to the supervisor's information set for Round 3b/4 dispatch shape. The mirror precedents are: D2 Round 2 → D3 cascade-ceiling waiver (supervisor reframed bounded cascade as wholesale-deletion-with-bounded-known-cascade); C2a Round 2 → C2 Round 3b sequential-after-Round-3a (supervisor reframed as joint atomic ~5k LoC commit); A2 Round 2 → this followup (supervisor reframed as mechanical lockstep flip). The honest finding here is: the 48-handler entry-point layer extends the atomic unit by ~1,500-2,500 LoC beyond the cascade-site count, and **per-handler refcount-soundness invariants must hold simultaneously across all 48 — single-author audit feasibility is borderline at this scope without per-handler-family sub-split**. **Recommendation for supervisor (Round 3b/4 dispatch shape)**: split A2-followup-mechanical into per-handler-family atomic sub-commits each landing in lockstep with their consumer arm migrations + a portion of the producer-gate flip — e.g. A2-followup-α covers `array_transform.rs` (slice/concat/take/drop/skip/filter/map/reverse) + corresponding producer sites + their consumer arm deletions; A2-followup-β covers `array_aggregation.rs` (first/last/sum/avg/min/max) + corresponding producer sites; etc. Each sub-commit is itself atomic (cargo check passes after each) and the union exhausts the cascade. The producer-gate flip lands in the LAST sub-commit (after all 48 handler entry points have v2-raw String/Decimal arms), so user-facing semantics flip atomically at gate-flip time even though the consumer-side work landed across multiple atomic units. This is structurally distinct from "preserve fallback for one period" because (i) the gate stays closed until all dispatch arms are wired (no W-series declare-victory window); (ii) each sub-commit lands a structurally complete piece (cargo check passes) rather than a transitional half-state; (iii) the architecture is forward-progress per sub-commit, not deferred-by-rename. Alternative: dispatch as single ~3-5k LoC atomic commit with explicit cascade-ceiling waiver (mirror of D2 → D3 precedent), accepting the single-author audit-feasibility risk; the supervisor disposes. **CONSUMER-CASCADE NOTE refinement**: kickoff says `dispatch_method_kinded` maps StringV2 → STRING_METHODS; DecimalV2 → NUMBER_METHODS preserving method-call shape — verified at `objects/mod.rs:549/552`. However, this routing applies to the **single-element scalar carrier** (`NativeKind::StringV2` slot bits as `*const StringObj` returned from element-read). For an `Array<string>` receiver (slot kind `NativeKind::UInt64` + ELEM_TYPE_STRING stamp), the receiver classifier at `objects/mod.rs:556-617` routes through `as_v2_typed_array` and `V2ElemType::String | V2ElemType::Decimal => None` falls back to `ARRAY_METHODS` (line 612). So **array method dispatch is preserved**, but the ARRAY_METHODS handler bodies are the 48-handler entry-point layer that requires the v2-raw arms. The kickoff's "method-call shape is preserved" claim is correct at the receiver-classification layer; the deeper finding is at the handler-body layer. **Pre-flight ground-truth discrepancies surfaced (13th supervisor-/audit-layer imprecision pattern instance — extends the 12-instance trend per status doc R20 close + Round 2 close findings)**: (G1) Kickoff prediction of 158 refs / 35 files; ground-truth at HEAD 4c7b1d9d = **159 refs / 36 files** (delta +1/+1 from intervening merge ceremony's addition of `decimal_obj.rs` doc-comment authority cite). (G4) Kickoff dispatch-shape claim "A2's architectural foundation at Round 2 commit c8ef1cc0" — verified at git log; merge commit is 4c269015, sub-cluster commit is c8ef1cc0.
