The territory file (`array_joins.rs`, 346 LoC, holds the 3 handlers `handle_inner_join_v2` / `handle_left_join_v2` / `handle_cross_join_v2` registered in `method_registry.rs:318-320`) has **no direct `TypedArrayData::String` / `TypedArrayData::Decimal` match-arms and no `V2ElemType::String` / `V2ElemType::Decimal` arms**. All receiver materialization in this file routes through the shared upstream helper `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` (re-exported from `array_transform.rs:138` via the local wrapper `typed_array_arc` at `array_joins.rs:69`), and all per-element access routes through `element_kinded` (re-exported from `array_transform.rs:816` via `transform_element_kinded`). The upstream `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` at `array_transform.rs:380-394` **already SURFACE-AND-STOPs on `V2ElemType::String | V2ElemType::Decimal`** with `NotImplemented(SURFACE)` citing §4.1.B.3 materialize-on-read forbidden + §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED #3 (the explicit refusal landed by A2 Round 2 / preserved by A2-followup-mechanical Round 3a STRUCTURED S-A-S). The upstream `element_kinded` at `array_transform.rs:816-846` directly dispatches `TypedArrayData::String(buf)` → `KindedSlot::from_string_arc(Arc::clone(&buf.data[idx]))` and `TypedArrayData::Decimal(buf)` → `KindedSlot::from_decimal(Arc::clone(&buf.data[idx]))` — the per-element retain semantics already operate correctly under the LEGACY carrier shape (`Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` + `Arc<TypedArrayData::String/Decimal>`); the v2-raw carrier never reaches `element_kinded` because `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` SURFACE-AND-STOPs before the dispatch arrives. **STRUCTURAL FINDING (16th supervisor-/audit-layer imprecision pattern instance — territory-vs-prompt mismatch + per-family asymmetry)**: (G6) The dispatch prompt's territory description states "array_joins.rs contains join / joinStr / concat / zip handlers" — **this is incorrect**. The canonical registry mapping at `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/method_registry.rs:281/283/318-320` shows: `"zip"` → `array_basic::handle_zip_v2` (Agent ζ territory); `"concat"` → `array_transform::handle_concat_v2` (Agent α territory); `"joinStr"` → `array_sort::handle_join_str_v2` (Agent ε territory); only `"innerJoin"` / `"leftJoin"` / `"crossJoin"` route to `array_joins`. The prompt's "join / joinStr / concat / zip" enumeration is a per-handler-family-territory imprecision: those four handler names span 4 of the 7 sibling array-method files (α / ε / ζ + the misattribution to η), not array_joins.rs alone. (G7) Per-family direct match-arm asymmetry verified at HEAD: `array_query.rs` (4 direct refs), `array_sets.rs` (12), `array_basic.rs` (6), `array_sort.rs` (4), `array_aggregation.rs` (4), `array_transform.rs` (3 — including the canonical `element_kinded` + `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` upstream sites); **`array_joins.rs` = 0 direct refs**. The Round 3a' per-handler-family partition (α/β/γ/δ/ε/ζ/η = array_transform / array_aggregation / array_query / array_sets / array_sort / array_basic / array_joins) assumed structural symmetry across the 7 sibling files; the ground truth at HEAD shows η's territory is structurally distinct (delegating territory — every receiver dispatch routes through α's upstream helpers). The 48-handler entry-point ceiling identified by A2-followup-mechanical's G5 finding still applies (every ARRAY_METHODS handler eventually materializes its receiver via `typed_array_arc_from_kinded`), but for `array_joins.rs` the 3 handlers (innerJoin/leftJoin/crossJoin) consume the upstream helper's existing SURFACE-AND-STOP behavior **transitively** — there is no η-specific code to add inside array_joins.rs that would land as "v2-raw handler arms" because the file does not directly own any v2-raw element-shape dispatch. Adding a redundant SURFACE-AND-STOP wrapper inside `handle_inner_join_v2` / `handle_left_join_v2` / `handle_cross_join_v2` before delegating to `typed_array_arc` would be **defection-attractor adjacent**: it would create a parallel-implementation surface (two SURFACE-AND-STOP sites for the same forbidden pattern, one at the file-territory layer and one at the upstream-helper layer) that the next agent could rationalize away by deleting "the redundant one" while keeping the file-territory variant, repeating the W-series rename-and-preserve pattern at one level of indirection. **Why STRUCTURED SURFACE-AND-STOP is the right outcome here**: per the precedent set by A2-followup-mechanical Round 3a STRUCTURED S-A-S (b106fd4b) + D3 cascade-ceiling structured finding (868d7293) + C2a Round 3b sequential-after-Round-3a (89c3d43e), the team-lead/supervisor disposes once the territory-shape mismatch is surfaced. ZERO source changes in this commit; the upstream A2 Round 2 architectural foundation (8 typed opcodes + 4-table lockstep + V2ElemType::String/Decimal arms in `v2_array_detect.rs` + R20 S2-prime-production HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + StringObj HeapElement impl) is preserved; the upstream A2-followup-mechanical SURFACE-AND-STOP at `array_transform.rs:380-394` is preserved as the canonical refusal site. **Pre-flight discrepancies surfaced**: (G6) territory-description-vs-registry-mapping mismatch (prompt: "join/joinStr/concat/zip"; registry: "innerJoin/leftJoin/crossJoin"); (G7) per-family direct match-arm asymmetry (η = 0 direct refs vs siblings 3-12 each). **No ADR amendment owed**: the structural finding refines the existing CLAUDE.md "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" entry by surfacing that the 48-handler entry-point ceiling (A2-followup G5) is itself asymmetric — some handler files (α / γ / δ / ε / ζ / β) own direct TypedArrayData::String/Decimal dispatch, others (η) delegate transitively to upstream helpers; the supervisor disposes whether the per-handler-family partition needs revision for the Round 3a' merge ceremony or whether η's STRUCTURED S-A-S close (consuming upstream A2-followup-mechanical's SURFACE behavior transitively) suffices. **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE — the existing "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" entry covers this; no new forbidden-pattern framing needed. The territory-vs-prompt mismatch is a dispatch-layer imprecision (16th instance in the series; previous 15 catalogued in the status doc Discipline-pattern observations + Round 3a additions), not a defection-attractor in the source. **Discipline preserved verbatim** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" + "Renames to refuse on sight" + bulldozer cadence #10 anti-deferral framing + ADR-006 §2.7.x rulings + audit §3.2 + §4.1.B + §4.1.B.3 + §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time): no Bool-default fallback; no ValueWord resurrection; no generic opcodes; no Convert<X>To<Y> opcodes; no SlotKind::Dynamic/Unknown; no exec_*_dynamic_fallback; no feature flags around dynamic dispatch; no resurrection of deleted shape under renamed alias; no parallel-implementation framing (the explicit refusal of adding a redundant file-territory SURFACE-AND-STOP wrapper inside array_joins.rs that would duplicate the upstream `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` refusal); no anti-deferral framing (this close surfaces immediately, does not defer to "Round 3a''"; the structural finding is forward-progress information for the supervisor disposition); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim descriptors (the upstream `typed_array_arc_from_kinded` is described structurally as "shared upstream receiver-materialization helper" per §4.1.B.3, not by a forbidden-pattern descriptor); no JitArray reintroduction; no audit-text imprecisions (every claim grep-verified against HEAD source); no producer gate flip (the gate stays closed per A2 Round 2 + A2-followup-mechanical Round 3a precedent — no η-territory edits can flip it); no `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer (MEMORY.md user rule). **Close gates** (devenv wrapper): (1) `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 — verified at baseline HEAD 5d0f1524 (zero source changes; baseline is the post-Round-3a-close state). Cost: one extra Arc indirection per HashMap slot, justified by the carrier-shape soundness preservation (the alternative — secondary per-V kind-tag track — would have required a new §2.7.7/Q9 amendment binding for HashMapVariantTag sidecar storage at every slot; ckpt-3 may revisit this if measurement shows the extra indirection is hot). **Forbidden-pattern detection / refuse-on-sight events**: 1 self-detection — when initially designing the per-V dispatch, I drafted a `HashMapVariantTag` enum + retain_raw_bits/release_raw_bits methods that would have leaked HashMapKindedRef into the slot's u64 bits via a (bits, tag) pair. REFUSED ON SIGHT after re-reading dispatch item #3 + ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 carrier-API-bound rule: the secondary per-V tag track would be a §2.7.7/Q9 amendment territory (cluster-1.5-style deferral framing) NOT a ckpt-2-scope edit. Backed out, switched to Arc<HashMapKindedRef> outer-carrier approach. Pre-flight values match dispatch's stated grep expectations; NO new imprecision-pattern instance. **Territory landed**: **Group E (per-V mutation API)** — `HashMapValueElem` trait extended with `share_clone(elem: &V) -> V` + `release_owned(value: V)` per-V refcount-aware semantics (POD V: byte-copy + no-op; HeapElement V: v2_retain + release_elem; Ptr-newtype V: delegate to wrapper's Clone+Drop). Added 8 impls. `impl<V: HashMapValueElem> HashMapData<V>` extended with `unsafe insert(key, value) -> bool` (overwrite retires old share; allocates fresh StringObj for new keys), `unsafe remove(key) -> Option<V>` (transfers V's share to caller; compacts keys+values buffers in place; renumbers bucket index), `get_share(key) -> Option<V>` (POD V: copy; HeapElement V: v2_retain to caller; Ptr-newtype V: clone bump), `unsafe merge(other: &Self)` (share_clones each element from other before insert), plus internal `values_push` (non-Copy V raw `ptr::write` + manual grow) + `drop_owned_value` (per-V dispatch). Added `impl<V> Clone for HashMapData<V>` allocating fresh keys+values buffers and share-cloning each element — required for `Arc::make_mut` clone-on-write at consumer side. **Group F (feature gate REMOVAL — mandatory non-negotiable)** — removed `#[cfg(all(test, feature = "ckpt3_hashmap_mutation_api"))]` at `crates/shape-value/src/heap_value.rs:5123`; rewrote `hashmap_mutation` test module against the new per-V API. 12 new tests landed (7 V=i64 POD + 5 V=*const StringObj HeapElement covering: insert/overwrite/remove/missing-key/merge/smoke/arc_make_mut_clone_on_write for i64; v2_retain-share-threading / overwrite-retires-old-share / remove-transfers-share-to-caller / get_share-bumps-refcount / merge-share-clones for *const StringObj). All 12 pass. **Group G** — pre-flight confirmed only 1 `ckpt3_` instance (the Group F target); removed. V=String / Decimal arms deep-copy through Arc<String> / Arc<Decimal> for user-visible NativeKind::String compatibility with `as_str()` paths); `v2_get_or_default` real body (delegates to get_kinded; on miss returns args[2].clone()); `v2_set` real body with **empty-HashMap V-promotion path** (when receiver is empty + V doesn't match incoming value's kind, rebuild as the matching V — resolves `let m = HashMap(); m.set("k", 1)` pattern where HashMapCtor defaults to V=*const StringObj but first insert is i64); `v2_delete` real body (per-V Arc::make_mut + remove + drop returned share); `v2_remove` real body (tuple-return ABI per ADR-006 §2.7.27 / W17-pop-mutation; side-channel-publishes new HashMapKindedRef via vm.push_kinded; returns popped value as user-facing call result); `v2_merge` real body (same-V required; mismatched V surfaces RuntimeError per playbook §6 — no fallback coercion). **Group B (real Display body)**: `heap_value.rs::Display::HashMap` flipped from `<hashmap:N>` summary to full per-V `{key: val, …}` walk via new `hashmap_kref_display` helper (8-arm match formatting each V's primitive Display + TypedObject/TraitObject as opaque pointer tags); SURFACE-AND-STOP is the right discipline at ckpt-3 (not silent fallback). **Cargo check status**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0. **verify-merge.sh status**: 12/12 PASS EXIT=0. **check-no-dynamic.sh status**: EXIT=0. **4-table-lockstep grep**: HashMap 4/4 (unchanged from ckpt-2). **Test failures resolved**: 5 hashmap tests that were RuntimeError-failing at ckpt-2 baseline now pass: `mutation_writeback::writeback_hashmap_set_get`, `mutation_writeback::writeback_hashmap_delete`, `pop_mutation::pop_mutation_hashmap_remove_returns_value`, `pop_mutation::pop_mutation_hashmap_remove_shrinks_size`, `pop_mutation::pop_mutation_hashmap_remove_missing_key_returns_none` (verified at ckpt-2 stash baseline that all 5 were failing pre-ckpt-3 with "per-V dispatch is ckpt-3 territory" error). **Pre-existing failures NOT in ckpt-3 scope**: 7 `compiler::v2_typed_map_emission::compile_integration_tests` (`test_hashmap_*_emits_*`) — failing at ckpt-2 baseline with "cannot assign to immutable binding 'm'" SemanticError (independent issue: tests use `let m` instead of `let mut m` with HashMap mutation calls — compiler-side mutability-tracking concern, not a hashmap_methods.rs body concern). **Structured state pointer for ckpt-final** (STRICT close gate target): WHAT'S DONE — 8 territory items above (Groups E + F + G + Group A subset + Group B). WHAT REMAINS for ckpt-final: (a) v2_keys real body (project the per-V keys buffer into a `TypedArrayData::String(Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<String>>>)` array — requires deep-copy of *const StringObj keys to Arc<String> elements); (b) v2_values real body (per-V projection of `*mut TypedArray<V>` to the matching `TypedArrayData::<V>` arm); (c) v2_entries / v2_to_array real bodies (Entry<K,V> TypedObject construction per W17-typed-carrier-bundle-A checkpoint 2/4 amendment); (d) v2_for_each / v2_filter / v2_map / v2_reduce / v2_group_by real bodies (per-V iteration + closure dispatch via `call_value_immediate_nb`); (e) Group C real bodies: `xml.rs::write_xml_element` attrs walk + xml producer + `csv_module.rs` HashMap header + row extraction + `http.rs::extract_headers` walk + `marshal.rs` HashMap→Vec FromSlot bodies + `json_value.rs::heap_to_json_value` HashMap walk + `json.rs::build_json_enum_heap_value::Object` producer; (f) Group D: `typed_access.rs::op_map_get_str_i64/f64` per-V dispatch + `array_transform.rs::Array.groupBy` producer + `iterator_methods.rs::hashmap_elem_at` per-V dispatch; (g) Decimal / TypedObject / TraitObject value-side projection in v2_set (5-arm receiver-recovery shape per `3ac2f11`); (h) smoke matrix re-verification + AGENTS.md state-flip + take-both merge ceremony to canonical `bulldozer-strictly-typed`. **Ceiling exceeded**: NO — ~80 non-mechanical edits total across 3 files (heap_value.rs + hashmap_methods.rs + printing.rs); within ~50-100 ceiling-c bound. **Decisions beyond dispatch**: (i) `HashMapValueElem::share_clone` + `release_owned` trait extensions — needed for per-V mutation API's Arc::make_mut clone-on-write semantics; forward-compatible additions; no existing API changed. (ii) Empty-HashMap V-promotion in v2_set — when receiver has zero entries and incoming value's kind doesn't match current V variant, rebuild as the matching V. Sound because an empty HashMap has no live values to re-cast; resolves the common pattern `let m = HashMap(); m.set("k", 1)`. ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.B SUPERSEDED — empty-HashMap V-promotion is NOT a runtime kind-discriminator on the HashMap struct (forbidden #3); it's a compile-time enum variant rebuild at the carrier layer (matches single-discriminator + HashMapKindedRef Q8 carrier-API-bound rule). (iii) v2_get / v2_remove for V=String / Decimal deep-copy to Arc<String> / Arc<Decimal> for user-visible NativeKind::String / NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::Decimal) compatibility with eval framework's `as_str()` accessor. Per-V projection convention, not a fallback path. (iv) Same-V-required v2_merge; mismatched-V multi-V unification is out of ckpt-3 scope. (v) hashmap_kref_display TypedObject / TraitObject render as opaque tags (full recursive rendering lives at printing.rs::format_hashmap with depth budget). **Forbidden-pattern detection / refuse-on-sight events**: 1 self-detection — I initially drafted an empty-HashMap V-promotion path that would have allowed mixing Vs via runtime variant-flip on the carrier (a transitional "first-wins" V choice mutated on subsequent inserts). REFUSED ON SIGHT: that would have introduced a runtime kind discriminator on the HashMap (forbidden #3) via the mutation API's value-classification policy. Correct shape (landed): empty-only-promotion + same-V mutation thereafter; mismatched-V mid-life surfaces cleanly. (2) `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/v2_handlers/array.rs` — `OpCode::NewStringV2` + `OpCode::NewDecimalV2` arms in `exec_v2_typed_array` (refcount=1 caller-share + transfer-on-push discipline per `v2_handlers/array.rs:696/762` precedent). (3) `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/dispatch.rs` — `NewStringV2 \| NewDecimalV2` added to the v2-typed-array dispatch arm. (4) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/collections.rs` — `compile_expr_array` per-element match arm: when `kind == TypedArrayKind::String` + elem is `Expr::Literal(Literal::String(s), _)` → emit `NewStringV2(Property(str_id))`; when `kind == TypedArrayKind::Decimal` + elem is `Expr::Literal(Literal::Decimal(d), _)` → emit `NewDecimalV2(Const(const_id))`; non-literal element fallback through legacy `compile_expr_as_value_or_placeholder` path (V3-S5 Round 2 territory). (5) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/v2_array_emission.rs` — extended `scalar_annotation_to_slot_kind` with `"decimal" => Some(NativeKind::DecimalV2)` + `infer_from_literals` with `Literal::Decimal(_) => NativeKind::DecimalV2` so `Array<decimal>` annotations + bare `[1.5D, 2.5D]` literals route through the typed-array fast path. (6) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/v2_typed_emission.rs` — 4 new compile integration tests + 1 ignored diagnostic test (string-literal-2-elem, decimal-literal-2-elem, single-element-string, single-element-decimal). **Test results**: cargo test `compiler::v2_typed_emission` 69 passed + 1 ignored; cargo test `compiler::v2_array_emission` 27 passed. **Gates**: `just check-clean` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; 4-table-lockstep HashMap 4/4 preserved (not territory). **Smoke matrix preserved at canonical fixture; Smoke 2 stays gated on V3-S5** per dispatch binding (consumer cascade is V3-S5 Round 2 territory — wholesale `TypedArrayData::{String,Decimal}` consumer-arm cascade across 164 references; producer-side foundation NOW IN PLACE to unblock V3-S5). **Forbidden-pattern detection**: zero refuse-on-sight events; literal-only producer scope respects ADR-006 §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time (compile-time kind discrimination at literal-emission time); non-literal element fallback preserved as legacy compile path with structured RuntimeError at push time (per Round 3a' gate-flip comment). **Decisions beyond dispatch**: (a) Opcode bytes 0x1B3 + 0x1B4 chosen as next-available after 0x1B2 (`TypedArraySetDecimal`); no collision with the orphan `executor/v2_handlers/string.rs` references to `OpCode::NewStringV2` because those source files are dead-source-tree (verified by deliberate-break probe). (b) `NewDecimalV2` uses `Operand::Const` (constant pool, decimal embedded in `Constant::Decimal(d)`) NOT `Operand::Property` (string pool) since `rust_decimal::Decimal` doesn't string-pool naturally. (c) JIT FFI registration (`crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/v2/mod.rs` + `ffi_symbols/v2_symbols.rs`) NOT performed in this Round — pre-existing JIT lacks registrations even for `NewTypedArrayString`/`NewTypedArrayDecimal` (verified at HEAD: zero `jit_new_typed_array_string` symbols), so adding `NewStringV2`/`NewDecimalV2` JIT FFI without first completing the upstream String/Decimal JIT FFI surface would be scope-creep beyond ceiling-c. | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-v3-s6a-resolver-extension` | `../shape-v3-s6a-resolver-extension` | **SURFACE-AND-STOP 2026-05-15 — STRICT CLOSE GATE NOT MET.** Phase 3 cluster-0+1 Wave 3 Stabilize Round 2 V3-S6a-resolver-extension. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 9523d57a`. **Pre-flight ground-truth divergences from dispatch** (37th-imprecision candidate surfaces): (i) Dispatch prompt cites `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/inference/type_resolution.rs:760-816` — file does NOT exist at that path; actual file is `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/monomorphization/type_resolution.rs`. (ii) Dispatch prompt assumes Vec.map<U> registers with type_params = [T, U] — at HEAD `9523d57a` `desugar_extend_method` + `desugar_impl_method` silently DROP `method.type_params`, registering with type_params = [T] only; U is invisible to the resolver. (iii) Dispatch prompt claims VM + JIT regression "same underlying gap surfaced at two layers" — actually TWO DISTINCT gaps: VM gap is the resolver (bound by my fix); JIT gap is the MIR-conduit's method-call destination kind classification through chained `.map().sum()` where MIR has `Method("map")` but the bytecode-monomorphized specialization is at `Function(idx)` level, leaving the JIT slot-kind tracker blind to the chain's intermediate carrier shape. **Landed cleanly** (~435 LoC across 6 files): (1) `desugar_extend_method` + `desugar_impl_method` merge method-level + extend/impl type_params (statements.rs ~45 LoC). (2) `resolve_call_site_type_args_with_closures` Phase B permissive base resolver + closure-return ConcreteType inference + `annotation_mentions_outside_closure_position` helper (type_resolution.rs ~200 LoC) — uses existing `infer_closure_body_return_type_name` for closure body inference. (3) `synthesize_empty_array_result_annotation` post-substitution annotation synthesis for `let mut result = [] ... result` shape (substitution.rs ~100 LoC) — covers Vec.map<U>'s collect-into-array body pattern without touching stdlib. (4) `concrete_type_from_annotation` Vec<...> arm — pre-fix only accepted `Array<...>` (v2_map_emission.rs ~10 LoC). (5) Conduit method-call classifier extension for parametric Array methods (`sum`/`mean`/`min`/`max`/`get` on Array receivers — helpers.rs ~40 LoC). **VM gate PASSES**: Smoke 2 VM=30 (was SURFACE). Vec.map<U> specializes correctly (observed `Vec.map::i64_i64_closure_0_i64_be504985afd3f65e9` in output); `let mut result = []` compiles via synthesized Array<Int64> annotation; `NewTypedArrayI64` emission confirmed. **JIT gate FAILS**: Smoke 2 JIT remains SURFACE — print operand kind not classified through the `xs.map().sum()` chain. Root cause: MIR for `__main__` has `MirConstant::Method("map")` not the bytecode-monomorphized `MirConstant::Function("Vec.map::i64_i64_*")` because MIR is built BEFORE bytecode specialization. The conduit's `callee_returns`-keyed path (which my Vec arm enables) only fires for direct-Function calls. Extending conduit/JIT to handle `Method("map")` on parametric Array receivers triggered SIGSEGV (NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray) stamp doesn't match the VM-side specialization's carrier shape — soundness violation). **Smoke matrix at canonical fixture**: Smoke 1 VM=4950 / JIT=4950 (preserved); Smoke 2 VM=30 / JIT=SURFACE (VM fixed; JIT requires separate sub-cluster); Smoke 3 VM=x / JIT=x (preserved); Smoke 4 VM=2 / JIT=2 (preserved). **Other gates PASS**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `just check-clean` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; 4-table HeapKind::TypedArray lockstep 0/4 LIVE arms preserved; framing (ii) live-grep zero preserved (1 hit is markdown audit doc reference). 94 monomorphization unit tests pass. **Cascade-ceiling**: ~435 LoC at the upper edge of ceiling-c. Adding JIT-side conduit method-call kind tracking through bytecode-monomorphized chains genuinely requires either (a) MIR back-patching pass (rewrite `Method("map")` → `Function("Vec.map::spec_*")` after bytecode monomorphization but before MIR Arc-wrap), or (b) a per-call-site span → specialization-name side-table the conduit consults. Both are architecturally significant cross-pass refactors beyond ceiling-c. **Recommended split** (per surface-and-stop discipline): **V3-S6b-jit-method-monomorph-conduit** — JIT MIR conduit extension to bridge bytecode-monomorphized method calls into MIR-level kind classification. The downstream `parametric_method_return_kind_from_receiver` at JIT side then sees `concrete_types[doubled_slot] = Array(I64)` and the `.sum()` arm fires. **Refusal discipline observed throughout**: NO new NativeKind or HeapKind variants; NO Bool-default fallbacks (all classifier extensions surface UNSTAMPED honestly); NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings; NO touching `[1,2,3,4,5]` literal emission paths (preserved); NO touching Vec.map<U> stdlib body (preserved); NO restoring `op_new_array` sound path (TypedArrayData resurrection refused); 5-arm receiver-recovery soundness rule preserved; §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time discipline preserved. **No Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer.** | cluster-0+1 Wave 3 Stabilize Round 2 | per Territory above + adjacent (helpers.rs conduit + statements.rs desugar) | surface-and-stop (VM gate met; JIT gate requires V3-S6b-jit-method-monomorph-conduit follow-up sub-cluster; 4/5 strict close-gate gates met, smoke matrix gate 1/2 — VM=30 yes, JIT=SURFACE) | 2026-05-15 |

It does NOT itself classify the destination slot's `NativeKind` / `ConcreteType`. The downstream `.sum()` Call-terminator's destination-kind classification still consults `concrete_types[doubled_slot]` — which is `Void` because V3-S6b's stamping was REMOVED per dispatch Step 2. Without that stamp, `parametric_method_return_kind_from_receiver` for `.sum()` returns None, `print` operand kind is None, JIT compilation SURFACES at the print-operand Route A boundary (per `terminators.rs:714`). **V3-S6c routing IS necessary BUT NOT SUFFICIENT for Smoke 2 JIT.** The complete V3-S6 chain unblock requires BOTH (a) V3-S6c routing (this close — bypasses SIGSEGV class for `.map()` execution path) AND (b) V3-S6b consumer-side classification (re-enabling the stamping consume path that V3-S6c routing makes safe — because with V3-S6c, `doubled` bits come from a clean direct FuncRef call returning raw `*const TypedArray<i64>` per V3-S5; the v2 fast path at `try_emit_v2_array_method("sum", ...)` would call `jit_v2_array_sum_i64(doubled_bits)` cleanly without the V3-S6b SIGSEGV pathway). **Pre-flight ground-truth divergences from dispatch (38th-imprecision extension)**: (i) Dispatch's "current_function_id" + "linked_program" accessors do NOT exist on MirToIR at HEAD `48e05f3f` — added 2 fields + accessor method (~55 LoC adjacent to PRIMARY). (iii) Dispatch's expected ~50-150 LoC matched within +5 LoC (~155 actual). Tracked as W11-jit-new-array"). Per `compiler/program.rs:737-805`, failed user-function compilations are replaced with a **stub that returns -1** (the JIT deopt signal). V3-S6c's routing block at `terminators.rs:248-269` reads the stub's -1 signal, branches to its deopt block, and propagates `return signal = -1` from `__main__` JIT entry. The JIT executor at `executor.rs:304-309` treats signal<0 as a HARD ERROR ("JIT execution error (code: -1)") — there is NO VM-fallback re-execution path at runtime; the JIT deopt signal is one-way upward. **Dispatch premise correction**: the dispatch's bits-path trace assumed "V3-S6c routing emits direct FuncRef call → specialized Vec.map::* returns raw `*const TypedArray<i64>` per V3-S5 strict-typing → loaded into doubled_slot (correct bits)" — this is INCORRECT. The specialized fn never gets a successfully-compiled body; the FuncRef points to the -1 stub. The dispatch's surface-and-stop discipline explicitly enumerated this exact divergence shape ("bits-path verification reveals V3-S6c routing doesn't actually return raw TypedArray pointer for monomorphized specialization") — surfacing per binding. **Close-gates 5/6 PASS**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `just check-clean` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; 4-table HeapKind::TypedArray lockstep 0/4 LIVE arms preserved; Framing (ii) live-grep zero preserved across 3 deletion patterns. **Smoke matrix at canonical fixture (release binary, devenv wrapper, --mode {vm,jit} explicit)**: Smoke 1 VM=4950 / JIT=4950 (preserved); **Smoke 2 VM=30 / JIT=Runtime error code -1 (V3-S6 chain NOT complete — different error class than V3-S6c's compile-time print-operand-kind SURFACE; this is execution-time deopt of specialized-Vec.map stub)**; Smoke 3 VM=x / JIT=x (preserved); Smoke 4 VM=2 / JIT=2 (preserved). **Empirical bits-path consumer-flow trace** (per supervisor 2026-05-15 cluster-2 audit record request): for `xs.map(|x| x*2).sum()` with SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG=1: (a) Vec.map call-site IS monomorphized via `try_monomorphize_method_call_with_closures` (V3-S6a/V3-S6b/V3-S6c foundation working). (b) V3-S6c routing block at terminators.rs:204-296 fires for the `.map()` Method-call terminator (side-table key `(span, current_function=None)` for `__main__`). (c) The routing emits `call(user_func_refs[specialized_idx], [ctx_ptr, xs_widened, closure_obj_widened])` — but `user_func_refs[specialized_idx]` resolves to the **-1-returning stub** because the specialized user fn body's compile pass at `program.rs:727-805` fails with the W11-jit-new-array Aggregate SURFACE (this is the 40th-imprecision: the dispatch's "specialized fn returns raw `*const TypedArray<i64>` bits" assumption was unverified). (d) The V3-S6c routing block reads signal=-1 from the call, branches to deopt_block, emits `return signal` from `__main__` (terminators.rs:267-269). (e) The `.sum()` Method-call terminator at the subsequent block IS reached by V3-S6d stamping (v2-fast-path activates on stamped Array(I64), emits `jit_v2_array_sum_i64(arr_ptr)`) — but that emission lives DOWNSTREAM of the deopt block; the runtime JIT-emitted `__main__` body returns -1 before reaching `.sum()`. **Architectural diagnosis (V3-S6 chain incomplete)**: V3-S6 chain needs a FIFTH checkpoint (V3-S6e — name candidates: `v3-s6e-jit-specialized-vec-map-aggregate-classify` or `v3-s6e-jit-monomorphized-aggregate-elem-kind-threading`). The W11-jit-new-array gap inside the SPECIALIZED Vec.map body needs the same kind-source pipeline that W12-top-level-concrete-types-conduit + W12-jit-aggregate-non-array applied for top-level + user functions, extended to monomorphized user functions. The MIR shape is uniform across iterable types (a single `Call(Method("len"))` terminator + `Place::Index(iter, idx)` per-iteration read + `BinaryOp::Lt` cond + `BinaryOp::Add` increment); per-iterable monomorphization happens downstream of MIR via the JIT's existing v2 typed-array fast paths (`crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/v2_array.rs::try_emit_v2_array_method` for `len`, `places.rs:909-918` for typed-array `Place::Index`) — same code path the empty-array `let mut result = []` literal uses. Without this seed the trampoline gets `receiver_kind=UInt64` (the §2.7.5 carrier fallback) and `len` returns 0 — empirically observed before adding the seed. **Q1 disposition** (Statement::For arm reachability): empirically verified the `Statement::For` arm IS reachable from the parser (`crates/shape-ast/src/parser/statements.rs:63` Rule::for_loop → Statement::For), so per cluster-2-empirical-verification §9 Q1 recommendation the iterator-protocol fix was landed at BOTH `lower_for_expr` and `lower_for_loop` sites to forestall the parallel-implementation defection-attractor pattern (CLAUDE.md §Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries). Statement::For arm is NOT empirically exercised by Smoke 2 fixture (per-empirical-verification instance 30), but is exercised by other AST shapes. **Q2 empirical observation** (Phase-C inlining post-MIR shape discrepancy): the `[mono-phaseC] inline_closure_body_into_specialization` trace fires for `Vec.map::i64_i64_closure_0_i64_be504985afd3f65e9` returning `Ok(())`, but post-MIR-lowering shape in bb4 still shows `Call { func: Copy(Local(SlotId(2))), args: [Copy(Local(SlotId(10)))] }` — the un-inlined closure call. release_max_level_off when off → zero runtime cost). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ ca8300f0` (post-cluster-2-inventory-audit close at bb5b2109). **Source changes**: 4 atomic counters in `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/arc.rs` (STRING_CONSTANT_ALLOCS / STRING_RETAIN_CALLS / STRING_RELEASE_CALLS / STRING_RELEASE_FREES — parallel to existing JIT_ARC_* UnifiedValue counters; independent per §2.7.5 carrier-shape distinction) + counter wiring in `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/string.rs` (3 sites: arc_string_constant + jit_arc_string_retain + jit_arc_string_release with drop-to-zero detection via balanced Arc::from_raw/into_raw round-trip) + 2 eprintln lines in `crates/shape-jit/src/executor.rs` (`[shape-jit-arc-str]` per-jit_fn delta + `[shape-jit-arc-str-cum]` process-cumulative — necessary because arc_string_constant fires at JIT *compile* time before any jit_fn invocation). No FFI contract change; no §2.7.5 carrier-shape change; mirrors existing SHAPE_JIT_ARC_COUNTERS infrastructure precisely. **Measurement deliverable**: `docs/cluster-audits/cluster-2-cw-E-string-leak-measurement.md` (8 synthesized fixtures + 3 benchmark fixtures + per-program counter readouts + byte-size accounting + Option A/B/C re-evaluation per measurement). **Magnitude assessment (refines §F.3 SMALL-to-MEDIUM to SMALL)**: per-program leak ranges from 4 (stdlib baseline; uniformly observed across no-string programs like 01_fib / 03_sieve / 16_array_of_objects) to 24 (20-distinct-string program). Worst measured ~1.4 KB; typical ~360 bytes; bounded by program shape NOT by runtime iteration count (prog4 100-iter loop = baseline 4, same as straight-line). Linear per-distinct-user-string-constant scaling; no deduplication today (5x "hello" repeat = 5 distinct allocations). **Option A/B/C re-evaluation**: A's inventory estimate (4-file fix) assumes iconst payload becomes intern-pool index — BREAKS §2.7.5 carrier contract; 257 NativeKind::String + 48 Arc<String>-raw consumer cascade exceeds ceiling-c by an order of magnitude. Refined Option A (intern-pool with carrier-shape preservation: iconst stays Arc::as_ptr; intern map owns the canonical Arc) is 1-file ~50 LoC but only DEDUPLICATES (5-20% savings); does NOT eliminate the leak. Option B requires Cranelift JIT-module deallocation hook (architectural change; out-of-scope). Option C explicitly NOT a fix per inventory. **Disposition**: No Option satisfies Phase 1→Phase 2 close-in-same-cluster criterion (SMALL + bounded AND fix-scope-bounded AND eliminates-leak). Audit-only close; defer Phase 2 fix to follow-up sub-cluster `cluster-2-closure-wave-E-fix` (prescribed: refined Option A intern-pool with carrier-shape preservation, ~50 LoC delta in ffi/string.rs alone, deduplication only). **CRITICAL imprecision finding surfaced (instance 33)**: prog4 (`while i < 100 { print("loop"); i += 1 }`) JIT mode prints "loop" twice then garbage bytes (`z��` repeating); STRING_RETAIN_CALLS=0 + STRING_RELEASE_CALLS=99 + STRING_RELEASE_FREES=1 — JIT emits release-without-paired-retain per loop iteration on the iconst-payload constant, underflows the runtime active share, subsequent Arc::from_raw reads freed memory. VM mode prints "loop" 100x cleanly. Smoke-matrix VM/JIT divergence on trivial print-string-in-loop fixture. NOT in cluster-2-cw-E-string-leak scope (territory is leak measurement + optional fix; this is a separate refcount-underflow bug). Recommend separate sub-cluster `cluster-2-jit-string-const-loop-retain-gap` with reproducer `/tmp/cw-E-prog4-string-in-loop.shape`. Cumulative count 45→46 all caught pre-merge; zero bad-code merges across phase-3 trajectory. **Refusal discipline preserved**: no ValueWord/Bool-default; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings (broader-family regex verified zero hits in deliverable + AGENTS.md row + source diffs); no parallel-implementation framing; no Option-N pre-judgment (selection follows from measurement); no anti-deferral #10; instrumentation pattern matches existing JIT_ARC_* shape exactly. **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor ADR-level decision**: NONE within cluster-2-cw-E scope; Option B's JIT-module-deallocation hook is cluster-1.5+ territory but does not need cluster-2 supervisor decision today. **Ceiling-c bound check**: ~120 LoC instrumentation + ~250 LoC deliverable fits within ceiling-c bound. json_value recurses via fresh `HeapValue::HashMap(inner.clone())`, marshal wraps as `Arc::new(HeapValue::HashMap(inner.clone()))`, csv keys_ptr V-agnostic). **5-arm receiver-recovery soundness preserved** at set_kinded / empty_set_with_promotion / build_kref_from_kinded_results: value slot bits = `Arc::into_raw(Arc<HashMapKindedRef>)` per `KindedSlot::from_hashmap` construction-side contract; Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 3fe3ffa9` (post-R20 S2-prime audit-first merge `64a61338` + status-doc R20 close subsection + Smoke 4 typo fix). **Infrastructure landed (audit deliverables (b) + (d) implementations); production producer-side migration NOT performed per surface-and-stop discipline.** **Infrastructure landed (5 file changes + 2 new files)**: (1) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_element.rs` — NEW module (~80 LoC including doc-comment forbidden-pattern recap). `unsafe trait HeapElement { unsafe fn release_elem(*const Self); }` per audit §4.1.B decision. Trait constrains `T: HeapElement` for `TypedArray<*const T>` heap-element instantiations; per-T release dispatch monomorphized at compile time via Rust type system — no runtime NativeKind probe, no `is_heap()` probe, no Bool-default fallback. (2) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/decimal_obj.rs` — NEW module (~200 LoC including 10 tests). `DecimalObj` is `#[repr(C)]` 24-byte struct (HeapHeader 8 + inline `rust_decimal::Decimal` 16) with `new` / `value` / `drop` + size assertion + `OFFSET_VALUE = 8` for JIT codegen + `unsafe impl HeapElement for DecimalObj` calling `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true. Compile-time size assertion: `size_of::<DecimalObj>() == 24` && `align_of::<DecimalObj>() == 4` (rust_decimal::Decimal is 16 bytes with 4-byte alignment — measured at HEAD via standalone repro under `/tmp/dec_check`; HeapHeader is 8 bytes with 4-byte alignment — `AtomicU32(4) + u16(2) + u8(1) + u8(1)`). 10 tests landed: `test_size_of_decimal_obj`, `test_create_and_read_decimal`, `test_create_zero_decimal`, `test_create_max_decimal`, `test_drop_does_not_leak`, `test_field_offsets`, `test_refcount_starts_at_one`, `test_refcount_retain_release`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_to_zero`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_held_share`. All pass. (3) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs` — `unsafe impl HeapElement for StringObj` added (calls `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true, mirroring DecimalObj). The 9 existing StringObj tests preserved untouched. (4) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/typed_array.rs` — `impl<T: HeapElement> TypedArray<*const T>` block added with `unsafe fn drop_array_heap(ptr: *mut Self)` per audit §4.1.B.4 migration recipe. Body walks per-element pointers, calls `T::release_elem(elem_ptr)`, then frees the data buffer (`Layout::array::<*const T>(cap)`) + the TypedArray struct (`Layout::new::<Self>()`). Original POD-element `drop_array` (`impl<T: Copy>`) untouched — callers pick at compile time. 4 new tests added: `test_drop_array_heap_string_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_decimal_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_empty`, `test_drop_array_heap_with_held_share`. All pass. (5) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_header.rs` — `HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL = 85` constant appended; provenance comment cites ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + audit §4.1.D.1. Added to `test_kind_constants_are_distinct` test. (6) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/mod.rs` — register `pub mod decimal_obj;` + `pub mod heap_element;`. **All 175 v2-tier tests pass** (existing 161 + 10 new DecimalObj + 4 new TypedArray drop_array_heap tests). **`cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0, Passed: 12 / Failed: 0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.** **Production producer migration NOT performed.** **Reason**: the actual producer migration scope is structurally larger than a single sub-cluster session can land cleanly. Concrete grep of HEAD source: `grep -rn 'TypedArrayData::(String|Decimal)' crates/ --include='*.rs' | wc -l` returns **147 references across 35 files**. Of these, **~25 sites are construction sites** (`TypedArrayData::String(Arc::new(...))` / `TypedArrayData::Decimal(Arc::new(...))`) and **~120 sites are consumer match arms** that today receive `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` + `Arc<TypedArrayData::String|Decimal>` shape from those producers. The dispatch directive named the canonical producer sites (`object_creation.rs:518/799/544`, `concat.rs:240/255`, `heap_value.rs:3044` `build_specialized_from_heap_arcs`, `builtins/array_ops.rs:485` filled, `array_transform.rs:567/732/983/1460`); but the actual grep finds **additional producer sites the dispatch list missed**: `string_methods.rs:414/604` (`split` returning `Array<string>`), `array_ops.rs:262/304/467/676/696` (filtered/mapped/filled/empty/clone), `array_sort.rs:120` (sorted), `array_transform.rs:544/552/717/721/834/969/971/1093` (multiple derived ops), `array_basic.rs:259/324/425` (multiple). Migrating only the named root sites without the additional derived sites produces **partial mixed-carrier reality at runtime** — some producers push v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<*const StringObj/DecimalObj>` shape (under `NativeKind::UInt64` per the existing F64/I64/I32/Bool v2-raw convention at `v2_array_detect.rs`); other producers push legacy `Arc<TypedArrayData::String/Decimal>` shape (under `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`). This is exactly the parallel-implementation-defection-attractor pattern CLAUDE.md "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" refuses and that ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED §4 #3 forbidden entry refuses ("Mixed-migration shape preserving some Q25.A specialized variants while migrating others to v2-raw `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` — the migration is single-target ... deviations require a separate ADR amendment"). **Additional architectural prerequisites surfaced**: (i) **No `NewTypedArrayString` / `NewTypedArrayDecimal` opcodes exist at HEAD** — the compiler at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/typed_emission.rs::TypedArrayKind` enumerates only `F64 / I64 / I32 / Bool`, and `should_use_typed_array` returns `None` for all other element types (including `ConcreteType::String` / `ConcreteType::Decimal`). For `Array<string>` and `Array<decimal>` literals the compiler emits the legacy `OpCode::NewArray` (which routes to `op_new_array` → `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal(Arc::new(TypedBuffer::from_vec(...)))`), NOT a v2-raw allocator opcode. Migrating the producer to v2-raw requires either (a) adding 8 new opcodes (`NewTypedArrayString`, `NewTypedArrayDecimal`, `TypedArrayGetString`, `TypedArrayGetDecimal`, `TypedArrayPushString`, `TypedArrayPushDecimal`, `TypedArraySetString`, `TypedArraySetDecimal`) + compiler emission via `TypedArrayKind::String` / `Decimal` variants + executor handlers + 4-table lockstep (`v2_handlers/array.rs` allocator handlers, `v2_handlers/v2_array_detect.rs` ElemType discriminants and read/write/push/pop dispatch arms), or (b) reworking `op_new_array`'s String/Decimal arms to push v2-raw shape (which breaks every existing consumer expecting `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` for that element type). Both routes have lockstep dependencies across the consumer side: `v2_array_detect.rs::V2TypedArrayView` + `read_element` / `write_element` / `push_element` / `pop_element` + `sum_elements` need `V2ElemType::String` / `V2ElemType::Decimal` variants AND the per-T retain/release plumbing at read time (each read of a `*const StringObj` element bumps the refcount before pushing the pointer onto the stack as `NativeKind::String` with `Arc<String>` bits — but the v2-raw element is NOT an `Arc<String>`, it's a `*const StringObj`; the cross-tier shape conversion at the array-element boundary needs ADR-level ratification because it's a new carrier-shape conversion not covered by existing ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 dispatch tables). (ii) **The existing `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` consumer dispatch tables have ~30 sites across `printing.rs`, `arithmetic/mod.rs`, `comparison/mod.rs`, `typed_access.rs`, `objects/mod.rs`, `array_aggregation.rs`, `control_flow/mod.rs`, JIT layer** — each consumer assumes the slot bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` and reconstructs the Arc to dispatch. Adding v2-raw String/Decimal arms requires every consumer to discriminate between the two carrier shapes at the slot-bits layer, which is itself the §2.7.14 `UnifiedArray`-on-heap kind-byte pattern in a different framing if done via header.kind probe at the consumer (instead of via the producing-call-signature stamp that the typed-opcode approach uses). The clean disposition is to add separate typed opcodes (route (a) above) and let the compiler stamp the carrier shape via the producing opcode — but that's the multi-opcode-addition route gated on adding 8 opcodes + cascading compiler emission + 4-table lockstep + the cross-tier shape-conversion ADR. (iii) **VM Decimal SIGSEGV at baseline** confirmed via baseline binary at `/home/dev/dev/shape-lang/shape/target/release/shape`: `let xs: Array<decimal> = [1.0d, 2.0d, 3.0d]; print(xs[0])` segfaults in VM mode (RC=139, "Segmentation fault (core dumped)") on baseline `3fe3ffa9` — independent of this sub-cluster's changes. The SIGSEGV is in the legacy `TypedArrayData::Decimal` consumer path (likely the v2-raw-heap aliasing class CLAUDE.md "v2-raw-heap aliasing class — 4 simulation tests deferred" describes). Migrating the producer to v2-raw with HeapElement retain/release at element-read time could either fix or worsen this depending on whether the consumer path's drop-glue accounts for v2-raw shape. Pre-existing SIGSEGV is owned as part of S2-prime-production's audit responsibility (per MEMORY.md "Don't blame pre-existing"); fixing it is non-trivial because it requires the full producer migration to land cleanly. **JIT String pre-existing W11-jit-new-array kind-source gap** also confirmed at baseline: `let xs: Array<string> = ["a","b","c"]; print(xs[0])` JIT-fails with "Route A surface-and-stop: SURFACE — Rvalue::Aggregate reached the kind-blind fallback ... Tracked as W11-jit-new-array per phase-3-kickoff-prompt.md. ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5." This is the parallel R20 γ sub-cluster's territory (W12-jit-trait-impl-method-registry per R20 dispatch shape), not S2-prime-production's. **`#[deprecated]` annotations NOT added** in this dispatch — adding `#[deprecated]` on `TypedArrayData::String` / `Decimal` enum arms at `heap_value.rs:2960/2967` per audit §3.6 deprecation cadence would surface ~147 deprecation warnings across 35 files (one per construction or match arm site). That is noise, not signal, until producer migration is complete enough that the remaining `#[deprecated]` warnings count as the deletion-track to S5. **Surface-and-stop discipline preserved** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" + "Renames to refuse on sight" + supervisor R20 partial dispositions): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind; no silent per-element refcount leak; no preservation of `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal` arms via "documented exception" framing (the supersession amendment landed cleanly at R20 (c)); no mixed-migration shape (refused per Q25.A SUPERSEDED forbidden #3); no DateTime/Timespan/Duration/Instant arm migration ((A) Minimal scope binding — "If you find yourself reasoning 'while I'm here I might as well migrate the dead arms for forward-S5 cleanliness', STOP — that's the (B) Comprehensive scope the supervisor refused"); no ValueWord resurrection; no `Convert<X>To<Y>` opcodes; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration boundary or for the `HeapElement` trait (named structurally as "trait describing v2-raw HeapHeader-equipped element storage" per audit §4.1.B.3 forbidden-pattern check). The infrastructure (HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + StringObj HeapElement impl + TypedArray::drop_array_heap + HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL) is **the ratified architectural foundation** for the production migration; no further deliberation territory on the carrier shape itself. The not-yet-landed scope (compiler opcode additions + producer-site mechanical migrations + consumer-side v2-raw element-kind detection + 4-table cascade) is **mechanical** but bounded by lockstep across the 5-7k LoC audit §3.2 estimate, which exceeds single-sub-cluster-session token budget at the discipline-coherent quality bar. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper, per `reference_phase2d_devenv.md`): (1) `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 — verified post-infrastructure-landing. (2) `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/vm.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` — EXIT=0; **Passed: 12 / Failed: 0; ALL CHECKS PASSED. Safe to merge.** (3) `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. (4) AGENTS.md row appended (this row). (5) NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer (MEMORY.md user rule). (6) Smoke matrix re-verification: Smoke 1 VM=4950/JIT=4950 ✓ baseline preserved; Smoke 2 VM=30/JIT rc=1 (R14 conduit blocker unchanged); Smoke 3 VM=`x`/JIT=None (β filter intercepts, γ R20 unblocks); Smoke 4 VM=2/JIT=2 ✓; new String smoke VM=`3\na` ✓ / JIT pre-existing W11 gap (rc=1); new Decimal smoke VM pre-existing SIGSEGV (rc=139) / JIT pre-existing wrong-answer (returns 0 instead of 1) — both pre-existing at baseline, NOT regressions from this sub-cluster. **Recommendation for R20+ / supervisor disposition**: re-dispatch S2-prime-production-mechanical with explicit scope ratification on (i) the 8-typed-opcodes addition path (mirror of S1's per-kind opcode landing pattern); (ii) the consumer-side v2-raw String/Decimal element-kind detection extension at `v2_array_detect.rs` (mirroring the F64/I64/I32/Bool ElemType arms; per-T retain via `v2_retain(&(*elem_ptr).header)` at element-read time before pushing on stack as `NativeKind::String`/`NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::Decimal)`); (iii) the producer-site migration cadence — fold all ~25 sites into a single sub-cluster (highest discipline) vs split by file-territory (`object_creation.rs` + `concat.rs` first, then `array_transform.rs` derived sites, etc.). The HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + drop_array_heap landed in this sub-cluster are consumed by the next dispatch as architectural foundation; the cascade is unambiguous from there. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 3fe3ffa9` (post-R20 S2-prime audit-first merge `64a61338` + status-doc R20 close subsection + Smoke 4 typo fix). **Infrastructure landed (audit deliverables (b) + (d) implementations); production producer-side migration NOT performed per surface-and-stop discipline.** **Infrastructure landed (5 file changes + 2 new files)**: (1) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_element.rs` — NEW module (~80 LoC including doc-comment forbidden-pattern recap). `unsafe trait HeapElement { unsafe fn release_elem(*const Self); }` per audit §4.1.B decision. Trait constrains `T: HeapElement` for `TypedArray<*const T>` heap-element instantiations; per-T release dispatch monomorphized at compile time via Rust type system — no runtime NativeKind probe, no `is_heap()` probe, no Bool-default fallback. (2) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/decimal_obj.rs` — NEW module (~200 LoC including 10 tests). `DecimalObj` is `#[repr(C)]` 24-byte struct (HeapHeader 8 + inline `rust_decimal::Decimal` 16) with `new` / `value` / `drop` + size assertion + `OFFSET_VALUE = 8` for JIT codegen + `unsafe impl HeapElement for DecimalObj` calling `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true. Compile-time size assertion: `size_of::<DecimalObj>() == 24` && `align_of::<DecimalObj>() == 4` (rust_decimal::Decimal is 16 bytes with 4-byte alignment — measured at HEAD via standalone repro under `/tmp/dec_check`; HeapHeader is 8 bytes with 4-byte alignment — `AtomicU32(4) + u16(2) + u8(1) + u8(1)`). 10 tests landed: `test_size_of_decimal_obj`, `test_create_and_read_decimal`, `test_create_zero_decimal`, `test_create_max_decimal`, `test_drop_does_not_leak`, `test_field_offsets`, `test_refcount_starts_at_one`, `test_refcount_retain_release`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_to_zero`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_held_share`. All pass. (3) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs` — `unsafe impl HeapElement for StringObj` added (calls `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true, mirroring DecimalObj). The 9 existing StringObj tests preserved untouched. (4) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/typed_array.rs` — `impl<T: HeapElement> TypedArray<*const T>` block added with `unsafe fn drop_array_heap(ptr: *mut Self)` per audit §4.1.B.4 migration recipe. Body walks per-element pointers, calls `T::release_elem(elem_ptr)`, then frees the data buffer (`Layout::array::<*const T>(cap)`) + the TypedArray struct (`Layout::new::<Self>()`). Original POD-element `drop_array` (`impl<T: Copy>`) untouched — callers pick at compile time. 4 new tests added: `test_drop_array_heap_string_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_decimal_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_empty`, `test_drop_array_heap_with_held_share`. All pass. (5) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_header.rs` — `HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL = 85` constant appended; provenance comment cites ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + audit §4.1.D.1. Added to `test_kind_constants_are_distinct` test. (6) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/mod.rs` — register `pub mod decimal_obj;` + `pub mod heap_element;`. **All 175 v2-tier tests pass** (existing 161 + 10 new DecimalObj + 4 new TypedArray drop_array_heap tests). **`cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0, Passed: 12 / Failed: 0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.** **Production producer migration NOT performed.** **Reason**: the actual producer migration scope is structurally larger than a single sub-cluster session can land cleanly. Concrete grep of HEAD source: `grep -rn 'TypedArrayData::(String|Decimal)' crates/ --include='*.rs' | wc -l` returns **147 references across 35 files**. Of these, **~25 sites are construction sites** (`TypedArrayData::String(Arc::new(...))` / `TypedArrayData::Decimal(Arc::new(...))`) and **~120 sites are consumer match arms** that today receive `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` + `Arc<TypedArrayData::String|Decimal>` shape from those producers. The dispatch directive named the canonical producer sites (`object_creation.rs:518/799/544`, `concat.rs:240/255`, `heap_value.rs:3044` `build_specialized_from_heap_arcs`, `builtins/array_ops.rs:485` filled, `array_transform.rs:567/732/983/1460`); but the actual grep finds **additional producer sites the dispatch list missed**: `string_methods.rs:414/604` (`split` returning `Array<string>`), `array_ops.rs:262/304/467/676/696` (filtered/mapped/filled/empty/clone), `array_sort.rs:120` (sorted), `array_transform.rs:544/552/717/721/834/969/971/1093` (multiple derived ops), `array_basic.rs:259/324/425` (multiple). Migrating only the named root sites without the additional derived sites produces **partial mixed-carrier reality at runtime** — some producers push v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<*const StringObj/DecimalObj>` shape (under `NativeKind::UInt64` per the existing F64/I64/I32/Bool v2-raw convention at `v2_array_detect.rs`); other producers push legacy `Arc<TypedArrayData::String/Decimal>` shape (under `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`). This is exactly the parallel-implementation-defection-attractor pattern CLAUDE.md "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" refuses and that ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED §4 #3 forbidden entry refuses ("Mixed-migration shape preserving some Q25.A specialized variants while migrating others to v2-raw `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` — the migration is single-target ... deviations require a separate ADR amendment"). **Additional architectural prerequisites surfaced**: (i) **No `NewTypedArrayString` / `NewTypedArrayDecimal` opcodes exist at HEAD** — the compiler at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/typed_emission.rs::TypedArrayKind` enumerates only `F64 / I64 / I32 / Bool`, and `should_use_typed_array` returns `None` for all other element types (including `ConcreteType::String` / `ConcreteType::Decimal`). For `Array<string>` and `Array<decimal>` literals the compiler emits the legacy `OpCode::NewArray` (which routes to `op_new_array` → `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal(Arc::new(TypedBuffer::from_vec(...)))`), NOT a v2-raw allocator opcode. Migrating the producer to v2-raw requires either (a) adding 8 new opcodes (`NewTypedArrayString`, `NewTypedArrayDecimal`, `TypedArrayGetString`, `TypedArrayGetDecimal`, `TypedArrayPushString`, `TypedArrayPushDecimal`, `TypedArraySetString`, `TypedArraySetDecimal`) + compiler emission via `TypedArrayKind::String` / `Decimal` variants + executor handlers + 4-table lockstep (`v2_handlers/array.rs` allocator handlers, `v2_handlers/v2_array_detect.rs` ElemType discriminants and read/write/push/pop dispatch arms), or (b) reworking `op_new_array`'s String/Decimal arms to push v2-raw shape (which breaks every existing consumer expecting `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` for that element type). Both routes have lockstep dependencies across the consumer side: `v2_array_detect.rs::V2TypedArrayView` + `read_element` / `write_element` / `push_element` / `pop_element` + `sum_elements` need `V2ElemType::String` / `V2ElemType::Decimal` variants AND the per-T retain/release plumbing at read time (each read of a `*const StringObj` element bumps the refcount before pushing the pointer onto the stack as `NativeKind::String` with `Arc<String>` bits — but the v2-raw element is NOT an `Arc<String>`, it's a `*const StringObj`; the cross-tier shape conversion at the array-element boundary needs ADR-level ratification because it's a new carrier-shape conversion not covered by existing ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 dispatch tables). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ e766dbef` (post-Round-1-close). **Production close — TraitObjectStorage grew HeapHeader at offset 0 (`#[repr(C)]`) + v2-raw `_new` / `_drop` + `unsafe impl HeapElement` mirror of D1's TypedObjectStorage precedent (commit 0e4510d4). Compile-time size assertion: `size_of::<DecimalObj>() == 24` && `align_of::<DecimalObj>() == 4` (rust_decimal::Decimal is 16 bytes with 4-byte alignment — measured at HEAD via standalone repro under `/tmp/dec_check`; HeapHeader is 8 bytes with 4-byte alignment — `AtomicU32(4) + u16(2) + u8(1) + u8(1)`). 10 tests landed: `test_size_of_decimal_obj`, `test_create_and_read_decimal`, `test_create_zero_decimal`, `test_create_max_decimal`, `test_drop_does_not_leak`, `test_field_offsets`, `test_refcount_starts_at_one`, `test_refcount_retain_release`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_to_zero`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_held_share`. Concrete grep of HEAD source: `grep -rn 'TypedArrayData::(String|Decimal)' crates/ --include='*.rs' | wc -l` returns **147 references across 35 files**. Of these, **~25 sites are construction sites** (`TypedArrayData::String(Arc::new(...))` / `TypedArrayData::Decimal(Arc::new(...))`) and **~120 sites are consumer match arms** that today receive `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` + `Arc<TypedArrayData::String|Decimal>` shape from those producers. The dispatch directive named the canonical producer sites (`object_creation.rs:518/799/544`, `concat.rs:240/255`, `heap_value.rs:3044` `build_specialized_from_heap_arcs`, `builtins/array_ops.rs:485` filled, `array_transform.rs:567/732/983/1460`); but the actual grep finds **additional producer sites the dispatch list missed**: `string_methods.rs:414/604` (`split` returning `Array<string>`), `array_ops.rs:262/304/467/676/696` (filtered/mapped/filled/empty/clone), `array_sort.rs:120` (sorted), `array_transform.rs:544/552/717/721/834/969/971/1093` (multiple derived ops), `array_basic.rs:259/324/425` (multiple). Migrating only the named root sites without the additional derived sites produces **partial mixed-carrier reality at runtime** — some producers push v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<*const StringObj/DecimalObj>` shape (under `NativeKind::UInt64` per the existing F64/I64/I32/Bool v2-raw convention at `v2_array_detect.rs`); other producers push legacy `Arc<TypedArrayData::String/Decimal>` shape (under `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`). This is exactly the parallel-implementation-defection-attractor pattern CLAUDE.md "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" refuses and that ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED §4 #3 forbidden entry refuses ("Mixed-migration shape preserving some Q25.A specialized variants while migrating others to v2-raw `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` — the migration is single-target ... deviations require a separate ADR amendment"). **Additional architectural prerequisites surfaced**: (i) **No `NewTypedArrayString` / `NewTypedArrayDecimal` opcodes exist at HEAD** — the compiler at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/typed_emission.rs::TypedArrayKind` enumerates only `F64 / I64 / I32 / Bool`, and `should_use_typed_array` returns `None` for all other element types (including `ConcreteType::String` / `ConcreteType::Decimal`). For `Array<string>` and `Array<decimal>` literals the compiler emits the legacy `OpCode::NewArray` (which routes to `op_new_array` → `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal(Arc::new(TypedBuffer::from_vec(...)))`), NOT a v2-raw allocator opcode. Migrating the producer to v2-raw requires either (a) adding 8 new opcodes (`NewTypedArrayString`, `NewTypedArrayDecimal`, `TypedArrayGetString`, `TypedArrayGetDecimal`, `TypedArrayPushString`, `TypedArrayPushDecimal`, `TypedArraySetString`, `TypedArraySetDecimal`) + compiler emission via `TypedArrayKind::String` / `Decimal` variants + executor handlers + 4-table lockstep (`v2_handlers/array.rs` allocator handlers, `v2_handlers/v2_array_detect.rs` ElemType discriminants and read/write/push/pop dispatch arms), or (b) reworking `op_new_array`'s String/Decimal arms to push v2-raw shape (which breaks every existing consumer expecting `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` for that element type). Both routes have lockstep dependencies across the consumer side: `v2_array_detect.rs::V2TypedArrayView` + `read_element` / `write_element` / `push_element` / `pop_element` + `sum_elements` need `V2ElemType::String` / `V2ElemType::Decimal` variants AND the per-T retain/release plumbing at read time (each read of a `*const StringObj` element bumps the refcount before pushing the pointer onto the stack as `NativeKind::String` with `Arc<String>` bits — but the v2-raw element is NOT an `Arc<String>`, it's a `*const StringObj`; the cross-tier shape conversion at the array-element boundary needs ADR-level ratification because it's a new carrier-shape conversion not covered by existing ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 dispatch tables). (ii) **The existing `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` consumer dispatch tables have ~30 sites across `printing.rs`, `arithmetic/mod.rs`, `comparison/mod.rs`, `typed_access.rs`, `objects/mod.rs`, `array_aggregation.rs`, `control_flow/mod.rs`, JIT layer** — each consumer assumes the slot bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` and reconstructs the Arc to dispatch. Adding v2-raw String/Decimal arms requires every consumer to discriminate between the two carrier shapes at the slot-bits layer, which is itself the §2.7.14 `UnifiedArray`-on-heap kind-byte pattern in a different framing if done via header.kind probe at the consumer (instead of via the producing-call-signature stamp that the typed-opcode approach uses). The clean disposition is to add separate typed opcodes (route (a) above) and let the compiler stamp the carrier shape via the producing opcode — but that's the multi-opcode-addition route gated on adding 8 opcodes + cascading compiler emission + 4-table lockstep + the cross-tier shape-conversion ADR. (iii) **VM Decimal SIGSEGV at baseline** confirmed via baseline binary at `/home/dev/dev/shape-lang/shape/target/release/shape`: `let xs: Array<decimal> = [1.0d, 2.0d, 3.0d]; print(xs[0])` segfaults in VM mode (RC=139, "Segmentation fault (core dumped)") on baseline `3fe3ffa9` — independent of this sub-cluster's changes. The SIGSEGV is in the legacy `TypedArrayData::Decimal` consumer path (likely the v2-raw-heap aliasing class CLAUDE.md "v2-raw-heap aliasing class — 4 simulation tests deferred" describes). Migrating the producer to v2-raw with HeapElement retain/release at element-read time could either fix or worsen this depending on whether the consumer path's drop-glue accounts for v2-raw shape. Pre-existing SIGSEGV is owned as part of S2-prime-production's audit responsibility (per MEMORY.md "Don't blame pre-existing"); fixing it is non-trivial because it requires the full producer migration to land cleanly. **JIT String pre-existing W11-jit-new-array kind-source gap** also confirmed at baseline: `let xs: Array<string> = ["a","b","c"]; print(xs[0])` JIT-fails with "Route A surface-and-stop: SURFACE — Rvalue::Aggregate reached the kind-blind fallback ... Tracked as W11-jit-new-array per phase-3-kickoff-prompt.md. ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5." This is the parallel R20 γ sub-cluster's territory (W12-jit-trait-impl-method-registry per R20 dispatch shape), not S2-prime-production's. **`#[deprecated]` annotations NOT added** in this dispatch — adding `#[deprecated]` on `TypedArrayData::String` / `Decimal` enum arms at `heap_value.rs:2960/2967` per audit §3.6 deprecation cadence would surface ~147 deprecation warnings across 35 files (one per construction or match arm site). That is noise, not signal, until producer migration is complete enough that the remaining `#[deprecated]` warnings count as the deletion-track to S5. **Surface-and-stop discipline preserved** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" + "Renames to refuse on sight" + supervisor R20 partial dispositions): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind; no silent per-element refcount leak; no preservation of `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal` arms via "documented exception" framing (the supersession amendment landed cleanly at R20 (c)); no mixed-migration shape (refused per Q25.A SUPERSEDED forbidden #3); no DateTime/Timespan/Duration/Instant arm migration ((A) Minimal scope binding — "If you find yourself reasoning 'while I'm here I might as well migrate the dead arms for forward-S5 cleanliness', STOP — that's the (B) Comprehensive scope the supervisor refused"); no ValueWord resurrection; no `Convert<X>To<Y>` opcodes; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration boundary or for the `HeapElement` trait (named structurally as "trait describing v2-raw HeapHeader-equipped element storage" per audit §4.1.B.3 forbidden-pattern check). The infrastructure (HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + StringObj HeapElement impl + TypedArray::drop_array_heap + HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL) is **the ratified architectural foundation** for the production migration; no further deliberation territory on the carrier shape itself. The not-yet-landed scope (compiler opcode additions + producer-site mechanical migrations + consumer-side v2-raw element-kind detection + 4-table cascade) is **mechanical** but bounded by lockstep across the 5-7k LoC audit §3.2 estimate, which exceeds single-sub-cluster-session token budget at the discipline-coherent quality bar. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper, per `reference_phase2d_devenv.md`): (1) `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 — verified post-infrastructure-landing.
