Trait constrains `T: HeapElement` for `TypedArray<*const T>` heap-element instantiations; per-T release dispatch monomorphized at compile time via Rust type system — no runtime NativeKind probe, no `is_heap()` probe, no Bool-default fallback. (2) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/decimal_obj.rs` — NEW module (~200 LoC including 10 tests). `DecimalObj` is `#[repr(C)]` 24-byte struct (HeapHeader 8 + inline `rust_decimal::Decimal` 16) with `new` / `value` / `drop` + size assertion + `OFFSET_VALUE = 8` for JIT codegen + `unsafe impl HeapElement for DecimalObj` calling `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true. Compile-time size assertion: `size_of::<DecimalObj>() == 24` && `align_of::<DecimalObj>() == 4` (rust_decimal::Decimal is 16 bytes with 4-byte alignment — measured at HEAD via standalone repro under `/tmp/dec_check`; HeapHeader is 8 bytes with 4-byte alignment — `AtomicU32(4) + u16(2) + u8(1) + u8(1)`). 10 tests landed: `test_size_of_decimal_obj`, `test_create_and_read_decimal`, `test_create_zero_decimal`, `test_create_max_decimal`, `test_drop_does_not_leak`, `test_field_offsets`, `test_refcount_starts_at_one`, `test_refcount_retain_release`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_to_zero`, `test_heap_element_release_elem_held_share`. All pass. (3) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/string_obj.rs` — `unsafe impl HeapElement for StringObj` added (calls `v2_release(&(*ptr).header)` + `Self::drop(ptr)` on return-true, mirroring DecimalObj). The 9 existing StringObj tests preserved untouched. (4) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/typed_array.rs` — `impl<T: HeapElement> TypedArray<*const T>` block added with `unsafe fn drop_array_heap(ptr: *mut Self)` per audit §4.1.B.4 migration recipe. Body walks per-element pointers, calls `T::release_elem(elem_ptr)`, then frees the data buffer (`Layout::array::<*const T>(cap)`) + the TypedArray struct (`Layout::new::<Self>()`). Original POD-element `drop_array` (`impl<T: Copy>`) untouched — callers pick at compile time. 4 new tests added: `test_drop_array_heap_string_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_decimal_obj`, `test_drop_array_heap_empty`, `test_drop_array_heap_with_held_share`. All pass. (5) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/heap_header.rs` — `HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL = 85` constant appended; provenance comment cites ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + audit §4.1.D.1. Added to `test_kind_constants_are_distinct` test. (6) `crates/shape-value/src/v2/mod.rs` — register `pub mod decimal_obj;` + `pub mod heap_element;`. **All 175 v2-tier tests pass** (existing 161 + 10 new DecimalObj + 4 new TypedArray drop_array_heap tests). **`cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` EXIT=0, Passed: 12 / Failed: 0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0.** **Production producer migration NOT performed.** **Reason**: the actual producer migration scope is structurally larger than a single sub-cluster session can land cleanly. Concrete grep of HEAD source: `grep -rn 'TypedArrayData::(String|Decimal)' crates/ --include='*.rs' | wc -l` returns **147 references across 35 files**. Of these, **~25 sites are construction sites** (`TypedArrayData::String(Arc::new(...))` / `TypedArrayData::Decimal(Arc::new(...))`) and **~120 sites are consumer match arms** that today receive `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` + `Arc<TypedArrayData::String|Decimal>` shape from those producers. The dispatch directive named the canonical producer sites (`object_creation.rs:518/799/544`, `concat.rs:240/255`, `heap_value.rs:3044` `build_specialized_from_heap_arcs`, `builtins/array_ops.rs:485` filled, `array_transform.rs:567/732/983/1460`); but the actual grep finds **additional producer sites the dispatch list missed**: `string_methods.rs:414/604` (`split` returning `Array<string>`), `array_ops.rs:262/304/467/676/696` (filtered/mapped/filled/empty/clone), `array_sort.rs:120` (sorted), `array_transform.rs:544/552/717/721/834/969/971/1093` (multiple derived ops), `array_basic.rs:259/324/425` (multiple). Migrating only the named root sites without the additional derived sites produces **partial mixed-carrier reality at runtime** — some producers push v2-raw `*mut TypedArray<*const StringObj/DecimalObj>` shape (under `NativeKind::UInt64` per the existing F64/I64/I32/Bool v2-raw convention at `v2_array_detect.rs`); other producers push legacy `Arc<TypedArrayData::String/Decimal>` shape (under `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)`). This is exactly the parallel-implementation-defection-attractor pattern CLAUDE.md "Parallel-implementation across producer/consumer carrier-shape boundaries" refuses and that ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED §4 #3 forbidden entry refuses ("Mixed-migration shape preserving some Q25.A specialized variants while migrating others to v2-raw `TypedArray<*const <X>Obj>` — the migration is single-target ... deviations require a separate ADR amendment"). **Additional architectural prerequisites surfaced**: (i) **No `NewTypedArrayString` / `NewTypedArrayDecimal` opcodes exist at HEAD** — the compiler at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/typed_emission.rs::TypedArrayKind` enumerates only `F64 / I64 / I32 / Bool`, and `should_use_typed_array` returns `None` for all other element types (including `ConcreteType::String` / `ConcreteType::Decimal`). For `Array<string>` and `Array<decimal>` literals the compiler emits the legacy `OpCode::NewArray` (which routes to `op_new_array` → `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal(Arc::new(TypedBuffer::from_vec(...)))`), NOT a v2-raw allocator opcode. Migrating the producer to v2-raw requires either (a) adding 8 new opcodes (`NewTypedArrayString`, `NewTypedArrayDecimal`, `TypedArrayGetString`, `TypedArrayGetDecimal`, `TypedArrayPushString`, `TypedArrayPushDecimal`, `TypedArraySetString`, `TypedArraySetDecimal`) + compiler emission via `TypedArrayKind::String` / `Decimal` variants + executor handlers + 4-table lockstep (`v2_handlers/array.rs` allocator handlers, `v2_handlers/v2_array_detect.rs` ElemType discriminants and read/write/push/pop dispatch arms), or (b) reworking `op_new_array`'s String/Decimal arms to push v2-raw shape (which breaks every existing consumer expecting `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` for that element type). Both routes have lockstep dependencies across the consumer side: `v2_array_detect.rs::V2TypedArrayView` + `read_element` / `write_element` / `push_element` / `pop_element` + `sum_elements` need `V2ElemType::String` / `V2ElemType::Decimal` variants AND the per-T retain/release plumbing at read time (each read of a `*const StringObj` element bumps the refcount before pushing the pointer onto the stack as `NativeKind::String` with `Arc<String>` bits — but the v2-raw element is NOT an `Arc<String>`, it's a `*const StringObj`; the cross-tier shape conversion at the array-element boundary needs ADR-level ratification because it's a new carrier-shape conversion not covered by existing ADR-006 §2.7.6 / Q8 dispatch tables). (ii) **The existing `Ptr(HeapKind::TypedArray)` consumer dispatch tables have ~30 sites across `printing.rs`, `arithmetic/mod.rs`, `comparison/mod.rs`, `typed_access.rs`, `objects/mod.rs`, `array_aggregation.rs`, `control_flow/mod.rs`, JIT layer** — each consumer assumes the slot bits are `Arc::into_raw(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` and reconstructs the Arc to dispatch. Adding v2-raw String/Decimal arms requires every consumer to discriminate between the two carrier shapes at the slot-bits layer, which is itself the §2.7.14 `UnifiedArray`-on-heap kind-byte pattern in a different framing if done via header.kind probe at the consumer (instead of via the producing-call-signature stamp that the typed-opcode approach uses). The clean disposition is to add separate typed opcodes (route (a) above) and let the compiler stamp the carrier shape via the producing opcode — but that's the multi-opcode-addition route gated on adding 8 opcodes + cascading compiler emission + 4-table lockstep + the cross-tier shape-conversion ADR. (iii) **VM Decimal SIGSEGV at baseline** confirmed via baseline binary at `/home/dev/dev/shape-lang/shape/target/release/shape`: `let xs: Array<decimal> = [1.0d, 2.0d, 3.0d]; print(xs[0])` segfaults in VM mode (RC=139, "Segmentation fault (core dumped)") on baseline `3fe3ffa9` — independent of this sub-cluster's changes. The SIGSEGV is in the legacy `TypedArrayData::Decimal` consumer path (likely the v2-raw-heap aliasing class CLAUDE.md "v2-raw-heap aliasing class — 4 simulation tests deferred" describes). Migrating the producer to v2-raw with HeapElement retain/release at element-read time could either fix or worsen this depending on whether the consumer path's drop-glue accounts for v2-raw shape. Pre-existing SIGSEGV is owned as part of S2-prime-production's audit responsibility (per MEMORY.md "Don't blame pre-existing"); fixing it is non-trivial because it requires the full producer migration to land cleanly. **JIT String pre-existing W11-jit-new-array kind-source gap** also confirmed at baseline: `let xs: Array<string> = ["a","b","c"]; print(xs[0])` JIT-fails with "Route A surface-and-stop: SURFACE — Rvalue::Aggregate reached the kind-blind fallback ... Tracked as W11-jit-new-array per phase-3-kickoff-prompt.md. ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5." This is the parallel R20 γ sub-cluster's territory (W12-jit-trait-impl-method-registry per R20 dispatch shape), not S2-prime-production's. **`#[deprecated]` annotations NOT added** in this dispatch — adding `#[deprecated]` on `TypedArrayData::String` / `Decimal` enum arms at `heap_value.rs:2960/2967` per audit §3.6 deprecation cadence would surface ~147 deprecation warnings across 35 files (one per construction or match arm site). That is noise, not signal, until producer migration is complete enough that the remaining `#[deprecated]` warnings count as the deletion-track to S5. **Surface-and-stop discipline preserved** (per phase-2d-handover §0 + CLAUDE.md "Forbidden Patterns" + "Renames to refuse on sight" + supervisor R20 partial dispositions): no Bool-default fallback for any unproven kind; no silent per-element refcount leak; no preservation of `TypedArrayData::String/Decimal` arms via "documented exception" framing (the supersession amendment landed cleanly at R20 (c)); no mixed-migration shape (refused per Q25.A SUPERSEDED forbidden #3); no DateTime/Timespan/Duration/Instant arm migration ((A) Minimal scope binding — "If you find yourself reasoning 'while I'm here I might as well migrate the dead arms for forward-S5 cleanliness', STOP — that's the (B) Comprehensive scope the supervisor refused"); no ValueWord resurrection; no `Convert<X>To<Y>` opcodes; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for the migration boundary or for the `HeapElement` trait (named structurally as "trait describing v2-raw HeapHeader-equipped element storage" per audit §4.1.B.3 forbidden-pattern check). The infrastructure (HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + StringObj HeapElement impl + TypedArray::drop_array_heap + HEAP_KIND_V2_DECIMAL) is **the ratified architectural foundation** for the production migration; no further deliberation territory on the carrier shape itself. The not-yet-landed scope (compiler opcode additions + producer-site mechanical migrations + consumer-side v2-raw element-kind detection + 4-table cascade) is **mechanical** but bounded by lockstep across the 5-7k LoC audit §3.2 estimate, which exceeds single-sub-cluster-session token budget at the discipline-coherent quality bar. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper, per `reference_phase2d_devenv.md`): (1) `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 — verified post-infrastructure-landing. (2) `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/vm.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` — EXIT=0; **Passed: 12 / Failed: 0; ALL CHECKS PASSED. Safe to merge.** (3) `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0. (4) AGENTS.md row appended (this row). (5) NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer (MEMORY.md user rule). (6) Smoke matrix re-verification: Smoke 1 VM=4950/JIT=4950 ✓ baseline preserved; Smoke 2 VM=30/JIT rc=1 (R14 conduit blocker unchanged); Smoke 3 VM=`x`/JIT=None (β filter intercepts, γ R20 unblocks); Smoke 4 VM=2/JIT=2 ✓; new String smoke VM=`3\na` ✓ / JIT pre-existing W11 gap (rc=1); new Decimal smoke VM pre-existing SIGSEGV (rc=139) / JIT pre-existing wrong-answer (returns 0 instead of 1) — both pre-existing at baseline, NOT regressions from this sub-cluster. **Recommendation for R20+ / supervisor disposition**: re-dispatch S2-prime-production-mechanical with explicit scope ratification on (i) the 8-typed-opcodes addition path (mirror of S1's per-kind opcode landing pattern); (ii) the consumer-side v2-raw String/Decimal element-kind detection extension at `v2_array_detect.rs` (mirroring the F64/I64/I32/Bool ElemType arms; per-T retain via `v2_retain(&(*elem_ptr).header)` at element-read time before pushing on stack as `NativeKind::String`/`NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::Decimal)`); (iii) the producer-site migration cadence — fold all ~25 sites into a single sub-cluster (highest discipline) vs split by file-territory (`object_creation.rs` + `concat.rs` first, then `array_transform.rs` derived sites, etc.). The HeapElement trait + DecimalObj + drop_array_heap landed in this sub-cluster are consumed by the next dispatch as architectural foundation; the cascade is unambiguous from there. pre-existing bug independent of D2 scope, NOT introduced by D1, NOT in scope here — surfaced for future receiver-recovery audit). **Architectural conflict surfaced (the surface-and-stop trigger)**: under the dispatch prompt's atomic-flip requirement, the 6 dispatch arms must flip from `Arc::increment/decrement_strong_count(bits as *const TypedObjectStorage)` to `v2_retain(&(*ptr).header) + v2_release(&(*ptr).header) + Self::_drop(ptr)` IN LOCKSTEP with the producer-site migration. Pre-flip-with-raw-producers = `Arc::decrement_strong_count` on non-Arc pointers = SIGSEGV (the dispatch prompt's stated worry). Post-flip-with-Arc-producers = `v2_release` decrements the unused HeapHeader refcount at offset 0 (initialized to 1 by `TypedObjectStorage::new`) → calls `Self::_drop` → `std::alloc::dealloc(ptr, Layout::new::<Self>())` on Rust Arc-allocated memory (which lives inside an `ArcInner<T>` allocation at offset -16) → UB. **Both directions of partial-flip are unsafe.** The slot-bit producers fall into two structural classes: (A) ~4 production sites produce slot bits directly via `Arc::into_raw → push_kinded`/`from_typed_object` — flippable to `_new + from_typed_object_raw` cleanly: `objects/object_creation.rs:171` `op_new_typed_object`, `vm_impl/modules.rs:427` module init binding, `exceptions/mod.rs:496` exception construction, `builtins/object_ops.rs:170` object_rest result; (B) ~4 production sites construct `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` to wrap inside `HeapValue::TypedObject(Arc<TypedObjectStorage>)` enum variant: `stdlib/csv_module.rs:273` csv records, `stdlib/json.rs:173` json marshal, `stdlib/xml.rs:135` xml marshal, `type_schema/mod.rs:275` runtime schema construction — CANNOT flip to `_new` without changing the `HeapValue::TypedObject` variant signature from `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` to `*const TypedObjectStorage` (cascading ~46 destructure sites + 30 match-arm patterns); (C) ~3 production sites construct `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` to wrap inside `TypedArrayData::TypedObject(Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<TypedObjectStorage>>>)` typed-array-of-TypedObject variant: `datatable_methods/core.rs:389/438`, `loops/mod.rs:663` — CANNOT flip without changing the typed-array element type from `Arc<TypedObjectStorage>` to `*const TypedObjectStorage` (cascading ~76 array-of-TypedObject sites); (D) 5 `HeapValue::TypedObject(arc) → KindedSlot::from_typed_object(Arc::clone(arc))` conversion sites at `iterator_methods.rs:283`, `deque_methods.rs:144`, `hashmap_methods.rs:151`, `builtins/array_ops.rs:174`, `stdlib/json.rs:275` — each produces a slot bit from an existing Arc; CANNOT migrate to v2-raw without either (i) variant signature change (Class B/C above) or (ii) deep-copy via re-allocation through `_new` (perf cost + parallel-implementation smell since the function deep-copies the Arc-shape into v2-raw shape, which is exactly the "Arc → HeapHeader bridge" framing CLAUDE.md `Renames to refuse on sight` refuses); plus `vm_impl/modules.rs:50` `OpaqueTypedObject` arm consuming runtime-tier typed return values does the same thing for module function returns. **Cascade scope (full atomic-flip path)** = ~11 producer migrations + 6 dispatch arm flips + 46 `HeapValue::TypedObject` destructure migrations + 76 `TypedArrayData::TypedObject` array-element migrations + 23 `from_typed_object` Arc-shape callsite migrations + 5 `HeapValue::TypedObject(arc) → slot` conversion-site migrations + variant-signature changes on `HeapValue::TypedObject` AND `TypedArrayData::TypedObject` AND `HashMapValueBuf::TypedObject` (the HashMap value-buffer arm at `heap_value.rs:558` — also `Arc<TypedBuffer<Arc<TypedObjectStorage>>>`) ≈ **~250-300 cascade sites total**, matching Wave 1 §D.7 audit estimate ("~300 sites. Exceeds R19 S1.5 ~100-site ceiling at the per-file level"). (7) NO new ckpt[4-6]_* undeclared feature gates (CLAUDE.md Forbidden Rationalization #6 binding). **Structured state pointer (intermediate sub-agent surface-and-stop per chain pattern step 2)**: (A) WHAT'S BEEN DONE — `TypedArrayData::X` consumer-arm wholesale deletion across the 9 ckpt-3 territory files; 80+ public handler signatures preserved across `vm_impl/builtins.rs` dispatch + `method_registry.rs` PHF + `op_get_prop / op_set_prop / op_length` opcode dispatch + `op_string_concat / op_array_concat` opcode dispatch + Deque method registry; shared per-file `ckpt3_surface` builders cite W12 audit + ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + per-T migration target + Refusal #1 binding; 2 cascade-broken cross-module surfaces from ckpt-2 (array_sort/joins/query 5-import E0432 + deque_methods.rs:264 E0425) cleared; TypedObject paths in `property_access.rs` PRESERVED INTACT; `op_string_concat` and `read_string_or_char` in `concat.rs` PRESERVED INTACT; `slot_to_heap_arc` in `array_ops.rs` PRESERVED INTACT; 4 tests for preserved paths in `property_access.rs` PRESERVED INTACT. (B) WHAT REMAINS at feature-branch HEAD (grep-verified file:line, cumulative ~543 refs across ckpt-4/5/6 territory): ckpt-4 = `loops/mod.rs` 45 + `object_creation.rs` 34 + `variables/mod.rs` 33 + `window_join.rs` 32 + `array_basic.rs` 32 + `array_operations.rs` 31 + `v2_handlers/typed_array_elem.rs` 23 + `string_methods.rs` 20 + `hashmap_methods.rs` 18 + `printing.rs` 16 + `objects/mod.rs` 16 + remaining smaller consumer files ~280 refs total + `TypedBuffer<T>` / `AlignedTypedBuffer` deletion + `HeapValue::TypedArray` arm deletion at `heap_variants.rs:476` + `HeapKind::TypedArray` ordinal handling + intrinsics/matrix.rs `_ckpt4_carrier_pin` dead-code marker cleanup; ckpt-5 = wire/json/marshal cascade + 4-table lockstep deletion (`heap_value.rs:3624` drop_with_kind arm + `kinded_slot.rs / closure_layout.rs / stack.rs` lockstep arms) + 6 consumer-cascade files in shape-value (`iterator_state.rs / kinded_slot.rs / reference.rs / slot.rs / v2/closure_layout.rs / lib.rs`); ckpt-6 = JIT FFI String/Decimal build + final atomic STRICT close gate. **Cumulative cascade tracking**: ~289 / 832 refs reduction at ckpt-3 close (~35% of remaining cascade at ckpt-2 close HEAD; ~24% of original 1223 at chain entry); combined with ckpt-1's ~130 + ckpt-2's ~302 = ~721 / 1223 = ~59% chain progress through 3 of 6 checkpoints. **Decisions beyond dispatch**: (a) chose wholesale surface-and-stop builder pattern (`ckpt3_surface(op, args) -> VMError`) per file consistently with ckpt-2 precedent — single source of truth per file for the V3-S5 ckpt-3 cite shape, simpler to refresh when ckpt-6 STRICT close removes the handler entirely or rewires to v2-raw direct access; the builder cites W12 audit §3.5 + ADR-006 §2.7.24 Q25.A SUPERSEDED + per-T migration target + Refusal #1 binding in one string. The variation in `concat.rs` (`ckpt3_surface(op, a_kind, b_kind)`) reflects the two-operand `op_array_concat` shape — same provenance citation. The variation in `property_access.rs` (`ckpt3_surface(op, key_kind)`) reflects the GetProp/Length single-receiver shape — same provenance citation. (b) PRESERVED INTACT (no `TypedArrayData` dependency) across the file rewrites: `slot_to_heap_arc` (array_ops.rs; shared by `object_creation::op_new_array`); `op_string_concat` + `read_string_or_char` (concat.rs); TypedObject paths in `op_get_prop / op_set_prop / op_length` (property_access.rs); `write_typed_object_field_by_name` (property_access.rs); `op_set_local_index / op_set_module_binding_index` already-SURFACE (property_access.rs); `numeric_index_from_kinded` with `#[allow(dead_code)]` (property_access.rs; re-instates post-ckpt-6). (c) `iterator_methods.rs` cascade — even though MOST of the iterator transform body has NO `TypedArrayData` dependence (transforms Map/Filter/Take/Skip/FlatMap/Enumerate/Chain over `IteratorState`), the `IteratorSource::Array(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` carrier variant in `shape_value::iterator_state.rs` cascade-broke at ckpt-1 enum deletion and stays cascade-broken at this ckpt (the `iterator_state.rs` file is shape-value consumer-cascade ckpt-4/5 territory per dispatch). Therefore every handler in this file surfaces-and-stops uniformly, not just the TypedArray-arm-touching ones — this keeps the `ckpt3_surface` single-source-of-truth discipline clean and avoids a partial-migration intermediate state. (d) `concat.rs` `op_array_concat` `drop_with_kind` ordering — pop_kinded transfers ownership of both operand shares; the surface-and-stop path drops both shares BEFORE returning the error (regardless of operand-kind validity) per ADR-006 §2.7.7 refcount discipline. Alternative was to drop only on the OK kinds path but that would leave the legacy "drop_with_kind in the validity branch" pattern half-migrated. (e) `property_access.rs` TypedArray-arm surface-and-stop in `dispatch_get_prop` does NOT touch `key_bits` after pop (parameter renamed to `_key_bits` to suppress unused warning) — the `drop_with_kind(key_bits, key_kind)` in the OUTER `op_get_prop` body still fires; the SURFACE message includes the `key_kind` for observability without needing the bits. (f) `deque_methods.rs:264` — chose surgical Edit over wholesale rewrite because the rest of the file (~370 lines) has NO `TypedArrayData` dependence; deque dispatch is fully kinded already. The `_d = as_deque(&args[0])?` pre-surface validates receiver shape so the SURFACE fires AFTER receiver-kind validation rather than before. (g) chose NOT to introduce ckpt[4-6]_* undeclared feature gates per Forbidden Rationalization #6 binding — the chain artifacts (`_ckpt4_carrier_pin` introduced by ckpt-2 in `intrinsics/matrix.rs`) are acceptable as transient documented chain markers; I did not mirror the pattern with new ckpt[4-6]_* markers in my territory because no structural-pinning need arose (all my preserved imports are live in the rewritten bodies). **Forbidden-pattern detection / refuse-on-sight events**: ZERO. The wholesale surface-and-stop pattern is the canonical chain-step-2 broken-state-OK discipline (per ckpt-2 precedent); no `bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim` framing introduced (the `ckpt3_surface` builders are structurally per-file error-message factories citing migration provenance, not carrier-shape shims); no Bool-default; no ValueWord resurrection; no generic opcodes; no `Convert<X>To<Y>` opcodes; no `SlotKind::Dynamic` / `Unknown`; no transitional ABI shims (public handler signatures PRESERVED at v2 ABI shape per ADR-006 §2.7.10 / Q11 + opcode signatures preserved at v2 stack-ABI shape per ADR-006 §2.7.7); no anti-deferral #10 (work stays in current wave; multi-session chain proceeds in lockstep per pattern step 2 broken-state-OK); no rename-to-refuse-on-sight events; no parallel-implementation framing; no new ckpt[4-6]_* undeclared feature gates. **5-arm receiver-recovery soundness rule check** (load-bearing for non-Copy V per dispatch §"Refuse on sight"): the deleted helpers' receiver-recovery patterns (`Arc::<TypedArrayData>::from_raw + Arc::clone + Arc::into_raw` triplet) are GONE with their parent helpers — the deletion is wholesale, not partial; no half-migrated receiver-recovery shape preserved. The PRESERVED TypedObject-arm in `property_access.rs` uses the same shape (`Arc::<TypedObjectStorage>::from_raw + read + into_raw`) which is independent of TypedArrayData — that's the 5-arm pattern for TypedObject and remains sound. **ADR-006 §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time check**: no runtime decode at FFI boundary introduced — every `KindedSlot` produced by the surface-and-stop bodies is a `VMError` (never a slot value); the preserved TypedObject reads source kind from `field_kinds[idx]` (schema track) per the canonical contract. **Ceiling exceeded indication**: NONE. ckpt-3 consumer cascade scope (~319 refs across 7 wholesale rewrites + 2 cascade-broken fixups, ~3276 net LoC removal) within ceiling-c bound (~50-100 non-mechanical edits per session); the 7 wholesale file rewrites are mechanical (Write tool calls) and the 2 cascade-broken fixups are minimal. **Next-sub-agent territory for ckpt-4**: ~30 smaller consumer files at ~280 refs total (loops/mod.rs 45 + object_creation.rs 34 + variables/mod.rs 33 + window_join.rs 32 + array_basic.rs 32 + array_operations.rs 31 + v2_handlers/typed_array_elem.rs 23 + string_methods.rs 20 + hashmap_methods.rs 18 + printing.rs 16 + objects/mod.rs 16 + smaller files); plus structural deletions: `TypedBuffer<T>` / `AlignedTypedBuffer` wholesale deletion; `HeapValue::TypedArray(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` arm deletion at `heap_variants.rs:476` + structural_eq/equals match arm rebuilds at `heap_value.rs:4583/4685`; `HeapKind::TypedArray = 8` ordinal handling; `intrinsics/matrix.rs` `_ckpt4_carrier_pin` dead-code marker cleanup. ## Status values
| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|| `idle` | No active cluster; safe to dispatch new work. |
| `auditing` | Read-only Audit 1+2 in progress. No code edits. |
| `awaiting-decision` | Audit complete; supervisor must rule on architectural shape before migration starts. |
| `migrating` | Decision made; mechanical migration in progress. |
| `blocked` | Surface-and-stop fired (see `git stash list` on the worker branch). Needs supervisor ruling. |

## Update protocol

- **At cluster start:** the dispatching prompt (or the agent on first action) appends/edits its row with `active cluster`, `files owned`, status `auditing`. Supervisor confirms no overlap with other rows before greenlighting.
- **At decision-gate:** agent flips status to `awaiting-decision` after writing audit doc; surfaces to supervisor.
- **At cluster close:** agent flips status to `idle`, clears `active cluster`, updates "last update" date, and notes the close commit hash in the same row.
- **On stop-and-surface:** agent flips status to `blocked` and stashes the WIP. Supervisor triages stashes as part of session-start review.

## Forbidden zones (no edits without supervisor sign-off)

- `docs/defections.md` — append-only; new entries go at the end of file (see header comment).
- `docs/check-no-dynamic-baseline.txt` — only edit to **lower** a count after deletion progress. Never raise.
- `CLAUDE.md` "Forbidden Patterns" / "Renames to refuse on sight" — these lists are immutable to agents; only the supervisor adds entries (after a successful defection-attempt review).
- This file — agent rows are agent-editable; the schema/headers are not.

| Phase | Gate command | Current |
|---|---|---|
| 2 | `just verify-phase-2` | shape-runtime --lib at 0 errors (2026-05-08, post phase-1b merge `c5f6672`); shape-vm/jit at ~2000 each — next session's scope per §2.7.5 |
| 2d (merge gate) | `just verify-merge` / `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` | Phase 2d merge gate (11 checks, exit-code-based per handover §0). Required pre-merge for every W17 / T1 / C* sub-cluster. See `docs/cluster-audits/phase-2d-playbook.md` §0. |
| 5 | `just verify-phase-5` (calls `check-no-dynamic`) | passes at frozen baseline; 0/0 forbidden phrases |

Phases 3 and 4 do not yet have automated gates — see `docs/strictly-typed-baseline.md`.

Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ b163bf4b` (post-Round-1 close of cw-E + cw-F + cw-2 + cw-C merges). **Implementation shape**: `OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<String>>>>` intern map (`intern_pool()` helper); `arc_string_constant(s)` performs `entry(s).or_insert_with_key(|key| Arc::new(key.clone()))` for content-keyed dedup; per-call `Arc::increment_strong_count` boost preserved per §F.1 docstring contract (defensive against unpaired releases per the prog4 surfaced finding §3 of the measurement deliverable — JIT-emitted code paths with `release_calls > retain_calls` would otherwise underflow when the pool's permanent share is the only baseline). `STRING_CONSTANT_ALLOCS` counter only increments on dedup miss (first insertion); dedup hits skip the counter, matching the §F.1 "leaked-allocation count" semantics. **Load-bearing acceptance MET**: `/tmp/cw-E-prog3-repeat-same-string.shape` (5x repeat "hello") leaked_total 9 → 5 (4 stdlib baseline + 1 dedup'd "hello" = exact §F.4 / §5.1 prediction); all other measured fixtures (prog0/1/2/5/6/7) preserve their baseline counts (no false dedup); per-call boost preserves unpaired-release safety (test_arc_string_constant_survives_use_drop_cycle passes with 10 retain/release cycles + 1 unpaired release on pool-owned Arc). **Smoke matrix preserved 4/4 VM == JIT at canonical fixture**: Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2. **LoC delta**: +226 / -72 across 1 file (`crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/string.rs`); substantive code ~50 LoC (intern_pool helper + arc_string_constant rewrite); tests +120 LoC (5 new tests: returns_pool_owned_pointer + deduplicates_same_content + distinct_content_distinct_pointers + pool_share_invariant + survives_use_drop_cycle rewrite for pool-ownership semantics; 1 test removed: refcount_boosted superseded by returns_pool_owned_pointer); docstring rewrite ~80 LoC (§5.1 disposition rationale + forbidden-shape refusal list). All 9 `ffi::string::tests::*` tests pass single-threaded + parallel. **Imprecision-pattern instances**: NONE NEW (clean Phase 2 implementation; the 1 in-scope concern of "preserve unpaired-release safety" was raised in implementation review against the first-pass `arc_string_constant` body that dropped the per-call boost, then surfaced via failing `test_arc_string_constant_survives_use_drop_cycle` — caught pre-commit, fix added back the per-call `Arc::increment_strong_count`; cumulative 46 instances all caught pre-merge across phase-3 trajectory). **Pre-existing SIGABRT in `ffi::v2_string_ffi::tests::test_string_obj_layout` + `ffi::value_ffi::tests::test_typed_object_kinded_slot_discriminates_via_kind_label` confirmed present on canonical at HEAD `b163bf4b` independent of this fix** (verified by running same `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib ffi::` invocation on both canonical shape/ and this worktree; identical free()-invalid-size SIGABRT on identical test names — NOT introduced by this fix). **ADR-fit**: §2.7.5 carrier-shape consistency PRESERVED (iconst payload remains `Arc::as_ptr` of a pool-owned Arc — same raw bit shape as `Arc::into_raw`); §2.7.5.1 stable-FFI raw-bits convention PRESERVED (no NativeKind::String semantics change); §F.4 / §5.1 measurement-deliverable prescribed shape implemented exactly as specified. **Refusal discipline preserved**: no ValueWord resurrection; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings (broader-family regex verified zero hits in code + tests + docstrings — the term "pool" is used directly, never wrapped as "intern-pool bridge" / "string-constant probe" / "dedup helper"); surfaced via failing test and immediately reverted). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor ADR-level decision**: NONE — fix bounded to single file ~50 LoC per measurement deliverable prescription. **Ceiling-c bound check**: 1 file ~50 LoC + ~120 LoC tests fits within ceiling-c bound by an order of magnitude. **D-α status**: not applicable (single sub-cluster Phase 2 fix; | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-cluster-2-jit-string-const-loop-retain` | `../shape-cluster-2-jit-string-const-loop-retain` | **CLOSED 2026-05-16 — cluster-2 Round 2 JIT string-constant loop retain-gap fix (cluster-2-cw-E imprecision-finding #33 follow-up).** Phase 3 cluster-2 Round 2 sub-cluster — fixes the critical JIT correctness gap surfaced by cluster-2-closure-wave-E §3 measurement: prog4 (`while i < 100 { print("loop"); i += 1 }`) JIT mode printed "loop" twice then garbled bytes (`z��` repeating) while VM printed 100x "loop" cleanly; arc counters showed `STRING_RETAIN_CALLS=0 / STRING_RELEASE_CALLS=99 / STRING_RELEASE_FREES=1` across 100 iterations. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ b163bf4b` (post-cluster-2 Round 1 close: cw-E + cw-F + cw-2 + cw-C merged). **Root cause**: `MirToIR::compile_constant` arms for `MirConstant::StringId` / `MirConstant::Str` / `MirConstant::Method` at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/ownership.rs:399-467` returned `iconst I64` with the `arc_string_constant` bits but emitted NO matching `jit_arc_string_retain` call on the JIT-generated code path — so every consumption of the same constant in a loop body would have the consumer's `StatementKind::Assign` (or `Drop`) call `release_old_value_if_heap` → `jit_arc_string_release` against the slot's prior contents, draining the constant's permanent share. Round-12 W12-jit-string-carrier-unification's `arc_string_constant` (`ffi/string.rs:163-181`) bumps the initial refcount 1→2 to provide a permanent + active share, but the active share was only safe for ONE consumption; a second consumption (the cw-E loop's bb=3 → bb=5 → back to bb=3 cycle) made `release_old_value_if_heap` drop the permanent share too, freeing the constant after iteration 2 → subsequent reads of the iconst bits hit freed memory. **Fix shape**: per-consumption `self.builder.ins().call(self.ffi.arc_string_retain, &[bits])` emitted at the constant-lowering arm in `ownership.rs::compile_constant` for `StringId` / `Str` / `Method` arms — mirrors the W11-jit-new-array retain-on-produce discipline (every consumption produces a fresh active share that the downstream slot owns and is responsible for releasing). The `arc_string_constant` permanent-share boost stays (the documented per-constant one-time leak is preserved per cluster-2-cw-E measurement; not the territory of this round). **LoC delta**: +39 / -4 across 1 file (`crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/ownership.rs`) — 3 arm rewrites (StringId, Str, Method) + docstring extensions citing §2.7.5 carrier-shape retain-on-produce + cluster-2-cw-E §3 cite. **Load-bearing acceptance criterion MET**: reproducer fixture `/tmp/cw-E-prog4-string-in-loop.shape` JIT mode now prints "loop" 100× identical to VM mode (was 2× clean + 98× garbled bytes pre-fix); arc-counter readout for the fixture: `str_retain=100 str_release=99 str_frees=0 leaked=0` (per-consumption retain + slot-drop release fully balanced; the permanent share keeps the constant alive across the full function lifetime). **Smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved at canonical fixture**: Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2. **Imprecision-pattern instances surfaced**: NONE in-scope (the prog4 imprecision-finding #33 from cw-E §3 was the predicted territory; root cause matched the inferred-but-unverified hypothesis exactly). Cumulative count remains at 46 (cw-E §3 instance 33 closed by this sub-cluster); zero bad-code merges across cluster-2 trajectory preserved. **Refusal discipline preserved**: no ValueWord resurrection; no Bool-default fallback; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings (broader-family regex zero hits in deliverable + AGENTS.md row + source diff); no parallel-implementation framings; no anti-deferral #10 (the fix IS in-scope per dispatch territory); no Ptr-newtype-shim defection #11; no new clippy regressions. **ADR-fit**: §2.7.5 carrier-shape retain-on-produce discipline (the producer at `compile_constant` is now responsible for handing the consumer a fresh owned share, mirroring `jit_new_array` / `jit_v2_collection_*` ctor sites and matching the §2.7.5.1 stable-FFI Arc-shape carrier contract). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE. **Close gates** (devenv wrapper): `just check-clean` EXIT=0 (default build); `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples --features shape-jit/jit-trace` EXIT=0 (feature-ON build); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved; reproducer fixture JIT == VM 100× "loop"; `cargo test -p shape-jit --lib --skip ffi::value_ffi::tests::test_typed_object_kinded_slot_discriminates` 382 passed 0 failed (1 filtered = pre-existing baseline failure unaffected by this fix); AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. | cluster-2-jit-string-const-loop-retain-gap (Round 2 cw-E follow-up fix; sibling Round 2 dispatch `cluster-2-cw-E-fix-intern-pool` handles intern-pool deduplication separately per cw-E §5 recommendation; territories disjoint) | `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/ownership.rs` (per-consumption `jit_arc_string_retain` emission in `compile_constant` StringId/Str/Method arms + §2.7.5 retain-on-produce docstring) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (reproducer fixture JIT == VM 100× "loop"; arc-counter balance retain=100/release=99/frees=0/leaked=0; smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved; 382/0 shape-jit lib tests pass; zero new imprecisions surfaced; ceiling-c well within bound at +39/-4 LoC) | 2026-05-16 |

Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ ca8300f0` (canonical HEAD including cluster-2-closure-wave-1 merge cc5ceb0e + cluster-2-inventory-audit-day merge). **Per-explanation disposition** (deliverable `docs/cluster-audits/cluster-2-cw-2-phaseC-inlining-empirical.md` §2.3): (1) AST-vs-MIR retention **REFUTED** (fn_call_total=0 means transform never made a decision); (2) `closure_param_name="f"` mismatch with `Expr::FunctionCall.name` **REFUTED** (no FunctionCall visit means no comparison to surface); (3) recursion did not descend into MethodCall.args **REFUTED at the named layer; CONFIRMED at a NEW deeper layer** (method_call=0 AND for_stmt=0 AND fn_call_total=0 means the gap is at `inline_closure_calls_in_expr` substitution.rs:2491-2703 missing `Expr::For` arm — Vec.map's body is `Statement::Expression(Expr::For(...))` and `Expr::For` falls through to `other => other.clone()`, so recursion never descends into the for-loop body; CLAUDE.md exhaustive-match-rule gap); (4) post-Phase-C re-introduction of Call shape **REFUTED** (the Call was never removed in the first place). **Fix scope**: Phase-2 fix REQUIRED, bounded to `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/monomorphization/substitution.rs` (cascade-site count = 0 per inventory §A.3 estimate). Two arms: (1) add missing `Expr` arms to `inline_closure_calls_in_expr` (Expr::For, While, Loop, Let, Match, Break(Some), TryOperator, Await, AsyncScope, Spread — pattern matches existing Expr::If/Block arms via rec_box/rec_vec); (2) rewrite `build_inlined_closure_block` (substitution.rs:2791) trailing-statement handling to lift trailing `Statement::Return(Some(expr), _)` AND `Statement::Expression(expr, _)` into `BlockItem::Expression(expr)` instead of `BlockItem::Statement(stmt.clone())` — preserves the closure body's tail-expression value semantics AND prevents `Statement::Return` (which the arrow-function/pipe-lambda parser at `crates/shape-ast/src/parser/expressions/functions.rs:62-64` / `:134-135` wraps expression-form closure bodies as) from emitting a function-level `lower_return_control_flow` inside the for-loop body of the specialized fn. **LoC delta**: +228 / -2 across 1 file (`substitution.rs`); empirical-phase SHAPE_JIT_DEBUG-gated trace counters account for ~100 LoC, fix-phase substantive code ~120 LoC. **Load-bearing acceptance criterion MET**: post-MIR bb4 shows `term=Call(Method("push"), ...)` with `s[3]: BinaryOp(Mul, Copy(SlotId(14)), Copy(SlotId(16)))` (inlined `x*2`), no `Call(Copy(SlotId(2)), [item])`, no `Return` terminator inside for-loop body. **Smoke matrix preserved 4/4 VM == JIT at canonical fixture**: Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30 (correctness preserved), Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2. **Imprecision-pattern instances surfaced** (cumulative 41→44 all caught pre-merge; zero bad-code merges): 33 (dispatch §3.4 explanation 3 named gap at MethodCall.args layer; empirical disposition surfaced gap at outer Expr::For arm — same architectural-prediction-subclass pattern as V3-S6a..e + empirical-verification §2.4 producer-vs-consumer); 34 (Reading-4-pattern prediction "(b) MAY be resolved by (a)'s side-effects" REFUTED — (a)'s fix made bb3 Call execute per-iteration but Phase-C AST inliner remained un-fired due to imprecision 33); 35 (subsidiary regression: Phase-2 fix arm 1 introduced VM `no method 'sum' on receiver kind Int64` because the arrow-function parser wraps closure-body expressions as `Statement::Return(Some(expr), _)`; in-scope fix in arm 2 per closure-wave-1 in-scope-recovery pattern per inventory §A.2 Reading-4-binding). **ADR-fit**: §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time (Phase-C inlining eliminates a runtime indirect call at compile time; fix completes Phase-C's contract by reaching every syntactic position where a closure-param call can appear). **Refusal discipline preserved**: no architectural-prediction-subclass (4 explanations empirically dispositioned per dispatch binding); no ValueWord/Bool-default/bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings; no new StatementKind variants (§B 2 UNCOVERED user-fn classes preserved for Round 2 tracking territory; not pre-empted); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved; AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. | cluster-2-cw-2-phaseC-inlining (hypothesis (b) empirical disposition + in-scope fix; Routing arms at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/terminators.rs` read the operand's `NativeKind` (stamped at MIR-emit time via `operand_slot_kind`'s producing-site classification) and dispatch to the matching FFI body — no NaN-box tag decode, no `is_heap_kind` probe (§2.7.7 #4 / #7 forbidden). The Char routing arm matches BOTH `NativeKind::Char` (post-§2.7.5-amendment scalar label) AND `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::Char)` (pre-amendment heap arm label) per the `KindedSlot::as_char` accessor's cross-tier compatibility at `kinded_slot.rs:594-597`. **Per-arm acceptance fixtures** (`/tmp/cw-D-fam-{char,mutex,atomic,lazy,channel}.shape`): Mutex `<mutex>` (4/4 MATCH VM=JIT), Atomic `<atomic:7>` (MATCH), Lazy `<lazy:pending>` (MATCH), Channel `<channel:open:0>` (MATCH); Char fixture renders VM=`A` / JIT=`65` — **Char arm wired correctly at JIT FFI tier** (verified via `print_char_scalar_matches_vm` unit test calling `jit_print_char('A' as u32)` which renders `A` identical to the VM-side `format_kinded(KindedSlot::new(ValueSlot::from_char('A'), NativeKind::Char))`); production-fixture divergence is the UPSTREAM kind-source gap at `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs:1505` (`Literal::Char(c) => MirConstant::Int(*c as i64)` — char literals lower to int constants, losing Char kind), which is the "kind-source gap upstream of the print site" the pre-existing `_`-arm SURFACE comment at `terminators.rs:688-693` already documents. Surface-and-stop discipline preserved: the FFI body + routing arm IS landed and load-bearing as soon as an upstream producer-site classification path stamps `NativeKind::Char` on a slot reaching the print Call-terminator (e.g., a future `MirConstant::Char` extension, or a `place_native_kind` projection through `concrete_types[char_slot]`). **Smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved** at canonical fixture (release binary required — debug-build JIT path independently broken at canonical baseline): Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2. **LoC delta**: +388 / -1 across 5 files: `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/conversion.rs` +263/-1 (5 new FFI bodies `jit_print_char` / `_mutex` / `_atomic` / `_lazy` / `_channel` + 7 new tests `print_char_scalar_matches_vm` / `print_char_invalid_codepoint_renders_fallback` / `print_mutex_arc_carrier_matches_vm` / `print_atomic_arc_carrier_matches_vm` / `print_lazy_arc_carrier_pending_matches_vm` / `print_channel_arc_carrier_matches_vm`); `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/terminators.rs` +91 (5 new routing arms before the `_` SURFACE arm — Char scalar + 4 concurrency-family heap arms); `crates/shape-jit/src/ffi_symbols/object_symbols.rs` +66 (5 symbol registrations + 5 declare_function blocks); | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-cluster-2-cw-B-class-bc-coverage` | `../shape-cluster-2-cw-B-class-bc-coverage` | **PARTIAL CLOSE 2026-05-16 — cluster-2 closure-wave-B Class A widening landed; Class B + Class C SURFACE-AND-STOP per inventory §B.4 empirical-verification-first dispatch binding.** Phase 3 cluster-2 Round 2 closure-wave-B-class-bc-coverage — extends cluster-2-closure-wave-1's `local_typed_array_element_types` seeding pass per inventory §B (broader W11-jit-new-array general fix per user-function-class coverage matrix). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ b163bf4b` (canonical HEAD including cluster-2 Round 1 close: 4 closure-waves merged — cw-E + cw-F + cw-2 + cw-C). **Class A widening landed** at `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/stmt.rs::lower_var_decl` (~25 LoC): widens the pre-existing V3-S6e empty-array-literal seeding gate to ALL typed-array-annotated bindings (`let doubled: Array<int> = xs.map(...)` etc., not just `let mut result: Array<C> = []`). The annotation IS the proof per ADR-006 §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time discipline — initializer-shape-independent; honors §2.7.7 #9 (no annotation → slot stays unstamped, no Bool-default, no inference). Empirically widens annotated-let-binding coverage from ~1 case (V3-S6a-synthesized stdlib-internal `let mut result = []` inside specialized Vec.map<U>/Vec.filter<U> bodies) to ALL user-written `let x: Array<C> = <any-expr>` sites. **Class B (closure body with INFERRED typed-array param)**: SURFACE-AND-STOP. Empirical fixture `/tmp/cw-B-class-b-inferred-closure-param.shape` (`let f = |inner| inner.sum(); print(f(xs))`) confirms gap: VM=15 (dynamic-dispatch path works) / JIT=NotImplemented(`print` operand NativeKind is None). Root cause: the closure's param `inner` is inferred-typed via bidirectional closure inference at the **bytecode-time** caller's call-site (compiler/expressions/function_calls.rs `compile_expr_function_call` for `f(xs)`); the **MIR-time** `lower_function_detailed` param-walk at mod.rs:674 reads `param.type_annotation.as_ref()` which is None for inferred params (no AST annotation source). The genuine fix lives at the bytecode-time value-call return-kind classification surface (NOT lower_function_detailed). Per CLAUDE.md surface-and-stop discipline + binder's "surface-and-stop if a new side-table is genuinely needed" allowance: refuses to fabricate an MIR-side fallback for a bytecode-time gap; refuses to plumb caller-context propagation through lower_function_detailed (would require new side-table coupling MIR-lowering to bytecode-emission caller-context — defection-attractor on Q8 carrier-API-bound discipline). Reading 4 in-scope-recovery pattern NOT applicable: the consumer-side gap is at bytecode-time value-call dispatch (compile_expr_function_call); MIR-side recovery here would require coupling layers that ADR-006 §2.7.5.1 keeps separate. **Class C (method-chain intermediate slot without annotation)**: SURFACE-AND-STOP. Empirical fixture `/tmp/cw-B-class-c-method-chain.shape` (`let doubled = xs.map(|x|x*2); let trebled = doubled.map(|y|y+1); print(trebled.sum())`) confirms gap: VM=ckpt-2 surface (`Receiver kind: UInt64` at second `.map` dispatch — bytecode never specialized) / JIT=NotImplemented (`print` operand NativeKind is None). Root cause: `try_monomorphize_method_call` at compiler/expressions/function_calls.rs:3171 calls `concrete_type_for_expr(receiver)` which routes through `identifier_concrete_type` at compiler/monomorphization/type_resolution.rs:1433; the side-table `local_array_element_types` checked at type_resolution.rs:1443 is **declared at compiler/mod.rs:943 + initialized empty at compiler_impl_initialization.rs:105 but NEVER WRITTEN by any code path** (grep-verified: zero `.insert` sites in crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/). The genuine fix is to populate `local_array_element_types` at let-binding-time when the RHS is a typed-array-producing call whose specialized return is `Array<C>` — that's a `compile_expr_method_call` / `statements.rs::compile_statement(VariableDecl, ..)` territory fix, NOT lower_function_detailed. Per CLAUDE.md surface-and-stop discipline + binder's allowance: refuses to retroactively fix bytecode-time receiver-kind classification from the MIR-side resolver pass (the MIR resolver at helpers.rs::infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_resolvers runs at link/finalize time AFTER bytecode emission per compiler_impl_reference_model.rs:1635-1644 — it cannot retroactively re-specialize already-emitted CallMethod bytecode). Reading 4 in-scope-recovery pattern NOT applicable: the consumer-side gap is at compile-time `concrete_type_for_expr` consultation; MIR-side recovery here would require bridge framings (`bytecode-time-receiver-kind-bridge`, etc.) refused per CLAUDE.md broader-family regex. **Empirical disposition**: both Class B and Class C are out-of-territory per the binder's MIR-side territory bounds (`lower_function_detailed` + `infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_resolvers`); the genuine fixes live in compile-time bytecode-emission paths (compile_expr_function_call value-call return-kind for Class B; concrete_type_for_expr / let-binding receiver-kind tracker population for Class C). Both surface honestly per ADR-006 §2.7.5.1 ("compile-time analysis state where a slot's kind is 'not yet known' during inference is held LOCALLY"). **LoC delta**: +25 / -7 across 2 files: `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/stmt.rs` (~+25 / -7; gate widening + design comment block) + `AGENTS.md` (this row). **Load-bearing acceptance** (binder-stated): Smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT PRESERVED (Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2). Class B fixture VM != JIT (Class B gap remains surface; VM=15 dynamic / JIT=NotImplemented). Class C fixture VM != JIT (Class C gap remains surface; both surface but at different layers — VM at ckpt-2 cascade, JIT at print operand kind). **Per-class user-function coverage matrix** post-fix per inventory §B.2: Class A = COVERED (closure-wave-1 + cluster-2-cw-B annotated-binding widening), Class B = SURFACE-AND-STOP (genuine fix in compile_expr_function_call value-call return-kind territory), Class C = SURFACE-AND-STOP (genuine fix in let-binding receiver-kind tracker population territory). **Imprecision-pattern instances surfaced** (cumulative 44→48; 4 new instances all caught pre-merge): 45 (binder's "extend lower_function_detailed seeding for Class B/C" prediction REFUTED by empirical evidence — closure bodies with inferred typed-array params have no annotation source available at lower_function_detailed; same architectural-prediction-subclass pattern as V3-S6a..e); 46 (binder's "Class C migration is MIR-side resolver extension" prediction PARTIALLY REFUTED — V3-S6b monomorph_method_returns resolver DOES stamp method-call destinations BUT only when the second .map specializes; bytecode-time specialization decision gates the chain; out-of-territory); 47 (annotated-binding widening fix lands cleanly but doesn't close Class B or Class C — narrows the gap by handling explicitly-annotated chain bindings without addressing the inferred case); 48 (cluster-2-cw-B Round 2 territory bound `lower_function_detailed + helpers.rs` does not actually contain Class B/C genuine fixes — territory was MIR-side per binder but genuine fixes are compile-time bytecode-emission layer; surface-and-stop discipline preserved over fabricated MIR-side recovery). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE — fix bounded to existing infrastructure; Class B + Class C surface-and-stop per existing discipline. **Refusal discipline preserved**: no ValueWord/Bool-default; no parallel-implementation framings; no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim descriptors for the deferred Class B/C territories; no new StatementKind variants; refuse #10 anti-deferral honored (Class B/C are NOT "preserve for cluster-1+" / "defer to follow-up" — they ARE genuinely-intractable within this binder's MIR-side territory bound per §2.7.5.1 layer-separation discipline). **Close gates** (devenv wrapper): `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 (clean per `just check-clean`); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT PRESERVED; AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. | cluster-2-cw-B-class-bc-coverage Round 2 (Class A widening landed + Class B + Class C SURFACE-AND-STOP per empirical-verification-first dispatch + Reading 4 in-scope-recovery NOT applicable — fix territories are compile-time bytecode-emission layer, not MIR-side per binder's territory bound) | `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/stmt.rs::lower_var_decl` (Class A widening: empty-array-literal gate widened to ALL typed-array-annotated bindings) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | partial-close (Class A widening landed; | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-cluster-2-cw-IC-class-c` | `../shape-cluster-2-cw-IC-class-c` | **CLOSED 2026-05-16 — cluster-2-cw-IC-class-c Class C coverage fix per cw-B-class-bc-coverage close report empirical disposition.** Phase 3 cluster-2 Round 3 cw-IC-class-c — closes Class C method-chain intermediate slot coverage by populating `local_array_element_types` / `module_binding_array_element_types` at let-binding time when the RHS is a typed-array-producing method call whose specialized return is `Array<C>`. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 2a12929f` (post-Round-2 close including cw-B Class A widening + jit-string-const-loop-retain + cw-E-fix). **Class C territory**: per cw-B close report empirical finding (commit `ad8b277b` deliverable), `local_array_element_types` at `compiler/mod.rs:943` was declared + initialized empty at `compiler_impl_initialization.rs:105` but NEVER WRITTEN by any code path (grep-verified zero `.insert` sites in `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/`); the side-table reader at `monomorphization/type_resolution.rs:1443` was therefore dead-code. The canonical Class C fixture `/tmp/cw-B-class-c-method-chain.shape` (`let xs: Array<int> = [1,2,3,4,5]; let doubled = xs.map(|x|x*2); let trebled = doubled.map(|y|y+1); print(trebled.sum())`) confirmed the gap pre-fix: the SECOND `.map(...)` could not specialize because `concrete_type_for_expr(doubled)` returned None at bytecode-emission time (the V3-S6b conduit MIR-resolver runs at link/finalize time AFTER bytecode emission per `compiler_impl_reference_model.rs:1635-1644` — it cannot retroactively re-specialize already-emitted CallMethod bytecode). **Fix shape**: two-layer wire at `compile_statement(VariableDecl)` in `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/statements.rs` for both local-slot (line ~4878) and module-binding (line ~4595) paths. Layer 1: populate `local_array_element_types` / `module_binding_array_element_types` via the new helper `specialized_call_return_concrete_type` (added in `monomorphization/type_resolution.rs`) which chains AST span + `current_function` → `monomorphized_method_call_sites[(span, current_fn)]` (populated by `try_monomorphize_method_call` / `_with_closures` per ADR-006 §2.7.5 V3-S6b conduit) → specialized FunctionId → `function_defs[specialized_name].return_type` (the substituted AST annotation written by `substitution::substitute_function_def`) → `concrete_type_from_annotation(annotation)` reduction. Layer 2: mirror `set_local_type_info` / `set_module_binding_type_info` write of the `Vec<elem>` type name into the type_tracker. **Both layers are load-bearing**: Layer 1 alone closes `identifier_concrete_type`'s side-table arm but the second `.map(...)` still falls through to the generic `CallMethod` path because `resolve_receiver_extend_type` (`helpers.rs:3826`) consults the type_tracker (NOT the side-table) to gate the UFCS extend-function lookup for `Vec.map` at `compile_expr_method_call:2116`; Layer 2 supplies the type_tracker entry. **Placement discipline**: the fix runs AFTER `propagate_initializer_type_to_slot` because the propagation path's fallback writes `VariableTypeInfo::unknown()` (helpers.rs:3590-3596) when the method-call's cleared `last_expr_type_info` provides no propagation source — wiping a prior type_tracker entry. Method-call return-kind clearing happens at `compile_expr_method_call` line 2197/2287/2438 per the method-result clearing pattern, so the initializer-propagate fallback unconditionally hits the unknown-write arm for method-call RHS. **LoC delta**: +190 / -0 across 2 files: `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/monomorphization/type_resolution.rs` (+59 — new `specialized_call_return_concrete_type` public helper with comprehensive §2.7.5 / §2.7.7 #9 rationale) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/statements.rs` (+131 — module-binding-path block at line ~4595 + local-slot-path block at line ~4878 with placement-rationale doc-comments) + `AGENTS.md` (this row). **Load-bearing acceptance criterion MET**: Class C fixture VM=35 / JIT=35 (was VM=ckpt-2 surface "Receiver kind: UInt64" / JIT=NotImplemented(SURFACE) pre-fix); VM == JIT preserved. Smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved: Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=20/20, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2 (binder Smoke 2 brief stated 30; canonical local fixture is `xs=[1,2,3,4]` not `[1,2,3,4,5]`, yielding 2+4+6+8=20 — measurement matches actual fixture content). Test4 fixture `/tmp/cw-B-test4-explicit-doubled-annot.shape` (explicit-annotation chain) preserved at VM=35 / JIT=35 (was 35 pre-fix per cw-B Class A widening). cw-B fixtures preserved: test1 (`xs.map(|x|x*2).sum()` direct) VM=30 / JIT=30; test2 (chain with sum) VM=90 / JIT=90; test3 (`print([2,4,6,8,10])`) VM=`[2,4,6,8,10]` / JIT=SURFACE on `print` Ptr(TypedArray) FFI body wiring — pre-existing baseline failure unaffected by this fix (verified by git stash + re-run at HEAD 2a12929f). Class B fixture preserved: VM=15 / JIT=SURFACE — unchanged (sibling cw-IB-class-b territory). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE — fix bounded to existing infrastructure (the existing `local_array_element_types` + `module_binding_array_element_types` side-tables + the existing type_tracker `set_*_type_info` helpers; no new StatementKind variants; no new opcodes; no new side-tables). **Refusal discipline preserved**: no ValueWord resurrection; no Bool-default fallback (the helper returns None on any chain link absence → slot stays unstamped → §2.7.7 #9 surface-and-stop preserved); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings (broader-family regex zero hits in deliverable + diff + AGENTS.md row); no parallel-implementation framings (the fix populates the EXISTING dead-code side-table per binder's explicit "populate the EXISTING `local_array_element_types`" directive — does NOT add a parallel kind-classification side-table); no anti-deferral #10 (the fix IS in-scope per Class C territory bound at `compile_statement(VariableDecl)`); no Ptr-newtype-shim defection #11; no new clippy regressions on touched code. **ADR-fit**: §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time (the specialized callee's substituted return-type annotation IS the proof — no runtime decode, no inference fabrication, no Bool-default; walks the same side-tables used by the link-time MIR resolver but at bytecode-emission time so subsequent statements see the type before the resolver runs); §2.7.6 Q8 carrier-API-bound discipline preserved (no per-heap-variant accessor leakage). **Imprecision-pattern instances surfaced** (cumulative 48→52; 4 new instances all caught pre-merge): 49 (binder's "binder-specified expected output VM=20 / JIT=20" REFUTED by arithmetic — `[1,2,3,4,5].map(|x|x*2)` = `[2,4,6,8,10]`; `.map(|y|y+1)` = `[3,5,7,9,11]`; `.sum()` = 35; binder's "20" likely confused Smoke 2 fixture output with Class C fixture output during brief drafting; load-bearing VM == JIT criterion empirically met at the correct arithmetic value 35); 50 (cw-B close report's "the side-table `local_array_element_types` checked at type_resolution.rs:1443 is declared but NEVER WRITTEN" was CORRECT but the Layer 2 type_tracker requirement was UNSTATED — the close report identified the dead-code side-table but did not surface that populating it alone was insufficient because `resolve_receiver_extend_type` consults the type_tracker for UFCS extend-func lookup; my fix surfaces this two-layer requirement empirically via the iterative debug-then-fix cycle); 51 (placement-order constraint: my initial fix at the wrong site BEFORE `propagate_initializer_type_to_slot` was silently wiped by the propagation's unknown-fallback arm; surfaced via debug trace `[cw-IC-DBG-LOAD] got=Some(None)` — same architectural-prediction-subclass pattern as V3-S6a..e + empirical-verification §2.4 producer-vs-consumer); 52 (Class A widening fix at MIR-side `local_typed_array_element_types` covers a DIFFERENT side-table than Class C bytecode-side `local_array_element_types` — same naming-collision class as cw-B close report's confusion between the two; empirically dispositioned via grep + the V3-S6b conduit producer/consumer chain trace). **Close gates** (devenv wrapper): `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 (clean per `just check-clean`); `cargo check --workspace --lib --features shape-jit/jit-trace` EXIT=0 (feature-ON build); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved; Class C fixture VM == JIT = 35; AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. | cluster-2-cw-IC-class-c (Round 3 Class C coverage fix per cw-B close report empirical disposition; sibling Round 3 dispatches `cluster-2-cw-IB-class-b` for Class B + `cluster-2-cw-D-fam12-jit-print` for §E per-HeapKind kinded jit_print — territories disjoint) | `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/monomorphization/type_resolution.rs` (new `specialized_call_return_concrete_type` public helper) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/statements.rs` (Class C wire at compile_statement(VariableDecl) for local-slot + module-binding paths) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (load-bearing acceptance criterion MET: Class C fixture VM == JIT = 35; | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-cluster-2-char-literal-mir` | `../shape-cluster-2-char-literal-mir` | **CLOSED 2026-05-16 — cluster-2 Round 4 cw-D-fam12 Char-literal MIR-lowering kind-source gap fix (instance 57 follow-up).** Phase 3 cluster-2 Round 4 sub-cluster — closes the upstream MIR-lowering kind-source gap surfaced by cw-D-fam12 Round 3 instance 57 (Char production-fixture VM=A / JIT=65 divergence). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ eca52df7` (post-Round-3 close: cw-D-fam12 + cw-IC + cw-IB merged; smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved). **Pre-fix divergence reproducer** (`print('A')` under release binary): VM prints `A` (compiler/literals.rs:32 `Literal::Char(c) => Constant::Char(*c)` preserves Char kind through bytecode emission) / JIT prints `65` (MIR lowering at `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs:1505` `Literal::Char(c) => MirConstant::Int(*c as i64)` lost Char kind; JIT's `operand_slot_kind` stamped `NativeKind::Int64` at the `MirConstant::Int(_)` arm; print Call-terminator dispatch matched the `NativeKind::Int64` arm at `terminators.rs:461` and routed to `jit_print_i64(65)` instead of the `NativeKind::Char` arm at `terminators.rs:679` that routes to `jit_print_char(65 as u32)` → renders `A`). **Post-fix**: VM=A / JIT=A (VM == JIT load-bearing acceptance MET). **Fix shape — Design Option A** (new `MirConstant::Char(char)` variant; parallel to existing `MirConstant::Str` vs `MirConstant::StringId` and `MirConstant::Float` vs `MirConstant::Int` discrimination; no ADR amendment required because §2.7.5 amendment Round 19 S1.5 W12-nativekind-scalar-additions already lands `NativeKind::Char` as a 4-byte scalar variant per `kinded_slot.rs:594-597` `KindedSlot::from_char` / `as_char` infrastructure — this fix mechanically threads the Char kind through the MIR producer layer to the JIT operand-kind classifiers that consume it). **Why Option A over Option B (side-table)**: Option B would add a parallel kind-classification side-table at MIR-lowering time to mark slots holding Char codepoints — refused per CLAUDE.md "do NOT add a parallel kind-classification side-table; extend existing infrastructure" + ADR-005 §1 single-discriminator discipline + the deletion-fate of all "ValueBits shim / FFI-boundary bridge / decode hop" framings. The MirConstant enum IS the existing kind-discriminator infrastructure at the MIR layer; adding a Char variant extends it in the same shape as MirConstant::Float (a scalar variant with no Arc payload). **Edits across 6 files** (+59/-1 LoC): (1) `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/types.rs::MirConstant` — added `Char(char)` variant + Display arm (`'<c>'` mirror of `Literal::Char` Display in `shape-ast/src/ast/literals.rs:80`); (2) `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/lowering/expr.rs:1505` — `Literal::Char(c) => MirConstant::Char(*c)` (was `MirConstant::Int(*c as i64)`); (3) `crates/shape-vm/src/mir/return_ownership.rs::classify_constant` — added `MirConstant::Char(_)` to the `NewlyOwned` scalar group alongside Int/Bool/Float (no Arc payload, no Static lifetime — same as Int per §2.7.5 R19 S1.5 "Drop/Clone arms for `NativeKind::Char`: no-op (inline scalar; no `Arc<T>` payload)"); (4) `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs::infer_constant_kind` — added `MirConstant::Char(_) => Some(NativeKind::Char)`; (5) `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/ownership.rs::compile_constant` — added `MirConstant::Char(c)` arm emitting `iconst(types::I32, *c as i64)` (Cranelift I32 width matches `ValueSlot::from_char`'s low-32-bits storage shape per `kinded_slot.rs` constructor; the print dispatch's `NativeKind::Char` arm at `terminators.rs:687-703` already narrows I32/I64/I8 to I32 before calling `jit_print_char(u32)`); (6) `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs::operand_slot_kind` — added `Operand::Constant(MirConstant::Char(_)) => Some(NativeKind::Char)` arm mirroring `infer_constant_kind` for the parallel-kind track at consumer sites. **Acceptance criteria MET**: Char-literal fixture (`print('A')`) — VM=`A` / JIT=`A` (was VM=`A` / JIT=`65` pre-fix); Smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT PRESERVED at canonical fixture (Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2); pre-existing JIT-FFI tier `print_char_scalar_matches_vm` unit test still passes (13/13 `ffi::conversion::heap_arm_print_tests::*` PASS). **VM impact**: NONE — VM bytecode compiler does not consume MirConstant scalars; it has its own pipeline through `compiler/literals.rs` (`Literal::Char(c) => Constant::Char(*c)`) and emits the Char kind via VM-side stack ABI per ADR-006 §2.7.7 parallel-kind track. Only the JIT side reads MirConstant for code-emission (verified via `grep -rn "MirConstant" crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/` showing only `ClosurePlaceholder` + `Function` arms touched by VM compiler — both unrelated to scalar Char). **Imprecision-pattern instances surfaced** (cumulative 70→71; 1 new instance caught pre-merge in-scope): 71 (related but separate kind-source gap surfaced at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/monomorphization/type_resolution.rs:1482` `Literal::Char(_) => Some(ConcreteType::I8)` — the monomorphization type-resolution pipeline maps Char to `ConcreteType::I8` rather than the existing `ConcreteType::Char` variant defined by §2.7.5 R19 S1.5 W12-nativekind-scalar-additions; companion gap at `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/concrete_conv.rs:280` `"i8" | "char" => return Ok(ConcreteType::I8)` likewise erases the char→Char path; out-of-territory per binder's cw-D-fam12 follow-up scope which bounds to MIR-lowering kind-source gap at `mir/lowering/expr.rs:1505`; same architectural-prediction-subclass pattern as cw-IC instance 60 — Layer-N+1 surface revealed post-Layer-N fix; tracked for a future cluster-2-monomorph-char-concrete-type or cluster-1 follow-up sub-cluster). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE — fix bounded to existing infrastructure (extends existing MirConstant enum + 5 consumer-site arms in the canonical exhaustive-match form). **Refusal discipline preserved**: no ValueWord resurrection; Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 2a12929f` (canonical HEAD post-Round-2 close: cw-B annotated-binding widening + jit-string-const-loop-retain + cw-E-fix merged). **Pre-fix Class B fixture** (`let xs: Array<int> = [1,2,3,4,5]; let f = |inner| inner.sum(); print(f(xs))`): VM=15 / JIT=NotImplemented(SURFACE) — `print` operand NativeKind is None per Route A surface-and-stop dispatch. **Post-fix Class B fixture**: VM=15 / JIT=15 (VM == JIT load-bearing acceptance MET). **Genuine fix territory** (per cw-B Round 2 close report empirical disposition): bytecode-emission value-call return-kind classification at `compile_expr_function_call::compile_expr_function_call` (`crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/function_calls.rs:498-619`), NOT MIR-side `lower_function_detailed` (which has no annotation source for inferred params per cw-B Round 2 imprecision 45). **Two-track fix shape** (ADR-006 §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time discipline; no tag-bit decode, no Bool-default, no fabricated default): (1) **Side-table** `BytecodeProgram.value_call_return_concrete_types: HashMap<(Span, Option<usize>), ConcreteType>` populated at value-call site with the caller-context-inferred return ConcreteType (mirrors `monomorphized_method_call_sites` shape exactly per Q11/Q12 composite-key discipline); consumed by new resolver `value_call_returns` in `compiler_impl_reference_model.rs::propagate_concrete_types_through_mir` and new value-call destination-stamping pass in `helpers.rs::infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_resolvers` that walks Call-terminators with `Operand::Copy/Move/MoveExplicit(Place::Local)` callees and stamps the destination ConcreteType — closes the print operand kind classification chain. (2) **Closure-body MIR seed** — retroactively populate `mir.local_typed_array_element_types[param_slot] = Array<elem>` for the closure body's MIR via `Arc::get_mut` on `func.mir_data` at value-call site (Arc strong-count invariant: exclusive at bytecode-emission time, before content-addressed program build runs); the existing seed pass at `helpers.rs:623` consumes this at `propagate_concrete_types_through_mir` time, the closure body's `slot_kinds[inner]` gets `Ptr(TypedArray)`, and the JIT-MIR `.len()`/`.sum()` dispatch reaches its kinded fast path → raw Int64=15 return bits (was TAG_NULL=`0xfffb000000000000` pre-fix because the closure body's JIT compilation had no type info for `inner`). Both tracks are required: track 1 alone gives "no SURFACE" but the closure body returns TAG_NULL bits and print prints garbage; track 2 alone fixes the closure body return but the outer Call-terminator destination stays unstamped and print still surfaces. **Infrastructure additions**: `BytecodeProgram.value_call_return_concrete_types` + `ContentAddressedProgram.value_call_return_concrete_types` + `LinkedProgram.value_call_return_concrete_types` side-tables (linker propagation through all 3 sites: linker.rs parallel + sequential paths + linked_to_bytecode_program reverse); `ClosureBodyPeek { params, body, return_type, function_index }` struct + `BytecodeCompiler.local_callable_closure_bodies: HashMap<u16, ClosureBodyPeek>` + `module_binding_callable_closure_bodies` (parallel local/module-binding maps mirror existing `local_callable_*` infrastructure); `update_callable_binding_from_expr` populates peek at let-binding time including `function_index` lookup via `__closure_<n>` name pattern; `clear_callable_binding` releases peek; per-function snapshot/restore in `functions.rs` `compile_function` alongside existing `local_callable_*` save/restore sites (3 sites: take, early-return restore, late-return restore); `infer_closure_body_return_type_name_with_caller_context` new public function (extends `_with_outer` variant with `caller_arg_type_names: &[Option<String>]` slice that seeds `param_types[name]` when no annotation/body-literal pairing exists); `expr_type` walker `Expr::MethodCall` arm extended for invariant-return methods (size/len/length/count → int, isEmpty/contains/has → bool) + parametric Vec<scalar> methods (sum/mean/min/max/get → element type) — mirrors JIT-side `well_known_method_return_kind` + `parametric_method_return_kind_from_receiver` classifier shape (`crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/types.rs:818-1019`) as the single source of truth for kind-classification across both bytecode-emission and JIT-MIR layers. **Smoke matrix 4/4 VM == JIT preserved at canonical fixture**: Smoke 1=4950/4950, Smoke 2=30/30, Smoke 3=x/x, Smoke 4=2/2. **Class A regression test** (`let xs: Array<int> = [..]; let doubled: Array<int> = xs.map(|x|x*2); let trebled = doubled.map(|y|y+1); print(trebled.sum())`): VM=35 / JIT=35 (no regression to Round 2 cw-B annotated-binding widening). **LoC delta**: +875 / -3 across 15 files: `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/core_types.rs` (+42) + `crates/shape-vm/src/bytecode/content_addressed.rs` (+29) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/mod.rs` (+60; the closure-body MIR seed populates `mir.local_typed_array_element_types` (MIR-side) instead, which is the JIT-MIR conduit's actual seed source per `helpers.rs:623`; the two side-tables have similar names but different layers (bytecode-side vs MIR-side) — no territory overlap with cw-IC); no Bool-default fallback (skipped seed when caller arg type unresolved); no bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framings in code/comments/AGENTS.md row (broader-family regex zero hits — the new helpers are `value_call_returns_top` / `_per_fn` / `infer_closure_body_return_type_name_with_caller_context`, not adapter-style names);
