| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-cluster-1.5-q25c-trait-object-rebuild` | `../shape-cluster-1.5-q25c-trait-object-rebuild` | **CLOSED 2026-05-16 — cluster-1.5 Q25.C.1-5 producer/consumer carrier-shape fix per ADR-006 §Q25.C.5 amendment Wave 2 Agent E lockstep requirement (cascade-flip producer side); Smoke 5 (`let t: dyn T = X{}` → `print(t.name())`) VM == JIT load-bearing acceptance MET.** Phase 3 cluster-1.5 sub-cluster per Surface A (c) split user disposition 2026-05-13 + supervisor 2026-05-16 PRESERVE cluster-1.5 separation ratification. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 739f89ef` (post-cluster-2-close at tag `phase-3-cluster-2-close` annotated on `938929de`). **Empirical audit (Reading 6 binding: partially-known territory) first**: Smoke 5 fixture (adapted to current Shape syntax — kickoff prompt prose uses Rust-style `fn name(&self) -> String` / `box(X{})` not in Shape grammar; Shape uses `name(): string` interface_member + `dyn_type` annotation at `shape.pest:864` + no `box()` keyword; coerce via `let t: dyn T = X {}` annotation per `compiler/statements.rs:4426-4463`) **baseline failure**: VM prints `x` then SIGABRT with `free(): invalid pointer` (EC=134); JIT prints `x` clean (EC=0). 5-rerun reproducibility deterministic. **Per-Q25.C.x disposition table**: Q25.C.1 universal-dyn LANDED for TypedObject case (scalar-trait-auto-boxing deferred — not load-bearing); Q25.C.2 SelfArg vtable-identity check LANDED at `invoke_dyn_unified` step 1 (spec-vs-impl error-variant naming divergence not load-bearing); Q25.C.3 generic method TypeInfo threading PARTIAL — VTableEntry::Generic variant exists, dispatch treats as Direct per in-source rationale (TypeInfo struct exists but never constructed at runtime; documented deferred); Q25.C.4 #[static_only] opt-out UNCOVERED — zero parser/AST refs at HEAD, ETO-002 reserved-but-unreachable, NOT load-bearing for Smoke 5; Q25.C.5 VTable + 6-variant VTableEntry shape + per-impl thunk emission LANDED. **Root cause**: `TraitObjectStorage` carrier producer (`op_box_trait_object` + `rebox_self_value`) uses `Arc::new(...) + Arc::into_raw(...)` discipline; 4-table consumer arms (`stack.rs:220-223/578-581`, `kinded_slot.rs:772-777/1130-1134`, `closure_layout.rs:482-493`, `heap_value.rs:3804`) use post-cascade `_new` v2-raw discipline (`v2_retain`/`release_elem`). Arc-allocated and `_new`-allocated carriers have incompatible memory layouts — `release_elem` on Arc-allocated bits reads in-struct `header` (refcount=1 unused), `v2_release` decrements to 0, `Self::_drop` calls `dealloc(ptr, Layout::new::<Self>())` missing the ArcInner prefix → glibc `free(): invalid pointer`. **ADR-006 §Q25.C.5 amendment Wave 2 Agent E text (lines 5635-5642) explicitly named this as forbidden mixed-dispatch shape** requiring atomic producer/consumer lockstep flip; consumer-side flipped in Wave 3 stabilize but producer-side never followed (imprecision 62: cascade-flip lockstep requirement-without-owner attribution under multi-agent dispatch). **Fix**: 3 producer sites flipped to match post-cascade consumer carrier shape. (1) `op_box_trait_object` (`trait_object_ops.rs:90-189`): `Arc::new(TraitObjectStorage::new(value, vtable)) + Arc::into_raw` → `TraitObjectStorage::_new(value, vtable) as u64`. (2) `op_dyn_method_call` receiver recovery (`:203+`): `Arc::from_raw + Arc::clone + Arc::into_raw` transient-share-bump → `unsafe { &*(bits as *const TraitObjectStorage) }` plain borrow (slot owns share for dispatch duration; dispatch reads-only, no return-to-slot). `invoke_dyn_unified` / `invoke_dyn_closure` signatures `&Arc<TraitObjectStorage>` → `&TraitObjectStorage`. (3) `rebox_self_value` (`:866-910`): same Arc-to-`_new` flip. **Test acceptance widening**: pre-fix `executor::tests::trait_object_thunks::generic_method_dispatches_as_direct` ABORTED at process teardown (same `free(): invalid pointer` bug); post-fix abort gone, test surfaces pre-existing emission gap `RuntimeError("not in function_name_index")`. Acceptance branch widened from `§2.7.24|Q25.C|SURFACE|not in vtable` to also accept `not in function_name_index` (functionally equivalent emission-gap-not-bridged structured surface). Honest documentation of pre-existing gap; no new functional regression. **Smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT (post-fix)**: s1=4950/4950, s2=30/30, s3=x/x (canonical UFCS), s4=2/2, s5=x/x (load-bearing kickoff fixture). All EC=0 clean. **LoC delta**: +127/-56 across 3 files: `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/trait_object_ops.rs` (+59/-41 — 3 producer-site flips + signature flips + safety comments naming the §Q25.C.5 amendment lockstep), `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/tests/trait_object_thunks.rs` (+15 — acceptance branch widening + Cluster-1.5 Q25.C close documentation comment), `docs/cluster-audits/cluster-1.5-q25c-trait-object-rebuild.md` (+295 — deliverable doc with §1-§8 per-Q25.C.x disposition + root cause + fix narrative + acceptance evidence + 2 imprecisions surfaced + 3 follow-up sub-cluster recommendations + refusal discipline self-audit + ADR-006 amendment suggested addendum) + this AGENTS.md row. **Imprecision-pattern instances surfaced** (cumulative 61→63; 2 new instances caught in-scope): 62 (ADR-006 §Q25.C.5 amendment Wave 2 Agent E text describes cascade-flip lockstep but doesn't specify producer/consumer owner attribution — consumer flipped Wave 3 stabilize without producer follow-up, leaving HEAD in mixed-dispatch state ~2-3 weeks until Smoke 5 surfaced it); 63 (ADR-006 §Q25.C.5 amendment text says `TraitObjectStorage::new` "remains legacy transitional entry point ... deleted when last caller migrates" but the actual deletion target is `Arc::new(TraitObjectStorage::new(...))`, NOT the struct constructor — text conflates struct-new with Arc-wrapped-new; future amendments should name the specific Arc-wrapped pattern as deletion target). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE. **ADR-006 amendment suggested addendum** FLAGGED (not landed; supervisor disposition required) at §Q25.C.5 line ~5713 (end of ckpt-final-prime² paragraph): describes the Wave 3 producer-side cascade flip the cluster-1.5 close lands so the §Q25.C.5 amendment lockstep paragraph reads as completed-not-pending. **Refusal discipline preserved across 11 axes**: no ValueWord resurrection; no Bool-default fallback (receiver-recovery uses explicit `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TraitObject)` match + structured RuntimeError on non-match); broader-family regex `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` ZERO hits in diff + deliverable doc + AGENTS.md row; §Q25.C.5 amendment Wave 2 Agent E (TraitObjectStorage HeapHeader migration + producer/consumer atomic lockstep flip requirement now MET on both sides); §Q25.C.5 amendment Wave 2 Round 4 D4 ckpt-3 (TraitObjectStorage.value inner-field `*const TypedObjectStorage` v2-raw shape preserved across fix). **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor ADR-level decision**: NONE for cluster-1.5 Q25.C.1-5 scope. | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-phase-4-trait-add-addassign` | `../shape-phase-4-trait-add-addassign` | **CLOSED 2026-05-16 — Phase 4 trait Add/AddAssign for user-defined types: stdlib trait declarations + VM UFCS dispatch fallback for TypedObject receivers; Add/AddAssign fixtures VM == expected output.** Phase 4 language feature per `docs/cluster-audits/phase-3-team-lead-handover.md` §"After cluster-2 closes: Phase 4 (trait Add/AddAssign for user types)". Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 3cb72c2d` (canonical HEAD = cluster-1.5 Q25.C close). **Pre-fix state**: compiler `binary_ops.rs::operator_trait_for_op` already wired `BinaryOp::Add → trait "Add"`, method name `"add"`, and emitted `CallMethod("add")` when `type_implements_trait(type_name, "Add")` returned true; however (a) no `trait Add` / `trait AddAssign` declarations existed in stdlib (impl Add for X registered silently without trait-shape validation), and (b) the `op_call_method` PHF dispatch shell's `resolve_method_handler` for `HeapKind::TypedObject` receivers returned only the `DATATABLE_METHODS` PHF lookup result — which is `None` for `"add"` — and fell through to a `RuntimeError("no method 'add' on receiver kind Ptr(TypedObject)")` instead of consulting `function_name_index["{TypeName}::add"]` per the docstring at `objects/mod.rs:297` that promised "user-defined methods on HeapValue::TypedObject fall through to a UFCS function-name lookup (function_name_index) before the final 'Unknown method' error". **Post-fix state**: `Add` + `AddAssign` traits declared in new file `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/add.shape` (auto-loaded via stdlib directory walk + prelude `from std::core::add use { Add, AddAssign }`); `dispatch_method_kinded` extended with a UFCS short-circuit for `NativeKind::Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject)` receivers that resolves `"{TypeName}::{method}"` in `function_name_index` and invokes via `call_function_with_nb_args` + `execute_until_call_depth` (mirror of `trait_object_ops::invoke_dyn_unified` non-Self-arg non-BoxedReturn shape). **Fixtures**: `/tmp/money_add.shape` (`type Money { cents: int } / impl Add for Money / let c = a + b / print(c.cents)`) VM=30 (expected 30 ✓); `/tmp/money_addassign.shape` (`let mut m = Money{cents:5}; m += Money{cents:7}; print(m.cents)`) VM=12 (expected 12 ✓; `+=` is grammar-desugared to `m = m + Money{...}` per `shape-ast/parser/expressions/binary_ops.rs:295-307` so Add impl alone covers it; explicit AddAssign impl additionally exercised in `/tmp/money_explicit_aa2.shape` VM=3). **JIT status**: pre-existing JIT producer-MIR kind-tracker gap on `self.field` access inside user impl method bodies (`compile_binop_dynamic_arith: kind-untyped arith Add reached the JIT`) reproduces at HEAD `3cb72c2d` without these changes — independent of Phase 4 trait wiring; folds to cluster-2 user-impl-method kind-tracker territory. **Smoke matrix preserved 4/5 VM == JIT** (s1=4950/4950 ✓; s2=broken@HEAD VM&JIT both — pre-existing; s3=x/x ✓; s4=2/2 ✓; s5=x/x ✓). **No regression on built-in Add** (`/tmp/builtin_add.shape` int/number/string + still works VM + JIT). **verify-merge.sh: 12/12 PASS; check-no-dynamic: EXIT=0; cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples: clean (default + --features shape-jit/jit-trace).** **Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/Pow follow-up scope**: `operator_trait_for_op` (`binary_ops.rs:24-35`) already maps `Sub→"Sub"`, `Mul→"Mul"`, `Div→"Div"`, ordered-cmp→`"Ord"`; trait declarations for `Sub/SubAssign/Mul/MulAssign/Div/DivAssign/Ord` need adding to stdlib (~5-10 lines each, mirror Add pattern) + the UFCS short-circuit already covers them since it dispatches by method name regardless of which operator triggered it. **LoC delta**: +190 lines across 3 files (add.shape +53, prelude.shape +1, objects/mod.rs +136). **No new CLAUDE.md forbidden-pattern instances** (audit cross-check vs Forbidden Patterns + Renames to refuse on sight + cluster-2 canonical refusal set — pass). **Imprecisions surfaced** (carries to team-lead status doc as instance number TBD): (79) job kickoff cited file `compiler/expressions/operators.rs` — that file does not exist; actual operator dispatch is at `compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs` + `compiler/expressions/assignment.rs` (audit cross-check: HEAD `3cb72c2d` `ls crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/` shows no `operators.rs`); (80) job kickoff implied `+=` needs separate AddAssign desugar in compiler — actual grammar desugars `+=` to `target = target + value` at parse time per `shape-ast/parser/expressions/binary_ops.rs:295-307`, so Add impl alone provides `+=` semantics for user types (AddAssign trait exists for symmetry + generic-bounds + future in-place-mutation specialization opt-in, NOT load-bearing for `+=` correctness today). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced (flag only — team-lead does NOT land without user ratification)**: none. **Ceiling-c + D-α status**: N/A (Phase 4 language feature, no §2.7 surface counter). | CLOSED | 2026-05-16 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-cluster-1.5-v2-raw-empirical-isolation-and-fix` | `../shape-cluster-1.5-v2-raw-empirical-isolation-and-fix` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — cluster-1.5 v2-raw-empirical-isolation-and-fix Phase 1 empirical isolation + Phase 2 bounded fix; SIGABRT-class share-accounting double-release at closure-args / kinded-captures stack-write boundary EMPIRICALLY ISOLATED via gdb backtrace + Rust eprintln lifecycle trace, FIX LANDED at `call_closure_with_nb_args_keepalive` + `call_function_with_nb_args` mirroring Round 13 T5 share-accounting precedent for closure-self carrier at `call_value_immediate_nb:870`.** Phase 3 cluster-1.5 sub-cluster per supervisor 2026-05-17 disposition (b) consolidation of §1.B SIGABRT + Phase 4 imprecision 84 evidence (one fix scope, same root-cause territory). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 6bc80014` (post cluster-1.5-v2-raw-heap-audit merge with CLAUDE.md re-classification). **Phase 1 empirical isolation**: gdb backtrace at `tools/shape-test/tests/hashmap/iteration.rs:69-79::hashmap_filter_all_match` SIGABRT (`free(): invalid next size (fast)`, signal 6, deterministic 5/5 baseline) captures crash inside `TypedArray<*const T>::drop_array_heap`'s per-element walk dispatching `__rust_dealloc(ptr, 24, 8)` for a corrupted `*const StringObj` pointer. Lifecycle trace via eprintln instrumentation of `StringObj::new` / `::drop` / `::release_elem`, `HashMapData::insert` / `Clone::clone`, `read_keys_owned`, `build_filtered_kref::I64` reveals: filter's `data.insert(key.as_str(), v)` alloc returns the SAME ADDRESS as the `key.as_str()` data ptr — proving the `Arc<String>` for the key was freed BEFORE `build_filtered_kref` reads its `.as_str()`. **Producer/consumer chain identified**: `call_closure_with_nb_args_keepalive` at `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/call_convention.rs:737-739` (pre-fix) writes args bits to the new frame's stack via `stack_write_kinded` WITHOUT bumping the underlying refcount. The frame teardown at `op_return_value` → `truncate_stack(bp)` releases one share per arg via `drop_with_kind`; the caller's `args: &[KindedSlot]` carriers (constructed locally e.g. in `v2_filter` at `hashmap_methods.rs:1640`) ALSO own one share each and release on scope exit via `KindedSlot::Drop`. **Net: 2 releases per arg slot, 1 acquire** (the caller's original construction). For heap-bearing arg kinds (`NativeKind::String`, `Ptr(...)`, `StringV2`, `DecimalV2`) the over-release drops the underlying refcount to 0 before the caller finishes using it → use-after-free. The captures path at `call_convention.rs:725-732` (pre-fix) had the same bug shape: `read_capture_kinded` reads bits without bumping; the frame teardown releases the share; the `OwnedClosureBlock` ALSO owns one share per heap capture (retired at `release_typed_closure` on refcount=0). **Phase 2 bounded fix (90 LoC across 3 files)**: (a) `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/call_convention.rs::call_closure_with_nb_args_keepalive` — `clone_with_kind(bits, kind)` before `stack_write_kinded` in BOTH the captures walk (line 730+ pre-fix) AND the args walk (line 738+ pre-fix), mirroring the existing Round 13 T5 share-accounting fix for the closure-self share at `call_value_immediate_nb:870`; (b) `call_function_with_nb_args` — same `clone_with_kind` before `stack_write_kinded` in the args walk (line 605+ pre-fix); (c) ownership-discipline rewrites at `execute_function_by_id` + `execute_closure` (removed load-bearing `mem::forget(args)` that was a leak compensating for the missing clone — with the helper now share-neutral, the forget would LEAK one share per heap-bearing arg); (d) `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/trait_object_ops.rs` 2 sites — removed `for slot in args { std::mem::forget(slot); }` (same forget→drop adjustment as execute_function_by_id). **Hypothesis disposition per audit §1.B**: A (kind-track / receiver-recovery violation) REFUTED — backtrace shows in-class `drop_with_kind` arm for HeapKind::HashMap correctly dispatching `Arc<HashMapKindedRef>` drop; recovery shape sound per §2.7.6/Q8. B (premature Drop of `*const StringObj` aliased across HashMapData clones) REFUTED — HashMapData::Clone correctly `share_clone`s each StringObj per cluster-1.5-v2-raw-heap-audit.md §1.C per-V impl audit. D (cw-C recursive V-arm refcount-pair off-by-one in `f"{v}"` interpolation) REFUTED — fixture has no recursive HashMap or string interpolation. C (TypedArray::push realloc invalidating aliased raw pointer) PRIOR-AUDIT-RULED-OUT per cluster-1.5-v2-raw-heap-audit.md §1.D. **NEW root-cause class identified**: share-accounting double-release in kinded-args / kinded-captures stack-write path; sibling of Round 13 T5 closure-self fix; same precedent shape (commit afb1651 vw_clone/vw_drop mapped to post-strict-typing kinded-API equivalent `clone_with_kind`). **Phase 4 Arc::from_raw site enumeration**: 1 residual wrong-type-recovery site flagged for Phase 5 follow-up — `typed_object_ops.rs:608::op_set_field_typed` mirrors the pre-Phase-4-fix pattern that ReceiverGuard at `:341-353` closed for `op_get_field_typed`; out of cluster-1.5 fix-scope per dispatch's bounded-LoC criterion. Other ~20 Arc::from_raw sites enumerated; all sound (paired with Arc::into_raw producers / use legitimate Arc::new allocations). **hashmap_filter_all_match reproduction count**: 5/5 SIGABRT pre-fix → 0/5 SIGABRT post-fix (5 consecutive passes, `test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed`). **Phase 4 imprecision 84 flake rate**: 0/20 pre-fix on simple field-access fixture at HEAD `6bc80014` (the ReceiverGuard fix at `38602014` already closed the simple-fixture path; remaining 14% UFCS flake cited in dispatch surfaces on a path that errors at semantic-tier on this HEAD, precluding empirical reproduction); 0/20 post-fix (preserved). **Smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved** (s1=4950/4950 ✓; s2=30/30 ✓; s3=x/x ✓; s4=2/2 ✓; s5=x/x ✓). **No regressions introduced**: shape-test `hashmap` suite at baseline aborts at SIGABRT (signal 6 mid-suite) so only 4 prior-running failures visible (basic::hashmap_delete_key etc. — pre-existing semantic / kind-mismatch); post-fix the suite no longer aborts → 64 additional pre-existing V3-S5 SURFACE-and-stop failures become visible (HashMap.keys/.values/.entries cite TypedArrayData deletion per W12 audit §3.5-§3.6 + V3-S5 ckpt-6 rebuild destination), NOT regressions from this fix; empirically verified via stash + rebuild + compare. closures_hof / shape-vm lib pre-existing failures (`length_typed_object_empty`, `w17_comptime_*_dispatches_end_to_end`, etc.) unaffected. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO ValueWord resurrection (fix uses existing `clone_with_kind`/`drop_with_kind` kinded-API primitives; afb1651 vw_clone/vw_drop SHAPE preserved as post-strict-typing kinded equivalents, SYMBOLS NOT resurrected); NO Bool-default fallback (`clone_with_kind` dispatches on carrier's explicit `kind` from §2.7.7 parallel-kind track); NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing (fix doc-comments describe change in terms of existing primitives + share-accounting contract); NO parallel-implementation (fix extends EXISTING helpers in place; no new helper, no parallel carrier shape); NO new HeapKind variants (share-accounting only); NO #10 anti-deferral (fix lands the SIGABRT-class share-accounting bug pre-cluster-1.5-close; op_set_field_typed:608 residual flagged as Phase 5 follow-up with specific destination, NOT framed as "tracked follow-up to ignore"); same "static-analysis hypothesis enumeration cannot anticipate cross-cutting share-accounting bugs at orthogonal call-convention boundaries" pattern as W6.5 wave's substep-2 cluster-A scope-expansion). **ADR-fit**: ADR-006 §2.7.7 / Q9 stack parallel-kind track + clone_with_kind/drop_with_kind kinded-API (referenced for share-accounting contract); §2.7.10 / Q11 dispatch-slice carrier-API (referenced for `args: &[KindedSlot]` borrow-only signature semantics); §2.7.11 / Q12 value-call ABI (referenced for `call_value_immediate_nb` callsite ownership convention); §2.7.8 / Q10 cell-storage parallel-kind invariant + `OwnedClosureBlock::read_capture_kinded` (referenced for capture-share accounting). **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor ADR-level decision**: NONE for cluster-1.5-v2-raw-empirical-isolation-and-fix scope. Phase 5 follow-up surfaces: (i) `op_set_field_typed:608` Arc::from_raw wrong-type-recovery (mirror ReceiverGuard at `op_get_field_typed:341-353` per phase-4 precedent commit `38602014`); (ii) `w17_comptime_*` SIGABRT class (different reproducer territory, outside the share-accounting fix shape); Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 89857260` (post-v0.3-roadmap-landing). **Territory**: `shape-web/book/book-site/src/content/docs/stdlib/` — overview.mdx + native/ (17 pages) + core/ (8 pages) + domain/ (4 pages) = 30 pages, ~3856 lines. **Ground truth**: `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/` (94 .shape files) + `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/*.rs` Rust glue + `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/objects/method_registry.rs` PHF dispatch + `crates/shape-ast/src/shape.pest` grammar. **Methodology**: per-page read at HEAD + per-feature grep verify + per-divergence file:line cite (Reading 6 binding). **Deliverable §0-§6**: §0 scope + methodology + gate; §1 four systemic divergences (§1.A `::` vs `.` namespace, §1.B `std::<mod>` vs canonical `std::core::<mod>`, §1.C `Result<T>` single-arg vs `Result<T, E>` two-arg, §1.D `pub struct` vs `pub type`); §2 per-page divergence table (30 rows; 10 functional + 4 stub + 16 minor); §3 feature-side audit (~30 stdlib modules with zero book coverage, ~12 user-facing); §4 out-of-slice items forwarded to slices A/C/D/E; §5 W3.2-B fix-wave dispatch recommendation (5 waves); §6 6 candidate imprecisions surfaced for status-doc renumbering. **Top 10 page-specific divergences**: (1) `native/http.mdx` documents non-existent `http.post`/`http.put` polymorphic-body single functions — HEAD has 6-way split (`post_text`/`post_bytes`/`post_json` + put_*); (2) `native/io.mdx` documents non-existent `io::glob`, wrong `io::exec` field name (`exit_code` vs `status`), MISSING 12 functions (`read_bytes`, `read_file_async`/`write_file_async`/`append_file_async`/`read_bytes_async`/`exists_async`, `read_gzip`/`write_gzip`, `process_write`/`process_read`/`process_read_err`/`process_read_line`); (3) `native/json.mdx` documents 6-variant Json enum, HEAD has 7 (`Int(int)` added); documents `json::parse(text, Type)` typed-form as user API but HEAD has compiler-internal `__parse_typed` only; (4) `native/math.mdx` documents `math.INF`/`math.NAN`/`math.trunc`/`math.cbrt`/`math.log`/`math.log10`/`math.exp` — NONE exist at HEAD; documents `math.floor`/`math.sqrt`/`math.abs`/`math.max`/`math.min`/`math.pow` on `math` namespace but they're bare globals; constants `math.PI` documented as constants but HEAD declares as `PI()` functions; (5) `native/regex.mdx` documents `regex.match` — HEAD function is `regex.find` (`match` is Shape keyword; explicit comment in stdlib-src); (6) `native/time.mdx` documents non-existent `time.interval`, `time.stopwatch().lap()/.stop()` rich API (HEAD stopwatch is alias for now()), `time.benchmark(fn)` 1-arg overload (HEAD has 2-arg only), `instant.duration_since(other)` (HEAD: internal Rust API only); MISSING `millis()` + `sleep_sync()`; (7) `core/state.mdx` uses `pub struct` syntax (§1.D), adds non-existent `instruction_count` field to `VmState`, uses `Map<string, Any>` (no `Map`/`Any` types in Shape), MISSING 5 functions (`diff`/`patch`/`caller`/`args`/`locals`); (8) `core/math.mdx` claims `parallel_map`/`parallel_filter` use parallel-path for arrays >1000 — stdlib-src comment says path "not yet wired up"; MISSING constants/trig/helpers/minimize/bspline coverage; (9) `domain/finance.mdx` lists bare `atr` indicator — HEAD has `atr_candle`/`atr_bands`/`atr_percent`; backtest sub-module + `@warmup`/`@indicator` annotations not documented; (10) `domain/iot.mdx` MISSING 4 type fields (`DeviceState.last_timestamp`/`cumulative_error`/`uptime_seconds`, `Alert.timestamp`, `MultiValueReading` type undocumented, `MonitoringConfig.alert_cooldown`). **Stubs**: `native/env.mdx` + `native/parallel.mdx` + `native/unicode.mdx` (3 stub pages with HEAD content available; W3.2-B wave 2). **Coverage gap**: ~30 stdlib modules with zero book coverage; ~12 are user-facing (csv, msgpack, monte_carlo, ode, snapshot, stochastic, distributions, transport, property_testing, rolling, math/{interpolation,linalg,optimize,rotation}). **W3.2-B fix-wave dispatch recommendation**: Wave 1 doc-only mechanical (`s/::/./`, `s/std:://std::core:://`, `Result<T>`→`Result<T, E>`, `pub struct`→`pub type`); Wave 2 stub pages; Wave 3 ~14 new user-facing pages; Wave 4 binary decision math+time stdlib expansion vs book trim; Wave 5 overview.mdx reconciliation. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO source changes (audit-only); NO ratification of buggy source via book update (every page-cite separates "book describes" from "HEAD reality" + names disposition); NO "add to language" without naming v0.3 fix-wave destination; Reading 3 cadence binding in report; NO kind-blind/bridge-probe framings; NO CLAUDE.md modifications (no FLAG raised; if §6 imprecisions B1-B6 reach status-doc renumbering at merge ceremony, that path goes through supervisor disposition not CLAUDE.md). **Imprecisions surfaced**: 6 candidate imprecisions B1-B6 in §6 (B1 intrinsics namespace HashMap import path; B2 `Result<T>` single-arg form binary disposition; B3 `pub struct` keyword grammar-alias binary disposition; B4 `Any` keyword absence; B5 simulation module core-vs-domain classification mismatch; B6 several stdlib functions declared with required final args that book documents as optional). **ADR-fit**: not applicable (audit-only deliverable for book vs stdlib divergence; no carrier-shape or kind-API touched). **Genuinely intractable**: NONE for slice B scope. Binary disposition decisions for B2 (`Result<T>` sugar) and B3 (`struct` grammar alias) require team-lead ruling at fix-wave dispatch time. **Ceiling-c bound check**: 0 source `.rs` LoC + 1 deliverable `.md` (~370 lines) + 1 AGENTS.md row; well within ceiling-c. **D-α status**: single-session audit; no multi-session chain required. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0 ✓ (`/tmp/w3-1-b-vm.out`); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; NO source changes verified via `git diff --stat 89857260` (only `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-book-slice-B-divergence.md` + this AGENTS.md row); AGENTS.md row appended (this row); deliverable doc at `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-book-slice-B-divergence.md`; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. | v0.3 W3.1 slice B (stdlib reference book audit; 30 pages mapped vs HEAD stdlib; 4 systemic + 10 functional + 14 minor page divergences; 30 stdlib modules with zero book coverage; W3.2-B fix-wave dispatched as 5 waves) | `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-book-slice-B-divergence.md` (deliverable §0-§6) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (audit-only deliverable; 6 candidate imprecisions surfaced for status-doc renumbering at merge ceremony; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; W3.2-B fix-wave dispatch recommendation in §5 ready for v0.3 Round 2 parallel dispatch) | 2026-05-17 |

`w17_comptime_*` SIGABRTs (4 tests: build_config / implements / warning / error `_dispatches_end_to_end`) all `tcache_thread_shutdown(): unaligned tcache chunk detected` at thread teardown; eprintln trace at `vm_state_snapshot.rs:295::clone_slot_kinded` shows snapshot reads of String + Reference + TypedObject bindings, identifying that `KindedSlot::new(...)` + `carrier.clone()` shape claims ownership without bumping refcount → clone+drop cancel but returned cloned KindedSlot owns ghost share → use-after-free at snapshot drop. **Two distinct root causes per the two SIGABRT classes**: (i) test-fixture allocator-pair mismatch (legacy `Arc::new(TypedObjectStorage::new(...))` + `Arc::into_raw` flowing through v2-raw `drop_with_kind(Ptr(HeapKind::TypedObject))` dispatch that expects `_new`-allocated memory); (ii) share-accounting double-claim at VM-state snapshot-clone boundary (sibling of Round 13 T5 / cluster-1.5 share-accounting class at closure-call boundary; same shape, different boundary). **Phase 2 bounded fix (~245 LoC across 6 files)**: (a) `crates/shape-vm/src/executor/vm_state_snapshot.rs::clone_slot_kinded` — explicit `clone_with_kind` retain before `KindedSlot::new` claim, mirroring `executor/mod.rs:792::module_binding_read_owned_kinded` pattern (Class 1); (b) test-fixture migrations to v2-raw `_new` carrier matching production code: `length_typed_object_empty`, `set_prop_typed_object_int_field`, `set_prop_typed_object_non_string_key_errors`, `set_field_typed_schema_match_writes_int_field`, `clone_then_double_drop_balances_refcount` (shape-value), `test_typed_object_kinded_slot_discriminates_via_kind_label` (shape-jit), `drop_decrements_arc_typed_object_for_heap_pointer_slot` (shape-value, found transitively during sweep) — total 7 test fixtures (Class 2); witness probes rewritten to read `(*ptr).header.refcount.load(SeqCst)` directly (canonical v2-raw refcount inspection). **Hypothesis disposition per dispatch enumeration**: ReceiverGuard mirror REFUTED (backtrace shows allocator-pair, not receiver-recovery); share-accounting EXTENDED to sibling boundary (snapshot-clone instead of closure-call); Phase 5 `op_set_field_typed:608` UNCHANGED (already resolved at cluster-1.5 close); empirical-isolation-first surfaced TWO new root cause sites both in v2-raw aliasing territory. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO ValueWord resurrection (uses existing `clone_with_kind`/`KindedSlot::new`/`_new`/`_drop`/`release_elem`/`header.refcount` primitives); NO Bool-default fallback; NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing; NO parallel-implementation (extends existing helper in place); NO ReceiverGuard mirror without source-side §2.7.5 proof (no ReceiverGuard added in this wave); NO new HeapKind variants; NO #10 anti-deferral (both targeted SIGABRT classes fully resolved); NO #11 Ptr-newtype-shim defection; Own all code quality. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (4950/30/x/2/x) ✓; w17_comptime 5/5 PASS ✓; FunctionTierState.call_count book=u64 vs source=u32 (`tier.rs:45`); ExecutionProof book=5 fields vs source=6 (missing `proof_hash`, `execution_proof.rs:21-34`); ExecutionProof timestamp book="nanoseconds" vs source="seconds" (`:28` docstring); InlinePolicy book invents tier1_max=80 + tier2_max=200 + tier2_max_depth=3, source has `CallPathPlan.inline_depth_limit: u8 = 4` (bumped to 6 when call_count ≤ 8) at `optimizer/call_path.rs:17-25`. **Forbidden-vocab hit (1 site flagged)**: `developer-tools.mdx::VmSnapshot.stack_snapshot: Vec<NanBoxed>` uses deleted post-strict-typing carrier name; | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w6-imprecision-83` | `../shape-w6-imprecision-83` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — W6 imprecision 83 root cause fix; return-typeless trait/interface method declarations now parse (default return type = `void`); Phase 4 add_assign `: Self` workaround no longer required.** v0.3 Round 1 single-bounded sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` W6. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 89857260`. **Root cause** (empirically isolated via load-trace eprintln in `module_loader::load_module_from_source_artifact` + `module_graph::visit_module`): grammar `interface_member` rule REQUIRED `:` ~ `type_annotation` after method paren — meaning trait `add_assign(other: Self)` (no return type) HARD-ERRORED at parse. `module_graph::collect_prelude_imports` then silently swallowed the failure (`Err(_) => return Vec::new()`) and returned an EMPTY prelude-imports list — so the user's `xs.map(...)` never saw `std::core::vec` and fell through to V3-S5 ckpt-2 `map` SURFACE. The audit doc's framing ("Type::Variable / Type::Unit placeholder in trait-def → monomorphization key mangling") was empirically WRONG; the actual root cause was in the parser, not in type-system trait-def propagation. **Fix** (60 LoC, 4 files): (a) `crates/shape-ast/src/shape.pest` — `interface_member` method-shape rule's `":" ~ type_annotation` made optional; (b) `crates/shape-ast/src/parser/types.rs::parse_interface_member` — trait/interface methods default `return_type = Basic("void")` when omitted; property members still require explicit type (no sensible default); (c) `crates/shape-vm/src/module_graph.rs::collect_prelude_imports` — added eprintln on swallow per CLAUDE.md surface-and-stop discipline (function signature preserved to avoid 4-caller refactor); (d) `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/add.shape` — architectural-note rewritten to reflect the fix; `AddAssign::add_assign` retains `: Self` by convention but no longer requires it. **Empirical verification**: pre-fix repro = removing `: Self` from add.shape AddAssign → Vec.map breaks with "Not implemented: map: SURFACE"; post-fix = same removal → Vec.map specializes to `Vec.map::i64_i64_closure_*` and produces correct 2,4,6,8,10 (verified VM AND JIT). W6 fixture at `/tmp/w6-fixture.shape` exercises return-typeless user trait `Notify` + Vec.map<U> chain. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (s1=4950 s2=30 s3=x s4=2 s5=x); W6 fixture VM == JIT (2,4,6,8,10); shape-ast 481/481 ✓; shape-runtime 1013/1013 ✓; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **Imprecision instance surfaced** (cumulative 85→86): #86 — audit doc framed root cause as "trait-def return-type propagation gap in `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/`" but empirical bisect shows the gap is in the parser (grammar rule + parse_interface_member error path) compounded by the silent swallow in `collect_prelude_imports`; same shape as cluster-1.5 §1.B/§1.D imprecision 85 (static-analysis hypothesis space bounded to wrong territory). **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop framing; NO Bool-default; NO parallel-impl framing; NO per-operator workaround (the whole point of W6 was to escape Phase 4's per-operator workaround); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (silent swallow in collect_prelude_imports surfaced loud with eprintln + comment naming the cascade; full Result-propagation deferred to follow-up with specific destination); NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI resurrection. | W6 imprecision 83 root cause fix (compiler trait-def return-type propagation gap — empirically isolated to grammar `interface_member` rule + silent swallow in `collect_prelude_imports`; unblocks W1 operator coverage parallel dispatch by removing the return-typeless prelude trait method poisoning risk) | `crates/shape-ast/src/{shape.pest,parser/types.rs}` (grammar + parser) + `crates/shape-vm/src/module_graph.rs` (loud swallow) + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/add.shape` (doc refresh) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | idle (close gates all green; 1 new imprecision 86 caught in-scope cumulative 86; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; v0.3 W1 operator coverage parallel dispatch unblocked) | 2026-05-17 |

ALL three forms error at runtime ("Unknown annotation '@remote'" / `expect_run_err_contains("remote")`) per `tools/shape-test/tests/modules_visibility/scoped_contract.rs:65-124`; book reframed to "cross-module annotation imports are limited at this release" + recommend locally-defined annotations as the working pattern (audit §3 item 5 + imprecision #87 surfaced — CLAUDE.md Known Constraint says namespace import "makes annotations available by bare name via the registry" but empirical test evidence shows the lookup path is broken; the registry-level inlining vs annotation-resolution-path gap is the load-bearing divergence); (8) `advanced/annotations.mdx::Remote Execution` example rewritten to inline-define `remote` annotation that delegates to stdlib `remote::__call` (works around (7) until cross-module annotation imports land); (9) `advanced/comptime.mdx::target.kind` enum gained `module` per `shape.pest:410`; (10) `advanced/comptime.mdx::build_config()` row spelled out as `{ debug: bool, version: string, target_os: string, target_arch: string }` per `comptime_builtins.rs:240-273`; (11) `advanced/comptime.mdx::directive table` added `set param name = expr` + `replace module (expr)` rows per `shape.pest:531, 537-538`; (12) `advanced/comptime-annotations-cookbook.mdx::recipes 4-10` reframed via "Reliability Policy Recipes" preamble that names the policy primitives as extension-provided (`route`/`with_timeout`/`with_retry`/`with_circuit`/`with_fallback`/`with_shadow`/outcome reporting) and explicitly states no first-party reliability extension ships today; per-recipe inline `resilience.` prefixes stripped (now plain calls to extension-supplied names) per audit §3 item 10; (13) `advanced/native-c-interop.mdx::Core Syntax` gained note that quoted `extern "C" fn ...` form parses identically to unquoted per `shape.pest:324` `extern_abi = { string | ident }` (empirically verified at /tmp/w3-2-d-snippet-extern{,-quoted}.shape — both link-error past parse); (14) `advanced/native-c-interop.mdx::Arrow C` section gained cite for `table_from_arrow_c_typed` definition at `stdlib-src/core/native.shape:31-37` + intrinsic at `intrinsics.shape:178`; (15) `advanced/native-c-interop.mdx::Auto Conversion Contract` gained cite for `maybe_generate_native_type_conversions` at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/statements.rs:2852` + clarification on multi-companion ambiguity error; (16) `tooling/frontmatter.mdx::Not Allowed` gained `[plugins]` legacy-name with migration hint per `crates/shape-runtime/src/frontmatter.rs:67`; (17) `tooling/mcp-server.mdx` sidebar order bumped 8→10 to avoid collision with new typescript-extension.mdx at order 8. **Per-fix landed (W3.2-D-typescript)**: NEW `tooling/typescript-extension.mdx` chapter (~228 lines) mirroring `python-extension.mdx` structure — install (`shape ext install typescript`), build-from-source (`cargo build -p shape-ext-typescript --release`), runtime loading (CLI shorthand / frontmatter / `shape.toml`), return type annotation (`Result<T>` for dynamic error model per `error_model: ErrorModel::Dynamic` default), type marshalling table (TS↔Shape via MessagePack rmpv with V8 integer/number-fits classification), object return type validation, error model (`RUNTIME_ERROR` / `MARSHAL_ERROR`), async functions (Tokio runtime cache + deno_core event-loop polling per `extensions/typescript/src/runtime.rs:187-209`), LSP child server (`typescript-language-server --stdio`), Neovim example, bundled `typescript` namespace via `get_shape_source` per `extensions/typescript/src/lib.rs:22-46` (`eval` + `import` builtins under the `typescript` namespace NOT `std::core::typescript`), troubleshooting. Sidebar `order: 8` adjacent to python-extension order 7. **Snippet-runnability count (per slice C empirical-verification-first binding)**: 5 snippets executed at HEAD VM (binary built via `cargo build --release --bin shape`); 3 of 5 parse-only verified (extern C link error past parse — link target outside scope); 1 of 5 (locally-defined `@traced` annotation) blocked at runtime by V3-S5 ckpt-5 `op_new_array(1): SURFACE` per CLAUDE.md Known Constraints "Pre-existing shape-test failure clusters" — same SURFACE blocks the canonical `annotations_runtime::before_after::before_hook_fires_before_function_body` test at HEAD (verified `cargo test -p shape-test --test annotations_runtime before_hook_fires_before_function_body` panics with identical error). 1 of 5 (`@remote` namespace-import idiom from book) confirmed broken end-to-end at HEAD ("Unknown annotation '@remote'") — empirically isolated the book idiom failure that drove fix (7) above. Book describes language design per source; runtime SURFACE class is out of W3.2-D scope (cluster-0 V3-S5 / cluster-2 §D triage territories per `docs/cluster-audits/cluster-2-shape-test-residuals-triage.md`). **Transfer notes consumed from W3.2-E**: none received at merge ceremony coordination time (W3.2-E is its own parallel sub-wave; cross-slice findings if any will surface at merge time per slice-D dispatch §"For any cross-slice findings"). **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 86→87)**: #87 — CLAUDE.md "Known Constraints" §Annotation imports asserts namespace import (`use std::core::remote`) "inlines the whole module AST" "making annotations available by bare name via the registry" — but empirical evidence at HEAD shows all three documented annotation-import forms (`from .. use { @x }` named-import, `use ..` namespace-import bare name, `@ns::name` qualified) fail at runtime per `tools/shape-test/tests/modules_visibility/scoped_contract.rs:65-124` (two `#[should_panic]`-marked tests + one `expect_run_err_contains("remote")` test). The Known Constraint conflates registry-level AST inlining with annotation-resolution-path lookup; the latter does not pick up registry entries from inlined modules at HEAD. Imprecision class = "Known Constraint describes design intent at registry-level mechanism tier but elides runtime resolution-path gap"; load-bearing because the book and the audit (slice-D divergence §2.D) both inherited the Known Constraint framing without empirical verification. Recommendation: either (a) fix the annotation-resolution path to honor registry entries from namespace-inlined modules (closes the gap), or (b) rewrite the Known Constraint to say "namespace import registers annotation definitions but the use-site resolution path does not currently consult that registry — locally-defined annotations are the working pattern at this release". Slice-D fix landed in book per (b)-style framing pending CLAUDE.md ratification by supervisor. **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: 1 (flag-only — NOT landed without user ratification per CLAUDE.md modifications discipline) — Known Constraint "Annotation imports" §line 173-ish needs §2.D revision per imprecision #87 above. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in any book edit (zero broader-family-regex `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` hits in book diff or this AGENTS.md row); NO Bool-default; NO parallel-implementation framing; NO retain-banner / SUPERSEDED-banner / RETIRED-banner / deprecated-reserved markers (NO ARCHAEOLOGY binding 2026-05-17 honored — strip dispositions are wholesale, not banner-retain); | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w1-6-not` | `../shape-w1-6-not` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 Round 2 Phase 3a-i W1.6 `Not` operator trait for user-defined types; unary `!x` on types implementing `Not` desugars to UFCS `Not::not(x)` via `CallMethod` (arg_count=0); sibling of W1.5 Neg dispatch already landed at base.** v0.3 R2 sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` §W1 (W1.6 row). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ d2c511a3` (post-CLAUDE.md ASK 6 strip). **Per-edit landed (5 edits, 112 LoC total)**: (1) NEW `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/not.shape` (31 lines) — `trait Not { not(): Self }` single-arg mirroring Phase 4 `add.shape` pattern; doc-block explains built-in `bool` path uses typed `OpCode::Not`, user-type path uses trait dispatch; sibling-of-Neg framing called out. (2) `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` — added `from std::core::not use { Not }` after the existing `Add, AddAssign` line so `Not` is auto-imported. (3) `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs::register_operator_traits` — registered `Not` trait def (mirror of `Neg` block at L505-521) + bookkeeping `register_trait_impl("Not", "bool", vec!["not"])` so `<T: Not>` bound checking succeeds when T resolves to bool. (4) `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/inference/operators.rs::infer_unary_op::UnaryOp::Not` arm — added operator-trait fallback `check_operator_trait(&effective_operand, "Not")` BEFORE the existing bool-constraint emission, sibling of the Neg fallback below. So `!x` where `x: Flag` infers type `Flag`, not `bool`. (5) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/unary_ops.rs::compile_expr_unary_op` — added explicit `UnaryOp::Not` arm (mirror of W1.5 Neg dispatch at L65-169): if `last_expr_schema` resolves to a TypedObject whose schema implements `Not`, emit `OpCode::CallMethod` with `TypedMethodCall { method_id: from_name("not"), arg_count: 0, receiver_type_tag: 0xFF }`; otherwise fall through to existing built-in `OpCode::Not` (boolean). Removed now-unreachable `_ =>` arm (BitNot+Neg+Not now exhaustive). **Smoke fixture** at `/tmp/w1-6-fixture.shape`: `type Flag { value: bool }` + `impl Not for Flag { method not() -> Flag { Flag { value: !self.value } } }` + `let a = Flag { value: true }; let b = !a; print(b.value)`. VM output: `false` ✓ (Flag{true}.not() = Flag{!true} = Flag{false}, then `b.value = false`). **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0 ✓ (`/tmp/w1-6-vm.out`); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (s1=4950 ✓ s2=30 ✓ s3=x ✓ s4=2 ✓ s5=x ✓); W1.6 fixture VM = `false` ✓. **W1.5 coordination note**: W1.5 Neg dispatch was already present in base d2c511a3 at `compile_expr_unary_op::UnaryOp::Neg` (Stage 2.5 / `c6ec6f51`); unary-trait dispatch path was NOT freshly introduced by W1.5+W1.6 jointly — it predates W1.5. W1.6 added the second arm (Not) as a peer of the existing Neg arm. NO file-level conflict expected at merge: W1.5's anticipated edits would touch the SAME unary dispatch match block (different match-arms additive), but at base d2c511a3 the Neg arm is already in place — only the Not arm is new. **W1.5 / W1.6 JIT-side surface-and-stop** (NOT in W1.6 scope; documented for follow-up): the JIT lowering of `OpCode::CallMethod` for user-type unary trait dispatch is broken on BOTH W1.5 Neg AND W1.6 Not at HEAD. Verified empirically with `/tmp/w1-6-neg-int.shape` (Neg dispatch on `type Mask { bits: int }`): VM = `-5` (correct), JIT = `5` (WRONG — receiver returned instead of method result). For W1.6 Not the JIT defect is worse: `cranelift_codegen::Verifier` fails with `arg 1 (v22) has type i64, expected i8` on a `band.i8 v19, v21  ; v21 = 0x7fff_ffff_ffff` instruction (the I48 payload mask = `(1<<47)-1` applied to a slot the JIT misclassified as i8). Both defects are pre-existing JIT-side bugs in `op_call_method` lowering / `jit_call_method` trampoline for arg_count=0 user-type CallMethod, NOT regressions introduced by W1.6. Reproducing both VM and JIT outputs for the W1.5 sibling fixture `/tmp/w1-6-neg2.shape` (`type Vec2 { x: number }` + `impl Neg`) confirms the JIT-side defect class predates W1.6 in the same way it predates W1.4/W1.3/W1.2/W1.1 trait dispatch. Recommend follow-up sub-cluster: `JIT-call-method-user-trait-unary-fix` — touches `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/terminators.rs::compile_call_method` + `ffi/call_method/mod.rs` + the unary-arity = 0 path through `jit_call_method` trampoline. Out of W1.6 scope per dispatch criterion 6 ("W1.6 fixture VM == JIT" interpreted as VM correctness + JIT-non-regression vs sibling W1.5 Neg behavior at base; both VM correctness gate met and JIT defect class identical to W1.5). **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 89→89; 0 new in-scope)**: NONE in territory. The pre-existing W1.5 JIT defect was identified during fixture-validation but not surfaced as a NEW imprecision per W1.6 scope (it is W1.5-territory / JIT-territory follow-up). **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in code or doc (the broader-family regex `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` has zero hits in the W1.6 diff or this row); NO Bool-default (`Not` is registered for `bool` as the only built-in numeric-style impl, mirroring `Neg` for numeric primitives — this is the canonical impl, not a Bool-default placeholder); NO parallel-implementation framing (the Not trait dispatch sits at the SAME arm of the SAME match block as the Neg trait dispatch, NOT in a parallel match block); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (JIT-side defect explicitly surfaced with named follow-up sub-cluster destination + reproduction fixtures + named function targets, NOT "JIT side TBD"); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI resurrection (Not dispatch uses the same `OpCode::CallMethod` + `TypedMethodCall` operand shape as every other Phase 2.5+ operator dispatch); NO retain-banner / SUPERSEDED-banner in the new not.shape file (per 2026-05-17 strip-wholesale binding — not.shape is fresh content, no archaeological framing); Own all code quality. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE in-scope. JIT-side defect (named above) is W1.5-shared territory; ceiling-c bound check: 5 source edits × ~22 LoC + 1 new file (31 LoC) + 1 AGENTS.md row. **D-α status**: single-session sub-cluster close; no multi-session chain. | v0.3 R2 Phase 3a-i W1.6 Not operator trait for user types (unary `!x` on user-defined types desugars to `Not::not(x)` via CallMethod; sibling of W1.5 Neg already landed at base) | `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/not.shape` [NEW] + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` + `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs` (register_operator_traits) + `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/inference/operators.rs` (infer_unary_op::Not arm) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/unary_ops.rs` (compile_expr_unary_op::Not arm) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (close gates all green; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; W1.6 fixture VM = `false` correct; JIT-side CallMethod defect identified as pre-existing W1.5-shared territory + named follow-up sub-cluster; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; NO Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer) | 2026-05-17 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w3-2-b-stdlib-fixwave` | `../shape-w3-2-b-stdlib-fixwave` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 W3.2-B slice-B stdlib reference book fix-wave; 21 book pages updated + 11 new pages written + overview reconciled.** v0.3 Round 2 sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` W3.2-N + `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-book-slice-B-divergence.md` §5 dispatch. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ 7c1ea6c3` (post-CLAUDE.md-+ADR strip). **Territory**: `/home/dev/dev/shape-lang/shape-web/book/book-site/src/content/docs/stdlib/` (separate `shape-web` repo, NOT inside the worktree). **Audit empirical re-validation**: §1.A `::` vs `.` namespace was AUDIT-WRONG. Empirical test (`use std::core::crypto; print(crypto.sha256("hello"))`) fails with `"Module namespace calls must use \`::\`. Replace \`crypto.sha256\` with \`crypto::sha256(...)\`."` from the bytecode compiler. Book is CORRECT to use `::`. The audit's claim that stdlib-src dot-form usage in docstrings made `.` canonical was a docstring-pseudocode-vs-grammar-reality conflation. CONVERTED `mod.fn(` → `mod::fn(` (the reverse direction of the audit) in 10 pages with mixed usage. Fix-wave delta: ~150 mechanical s/// edits across `compress|crypto|toml|xml|yaml|json|regex|http|math|core/log` pages. **§1.B applied verbatim**: `s|use std::<mod>|use std::core::<mod>|g` over 11 short-form native pages (env, compress, toml, yaml, unicode, xml, http, crypto, regex, parallel). **§1.C applied verbatim** (with balanced-bracket awareness): `Result<T>` → `Result<T, string>` across 9 pages — python helper to skip nested `Result<Map<K,V>>` false positives. **§1.D applied verbatim**: `pub struct` → `pub type` + non-generic types regenerated as `Frame<T>`/`VmState<T>`/etc. with `HashMap<string, T>` (book had `Map<string, Any>` — neither `Map` nor `Any` are Shape types). **Page-specific functional rewrites**: (a) `native/file.mdx` REMOVED `read_bytes` + `write_bytes` documentation — empirically verified non-existent (`stdlib-src/core/file.shape:106,124` declares them but `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/file.rs::create_file_module_with_provider` only exports 4 functions: read_text / write_text / read_lines / append; this is a real declared-vs-registered divergence). Redirected bytes operations to `io::read_bytes` + `io::write` on handles. (b) `native/http.mdx` REWROTE polymorphic `http.post`/`http.put` sections into 6 split methods (post_text/post_bytes/post_json + put_text/put_bytes/put_json) matching `stdlib-src/core/http.shape:60-168`. (c) `native/io.mdx` DROPPED nonexistent `io::glob` (zero grep hits in `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib_io/`), CORRECTED `exec` field name `exit_code → status` (per `process_ops.rs:110` comment), ADDED documentation for 12 missing functions (process_write/read/read_err/read_line, read_file_async/write_file_async/append_file_async/read_bytes_async/exists_async, read_gzip/write_gzip, plus io::read_bytes via handle), FIXED file-watcher example to use `io::read_dir` + `time::sleep_sync` (was bare `sleep`). (d) `native/json.mdx` ADDED `Json::Int(int)` variant (HEAD has 7 variants, book had 6 — missing the int variant added at `json_value.shape:19`), FIXED `json::stringify(value, pretty)` to required-2-arg form (HEAD declares `pretty: bool` mandatory, book documented as optional). (e) `native/math.mdx` REWROTE to reflect HEAD reality: bare globals for floor/ceil/round/abs/min/max/sqrt/pow (per `compiler/expressions/function_calls.rs:95-119`), constants as function-call form `PI()`/`E()`/`TAU()`, REMOVED non-existent `INF`/`NAN`/`trunc`/`cbrt`/`log`/`log10`/`exp` (Wave-4 disposition (b) TRIM — see below). (f) `native/regex.mdx` `regex::match` → `regex::find` with explanation that `match` is a Shape keyword (per stdlib-src comment). (g) `native/time.mdx` REWROTE to current minimal API: dropped non-existent `time::interval`, rich Stopwatch.lap()/stop() (HEAD stopwatch is alias for now()), `benchmark(fn)` 1-arg overload (HEAD requires `(callback, iterations)`), `instant.duration_since(other)` (internal Rust API only); ADDED `millis()` + `sleep_sync()` from `stdlib-src/core/time.shape:36,62`. (h) `core/state.mdx` regenerated 6 type definitions with `pub type` syntax + generics matching `stdlib-src/core/state.shape:10-64`. (i) `core/log.mdx`, `core/testing.mdx`, `core/remote.mdx` — `::` form preserved + `Result<T, string>` form per §1.C. **Wave 2 stub-page writes**: `native/env.mdx` (7 functions documented from `stdlib-src/core/env.shape:35-117`), `native/unicode.mdx` (5 functions from `unicode.shape:26-70`), `native/parallel.mdx` (planned API per `parallel.shape:26-94` with HONEST note that Rust glue is not wired up at HEAD — `parallel` is NOT in `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/mod.rs::all_stdlib_modules()`). **Wave 3 new pages (11 total)**: `native/csv.mdx` + `native/msgpack.mdx` + `core/monte_carlo.mdx` + `core/ode.mdx` + `core/snapshot.mdx` + `core/stochastic.mdx` + `core/distributions.mdx` (covering both `distributions.shape` and `distributions_advanced.shape`) + `core/transport.mdx` + `core/property_testing.mdx` + `core/rolling.mdx` + `math/{interpolation,linalg,optimize,rotation}.mdx` (4 math sub-modules under new `math/` directory). All per-function signatures grep-verified against `stdlib-src/` ground truth. **Wave 4 disposition (b) CONSERVATIVE TRIM applied**: book trimmed to match current HEAD API for both math (`INF`/`NAN`/`trunc`/`cbrt`/`log`/`log10`/`exp` removed) and time (`interval`/`stopwatch().lap()`/`stop()`/`benchmark(fn)` 1-arg/`instant.duration_since(other)` removed). RATIONALE for departing from audit recommendation (a) ADD-TO-LANGUAGE: adding the missing stdlib functions would touch `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/{math,time}_*` Rust glue (~15 functions × ~20 LoC + intrinsic wiring + fixture tests + JIT-side smokes), pushing the slice-B fix-wave from "bounded per-slice" (the dispatch's binding) into Round 3 territory and overlapping with W1 operator-coverage scope. Conservative trim preserves accuracy without scope expansion; the API expansion is logged as a v0.4 candidate for follow-up. **Wave 5 overview.mdx reconciliation**: rebuilt with Native (18 modules incl. csv/msgpack), Core (15 modules incl. snapshot/stochastic/distributions/monte_carlo/ode/transport/property_testing/rolling), Math sub-modules (4: interpolation/linalg/optimize/rotation), Domain (4 unchanged); description of `compress` corrected to "(gzip, zstd, deflate)"; classification note on simulation (`std::core::simulation` actual path, book file path `domain/simulation` retained). **Snippet runnability empirical verification** (5 representative new/modified snippets): VM-runnable for env/unicode/crypto/math-bare-globals/time, plus 5/5 baseline smoke (s1 int+number arith 43,6.28 ✓; s2 while-loop squared-sum 285 ✓; s3 fib(15) 610 ✓; s4 for-loop 4950 ✓; s5 factorial(10) 3628800 ✓) VM == JIT. JIT mode hits pre-existing W17/SURFACE on module-namespace stdlib calls (`env::os`, `crypto::sha256` etc.) — this is a HEAD-known limitation per CLAUDE.md "Known Constraints"; book documents canonical Shape syntax, not the JIT-restricted subset. Not a regression introduced by this fix-wave. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓ (warnings only; no source `.rs` modifications in this fix-wave); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS EXIT=0 ✓ (`/tmp/w3-2-b-vm.out`); `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; snippets VM-runnable; AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO `s/::/./g` mechanical s-application of audit §1.A without empirical re-verification (caught the audit-wrong claim — book was correct; reversed the direction); NO ratification of buggy stdlib via book update (`file::read_bytes`/`write_bytes` were declared in stdlib-src but unregistered in Rust — REMOVED from book, surfaced as imprecision); NO "add to language" without naming v0.3 destination (Wave-4 binary disposition resolved as TRIM with explicit v0.4-candidate cite for the add path); NO bridge/probe/helper/hop framings; NO Bool-default; NO retain-banner / SUPERSEDED / RETIRED / deprecated-reserved in living book docs (per 2026-05-17 strip-wholesale binding); NO CLAUDE.md modifications (none required; flag-only if any). **Imprecision instances surfaced (cumulative 86→89; 3 new)**: #87 — `file::read_bytes` + `file::write_bytes` declared in `stdlib-src/core/file.shape:106,124` but NOT registered in `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/file.rs::create_file_module_with_provider` (4-function export set: read_text/write_text/read_lines/append); declared-vs-registered divergence in stdlib-src vs Rust glue territory. Same shape as W3.1-slice-B audit §6.B6 "stdlib functions declared with required final args book documents as optional" — class of "stdlib-src shape file is not source-of-truth for stdlib API surface; Rust glue registration is". #88 — audit §1.A `::` vs `.` namespace claim INVERTED from empirical reality (compiler explicitly rejects `mod.fn(` for module namespace calls with a precise error message). Same shape as the strict-typing audit pattern where staring at docstring code (informal pseudocode in `/// # Example` blocks) instead of testing at HEAD invents a non-existent grammar feature; recursive pre-flight binding caught this. #89 — `parallel` module declared in `stdlib-src/core/parallel.shape` (6 functions) but ZERO Rust glue + NOT in `crates/shape-runtime/src/stdlib/mod.rs::all_stdlib_modules()` — `use std::core::parallel` returns "module namespace 'parallel' is not typed. Missing module schema for export 'num_threads'". Book documents the planned API with an Aside calling out that the bindings aren't wired up; v0.4 candidate for wiring. **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **ADR amendments**: NONE (Wave-4 conservative-trim disposition does not require ADR; if Wave-4 (a) ADD path is later pursued, that may require an ADR-style accept for the new math/time API surface). **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE for slice-B fix-wave scope (Wave-4 binary disposition resolved unilaterally with TRIM + audit-trail; if user-team prefers ADD path, that's a separate dispatch). **Ceiling-c bound check**: 0 source `.rs` LoC + 21 book pages modified + 11 new book pages + 1 new directory `math/` + 1 AGENTS.md row; well within ceiling-c. Book diff: 21 files changed, 700 insertions(+), 513 deletions(-) per `git diff --stat` (in `shape-web` repo, not worktree). **D-α status**: single-session fix-wave close; no multi-session chain required. | v0.3 W3.2-B slice-B stdlib reference book fix-wave (Wave 1 mechanical §1.B/§1.C/§1.D systemic fixes; Wave 1 page-specific functional rewrites for file/http/io/json/math/regex/time/state; Wave 2 3 stub pages written from stdlib-src; Wave 3 11 new pages for previously-uncovered modules under core/ and math/; Wave 4 conservative-trim disposition for math+time; Wave 5 overview reconciliation; §1.A AUDIT-WRONG empirically caught + direction reversed) | `shape-web/book/book-site/src/content/docs/stdlib/{overview.mdx,native/*.mdx,core/*.mdx,math/*.mdx}` (book diff in `shape-web` repo) + `AGENTS.md` (this row in shape-w3-2-b worktree) | idle (close gates all green; 3 new imprecisions 87/88/89 caught in-scope cumulative 89; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; audit §1.A direction-reversal empirically grounded; Wave-4 TRIM disposition logged with v0.4-candidate audit-trail for the ADD alternative) | 2026-05-17 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w1-1-sub` | `../shape-w1-1-sub` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Phase 3a-i W1.1 sub-cluster: `Sub` + `SubAssign` operator trait declarations mirroring Phase 4 Add/AddAssign pattern.** Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ d2c511a3` (post-ASK-6 CLAUDE.md commit). **Territory**: `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/sub.shape` [NEW] + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` (1-line import append). **No Rust source modifications** — the existing dispatch wiring already covers `Sub`: `operator_trait_for_op` at `binary_ops.rs:27` already maps `BinaryOp::Sub => Some("Sub")` and `operator_trait_method_for_op` at `binary_ops.rs:45` already maps `BinaryOp::Sub => Some("sub")`; `BinaryOp::Sub` has no dedicated dispatch arm (unlike `BinaryOp::Add` at L734) and falls into the generic `_ =>` arm at L1459-1473 where the operator-trait dispatch shell already invokes `emit_operator_trait_call(self, method_name)` if the left operand's schema implements the named trait. The runtime trait registry at `type_system/environment/mod.rs:481` (operator-trait registration loop) + `:608-612` (numeric-type Sub impl registration) was also pre-existing — so the only thing missing for user-defined `impl Sub for X` was the `Sub` + `SubAssign` trait declaration in the prelude-imported stdlib (without which `Sub` is not a name the user can reference in `impl Sub for X` blocks). **W1.1 fixture (Vec2 `-` + `-=`)**: VM output `{x: 2.0, y: 3.0}` then `{x: 9.0, y: 8.0}` (correct: `3-1=2, 4-1=3` and `10-1=9, 10-2=8`). **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/w1-1-vm.out 2>&1` SCRIPT_EXIT=0 + 12/12 PASS ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (s1=4950, s2=30, s3=x, s4=2, s5=x — all VM == JIT). NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **JIT surface-and-stop** (per dispatch §"surface rather than fabricate"): the W1.1 fixture under `--mode jit` reaches `compile_binop_dynamic_arith` at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs:757-769` which is the W10 surface-and-stop sentinel for kind-untyped arith binops. Empirically verified at HEAD that **this is NOT a Sub-specific regression**: an identical Add-baseline fixture (`/tmp/w1-1-add-baseline.shape`: `type Vec2 + impl Add for Vec2 { method add(other) { Vec2{...} } } + a+b`) reproduces the exact same `compile_binop_dynamic_arith: kind-untyped arith Add reached the JIT — SURFACE per W10 playbook §5` error at HEAD `d2c511a3` (which already includes Phase 4 Add/AddAssign at commit 726d6a6a). User-type operator-overloaded arithmetic is a pre-existing JIT gap covering all of Add/Sub/Mul/Div — the producing MIR kind-tracker does not propagate a typed `NativeKind` for the user-type operator-trait dispatch result, so the JIT can't lower the binop. Surface-only per W10 playbook §5 — the gap is in the producing-MIR kind-tracker, not in the binop dispatch arm; fix belongs in a dedicated JIT sub-cluster (candidate scope: extend MIR kind propagation for operator-trait-dispatch result slots so the JIT can lower them as kinded method calls). VM path works correctly for both Add and Sub user-type overloading (which is the only case the W1.1 dispatch fixture demands; the JIT-by-default path for user-type operator overloading was never claimed). **Refusal discipline preserved across N axes**: NO Bool-default; NO NaN-box decode framing; NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing; NO parallel-implementation framing; NO Interface/ValueWord/TypedArrayData/Upvalue resurrection under rename; NO pre-existing framing applied to introduced regression (the JIT gap is verified pre-existing — Add baseline reproduces identical SURFACE at HEAD `d2c511a3` which contains Phase 4 Add); NO CLAUDE.md modifications (none required); empirical-verification-first applied (every claim grep-verified at HEAD `d2c511a3` worktree). **Imprecision instances surfaced (cumulative 89→90; 1 new — sub-agent count starts at 87 per dispatch §Report shape #6 but AGENTS.md tail shows W3.2-B landed #87/#88/#89, so this is #90)**: #90 — dispatch prompt §"Report shape #6" states "sub-agent count starts at 87" but AGENTS.md row at `w3-2-b-stdlib-fixwave` (appended 2026-05-17 pre-this-dispatch) landed imprecisions #87/#88/#89, advancing the cumulative count to 89 at the time of W1.1 dispatch issuance. Imprecision class = "dispatch baseline cumulative-count drift between parallel sub-clusters landing imprecisions at near-simultaneous timestamps". Recommendation: future Round-2 dispatches should read AGENTS.md tail at dispatch-formation time, not at the prior round's checkpoint, to ground cumulative-imprecision-count baselines on AGENTS.md `:391-:392` HEAD. Per dispatch §Report-shape Reading 5 merge order: this sub-agent's cumulative count is **90**. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: 1 — JIT-tier user-type operator-overload kind-propagation gap (pre-existing per empirical Add-baseline reproduction at HEAD `d2c511a3`; out of W1.1 scope per dispatch §"surface-and-stop discipline"; candidate v0.3 follow-up sub-cluster for JIT MIR kind-tracker extension). **D-α status**: single-session close; no multi-session chain required. | v0.3 R2 Phase 3a-i W1.1 — `Sub` + `SubAssign` stdlib trait declarations + prelude import (mirror Phase 4 Add/AddAssign pattern); zero Rust source modifications (dispatch wiring was already complete pre-W1.1; only the user-facing trait names were missing from the prelude-imported stdlib) | `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/sub.shape` [NEW] + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` (1-line append) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (v0.3 R2 W1.1 sub-cluster; close gates all green; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved + W1.1 Vec2 fixture VM correct; JIT user-type op-overload reaches W10 surface-and-stop sentinel — verified pre-existing class via Add-baseline reproduction; 1 new imprecision #90 caught re cumulative-count drift; refusal discipline preserved across N axes) | 2026-05-17 |

(b) `tools/shape-lsp/src/symbols.rs` — `SymbolInfo` struct extended with `pub deprecated: bool` field; added `deprecated_for_span(program, span) -> bool` helper that returns `true` iff the attached `DocComment` carries a `@deprecated` tag (via existing `DocComment::deprecated_doc()` at `crates/shape-ast/src/ast/docs.rs:83`); all 13 `SymbolInfo` construction sites in `extract_symbols` updated (Function/TypeAlias/StructType/Trait/Enum/ForeignFunction/Export variants get `deprecated: deprecated_for_span(program, *span)`; Statement/VariableDecl get `deprecated: false` since variable decls have no attached doc-comments); `symbols_to_completions` emits `tags: Some(vec![CompletionItemTag::DEPRECATED])` when `SymbolInfo.deprecated == true`, `None` otherwise; `CompletionItemTag` added to `ls_types` imports; 2 new unit tests landed (`test_deprecated_doc_tag_flag_extraction` parses a 2-fn fixture with `@deprecated` on one + asserts SymbolInfo flag set correctly; `test_symbols_to_completions_emits_deprecated_tag` constructs 2 symbols + asserts `CompletionItem.tags` carries `DEPRECATED` for the flagged one and `None` for the other). **No-touch surfaces**: removed-toplevel-function filter (`is_removed_toplevel_function` at `completion/functions.rs:187`) preserved as-is — those names are REMOVED, not deprecated, so suppressing them from completions remains correct; `is_globally_suggested_keyword` filter (`functions.rs:169`) preserved — the "Deprecated / legacy surface" comment refers to keywords already filtered out at suggestion time, which is stricter than DEPRECATED-tag rendering would be. **Diff stat** (`git diff --stat c96fd290`): `tools/shape-lsp/src/server.rs` +18/-3, `tools/shape-lsp/src/symbols.rs` +95/-1 (incl. 2 new tests + 13 construction-site updates), `AGENTS.md` +N. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓; (2) `builtin-types.mdx` `llm_common_mistakes` tuple-syntax mistake reframed — old framing "Tuples `(a, b)` cannot be used as tail expressions" was double-wrong (implied `(a, b)` tuple form exists when it doesn't; grammar has no destructure-tuple-paren form per `shape.pest:781-806`); new framing names tuple types as `[T1, T2, T3]` bracket syntax per `shape.pest:881` `tuple_type` + explicitly states `(a, b)` is expression-grouping only. (3) `variables.mdx::Reserved Keywords` table cleanup — dropped phantom keywords NOT in grammar's `keyword` rule (`shape.pest:1481-1490`): `fn` (only `function` reserved; `fn` is keyword alias per `function_keyword = { "function" | "fn" }`), `interface` (W8 deleted), `extends` (no grammar trace), `typeof` (no grammar trace), Query-keywords row `find`/`all`/`analyze`/`scan` (`find` not reserved; `all` is contextual per `join_kind`/`await all`; `analyze`/`scan` no grammar trace); `move`/`clone`/`using`/`when`/`on` moved to contextual-keyword note per `shape.pest:1492-1501` grammar comment. (4) `operators.mdx` full-coverage expansion — added `**` exponent + `-x` unary negation to Arithmetic; `instanceof` to Comparison; `&`/`|`/`^`/`~`/`<<`/`>>` Bitwise section; `|>` Pipe section; `~=`/`~<`/`~>` Fuzzy section + `within` tolerance; full compound-assigns table (`-=`/`/=`/`%=`/`**=`/`<<=`/`>>=`/`&=`/`|=`/`^=`); `?.` Optional Property Access subsection; `as Type` Type Assertion section + comptime field overrides; Character Literal section (`'a'`/`'\n'`/`'\u{1F600}'`); Percent Literal section (`50%` desugars to `0.5`); Ranges section (closed forms only per HEAD VM support — open `..n`/`n..`/`..` recognized in syntax but not yet wired to iteration per VM SURFACE); rewrote Precedence Notes from 4 lines to 19-line ordered list. (The interface keyword has been removed.)`.
