| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w2-8-semantic-tokens` | `../shape-w2-8-semantic-tokens` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 Round 2 Phase 3a-ii W2.8 sub-cluster: LSP semantic-tokens delivery extensions (`textDocument/semanticTokens/range` feature 1.47 + `textDocument/semanticTokens/full/delta` feature 1.48 per `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-lsp-feature-spec.md` §3 W2.8 row + §5 item 8 out-of-LSP-scope finding).** Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ c96fd290` (post W3.2-A language-ref fix-wave merge). **Territory**: `tools/shape-lsp/src/server.rs` only (485 inserts / 62 deletes; one file). **Scope** (per audit §3 W2.8 SMALL row + audit §1 rows 1.47/1.48): two delivery methods beyond `textDocument/semanticTokens/full`. **Feature 1.47 range delivery**: handler `semantic_tokens_range(params: SemanticTokensRangeParams) -> Option<SemanticTokensRangeResult>` — builds the full document token list via shared `collect_full_semantic_tokens(uri)`, decodes to absolute coords via existing `decode_semantic_tokens`, filters tokens whose START position falls within `[range.start, range.end)` (inclusive-exclusive matching LSP semantic-tokens-range convention + rust-analyzer behavior for oversized straddling tokens — token start IN range, length unclamped → client renders whole token), re-encodes via existing `encode_semantic_tokens`, returns `SemanticTokensRangeResult::Tokens` with `result_id: None` (range results are not eligible as delta base per LSP §3.17). **Feature 1.48 delta delivery**: handler `semantic_tokens_full_delta(params: SemanticTokensDeltaParams) -> Option<SemanticTokensFullDeltaResult>` — checks `DashMap<Uri, CachedSemanticTokens>` cache (new field) for `previous_result_id` match; on hit computes minimal `Vec<SemanticTokensEdit>` via `compute_semantic_token_edits(old, new)` (common-prefix / common-suffix trimming around a single contiguous replacement span — token-aligned diff over the 5-element-per-token flat array per LSP `SemanticTokensEdit { start, delete_count, data: Option<Vec<SemanticToken>> }` spec; pure deletion emits `data: None`); returns `SemanticTokensFullDeltaResult::TokensDelta { result_id, edits }` on hit, or `SemanticTokensFullDeltaResult::Tokens { result_id, data }` (full payload) on cache miss / stale id — LSP-permitted fallback per ls-types-0.0.6 `SemanticTokensFullDeltaResult::Tokens(SemanticTokens)` variant. Cache always refreshes to latest snapshot after a /delta call so subsequent requests have a valid base. Existing `semantic_tokens_full` handler refactored to share `collect_full_semantic_tokens` with the two new methods — identical token data for identical inputs (no /full vs /range vs /delta divergence). **Cache hygiene**: `did_close` handler drops the cache entry alongside `last_good_programs` to prevent retention of last-snapshot token data for closed files. **Result-id minting**: `AtomicU64` counter on the server with `format!("st-{}", n)` — process-local, monotonic; LSP clients only ever echo the most-recent id per §textDocument_semanticTokens. **Capability advertisement** (server.rs:434 audit-finding §5 item 8): `SemanticTokensOptions.range: Some(false)` flipped to `Some(true)`; `full: Some(SemanticTokensFullOptions::Bool(true))` flipped to `Some(SemanticTokensFullOptions::Delta { delta: Some(true) })`. **New helper functions** (file-scope, plain `fn`, not methods on the server): `position_in_range(line, character, &Range) -> bool` (inclusive start, exclusive end per LSP convention); `compute_semantic_token_edits(old: &[SemanticToken], new: &[SemanticToken]) -> Vec<SemanticTokensEdit>` (single-replacement-span diff after common prefix/suffix trim, token-aligned + flat-index encoded per LSP wire spec); `semantic_tokens_eq(&SemanticToken, &SemanticToken) -> bool` (5-field equality for prefix/suffix comparison). **Tests landed** (12 new): `position_in_range_inclusive_start_exclusive_end`, `position_in_range_single_line`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_empty_when_identical`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_full_replacement_when_no_overlap`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_preserves_common_prefix`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_preserves_common_suffix`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_pure_insertion_emits_zero_delete`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_pure_deletion_emits_none_data`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_empty_to_empty_is_noop`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_empty_to_nonempty_is_insert`, `compute_semantic_token_edits_nonempty_to_empty_is_delete`, `semantic_tokens_eq_compares_all_fields` — all 12 pass; full `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` 543/543 pass (no regressions to existing 531 semantic_tokens + hover + completion + diagnostics + etc. tests). **File-overlap coordination**: W2.8 territory note (`tools/shape-lsp/src/server.rs`) overlaps with W2.2/W2.4/W2.5/W2.9 per audit §4 "Cross-wave non-overlap verification" — take-both at merge per dispatch directive. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing for cache lookup or diff computation (compute_semantic_token_edits is named for its function — produces edits; cache is named CachedSemanticTokens / semantic_tokens_cache); NO Bool-default; NO parallel-implementation framing (shared `collect_full_semantic_tokens` is the single producer; all three delivery methods consume it — no per-delivery duplicated collection); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (feature 1.47 + 1.48 both LANDED + tested in-wave; audit §5 finding #8 RESOLVED); NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI resurrection (LSP territory, none in scope); NO surface-and-stop deferral (both audit-prescribed features landed end-to-end; no SURFACE markers introduced); NO Reading 3 cadence violation (single supervisor relay paragraph + decision + commits). **Imprecisions caught (cumulative 90→90; ZERO new)**: dispatch prompt was empirically accurate — audit §3 W2.8 SMALL row matched actual territory scope (one file, ~485 inserts / 62 deletes), audit §5 finding #8 named `server.rs:434 range: Some(false)` is the exact site touched, audit §1 rows 1.47/1.48 named the LSP method names that matched the tower-lsp-server-0.23 / ls-types-0.0.6 type surface verbatim (`SemanticTokensRangeParams` / `SemanticTokensRangeResult` / `SemanticTokensDeltaParams` / `SemanticTokensFullDeltaResult` / `SemanticTokensEdit` / `SemanticTokensDelta` / `SemanticTokensFullOptions::Delta`). No cascade-site count miss; no scope inflation. **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Close gates**: `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓ (warnings-only baseline preserved); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/w2-8-vm.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` → SCRIPT_EXIT=0 + 12/12 PASS ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` 543/543 PASS ✓ (12 new + 531 pre-existing); Smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (NO VM / JIT / runtime / compiler / type-system source changes — territory is `tools/shape-lsp/src/server.rs` only, LSP runs as a separate process from VM/JIT critical path; baseline preserved by construction); `git diff --stat c96fd290` shows only `tools/shape-lsp/src/server.rs` (485 ins / 62 del) + `AGENTS.md` (this row); AGENTS.md row appended (this row) ✓; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer ✓. **Per-feature landed**: 1.47 textDocument/semanticTokens/range — LANDED (range filter on absolute coords, inclusive-start exclusive-end); 1.48 textDocument/semanticTokens/full/delta — LANDED (DashMap result_id cache with single-replacement-span diff; LSP-permitted full-payload fallback on cache miss). Audit §5 item 8 (server.rs:434 `range: Some(false)` rejects range requests outright) — RESOLVED. | v0.3 R2 Phase 3a-ii W2.8 LSP semantic-tokens delivery (range feature 1.47 + delta feature 1.48 landed; capability registration flipped to advertise both per audit §5 #8; shared collection path keeps /full + /range + /delta in lockstep; DashMap cache + AtomicU64 result-id minting + did_close hygiene) | `tools/shape-lsp/src/server.rs` (485 ins / 62 del) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (close gates all green; 12 new tests pass + 531 pre-existing tests pass — total 543/543; both audit-prescribed features LANDED end-to-end + RESOLVED audit §5 finding #8; zero new imprecisions caught; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; v0.3 acceptance criterion "LSP feature-spec en-par with rust-analyzer" advanced for the Semantic-tokens family — rows 1.47 + 1.48 of audit §1 flip from MISSING to COVERED) | 2026-05-17 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w2-9-hover-signature` | `../shape-w2-9-hover-signature` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Round 3a W2.9 sub-cluster (hover + signature polish; 2 LSP feature gaps closed); shape-lsp surfaces both an "Implementations for `T`" section on type-name hover (feature 1.03) and registers `textDocument/documentLink` as a first-class LSP capability for `@see`/`@link` doc-tag cross-references (feature 1.59).** v0.3 Round 3a sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` §W2 + `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-lsp-feature-spec.md` row W2.9 (SMALL). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ c96fd290` (post-W3.2-A merge). **Feature 1.03 — hover trait-impl listing**: new `trait_lookup::collect_impls_for_type(program, type_name, module_cache, current_file, workspace_root) -> Vec<ImplSummary>` (mirrors `resolve_trait_definition` shape — local AST + importable-module walk; results sorted+deduped by `(trait_name, impl_name, source_module)` for stable rendering). Wired into `hover::get_user_symbol_hover_from_program` after the `Shape:` line for `SymbolKind::Type`; renders via new `render_impls_section(type_name, impls)` helper as a markdown list (`**Implementations for `T`**\n- `impl Trait for T`\n- `impl Trait for T as Named` — _from_ `mod::path`` for cross-file). `get_user_symbol_hover_at` + `_from_program` signatures gained `module_cache` + `current_file` params; sole non-internal call-site `get_hover_for_word` already had them in scope. **Feature 1.59 — `textDocument/documentLink` LSP capability**: registered `document_link_provider: Some(DocumentLinkOptions { resolve_provider: Some(false), .. })` in `server::initialize`'s `ServerCapabilities` block (post call-hierarchy section); new `async fn document_link(&self, params)` handler parses the document (or falls back to `last_good_programs`) and invokes new `doc_links::collect_document_links(program, text, module_cache, current_file, workspace_root) -> Vec<DocumentLink>` which walks `program.docs.entries`, filters `DocTagKind::See | Link`, requires non-dummy `DocLink::target_span` + fully-qualified path + a successful `resolve_doc_link` call yielding a `Uri`, and emits one `DocumentLink { range: span_to_range(target_span), target: Some(uri), tooltip: Some("Go to `{target}`"), data: None }` per resolvable target. Dedupes by `(range, target_string)`; sorts by start position for stable client iteration. Reserved `walk_inline_doc_comments` extension hook for future inline doc-tag coverage if/when the parser starts attaching `DocComment` to non-item nodes. **Scope**: 5 file edits (1 LSP capability + 1 handler in server.rs; 1 new helper + 1 wiring edit in hover.rs; 1 new helper in trait_lookup.rs; 1 new helper + 2 tests in doc_links.rs; 2 new tests in hover_tests.rs) + 1 AGENTS.md row. **Forbidden patterns avoided**: NO `ValueWord` / generic opcodes / kind-blind ABI resurrection (LSP-only territory, but the discipline applies); NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim descriptors in any code or commit message (zero matches against the `(decode\|tag\|kind\|dispatch\|value.call\|closure.callback\|frame.setup\|callee\|capture) (bridge\|probe\|helper\|hop\|translator\|adapter\|shim)` broader-family regex); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO source `.rs` modifications outside `tools/shape-lsp/src/{server.rs, hover.rs, hover_tests.rs, trait_lookup.rs, doc_links.rs}`; NO touching of W2.2/W2.4/W2.5/W2.6/W2.8 territory inside server.rs (only new `document_link` handler + new `document_link_provider` capability slot + `DocumentLink` imports added; existing handlers + capability slots untouched). **Per-feature landed**: (1) 1.03 — `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` `hover::tests::test_hover_type_lists_local_trait_impls` PASS verifies hover on `type User { name: string }` with two `impl Display for User` + `impl Debug for User as Json` produces a markdown body containing both `impl Display for User` and `impl Debug for User as Json` under an `**Implementations for `User`**` heading; `trait_lookup::tests::collects_local_impls_for_type` PASS verifies sort order + dedup; `test_hover_type_omits_impls_section_when_none_present` PASS verifies no empty section emitted. (2) 1.59 — `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` `doc_links::tests::collect_document_links_skips_unresolvable_targets` PASS verifies a `/// @see std::core::math::sum` doc-tag with no module cache yields zero links (does not fabricate a link); `collect_document_links_skips_unqualified_targets` PASS verifies bare `sum` is dropped per `is_fully_qualified_doc_path`. **Test outcomes**: full `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` 538 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored (was 533 before W2.9; +5 new tests — 3 impl-section + 2 doc-link). **Close gates**: `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓ (workspace check unchanged warnings-only baseline); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS, SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` 538 passed ✓; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (s1=4950 s2=30 s3=x s4=2 s5=x identical on both modes — LSP-only changes, runtime untouched by construction); AGENTS.md row appended (this row) ✓; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer ✓; `git diff --stat c96fd290` source surface bounded to 5 tools/shape-lsp/src/ files + 1 AGENTS.md row. **Imprecisions surfaced**: none new — both features matched audit §2.1 row scope estimates exactly (1.03 + 1.59 both SMALL; combined ~458 line LoC delta covers both features + 5 unit tests + extension hook + cross-file impl support). **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE. **ADR amendments surfaced**: NONE. **File-overlap coordination with sibling W2.x waves**: server.rs touched by W2.2/W2.4/W2.5/W2.6/W2.8 sibling territory — W2.9 take-both compliance: only new `document_link_provider` capability field + new `async fn document_link` handler + 1 new `DocumentLink*` import line added to existing `tower_lsp_server::ls_types` use-list (alphabetized between `DocumentFormattingParams` and `DocumentOnTypeFormattingOptions`); zero modifications to existing handlers, capability slots, or imports owned by sibling waves. Merge-time conflict probability = standard take-both per `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-lsp-feature-spec.md` §3 territory matrix. | v0.3 R2 Round 3a W2.9 LSP hover + signature polish (1.03 trait-impl listing on type hover + 1.59 textDocument/documentLink capability exposure; both SMALL per audit; territory bounded to tools/shape-lsp/src/{server,hover,hover_tests,trait_lookup,doc_links}.rs; 5 new unit tests; cross-file impl listing supported via ModuleCache walk; doc-link resolution reuses existing `resolve_doc_link` infrastructure) | `tools/shape-lsp/src/{server.rs (+1 capability +1 handler +1 import group), hover.rs (+1 helper +1 wiring), hover_tests.rs (+2 tests), trait_lookup.rs (+1 helper +1 ImplSummary type +3 tests), doc_links.rs (+1 collector helper +1 push_links walker +1 extension hook +2 tests)}` + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (close gates all green; cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib 538 passed +5 new; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; v0.3 acceptance criterion "LSP feature-spec en-par with rust-analyzer" advanced for 1.03 + 1.59 rows of W2.9 sub-cluster) | 2026-05-17 |

`definition::tests::test_collect_module_scope_refs_finds_call_site` (cross-file helper finds 3+ refs for `helper` def+2-uses); `definition::tests::test_get_references_cross_file_module_scope` (two open docs both with `fn shared`; click on main → returns 4+ refs spanning both URIs; asserts at least one ref from `/other.shape`); `definition::tests::test_get_references_cross_file_local_binding_no_crossover` (local `let local = 1` inside `fn outer` does NOT cascade to `/other.shape`); `rename::tests::test_rename_cross_file_module_scope_fn` (cross-file rename of `shared` → `renamed` produces edits in BOTH `/main.shape` and `/other.shape`; asserts ≥3 edits in `/other.shape` matching def+2-refs); `rename::tests::test_rename_cross_file_local_binding_no_crossover` (local `let local` rename does NOT touch `/other.shape`); `code_lens::tests::test_count_references_scope_aware_excludes_shadowing` (shadowing inner `let foo = 2` does NOT inflate the outer-fn ref count — load-bearing because the text-search `count_references` text-counted ALL `foo` occurrences regardless of scope); `code_lens::tests::test_count_references_scope_aware_lens_integration` (end-to-end `get_code_lenses` produces "3 references" for `helper` with 3 module-scope uses); `module_cache::tests::test_enumerate_workspace_shape_files` (asserts `.git`/`target`/`*.txt` excluded; `*.shape` in nested dirs included). **Gate confirms**: `cargo check -p shape-lsp` EXIT=0; `cargo test -p shape-lsp --lib` 540 passed / 0 failed (added 6 of those); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/w2-6.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` → 12/12 PASS, SCRIPT_EXIT=0; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 (warnings-only baseline preserved). Take-both at merge — W2.6 adds cross-file functions + tests; W2.5 (per audit §3 partition) adds 1.33 typeDefinition + 1.34 implementation + 1.35 declaration + 1.60 documentHighlight. The two waves' additions are textually disjoint within `definition.rs`: W2.6 added pub fn `get_references_cross_file` + helpers `is_module_scope_symbol` + `collect_module_scope_refs_in_file` near the top + 4 new tests in `#[cfg(test)]`; W2.5 territory is the goto-definition fall-through arms in `find_definition_location` + new pub fns + register-providers in `server.rs`. **Workspace-indexing-groundwork consumption surface for W2.7** (documented for downstream agent): `ModuleCache::enumerate_workspace_shape_files(workspace_root: &Path) -> Vec<PathBuf>` returns all `.shape` files under the workspace root, capped at 4096; W2.7 wires this into the `symbol()` LSP handler at `server.rs:826-846` to replace the open-documents-only loop (audit §5 finding #3 "workspace/symbol silently scopes to open documents"); W2.7 also adds `did_change_watched_files` capability + handler to invalidate cached module entries via the existing `ModuleCache::invalidate(path)` (`module_cache.rs:226`); W2.7's executor + willRenameFiles work is independent. **Audit imprecisions 88+89 closed per dispatch**: #88 (`references_provider` characterized as "text-search single-file") + #89 (`workspace_symbol_provider` characterized as "iterates only open docs") — both held at the audit level as PARTIAL gaps; this sub-cluster closes #88 by making references AST-resolved + cross-file (via ScopeTree-driven per-file scan over open docs + workspace .shape files filtered to module-scope-visible symbols) and lays the workspace-enumeration infrastructure that W2.7 will use to close #89. **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 90 → 92; 2 new)**: #91 — dispatch prompt characterized `definition.rs::find_all_references` line 270 as the load-bearing gap to close for 1.36, but empirical at HEAD `c96fd290` showed `definition.rs::get_references_with_fallback` (the actual handler entry point at line 75) already uses `ScopeTree::references_of`; the text-search `find_all_references` at line 270 is invoked ONLY as a last-resort fallback when ScopeTree returns no spans. The PARTIAL gap class is correct (single-file scope), but the framing "replace text-search single-file with ScopeTree" overstated the same-file shortfall — same-file was already AST-resolved; the actual cross-file gap is what 1.36 closes. Imprecision class = "dispatch-prompt-cites-symptom-line-not-root-cause". Same pattern as audit imprecision #88 (caught at audit pre-flight grep — the audit row got it right; the dispatch prompt re-introduced the imprecision by anchoring on the line number without re-reading the entry-point). #92 — Shape v0.3 grammar requires `if` blocks INSIDE function bodies to use either expression-form `if cond { expr } else { expr2 }` (no `return`, both branches required, only-the-last-expr-of-each-block usage) OR the strict-typing inference for untyped `fn foo(n) { if n <= 1 {...} }` rejects the body even when the recursive call pattern is valid; the `recursive.rs` test pattern `fn fib(n) { if n <= 1 { n } else { fib(n-1)+fib(n-2) } }` parses cleanly at the bare `fib(10)` form but fails at the `print(fib(10))` form because the wrapping `print(...)` triggers strict-typing inference on the recursive return type. Workaround used in smoke fixtures: explicit `fn fib(n: int) -> int` + multi-line `return` body. Imprecision class = "Shape strict-typing × top-level-print interaction NOT YET DOCUMENTED in book §functions or §strict-typing". Recommendation: surface to a future doc-fix wave or strict-typing audit; not load-bearing for W2.6 itself (the smoke fixtures landed working VM == JIT after iteration). **Refusal discipline preserved across N axes**: NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO Bool-default; NO parallel-implementation framing (same-file `rename` + cross-file `rename_cross_file` are intentional API duality — the cross-file form wraps the same-file form, not a parallel impl; tests verify the wrapping is structural not just behavioral); NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI / Interface keyword resurrection; NO CLAUDE.md modifications (none required); NO source `.toml` / `.lock` changes (zero — only `tools/shape-lsp/src/{definition.rs, rename.rs, code_lens.rs, module_cache.rs, server.rs}` + `AGENTS.md`); NO ARCHAEOLOGY (deleted text-search-only path retained ONLY as resilient-parse fallback per the existing pattern in `get_references_with_fallback`); empirical-verification-first applied (every claim in this row grep-verified at HEAD `c96fd290` worktree). **ADR amendments surfaced**: NONE. **CLAUDE.md modifications surfaced**: NONE. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE — W2.6 scope fully closed; W2.7 inherits the workspace-enumeration infrastructure as a documented consumption surface; the strict-typing × top-level-print imprecision #92 is documentation-tier, not blocking. **D-α status**: single-session close; | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w1-9-bitwise` | `../shape-w1-9-bitwise` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W1.9 sub-cluster: `BitAnd` + `BitOr` + `BitXor` + their `*Assign` variants for user-defined types; mirrors Phase 4 Add/AddAssign + W1.4 Mod patterns.** v0.3 Round 2 sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` §W1.9. Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a2b04a26` (post-W2.6 merge). **Option A vs B choice**: Option B (combined `bitwise.shape` with all 3 traits + assign variants) chosen per dispatch operational call — bitwise ops form a cohesive group with identical dispatch shape, no per-op specialization (unlike Add's string-concat / Mul's matrix kernels per-op overload), and the same primitive-int-only bookkeeping. Per-trait file fragmentation would have added file churn without trait-isolation benefit. **Territory edited (4 files, 73 LoC net)**: (1) NEW `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/bitwise.shape` (114 lines) — 6 trait decls: `trait BitAnd { bitand(other: Self): Self }` + `trait BitAndAssign { bitand_assign(other: Self): Self }` + same shape for BitOr/BitOrAssign + BitXor/BitXorAssign. Doc-block names typed-int opcode dispatch path (`BitAndInt` / `BitOrInt` / `BitXorInt` for primitive int / `i8..i64` / `u8..u64`), UFCS user-type path, grammar-desugar cite for `&=`/`|=`/`^=` at `shape-ast/parser/expressions/binary_ops.rs:240-242`, Option-B-rationale section, and int-only-restriction section (floats / bool / string / decimal / bigint excluded because typed bitwise opcodes operate on `Int` width only and the grammar's bitwise productions reject floats). (2) `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` (+1 line: `from std::core::bitwise use { BitAnd, BitAndAssign, BitOr, BitOrAssign, BitXor, BitXorAssign }`). (3) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs` (+33/-2 lines): `operator_trait_for_op` gains `BinaryOp::BitAnd => Some("BitAnd")` + `BinaryOp::BitOr => Some("BitOr")` + `BinaryOp::BitXor => Some("BitXor")` (closing-comment updated `// Pow has no operator trait` → `// Pow / BitShl / BitShr have no operator trait (yet)` per W1.10 scope reservation); `operator_trait_method_for_op` gains `BinaryOp::BitAnd => Some("bitand")` + `BinaryOp::BitOr => Some("bitor")` + `BinaryOp::BitXor => Some("bitxor")`; **new** user-trait dispatch arm injected at the top of the existing `BinaryOp::BitAnd | BitOr | BitXor | BitShl | BitShr` arm (Phase R5.1C typed-int / dynamic-fallback arm, L1095-1195) — after `compile_expr(left)` captures `left_schema = self.last_expr_schema` (mirror of Add arm's L756 capture pattern) and `compile_expr(right)` pushes the rhs, the new arm checks if `op ∈ {BitAnd, BitOr, BitXor}` AND `left_schema` resolves to a TypedObject whose schema implements the matching trait, and if so emits `emit_operator_trait_call(self, method_name)` + `return Ok(())` BEFORE the typed-int / dynamic-fallback emission. `BitShl` / `BitShr` deliberately excluded from this arm (per W1.10 scope reservation per roadmap §W1.10). Mirror pattern of Add arm's L775-790 `left_has_add` check + Sub/Mul/Div/Mod arm's L1462-1475 `dispatches_via_trait` check. (4) `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs` (+32/-1 lines): binary operator trait registration tuple grows to include `("BitAnd", "bitand")` + `("BitOr", "bitor")` + `("BitXor", "bitxor")` after `("Mod", "mod")`; new W1.9 `bitwise_types` registration loop registers `BitAnd` / `BitOr` / `BitXor` impls for `int` / `i8..i64` / `u8..u64` only (no `number` / `f32` / `f64` per bitwise-is-integer-only restriction; mirror of W1.6 Not's bool-only registration shape rather than arithmetic's number-inclusive registration shape). **Surface-and-stop on grammar/keyword-conflict** (proactively assessed per dispatch §Surface-and-stop): No keyword conflict materialized — `shape.pest` `keyword` rule at L1481-1490 does NOT include `bitand` / `bitor` / `bitxor` / `and` / `or` / `xor` as reserved identifiers; the `item_sync_keyword` listing at L22 is sync-point-only error-recovery and does not constrain identifier resolution; `bitand` / `bitor` / `bitxor` parse cleanly as `ident` via `method_name = ident | "from"` at L277. Trait names `BitAnd` / `BitOr` / `BitXor` and method names `bitand` / `bitor` / `bitxor` preserved verbatim per dispatch §default disposition (no rename to e.g. `band` / `bor` / `bxor` required). **W1.9 fixture** (`/tmp/w1-9-fixture.shape` per dispatch §5; `type Mask { bits: int } / impl BitAnd / BitOr / BitXor for Mask / let a = Mask{bits:12} let b = Mask{bits:10} / print((a & b).bits) print((a | b).bits) print((a ^ b).bits) / let mut x = Mask{bits:12}; x &= Mask{bits:10}; print(x.bits)`): **VM mode** outputs `8` (12 & 10 = 0b1100 & 0b1010 = 0b1000) + `14` (12 | 10 = 0b1100 | 0b1010 = 0b1110) + `6` (12 ^ 10 = 0b1100 ^ 0b1010 = 0b0110) + `8` (x = 12 &= 10 = 8) — all 4 outputs correct. **JIT mode**: pre-existing baseline SURFACE `Rvalue::Aggregate reached the kind-blind fallback` (`crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/...` per ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5). Verified pre-existing on base `a2b04a26` via control fixture `/tmp/w1-9-base-test.shape` (`type Mask { bits: int } / let a = Mask { bits: 12 } / let b = Mask { bits: 10 } / print(a.bits & b.bits)`) which uses NO W1.9 trait dispatch (raw int & on TypedObject field access) — same SURFACE reproduces, confirming the JIT failure is the W11-jit-new-array pre-existing TypedObject-literal SURFACE, NOT a W1.9 regression. JIT-side gap folds to the same cluster-2 / W11 territory as the W1.1-W1.6 sibling rows. **Smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved**: s1=4950/4950 ✓ s2=30/30 ✓ s3=x/x ✓ s4=2/2 ✓ s5=x/x ✓. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 ✓ (5m01s clean; release `cargo build --bin shape --release` 5m53s clean); `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` SCRIPT_EXIT=0 + 12/12 PASS ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓ (`Finished dev profile target(s) in 37.29s`); smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved per above; W1.9 fixture VM correct (4/4 outputs); AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **Per-edit summary**: (a) bitwise.shape NEW = trait surface for user-defined `&`/`|`/`^`/`&=`/`|=`/`^=` opt-in; (b) prelude.shape +1 = auto-import so `impl BitAnd for X` resolves at user-code parse time; (c) binary_ops.rs +33/-2 = dispatch tables get bitwise entries + new explicit user-trait dispatch arm injected at the existing Phase R5.1C typed-int bitwise arm's top BEFORE the typed-int emission, mirror of Add arm's user-trait-first pattern; (d) environment/mod.rs +32/-1 = trait def + per-primitive-int bookkeeping impl registration for `<T: BitAnd>` bound checking. **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 92→92; 0 new in W1.9 territory)**: NONE. The bitwise dispatch arm at L1095 needed explicit user-trait wiring (unlike Sub/Mul/Div/Mod which inherit through the shared `_ =>` arm's `try_emit_trait_dispatch` paths) because the bitwise arm pre-W1.9 was a self-contained "typed-int OR dynamic-fallback" branch with no schema-aware path. This is the W1.9-specific deviation from the Sub/Mul/Div/Mod sibling pattern, explicitly named in the dispatch (`binary_ops.rs: BinaryOp::BitAnd + BinaryOp::BitOr + BinaryOp::BitXor dispatch`) and not an imprecision — the dispatch surface acknowledged the bitwise arm is distinct from the arithmetic arm. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in bitwise.shape or binary_ops.rs edits or this row (broader-family regex `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` zero hits in W1.9 diff); NO Bool-default (`BitAnd` registered for int-only types per bitwise-is-integer-only restriction — bool is excluded NOT defaulted); NO parallel-implementation framing (the new user-trait dispatch arm sits at the SAME match arm as the typed-int / dynamic-fallback emission, branched on `op ∈ {BitAnd, BitOr, BitXor}` + `left_implements`, NOT a parallel match block); NO retain-banner / SUPERSEDED-banner in bitwise.shape (per 2026-05-17 strip-wholesale binding — bitwise.shape is fresh content with NO archaeological framing); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (JIT-side SURFACE surfaced with explicit pre-existing-baseline cite via `/tmp/w1-9-base-test.shape` control + named W11-jit-new-array destination + ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5 cite, NOT silently swallowed); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI resurrection (the new bitwise dispatch arm uses existing `OpCode::CallMethod` + `TypedMethodCall` operand shape — same as every other Phase 2.5+ operator dispatch); Own all code quality (every claim file:line cited at HEAD a2b04a26 + per-edit verification empirically). **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE in-scope. JIT-side SURFACE (`Rvalue::Aggregate reached the kind-blind fallback`) is W11-jit-new-array territory per existing ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5 cite, not W1.9. **Ceiling-c bound check**: 33 source `.rs` LoC (binary_ops.rs) + 32 source `.rs` LoC (environment/mod.rs) + 114 LoC new stdlib `.shape` file (bitwise.shape) + 1 LoC stdlib `.shape` edit (prelude.shape) + 1 AGENTS.md row; well within ceiling-c. **D-α status**: single-session sub-cluster close; no multi-session chain required. **Territory disjoint from W1.1-W1.8 + W1.10-W1.11** (sibling W1.x sub-clusters touch their own per-operator stdlib files: `sub.shape` / `mul.shape` / `div.shape` / `modulo.shape` / `neg.shape` / `not.shape` / future `eq.shape` / `ord.shape` / `shift.shape` / `index.shape`; `bitwise.shape` is W1.9-exclusive new file; `prelude.shape` + `binary_ops.rs` + `environment/mod.rs` shared but W1.x edits are additive at distinct loci — W1.9's binary_ops.rs edits add new map entries + a new arm-internal dispatch path that does NOT overlap with Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod arms; W1.9's environment/mod.rs edits add new tuple entries + a new registration loop that does NOT overlap with the existing arithmetic_types / Not registration loops). | v0.3 R2 W1.9 BitAnd + BitOr + BitXor + Assign variants operator traits for user types (mirror Phase 4 Add/AddAssign + W1.4 Mod pattern with Option B combined `bitwise.shape`; bitwise dispatch arm extended with explicit user-trait dispatch path at L1095 because pre-W1.9 arm was self-contained typed-int/dynamic-fallback with no schema-aware path) | `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/bitwise.shape` [NEW] + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` (+1 line) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs` (+33/-2) + `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs` (+32/-1) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (close gates all green; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; W1.9 fixture VM 4/4 outputs correct (8, 14, 6, 8); JIT-side `Rvalue::Aggregate` SURFACE empirically pre-existing per `/tmp/w1-9-base-test.shape` control reproduction on base a2b04a26 + folds to W11-jit-new-array territory per ADR-006 §2.7.14 / §2.7.5 cite; | `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w1-11-index` | `../shape-w1-11-index` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 Round 2 Phase 3b W1.11 `Index` + `IndexMut` operator traits for user-defined types; `c[k]` desugars to UFCS `Index::index(c, k)` via `CallMethod` (arg_count=1) + `c[k] = v` desugars to UFCS `IndexMut::index_set(c, k, v)` via `CallMethod` (arg_count=2); DISTINCT from binary-op trait dispatch path — Index/IndexMut route through the index-access pipeline (`Expr::IndexAccess` / `compile_expr_index_access` / `compile_expr_assign IndexAccess`), NOT through `binary_ops.rs` BinaryOp dispatch.** v0.3 R2 sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` §W1 (W1.11 row). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a2b04a26` (post-W2.6-references-rename merge). **Per-edit landed (5 source edits, ~228 LoC + 1 new file 71 LoC, 0 ADR / 0 CLAUDE.md edits)**: (1) NEW `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/index.shape` (71 lines) — declares `trait Index<Key, Value> { index(key: Key): Value }` + `trait IndexMut<Key, Value> { index_set(key: Key, value: Value) }`; full architectural docstring covers (a) typed-array fast-path precedence (Array/HashMap/string use built-in opcodes, not trait dispatch), (b) sibling-of-Iterable<T> generic-params choice over Rust-style associated types (`trait Index<Idx> { type Output; ... }` would parse — `register_trait_impl_with_assoc_types_named` at `type_system/inference/items.rs:574` supports the assoc-type path — but generic params are simpler, mirror existing `Iterable<T>` precedent, and don't require `type Output = int;` line in each impl), (c) registration-by-name semantics (generic args stripped at `type_name_str` at `:948`, so `impl Index<string, int> for Cache` registers as ("Index", "Cache") and dispatch on `c["foo"]` resolves via `type_implements_trait("Cache", "Index")`). (2) `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` — added `from std::core::index use { Index, IndexMut }` after the existing `Not` line (placement is alphabetical-after-Not within the operator-trait import block; auto-imports trait names for impl-block authoring without explicit `use`). (3) `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs::register_operator_traits` — added Index + IndexMut trait definitions after the W1.6 Not registration block; both use `Some(vec![TypeParam::Type { name: "Key" / "Value", ... }])` for type_params (mirrors `register_iterable_trait` pattern at L407-439) with `FunctionParam`s typed as `TypeAnnotation::Reference("Key".into()) / "Value".into()`; index returns `TypeAnnotation::Reference("Value".into())`, index_set returns `TypeAnnotation::Basic("void".to_string())`. NO bookkeeping `register_trait_impl(...)` calls for built-in types (unlike Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Neg) because Array/HashMap/string have typed-opcode dispatch (`GetElemI64`/`GetElemF64`/`TypedArrayGet*`/etc.), NOT trait dispatch — and the bookkeeping registrations would require `Index<int, int>` / `Index<string, V>` / etc. multi-shape registrations which generic-params trait registration doesn't support 1:1 (registration is by name only, so `Index<int, int> for Array` and `Index<string, V> for HashMap` would collide as `("Index", "Array")` and `("Index", "HashMap")` — fine — but the per-key/value-type-pair distinction would be lost; deferred to a W4 follow-up if `<T: Index>` bound checking on built-in collections proves load-bearing). (4) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/property_access.rs::compile_expr_index_access` — added Index trait dispatch after the typed-array local-slot fast path (line 587) but BEFORE the generic GetProp fallback (line 597 was 588): when `end_index.is_none() && typed_kind.is_none()`, calls new `receiver_type_implements_trait(object, "Index")` helper; if true, compiles object + index then emits `CallMethod` via new `emit_index_trait_call(self, "index", 1)` helper. Two new helper functions added at end of file: (a) `receiver_type_implements_trait(&mut self, object: &Expr, trait_name: &str) -> bool` (pub(super)) does schema-first lookup via `tracker_type_name_for_identifier` + `type_inference.env.type_implements_trait`, falls back to `infer_expr_type` + `type_display_name` for non-Identifier receivers — mirrors `try_emit_trait_dispatch` at `binary_ops.rs:71-85`; (b) `emit_index_trait_call(compiler, method_name, arg_count: u16)` (pub(super), free function) emits `OpCode::CallMethod` with `Operand::TypedMethodCall { method_id: MethodId::from_name(method_name).0, arg_count, string_id, receiver_type_tag: 0xFF }` — mirrors `emit_operator_trait_call` at `binary_ops.rs:90-104`. (5) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/assignment.rs` IndexAccess { end_index: None } branch — added IndexMut trait dispatch immediately after `typed_kind` resolution (line 638) but BEFORE the `if let Expr::Identifier(name, _) = object.as_ref()` typed-array fast path: when `typed_kind.is_none() && receiver_type_implements_trait(object, "IndexMut")`, compiles object + index + value, stashes value to a temp local for the assignment-expression result, re-pushes value as third argument, calls `emit_index_trait_call(self, "index_set", 2)`, pops the void result, re-loads the stashed value. (6) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/mod.rs` — flipped `tracker_type_name_for_identifier` visibility from private to `pub(super)` so the new `receiver_type_implements_trait` helper in sibling `property_access.rs` can call it. **Associated-types vs generic-params choice**: generic-params per Iterable<T> precedent (see edit 1 docstring above). Rationale: (a) Shape's trait dispatch path strips generic args at `type_name_str` (verified at `type_system/inference/items.rs:948`), so `Index<Key, Value>` and `Index<Idx> { type Output }` register identically as ("Index", target_type); (b) generic-params mirror `Iterable<T>` so the trait shape feels native; (c) no `type Output = int;` line required in each impl, keeping author-facing surface lean; (d) the runtime dispatch (`CallMethod("index", arg_count=1)`) doesn't inspect the trait's type parameters — only the impl block's method signature is checked. **Index-access dispatch site cite**: read path = `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/property_access.rs::compile_expr_index_access` lines 588-602 (new Index trait dispatch block, after typed-array fast paths, before generic GetProp fallback); write path = `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/assignment.rs` IndexAccess { end_index: None } branch lines 639-672 (new IndexMut trait dispatch block, after typed_kind resolution, before Identifier-receiver typed-array fast path). **Smoke fixture** at `/tmp/w1-11-fixture-simple.shape`: `type Counter { value: int } + impl Index<int, int> for Counter { method index(key: int) -> int { return self.value + key } } + impl IndexMut<int, int> for Counter { method index_set(key: int, value: int) { self.value = value } } + let mut c = Counter { value: 10 }; let a = c[5]; print(a); c[0] = 99; let b = c[0]; print(b)`. VM output: `15` (= 10 + 5 via `Counter::index(5)`), then `99` (= 99 + 0 via `Counter::index(0)` after `c[0] = 99` invoked `Counter::index_set(0, 99)` to mutate `self.value` from 10 to 99) ✓. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh > /tmp/w1-11.out 2>&1; echo SCRIPT_EXIT=$?` → 12/12 PASS + SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (s1=4950 ✓ s2=30 ✓ s3=x ✓ s4=2 ✓ s5=x ✓); W1.11 fixture VM = `15` then `99` ✓ (Index read + IndexMut write both dispatch correctly through user-impl method bodies). **Built-in dispatch non-regression**: verified at `/tmp/w1-11-noregress.shape` that built-in typed-array indexing (`let arr = [10, 20, 30, 40]; print(arr[0])` etc.) still uses typed-opcode fast path (`GetElemI64`/`GetElemF64`) — Index trait dispatch fires ONLY for user TypedObject types when both `typed_kind.is_none()` and `try_resolve_typed_elem_get` returns None. **W1.11 / W1.x JIT-side surface-and-stop** (NOT in W1.11 scope; documented as W1-sibling-shared pre-existing class per W1.6 row + W1.3 row): the JIT lowering of `OpCode::CallMethod` for user-type trait dispatch produces wrong/garbage results when the impl method returns a scalar (Reproduction: `/tmp/baseline-jit2.shape` direct `c.index(5)` call in JIT prints `-1407374883553280` instead of `15` — same JIT MIR-kind-tracker imprecision documented at W1.3 row "shared pre-existing JIT MIR-kind-tracker imprecision for `CallMethod`-returned operator-trait `KindedSlot`"). Plus the print-on-TypedObject JIT SURFACE at `box_typed_object` (`crates/shape-jit/src/ffi/value_ffi.rs:516-518`) — pre-existing, tracked as `W17-jit-typed-object-arc-storage-migration`. Both are W1-sibling-shared (W1.1 through W1.10 share the same JIT defect class). **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 92→92; ZERO new)**: dispatch prompt was empirically accurate at all surface-and-stop points — index-access dispatch path correctly identified as differing from binary-op dispatch (separate pipeline via `Expr::IndexAccess` not BinaryOp), `compile_expr_index_access` correctly identified as the read emit site, `compile_expr_assign IndexAccess` branch correctly identified as the write emit site, associated-types-vs-generic-params choice correctly identified as a Surface-A decision point. Two minor type-coercion fixes during build (TypePath `.into()` for `TypeAnnotation::Reference("Key")` + `arg_count: u16` not `u8` for `TypedMethodCall`) caught at first `cargo check` — both expected adjustments to the AST and Operand surface types, NOT classification imprecisions. NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE. **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in code or doc (the broader-family regex `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` has zero hits in the W1.11 diff or this row — new helper `receiver_type_implements_trait` is named for its boolean question, `emit_index_trait_call` named for its emission action, both descriptive-by-purpose); NO Bool-default (Index/IndexMut do not register built-in impls for Array/HashMap/string — typed-opcode fast paths service those, registering a default-bookkeeping impl would require multi-shape parametric registration which the trait-impls-by-name semantics doesn't support cleanly, deferred to W4 follow-up if `<T: Index>` bound checking proves load-bearing); NO parallel-implementation framing (the new Index/IndexMut dispatch sits in the SAME `compile_expr_index_access` / `compile_expr_assign IndexAccess` paths as the typed-array fast paths — it's a sibling arm after the fast-path checks, NOT a parallel pipeline); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (W1-sibling-shared JIT defect class explicitly surfaced with named root cause + reproduction fixture + W1.3/W1.6 sibling-row cross-references); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI resurrection (Index/IndexMut dispatch uses the same `OpCode::CallMethod` + `TypedMethodCall` operand shape as every other Phase 2.5+ operator dispatch); NO retain-banner / SUPERSEDED-banner in the new index.shape file (per 2026-05-17 strip-wholesale binding — index.shape is fresh content, no archaeological framing); Own all code quality. **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE in-scope. JIT-side defect (named above) is W1-sibling-shared territory; ceiling-c bound check: 5 source edits + 1 new file + 1 AGENTS.md row, ~228 LoC + 71 LoC new file. **D-α status**: single-session sub-cluster close; no multi-session chain. | v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W1.11 Index + IndexMut operator traits for user-defined types (`c[k]` / `c[k] = v` desugars to UFCS `Index::index(c, k)` / `IndexMut::index_set(c, k, v)` via CallMethod; index-access dispatch pipeline distinct from binary-op pipeline; generic-params parametric pattern mirrors Iterable<T>; built-in typed-array fast paths unchanged) | `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/index.shape` [NEW] + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` + `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs` (register_operator_traits +Index +IndexMut) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/property_access.rs` (compile_expr_index_access +Index trait dispatch + 2 helpers) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/assignment.rs` (compile_expr_assign IndexAccess branch +IndexMut trait dispatch) + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/mod.rs` (tracker_type_name_for_identifier visibility flip) + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (close gates all green; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; W1.11 fixture VM = `15` then `99` correct; built-in typed-array fast path non-regression verified; JIT-side CallMethod defect identified as pre-existing W1-sibling-shared territory + named follow-up; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; NO Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer; v0.3 acceptance criterion "Operator coverage full set for user types" advanced for Index + IndexMut rows of W1 sub-cluster matrix — W1 family now COMPLETE for v0.3 (W1.1-W1.10 prior + W1.11 this row)) | 2026-05-17 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w1-8-ord` | `../shape-w1-8-ord` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W1.8 sub-cluster: `Ord` operator trait for user-defined types (`<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`); single-method `cmp(other: Self) -> int` dispatch (Rust convention) lowered to typed-int comparison against 0 via existing `emit_cmp_result_comparison`; binary-op dispatch arm at `binary_ops.rs:1461-1475` fixed to call the post-cmp comparison emitter for ordered ops.** Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a2b04a26`. **Ord shape choice — cmp+int (NOT 4-method lt/le/gt/ge)**: dispatch prompt §"surface-and-stop" listed two options; structural pre-wiring at HEAD fully determined the choice. `operator_trait_for_op(BinaryOp::Greater|Less|GreaterEq|LessEq) = Some("Ord")` already at `binary_ops.rs:31-33`; `operator_trait_method_for_op(...) = Some("cmp")` already at `binary_ops.rs:50-52`; `emit_cmp_result_comparison(compiler, op)` helper at `binary_ops.rs:57-69` already lowers an int-on-stack into the per-op typed int compare against 0 (`LtInt` / `GtInt` / `LteInt` / `GteInt`); `register_operator_traits` at `type_system/environment/mod.rs:563-580` already registers `trait Ord { cmp(self, other) -> int }` + primitive impls at `:593-612` for `int`/`i*`/`u*`/`number`/`f*`/`bool`/`string`. The cmp+int shape was the only viable option; the 4-method lt/le/gt/ge option would have required reverting all four pre-wired surfaces. **Territory (3 files; +83 LoC, -3 LoC)**: (a) NEW `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/ord.shape` (77 lines) — `trait Ord { cmp(other: Self): int }` with Phase 4 add.shape-mirror docstring naming the typed-comparison dispatch sites for built-in scalars + UFCS path for user types + explicit "negative when self<other, zero when equal, positive when self>other" semantics + Eq independence note (`Eq` is W1.7's parallel-territory sibling trait — implementing `Ord` does NOT auto-derive `Eq`); (b) `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` (+1 line; `from std::core::ord use { Ord }` after the existing `from std::core::not use { Not }` import); (c) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs` (+6 lines, -1 line at L1461-1480) — the Phase 2.5 operator-trait dispatch arm at L1461 was emitting `CallMethod("cmp")` then `return Ok(())` WITHOUT lowering the returned int via `emit_cmp_result_comparison`, so the user saw the raw `-10` int from `cmp` instead of `true`/`false` per operator semantics. Fix: insert `if is_ordered_comparison(op) { emit_cmp_result_comparison(self, op); }` between the trait call and the return, mirroring `try_emit_trait_dispatch` at L83 which had the correct shape. **W1.8 fixture VM correctness verified** (`/tmp/w1-8-fixture.shape`: `type Score { value: int } / impl Ord for Score { method cmp(other: Score) -> int { self.value - other.value } } / let a = Score{value:10} / let b = Score{value:20} / print(a<b) / print(a<=b) / print(a>b) / print(a>=b)`): VM output 4/4 correct — `true / true / false / false`. **JIT-side pre-existing SURFACE confirmed inherited from W1.1-W1.7 class** (NOT a W1.8 regression): JIT mode emits `compile_binop_dynamic_cmp: kind-untyped ordered cmp Lt reached the JIT — SURFACE per W10 playbook §5: producing-MIR kind-tracker gap` at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/rvalues.rs:784`; verified via control fixture `/tmp/w1-8-add-control.shape` that user-type `Add` (Phase 4) reproduces the same SURFACE class (`compile_binop_dynamic_arith: kind-untyped arith Add reached the JIT`) at `rvalues.rs:757` under JIT at HEAD `a2b04a26`. Both are the same cluster-2 territory user-impl-method `self.field` access kind-tracker gap that W1.1-W1.6 AGENTS rows already flagged; W1.8 inherits the same JIT-gap classification. **Close gates all green**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved (s1=4950/4950 ✓ s2=30/30 ✓ s3=x/x ✓ s4=2/2 ✓ s5=x/x ✓); W1.8 fixture VM 4/4 correct (`true`/`true`/`false`/`false`); AGENTS.md row appended (this row); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer ✓. **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 92→93; 1 new)**: #93 — the Phase 2.5 operator-trait dispatch arm at `binary_ops.rs:1461-1475` was incomplete for ordered comparison ops (Ord). The arm emitted `CallMethod(method_name)` followed by an unconditional `return Ok(())`, omitting the `emit_cmp_result_comparison` step that the parallel `try_emit_trait_dispatch` shell at L71-85 correctly performs at L83. Class = "parallel-implementation drift between Phase 2.5 main-arm dispatch and try_emit_trait_dispatch fallback shell — same trait-method-call pattern duplicated, one site updated for cmp-int lowering, other site silently missed". Caught only via the W1.8 fixture's empirical 4-output check (the raw `-10` int leaking to `print` was the smoking gun). Recommendation: a future cleanup wave could consolidate the two dispatch shells into one helper that handles all post-call lowering uniformly. Sibling-class concern: the `BinaryOp::Add` dedicated arm at L789 calls `emit_operator_trait_call(self, "add")` WITHOUT `emit_cmp_result_comparison` — correct for Add (no comparison needed), but the parallel-implementation pattern means any future operator that requires post-call lowering (e.g. a Try operator returning Result with `?` post-call) will need an explicit per-arm fix again. Documented for downstream awareness; not blocking. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in code or ord.shape doc (broader-family regex `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` zero hits in diff); NO ValueWord resurrection; NO Bool-default; NO parallel-implementation framing of the Eq/Ord pair (the ord.shape doc explicitly states `Ord` and `Eq` are independent traits in Shape, NOT a unified comparison trait with a structural-equivalence layer); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (the JIT-side `compile_binop_dynamic_cmp` SURFACE is named with class + destination cite to cluster-2 user-impl-method kind-tracker territory + W10 playbook §5 sibling, NOT silently absorbed); NO refuse #11 Ptr-newtype-shim; NO ARCHAEOLOGY (NEW ord.shape file is fresh content, no SUPERSEDED/RETIRED markers); NO CLAUDE.md modifications; NO ADR amendments; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; Own all code quality (the dispatch-arm bug was identified empirically via fixture-output check, not papered over with a workaround). **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE for W1.8 scope. JIT-side `compile_binop_dynamic_cmp` SURFACE is W1.1-W1.7 sibling territory + cluster-2 user-impl-method kind-tracker territory. **D-α status**: single-session sub-cluster close; no multi-session chain. **Territory disjoint from W1.7 (parallel Eq)**: W1.7's `eq.shape` + `BinaryOp::Equal`/`NotEqual` dispatch via `compile_typed_equality` is a fully separate dispatch path at `binary_ops.rs:426-470` + `:1233-1235`; W1.8's ordered-comparison dispatch path at `binary_ops.rs:1461-1480` + `try_emit_trait_dispatch` at L71-85 does not overlap. `prelude.shape` is shared but additive at distinct lines (`+from std::core::eq use { Eq }` vs `+from std::core::ord use { Ord }`). | v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W1.8 Ord operator trait for user types (single-method `cmp(other:Self)->int` per Rust convention; binary-op dispatch arm fix to call `emit_cmp_result_comparison` after trait call for ordered ops) | `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/ord.shape` [NEW] + `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs` + `AGENTS.md` (this row) | closed (close gates all green; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; W1.8 fixture VM 4/4 correct; JIT-side SURFACE inherited from W1.1-W1.7 user-impl-method kind-tracker gap; 1 new imprecision #93 surfaced re Phase 2.5 dispatch-arm vs try_emit_trait_dispatch parallel-implementation drift on post-call cmp lowering; refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes; NO Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer) | 2026-05-17 |

| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w1-7-eq` | `../shape-w1-7-eq` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W1.7 `Eq` operator trait for user-defined types; `a == b` / `a != b` on types implementing `Eq` desugars to UFCS `Eq::eq(a, b)` (with extra `Not` opcode for `!=`) via `CallMethod` (arg_count=1).** v0.3 R2 sub-cluster per `docs/v0.3-roadmap.md` §W1 (W1.7 row). Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a2b04a26` (post Phase 3a-ii FULL CLOSE). **Per-edit landed (3 edits, 1 new file)**: (1) NEW `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/eq.shape` (58 lines) — `trait Eq { eq(other: Self): bool }` mirroring Phase 4 `add.shape` / W1.1 `sub.shape` doc-block shape but distinguished by bool-return semantics (not Self) since equality is comparison not arithmetic; explicit module-level note that `!=` is auto-derived via `!eq()` desugar at compile time so user code only authors `eq`; reflexivity/symmetry/transitivity authoring conventions documented; Known-implementations table lists typed `EqInt`/`EqNumber`/`EqString`/`EqDecimal`+`NeqInt`/`NeqNumber` opcodes for primitives + user-type trait path. NO `EqAssign` sibling (no `==` compound-assign syntax in Shape, unlike Add/AddAssign for `+=`). (2) `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/prelude.shape` — added `from std::core::eq use { Eq }` after the existing `from std::core::not use { Not }` import so `Eq` is auto-imported. (3) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs` — extended `operator_trait_for_op` with `BinaryOp::Equal | BinaryOp::NotEqual => Some("Eq")` + `operator_trait_method_for_op` with same op pair → `Some("eq")`; appended user-type Eq dispatch block in `compile_typed_equality` AFTER the typed primitive emission and cross-numeric coercion attempts but BEFORE the strict-typing error fallback. The dispatch detects Vec2-shaped receivers via three sources in order: (a) slot tracker's `local_types` schema name for `Expr::Identifier(name)` when `resolve_local` matches, (b) slot tracker's `binding_types` for top-level module bindings, (c) `infer_expr_type` as final fallback. When the left operand's resolved type-name implements `Eq` per `type_inference.env.type_implements_trait`, compiles both operands (receiver + arg pushed in order), emits `CallMethod` with `TypedMethodCall { method_id: from_name("eq"), arg_count: 1, receiver_type_tag: 0xFF }` via the existing `emit_operator_trait_call` helper, then appends `OpCode::Not` for the `!=` case. Returns bool — `last_expr_type_info` set to `bool` with `StorageHint::Bool` so the implicit-return path emits `ReturnValueBool` and the host-boundary kind track re-tags raw bool bits. The slot-tracker-first detection (NOT `try_emit_trait_dispatch` shared with arithmetic) is necessary because `compile_typed_equality` runs BEFORE either operand is compiled, so `last_expr_schema` reflects the previous expression's residue, not the left operand. Eq trait declaration + primitive impl registrations already existed at `crates/shape-runtime/src/type_system/environment/mod.rs:544-561` + `:593-606` (pre-W1.7 wiring from Phase 3a Option β); NO `environment/mod.rs` edits required. **W1.7 fixture** (`/tmp/w1-7-fixture.shape` per dispatch §5; `type Vec2 { x: number, y: number } / impl Eq for Vec2 { method eq(other: Vec2) -> bool { self.x == other.x && self.y == other.y } } / let a = Vec2 { x: 3.0, y: 4.0 } / let b = Vec2 { x: 3.0, y: 4.0 } / let c = Vec2 { x: 5.0, y: 5.0 } / print(a == b) / print(a == c) / print(a != c)`): **VM mode** `true / false / true` ✓ (3.0==3.0 && 4.0==4.0 → true; 3.0==5.0 || 4.0==5.0 → false; negation of false → true). **JIT mode** `true / true / true` — JIT diverges on the `a == c` case (returns true instead of false); this is the pre-existing JIT MIR kind-tracker gap on user-impl-method `self.field` access inside the eq method body, identical class to the W1.5 Neg / W1.6 Not JIT defect surfaced in their respective rows (folds to `JIT-call-method-user-trait-{unary,binary}-fix` cluster-2 territory; W1.7 inherits the same shape per Phase 3b dispatch sequencing — W1.7 VM-correct gate is the acceptance criterion per dispatch §criterion 6). **Smoke matrix 5/5 VM == JIT preserved**: s1=4950/4950 ✓; s2=30/30 ✓; s3=x/x ✓; s4=2/2 ✓; s5=x/x ✓ (release binary `cargo build --release --bin shape` 3m41s; per-fixture VM + JIT verified). **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --tests` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓; smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved ✓; W1.7 fixture VM-correct (3/3 expected outputs) ✓; AGENTS.md row appended (this row) ✓; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer ✓. **Imprecisions surfaced (0 new in-scope)**: NONE. Sibling JIT defect class (user-impl-method CallMethod on TypedObject receivers returning wrong result) is W1.5/W1.6-shared territory already named in their rows, not W1.7 territory. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in any new helper or doc (zero `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` regex hits in diff — helpers named by semantics: `slot_type_name`, `has_eq_impl`, `trait_name`); NO Bool-default (`Eq` is registered for the explicit comparable-types set `{int, i8..i64, u8..u64, number, f32, f64, bool, string}` at `environment/mod.rs:593-606` — this is the canonical primitive coverage from Phase 3a Option β, not a Bool-default placeholder); NO parallel-implementation framing (the user-type Eq dispatch sits at the END of `compile_typed_equality`'s fall-through chain, AFTER all primitive-typed emission attempts; NOT a parallel match block); NO refuse #10 anti-deferral (JIT user-impl-method divergence surfaced with explicit fold-to W1.5/W1.6 sibling territory cite + class-of-issue framing); NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / Upvalue / Interface resurrection (Eq dispatch uses the same `OpCode::CallMethod` + `TypedMethodCall` operand shape as every other Phase 2.5+ operator trait dispatch — `Not` opcode for `!=` is the existing typed-bool-negation opcode, NOT a new conversion opcode); NO retain-banner / SUPERSEDED-banner in the new eq.shape file (per 2026-05-17 strip-wholesale binding — eq.shape is fresh content, no archaeological framing); Reading 3 cadence preserved (3 mandatory anchors read pre-edit: CLAUDE.md Forbidden Patterns + W1.1-W1.6 sibling stdlib patterns + binary_ops.rs::compile_typed_equality entry-point); CLAUDE.md mod requires FLAG (none required — W1.7 is stdlib + compiler dispatch tier, no runtime architectural change); Own all code quality (no fabricated "I proved it" — every type assertion goes through `type_inference.env.type_implements_trait` per established Phase 4 / W1.1-W1.6 pattern). **Genuinely intractable targets requiring supervisor disposition**: NONE for W1.7 scope. **D-α status**: single-session sub-cluster close; no multi-session chain. **Territory disjoint from W1.1-W1.6 + W1.8-W1.11**: sibling sub-clusters touch their own per-operator stdlib files (`add.shape` / `sub.shape` / `mul.shape` / `div.shape` / `modulo.shape` / `neg.shape` / `not.shape` exist; `eq.shape` is the W1.7-exclusive new file); `prelude.shape` shared but W1.x edits are line-additive at the same prelude section without overlap; `binary_ops.rs` shared with W1.1-W1.4 (Sub/Mul/Div/Mod arms) but W1.7 edits are confined to the `Equal | NotEqual` entries in two map-lookup helpers + a single new block inside `compile_typed_equality` (textually disjoint from the arithmetic-arm extensions). **LoC delta**: +75 net across 3 files (eq.shape +58 new, prelude.shape +1, binary_ops.rs +63/-5 net per `git diff --stat`). | v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W1.7 Eq operator trait for user types (`==`/`!=` on user-defined types desugars to `Eq::eq(a,b)` via CallMethod; (b) Field access `print(ti.name)` from comptime user code surfaces the pre-existing `MakeFieldRef SURFACE: field_type_tag 8 (FIELD_TAG_ANY) has no statically-sourceable NativeKind` error (ADR-006 §2.7.13 / Q14) — same blocker affects `build_config().version` and is the reason the `w17_comptime_build_config_dispatches_end_to_end` test wraps in `catch_unwind`; the W7 fixture validates dispatch without exercising the field-projection path. Both surfaces are W7-downstream (upstream marshal + MakeFieldRef MIR-side fixes), not W7 territory. (c) Recursive `Array<TypedObject>` field carriers for `TypeInfo.fields` / `TypeInfo.generic_params` deferred to W7-followup post V3-S5 ckpt-5/6 monomorphized Array carrier landing (CLAUDE.md "Known Constraints" v2-raw-heap-audit entry); current TypeInfo schema is `{ name, kind }` two-field — `fields` + `generic_params` slices are W7-followup. **Refusal discipline preserved**: NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing (zero broader-family regex hits in diff); NO Bool-default (the marshal-layer fallback surfaces `__type_info_marshal_pending__` sentinel + `TypeKind::Unknown` discriminator — explicit fallback path, not silent Bool-default); NO ValueWord / kind-blind ABI / Interface keyword resurrection; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO CLAUDE.md modification (L125 already accurate after W7 lands); NO ADR amendment (Q5 DEFER); empirical-verification-first applied (every claim grep-verified at worktree HEAD pre-edit). **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 92→94; 2 new)**: #93 — audit-doc §5.1 estimated 150-200 LoC for the recursive `TypeInfo` materializer assuming `Array<TypedObject>` field carriers were available; empirical at worktree HEAD `a2b04a26` showed `HeapValue::TypedArray(Arc<TypedArrayData>)` arm DELETED at V3-S5 ckpt-1/ckpt-4 + `HeapKind::TypedArray = 8` ordinal VACATED (per `crates/shape-value/src/heap_variants.rs:70-89`); the audit-doc framing didn't anticipate this V3-S5 territory closing the array-of-TypedObject carrier surface. W7 minimal slice ships the 2-field `{ name, kind }` shape that doesn't transit this surface. #94 — audit-doc §4.6 + dispatch prompt's `TypeKind::Array(TypeInfo)` parametric-payload enum-variant shape conflicts with the comptime-VM's per-program isolated `TypeSchemaRegistry` scope (`crates/shape-runtime/src/type_schema/current.rs::SyncRegistryScope`); enum-variant construction at the Rust side requires schema lookup that crosses the registry boundary cleanly which is not the current shape. W7 string-encoded `kind` is the cross-boundary stable form. Both imprecisions are framing-vs-infrastructure mismatches, NOT audit-correctness gaps — the audit-doc recommendation (b) is correct; the implementation slice is bounded by the V3-S5 + comptime-VM-registry-isolation reality. **Genuinely intractable in-wave**: NONE — W7 minimal slice closes per the bounded-scope reading of Q1-Q5 dispositions; the `TypeInfo.fields` + `TypeInfo.kind`-as-enum-value upgrades are W7-followup waves once their respective upstream constraints lift. | v0.3 R2 W7 `type_info(T)` comptime builtin per audit `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-w7-type_info-comptime-typed-return.md` Option (b) + Q1-Q5 user dispositions | `crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/{types.shape, prelude.shape}` + `crates/shape-runtime/src/builtin_metadata.rs` + `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/{comptime.rs, comptime_builtins.rs, expressions/function_calls.rs, expressions/mod.rs, statements.rs}` + `AGENTS.md` (this row); 8 files changed | closed (close gates all green; 12/12 verify-merge; check-no-dynamic exit 0; cargo check workspace exit 0; 6/6 comptime tests pass incl. new W7 contract test; smoke fixture runs end-to-end; 2 new imprecisions cumulative 92→94 — both framing-vs-infrastructure mismatches not audit-correctness gaps) | 2026-05-17 |
| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w9-annotation-imports` | `../shape-w9-annotation-imports` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W9 sub-cluster (annotation imports fix path (i) per supervisor disposition).** Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a2b04a26` (post-Phase 3a-ii FULL CLOSE). **Background**: W3.2-D imprecision 92 surfaced that CLAUDE.md Known Constraints §Annotation imports asserted namespace import worked but empirical evidence at HEAD showed all 3 documented forms failed with "Unknown annotation '@remote'": bare `use std::core::remote; @remote(...)`, qualified `use std::core::remote; @remote::remote(...)`, AND named `from std::core::remote use { @remote }; @remote(...)`. Per supervisor disposition path (i) 2026-05-17: FIX resolution path; cross-module annotation imports must work end-to-end. **Root-cause analysis (3-layer)**: (a) `register_graph_imports_for_module` Namespace branch did not iterate `dep_node.interface.exports` for `ModuleExportKind::Annotation` entries, so `imported_annotations` was never populated on `use std::core::remote`; (b) `resolve_compiled_annotation_name_str` short-circuited `name.contains("::")` with `return None`, killing qualified `@remote::remote` resolution before it could resolve the local prefix against `graph_namespace_map`; (c) the most load-bearing root cause: `build_module_graph_with_prelude_structure` Step 1 only attempted the Shape AST overlay probe via `loader.load_module(&ext.name)` when `ext.module_artifacts.iter().any(|a| a.source.is_some()) || !ext.shape_sources.is_empty()` — but native stdlib modules like `std::core::remote` (`shape-vm/src/executor/builtins/remote_builtins.rs::create_remote_module`) don't carry shape_sources on the ModuleExports struct; the Shape overlay (`crates/shape-runtime/stdlib-src/core/remote.shape` with `pub annotation remote(addr) {...}`) lives in the embedded stdlib registered via `EMBEDDED_STDLIB_MODULES` (build.rs-generated). The has-source heuristic missed the embedded-stdlib path entirely, so the Shape AST was never attached to the `std::core::remote` node, so `compile_module_from_graph` never compiled the annotation def into `compiled_annotations`, so all 3 resolution paths failed. **Fix shape (5 files)**: (1) `crates/shape-vm/src/module_graph.rs` — Step 1 always probes the loader for a Shape overlay regardless of the `has_shape_source` heuristic (the heuristic missed embedded-stdlib sources by design; cite added in code: "Source (b) does NOT show up on the `ext.module_artifacts` / `ext.shape_sources` fields"). (2) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/statements.rs::register_graph_imports_for_module` — Namespace branch iterates `dep_node.interface.exports` and registers each `ModuleExportKind::Annotation` entry in `imported_annotations` with `hidden_module_name = canonical_path` (no synthetic `__annimport__<hash>` needed; the dep module's `qualify_module_item` pass already qualified the annotation as `canonical_path::ann_name` in `compiled_annotations`). Named branch simplified: `hidden_module_name` now also = `canonical_path` (drops the `__annimport__<hash>` synthetic name; the synthetic name pointed to nothing — `compiled_annotations` only ever held the canonical qualified name). (3) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs::resolve_compiled_annotation_name_str` — qualified `@local::name` form now resolves the local prefix against `graph_namespace_map` first (canonical for graph compile), falling back to `module_scope_sources` (legacy / non-graph compile). The `name.contains("::") return None` early-exit is replaced with the qualified-form lookup. The inline import-scope-source registration loop in `compile()` drops the synthetic `hidden_module_name` for `Named { is_annotation }`, registering against `canonical_path` directly. (4) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/statements.rs::collect_module_runtime_exports` — annotations are dropped from the module-object's runtime exports (both `has_explicit_exports` and fallback branches). This was the second-order bug surfaced by the AST attach in (1): once `remote.shape`'s AST compiles, `compile_module_decl`'s `collect_module_runtime_exports` emits `module_object = { remote: std::core::remote::remote }` whose RHS has no runtime variable binding (annotations are compile-time only, live in `compiled_annotations` not as runtime values). Code cite added. (5) `crates/shape-jit/src/executor.rs` — JIT path passes `self.bytecode_executor.extensions()` to both `build_graph_and_stdlib_names` AND sets `compiler.extension_registry` via `with_extensions(extensions)`. Without this the JIT path was a parallel-but-broken implementation: it built the graph with empty extensions (so the hybridization probe couldn't find any native modules to attach Shape overlays to) AND it created the compiler without an extension_registry (so `is_native_module_export` always returned false and `__call` resolution failed inside the annotation handler body). **Hidden cleanup**: removed `hidden_annotation_import_module_name` + `is_hidden_annotation_import_module_name` from `crates/shape-vm/src/module_resolution.rs` (no callers left after the per-import-site changes; NO ARCHAEOLOGY discipline per 2026-05-17 — deleted wholesale, not banner-marked). Removed 2 `is_hidden_annotation_import_module_name` call sites in `compiler/helpers.rs::resolve_scoped_module_binding_name` + `compiler/statements.rs::compile_module_decl` (the predicates always returned `false` post-cleanup since `__annimport__` names are no longer created). **Test inversion (3 sites)**: `tools/shape-test/tests/modules_visibility/scoped_contract.rs:65-79` `scoped_contract_namespace_annotation_refs_use_double_colon` — `#[should_panic]` REMOVED; test now asserts qualified `@remote::remote(...)` resolves cleanly. `:81-96` `scoped_contract_named_annotation_import_enables_bare_annotation` — `#[should_panic]` REMOVED; test now asserts named-import + bare `@remote(...)` works. `:110-124` `scoped_contract_namespace_import_does_not_bind_bare_annotations` RENAMED to `scoped_contract_namespace_import_binds_bare_annotations` with `expect_run_err_contains("remote")` → `expect_output("ok")` (the test's original negative-contract intent is reversed by W9 per supervisor disposition path (i); the rename surfaces the contract reversal at the test-name level). **W9 fixture smoke** (`/tmp/w9-fixture.shape` + `/tmp/w9-debug-fixture.shape` + `/tmp/w9-qualified-fixture.shape`): all 6 paths (3 forms × 2 modes — namespace VM/JIT, named VM/JIT, qualified VM/JIT) print "ok" cleanly. **Smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved**: pre-existing JIT failure on smoke 5 (`fn add(a, b) { a + b }; print(add(2,3))` produces `JIT execution error (code: -1)`) is baseline at `a2b04a26` — confirmed via `git stash` reverify; NOT a W9 regression. **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 (warnings-only baseline preserved) ✓; `cargo test -p shape-test --test modules_visibility scoped_contract` — 11/14 pass (3 pre-existing failures: `scoped_contract_hashmap_requires_explicit_import` + `scoped_contract_result_constructors_require_explicit_import` + `scoped_contract_namespace_function_calls_use_double_colon`; all 3 fail identically at HEAD `a2b04a26` baseline per `git stash` reverify — NOT W9 regressions); all 3 W9-territory tests pass cleanly (2 inverted + 1 renamed) ✓; `cargo test -p shape-test --test modules_visibility` (full suite) — 126/136 pass; same 10 failures present at baseline (3 scoped_contract pre-existing + 7 unrelated: complex/inline_modules/imports_exports/visibility, all unrelated to annotation imports) ✓; AGENTS.md row appended (this row) ✓; CLAUDE.md Known Constraints §Annotation imports STRIPPED wholesale per supervisor disposition (`CLAUDE.md` line 336 deleted in commit) ✓; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer per dispatch ✓. **Refusal discipline preserved across 8 axes**: NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer; NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing in any new code or comment (zero `(decode|tag|kind|dispatch|value.call|closure.callback|frame.setup|callee|capture) (bridge|probe|helper|hop|translator|adapter|shim)` regex hits in diff — new code framed as "register annotation defs from imported module" + "qualified form lookup" + "always probe the loader for a Shape overlay", purpose-named); NO Bool-default; NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI resurrection (zero runtime carrier touched — fix is pure compiler/graph-build layer); NO parallel-implementation framing (the JIT-executor extensions fix collapses an unintentional parallel-but-broken duality between VM and JIT compile paths — both now uniformly pass `extensions()` to graph build + `with_extensions()` to compiler; explicitly NOT a defected duality, it's collapsing an existing one); NO ARCHAEOLOGY (deleted `__annimport__<hash>` synthetic-name helpers wholesale per supervisor 2026-05-17 NO ARCHAEOLOGY discipline + bundled CLAUDE.md Known Constraint strip into the same close commit); CLAUDE.md mod authorized (supervisor disposition 2026-05-17 bundled strip with W9 fix); Reading 3 cadence preserved (3 mandatory anchors read pre-edit: CLAUDE.md Forbidden Patterns + W3.2-D divergence audit + `crates/shape-runtime/src/annotation_context.rs` + 5 module-graph/module-loader/visitor source files per dispatch). **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 92→95; 3 new)**: #93 — dispatch prompt characterized the fix as "~50-100 LoC compiler work" focused on annotation-resolution path; empirical diagnosis surfaced 3 distinct root causes spanning 4 crates (shape-vm compiler + shape-vm module_graph + shape-jit executor) plus a second-order bug (annotations leaking into runtime-exports collection after AST attach). Actual diff ~110 LoC across 5 files + 1 test file. Imprecision class = "scope-estimate-undersized-by-root-cause-multiplicity". #94 — supervisor "Known Constraints §Annotation imports" entry claim "namespace import (`use std::core::remote`) inlines the whole module AST (`module_resolution.rs:582`), making annotations available by bare name" was WRONG at HEAD `a2b04a26`. Empirical reverify: bare `@remote` after `use std::core::remote` produces "Unknown annotation '@remote'" at baseline. The entry was aspirational, not descriptive. Imprecision class = "Known-Constraints-entry-aspirational-not-descriptive" — sibling of v0.3 R1 W3.1 slice-D imprecision #5 (book-claims-divergence-from-source). #95 — `shape run` default execution mode is JIT (per `bin/shape-cli/src/cli_args.rs:78-85`), not VM; smoke fixtures executed against `shape run` without `--mode vm` flag exercise the JIT path. Surfaced when the W9 fixture initially appeared to "still fail" until I switched to `--mode vm`; the failure was actually in the JIT path (root cause c above — JIT executor passing empty extensions). Imprecision class = "execution-mode-default-not-surfaced-in-dispatch". Resolution: JIT path fix landed alongside VM fix; both modes verified end-to-end. **CLAUDE.md modifications**: Known Constraints §Annotation imports STRIPPED (1 entry removed at line 336 per supervisor disposition; bundled into W9 close commit per supervisor 2026-05-17 NOT-A-SEPARATE-CEREMONY discipline). **ADR amendments**: NONE. **Territory disjoint verification**: Files touched: `crates/shape-vm/src/{module_graph.rs, module_resolution.rs, compiler/compiler_impl_reference_model.rs, compiler/helpers.rs, compiler/statements.rs}` + `crates/shape-jit/src/executor.rs` + `tools/shape-test/tests/modules_visibility/scoped_contract.rs` + `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md`.
