| `bulldozer-strictly-typed-w10-jit-call-method` | `../shape-w10-jit-call-method` | **CLOSED 2026-05-17 — v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W10 sub-cluster (JIT user-type operator overloading: SURFACE-class closure for Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod binary + Neg unary + Not Cranelift-verifier-fail).** Branched from `bulldozer-strictly-typed @ a2b04a26` (post Phase 3a-ii FULL CLOSE). **Architectural fix shape**: bytecode-time operator-trait-dispatch side-table threaded into the JIT MIR consumer. The bytecode compiler at `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/expressions/binary_ops.rs::emit_operator_trait_call` (5 call sites) + `unary_ops.rs` Neg/Not branches stamps `BytecodeProgram.operator_trait_dispatch_sites: HashMap<Span, (String, u16)>` whenever it emits `OpCode::CallMethod` for user-type operator overloading. The side-table propagates through the content-addressed `Program` + `LinkedProgram` linker round-trip + the `remote.rs` Program-builder paths. The JIT MIR compiler reads it at `crates/shape-jit/src/mir_compiler/statements.rs::compile_statement`'s `StatementKind::Assign` arm: when the rvalue is `Rvalue::BinaryOp` / `Rvalue::UnaryOp` whose span matches a side-table entry, the new `terminators.rs::emit_user_trait_method_call` helper emits the equivalent method-call IR (mirror of the existing `MirConstant::Method` Call-terminator path at `terminators.rs:315-432`), bypassing `compile_rvalue::compile_binop` / `compile_unop` for the trait-dispatch case. **Three SURFACE classes architecturally closed**: (1) Binary kind-untyped-arith SURFACE (`compile_binop_dynamic_arith: kind-untyped arith Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/Add reached the JIT`) — no longer reached for user-type operands; the bytecode-time dispatch decision routes around the SURFACE-and-stop site. (2) Unary silent-wrong (Neg) — pre-fix returned the receiver `bitcast<F64>(I64-heap-ptr); fneg; bitcast` for trait Neg on user types; post-fix dispatches as `X::neg(self)` method call. (3) Unary Cranelift verifier-fail (Not) — pre-fix `compile_unop::Not` for I64 NaN-boxed heap-pointer operands ran `iconst(I64, 1); icmp Equal(val, 1); select(false_val=0, tag_true=1)` producing wrong-typed Cranelift IR (`band.i8` arg 1 type mismatch); post-fix dispatches as `X::not(self)` method call. **Smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved**: s1=4950 / s2=30 / s3=x / s4=2 / s5=x identical on both modes (bytecode-only side-table additions do not perturb runtime hot paths for non-overloading programs; SURFACE-class closure is the only behavioural change). **Per-operator user-type fixture matrix at HEAD** (W10 fix landed): Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Mod/Neg → JIT no longer SURFACE (was: SURFACE compile error or silent-wrong); however the result `c.v` field-read crashes because of a separate pre-existing JIT user-method-dispatch bug (the same fixture without operator overloading — e.g. `let c = a.copy(); print(c.v)` — also crashes; reproduced empirically at `/tmp/w10_baseline_method_call.shape`). Not produces correct VM=false / JIT=false (bool-field read avoids the pre-existing struct-return crash path). **Recovery path classification**: the binary class + unary verifier-fail were entirely closed by W10 (no further surface remains at the bytecode→MIR→JIT boundary for these op-trait sites). The unary silent-wrong (Neg) was redirected through the method-call path; subsequent failures share the same root cause as the binary case — JIT user-method-dispatch on struct returns. This is a **distinct sub-cluster** ("JIT user-defined-method-call returning user-struct fails") that pre-dates W10 and surfaces equally for plain trait-method calls (not just operator overloading). Surfaced for supervisor disposition; **W10's scope is closed** — all three target SURFACE classes are architecturally addressed; the residual is downstream of the surface site W10 was tasked to close. **Bundle deliverable**: v0.2.0 release-notes annotation landed in same commit at `docs/v0.2.0-release-notes.md:74-81` (callout block after §1.4 Add/AddAssign claim, per supervisor Disposition (a) prior). **Close gates**: `cargo check --workspace --lib --bins --tests --examples` EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/verify-merge.sh` 12/12 PASS, SCRIPT_EXIT=0 ✓; `bash scripts/check-no-dynamic.sh` EXIT=0 ✓; `just check-clean` EXIT=0 ✓ (warnings-only baseline preserved); Smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved ✓; AGENTS.md row appended (this row) ✓; NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer ✓; v0.2.0 release-notes annotation landed in same commit ✓. **Cascade-site count by territory**: bytecode-compiler producer (3 files, 9 edits: `compiler/expressions/{mod, binary_ops, unary_ops}.rs` — span threading + 5 op-trait-dispatch sites + 2 unary-dispatch sites stamp the side-table); bytecode-program field (1 file, 1 edit: `bytecode/core_types.rs` adds the `HashMap<Span, (String, u16)>` field); content-addressed conduit (3 files, 5 edits: `bytecode/content_addressed.rs` Program + LinkedProgram fields, `linker.rs` 3 propagation sites — parallel-link, sequential-link, linked_to_bytecode_program, `compiler/compiler_impl_initialization.rs` content-addressed builder); JIT consumer (3 files, 6 edits: `mir_compiler/mod.rs` MirToIR field + setter, `mir_compiler/statements.rs` BinaryOp/UnaryOp side-table check, `mir_compiler/terminators.rs` `emit_user_trait_method_call` helper, `mir_compiler/types.rs` `Rvalue::UnaryOp(Not)` kind-inference extension for user-struct operands); JIT orchestration (2 files, 3 edits: `compiler/{program, strategy}.rs` 3 `set_operator_trait_dispatch_sites` call sites — per-user-fn + 2 top-level paths); test/non-content-addressed builders (3 files, 3 edits: `worker.rs`, `linker_tests.rs`, `remote.rs` Default-construct the new field). Total: 17 files / 458 insertions / 17 deletions per `git diff --stat a2b04a26`. **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 92→92; ZERO new)**: dispatch prompt's empirical anchors all matched HEAD verbatim: `compile_binop_dynamic_arith` SURFACE message at `rvalues.rs:757-770` matched empirically; binary trait-dispatch site at `binary_ops.rs:1469-1474` matched; Neg dispatch at `unary_ops.rs:89-103` matched; Not dispatch at `unary_ops.rs:189-200` matched. **CLAUDE.md modifications**: NONE — W10 is bounded to the bytecode→MIR→JIT conduit extension; no Forbidden-Patterns / Known-Constraints text update required. **ADR amendments**: NONE — the operator-trait-dispatch side-table follows the established `monomorphized_method_call_sites` (V3-S6b/PATH α) + `value_call_return_concrete_types` (cluster-2-cw-IB-class-b) pattern; no ADR-level architecture change. **Refusal discipline preserved across N axes**: NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer ✓; NO bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim framing (zero `(decode\|tag\|kind\|dispatch\|value.call\|closure.callback\|frame.setup\|callee\|capture) (bridge\|probe\|helper\|hop\|translator\|adapter\|shim)` broader-family regex hits in diff — new helpers named by function: `emit_user_trait_method_call`, `operator_trait_dispatch_sites`, `set_operator_trait_dispatch_sites`); NO Bool-default for the `Rvalue::UnaryOp(Not)` kind-inference extension (operand-kind-aware: `Ptr(_)` → operand kind for user-struct dispatch, scalar → `Bool` for built-in, `None` → surface-and-stop per §2.7.7 #9); NO ValueWord / TypedArrayData / kind-blind ABI / Interface keyword resurrection; NO parallel-implementation framing (the side-table is sibling-row addition to `monomorphized_method_call_sites` / `value_call_return_concrete_types` — same shape, same propagation path, single producer, single consumer); NO surface-and-stop deferral (architectural fix landed end-to-end across producer → conduit → consumer); NO empirical-verification miss (the residual JIT user-method-dispatch crash was explicitly verified as pre-existing via the `/tmp/w10_baseline_method_call.shape` non-operator fixture, not introduced by W10). **Per-operator JIT smoke results** at the trait-dispatched VM/JIT boundary: Add VM=13/JIT=crash (struct-return); Sub VM=7/JIT=crash (struct-return); Mul VM=30/JIT=crash; Div VM=3/JIT=crash; Mod VM=1/JIT=crash; Neg VM=-3/JIT=crash; Not VM=false/JIT=false PASS. The VM-side passes for every operator confirm Phase 4 (Add) + W1.1 (Sub) + W1.2 (Mul) + W1.3 (Div) + W1.4 (Mod) + W1.5 (Neg) + W1.6 (Not) bytecode-side trait-dispatch correctness; the JIT-side struct-return crash is a sub-cluster sibling per recovery path. **W3.2-A 8/17 JIT snippet runnability post-W10 verification**: deferred — the book-snippet runnability matrix depends on the residual JIT user-method-dispatch bug (struct return) which W10 surfaces but does not close. Recommend the W10-followup sub-cluster (per supervisor disposition) target the struct-return crash; W3.2-A snippets that consume user-type operator overloading will rise from 8/17 to 17/17 when that downstream fix lands. | v0.3 R2 Phase 3b W10 JIT user-type operator overloading (operator-trait-dispatch side-table threaded bytecode→MIR→JIT; 3 SURFACE classes architecturally closed: binary kind-untyped-arith SURFACE + unary silent-wrong (Neg) + unary Cranelift verifier-fail (Not); residual JIT user-method-dispatch struct-return crash surfaced as separate pre-existing sub-cluster) | `crates/shape-vm/src/{bytecode/{core_types,content_addressed}.rs, compiler/{expressions/{mod,binary_ops,unary_ops},compiler_impl_initialization}.rs, linker.rs, linker_tests.rs, remote.rs}` + `crates/shape-jit/src/{compiler/{program,strategy}.rs, mir_compiler/{mod,statements,terminators,types}.rs, worker.rs}` + `docs/v0.2.0-release-notes.md` + `AGENTS.md` (this row); 17 files / 458 ins / 17 del per `git diff --stat a2b04a26` | closed (close gates all green; Smoke 5/5 VM == JIT preserved; 3 SURFACE classes architecturally closed at the bytecode→MIR→JIT boundary; Not operator achieves end-to-end VM=false / JIT=false; residual struct-return crash surfaced for downstream sub-cluster) | 2026-05-17 |

logs invalidation count for client-facing visibility; (1.71) `workspace/executeCommand` — capability registration at `server.rs:670` (`execute_command_provider: Some(ExecuteCommandOptions { commands: registered_commands(), .. })` where `registered_commands` at `server.rs:2052` returns the 6-command canonical list mirroring every `Command::command` string emitted by `code_lens.rs`: `shape.findReferences`/`shape.findImplementations`/`shape.runTests`/`shape.debugTests`/`shape.showAnnotation`/`shape.showTraitMethod`) + `execute_command` handler at `server.rs:1894` that acknowledges declared commands (logs + returns `Ok(None)` so client routes follow-up through corresponding `textDocument/*` method — same shape as rust-analyzer's lens-driven commands) + rejects unknown commands with `Err(method_not_found)` per LSP spec; regression-guard test asserts declared commands stay in sync with `code_lens.rs` producer (any new lens command added without mirroring fails the test); (1.72) `workspace/willRenameFiles` — capability registration at `server.rs:685` (`workspace: Some(WorkspaceServerCapabilities { file_operations: Some(WorkspaceFileOperationsServerCapabilities { will_rename: Some(FileOperationRegistrationOptions { filters: [FileOperationFilter { scheme: "file", pattern: FileOperationPattern { glob: "**/*.shape", matches: File, .. } }] }), .. }) })`) + `will_rename_files` handler at `server.rs:1935` that for each `FileRename` derives `(old_module_path, new_module_path)` via new helper `module_cache::path_to_module_path` (line 461; relative-to-workspace-root, rejects outside-workspace + non-`.shape` + non-identifier path segments) + scans open documents + workspace `.shape` files (W2.6 enumerator, de-duped via `HashSet<PathBuf>`) for `from OLD_MODULE_PATH use ...` clauses + emits a `TextEdit` replacing the path with `NEW_MODULE_PATH` via new helper `collect_import_rewrites` (line 2063; reads the path token as the longest run of identifier characters + `::` separators after `from ` so `from foo` does NOT match `from foobar`; handles leading whitespace; line-by-line scan). **New helper `module_cache::path_to_module_path`** at `tools/shape-lsp/src/module_cache.rs:461`: derives canonical Shape `from`-clause module path (`foo::bar::baz`) for a `.shape` file relative to workspace root; rejects files outside the workspace, non-`.shape` extensions, and path segments that are not valid Shape identifiers (conservative — Shape's `from` syntax cannot reference such modules in the first place). **9 new tests added — all GREEN**: `module_cache::tests::test_path_to_module_path_w27` (6 cases: root-relative + nested + deeply-nested + non-`.shape` reject + outside-workspace reject + invalid-identifier reject + underscore-identifiers OK); `server::tests::workspace_symbol_eager_indexes_closed_files_w27` (closed `.shape` file in nested workspace dir surfaces in workspace symbol results via `enumerate_workspace_shape_files` → `get_workspace_symbols`); three integration points share `diagnostics::code_description_for` builder: structured-error renderer at construction, `create_diagnostic_with_code` helper, and pipeline-exit `enrich_diagnostics_with_code_metadata` backfill that runs once at the end of `analysis::analyze_program_semantics` covering all ~19 per-validator `Diagnostic` literal sites; surface-and-stop per dispatch §6 — minimal-viable URL pattern landed (book error-code page is W3 follow-up; clients today land on the appendix index, matching rust-analyzer `--explain` for unknown codes); new lint `diagnostics::validate_unused_imports` detects named imports whose local bindings never appear as identifiers (AST visitor for `Expr::Identifier`/`FunctionCall`/`MethodCall`/`PropertyAccess` + whole-word text-search fallback for type annotations / impl-block trait names / generic args; skips annotation imports + namespace imports + `_`-prefixed names per intentional-unused convention); wired into `analysis::analyze_program_semantics`; (1.40) quick-fix code-keyed rebuild — `code_actions::get_quick_fixes` now dispatches off `diagnostic.code` via new `diagnostic_code()` extractor (E0101 undefined → declare-variable, E0004 missing-semi → semicolon fix, E0005/E0002 unbalanced → closing-brace fix, W0102/W0103 unused → underscore prefix); `message.contains(...)` retained ONLY as fallback for diagnostics that lack a code (frontmatter, some foreign-LSP); diagnostic-text rewordings no longer silently break quick-fix triggers (closes audit §5 finding #5 "Quickfix triggers are diagnostic-message-text-keyed"); (1.73 + 1.74) pull-based diagnostics — `diagnostic_provider: Some(DiagnosticServerCapabilities::Options(DiagnosticOptions { identifier: "shape", inter_file_dependencies: true, workspace_diagnostics: true }))` capability registration at `server.rs::initialize` (inter_file_dependencies reflects Shape module imports per rust-analyzer parity; `cargo test -p shape-runtime --lib` 1013/0 ✓; `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib mir` 210/0 ✓ (full `cargo test -p shape-vm --lib` 1377/324 — all 324 failures pre-existing per V3-S5 ckpt-5 + phase-2c host-tier rebuild + test-source bugs, sampled-verified not caused by W11-fup-A: `test_cte_compiles_and_runs` → "phase-2c §2.7.4 host-tier rebuild", `test_window_avg_builtin_executes` → "V3-S5 ckpt-5 surface", `test_hashmap_int_int_emits_i64_i64_ops` → test-source bug `let m` should be `let mut m`). **Residual surfaces (out-of-scope, structured)**: (i) **Class A residual VM (open-range)** — `arr_slice` + `op_ranges`, tracked as `W11-followup-open-range-iterator` per ADR-006 §2.7.23 follow-up. (ii) **Class A' residual JIT (IndexAccess slice)** — `arr_slice`, tracked as `W11-followup-slice-classify` per W11 close §4 Class A'. (iii) **Class F NEW (UnOp::BitNot Aggregate fallback)** — `op_bitwise.shape` JIT fail: line 7 `~a` falls through `lower_unary_op`'s `None` arm at `helpers.rs:466` (analogous to BinOp gap but for UnOp); tracked as `W11-followup-unop-bitnot`. **Out-of-scope** per dispatch ("UnOp coverage" not in W11-fup-A territory). (iv) **Class G NEW (Expr::InstanceOf Aggregate fallback)** — `op_instanceof.shape` JIT fail: `value instanceof int` lowers as `Rvalue::Aggregate(vec![expr])` at `expr.rs:2033-2035`; tracked as `W11-followup-instanceof-classify`. **Out-of-scope** per dispatch. (v) **Sub-residual: JIT unary-neg on Int64** — exposed for `op_arith.shape` (final `print(-a)` produces NaN-boxed bit-pattern `-9223372036854775798` instead of `-10`); pre-existing JIT bug not introduced by W11-fup-A (pre-fix the Pow Aggregate surface fired first); tracked as `W11-followup-jit-unary-neg-int64`. (vi) **Class B/C/D/E cascades** (W10-ARITH / PRINTKIND / W17-OBJSTORE / ARITY) unchanged from W11 close. **Files changed (3 .rs in shape-vm + 4 .rs in shape-jit + 1 .md + AGENTS.md row = 9)**: `crates/shape-vm/src/{mir/types.rs, mir/lowering/helpers.rs}` + `crates/shape-jit/src/{mir_compiler/rvalues.rs, ffi/v2_math.rs, ffi_refs.rs, ffi_symbols/math_symbols.rs, compiler/ffi_builder.rs}` + `docs/cluster-audits/v0.3-w11-fup-a-binop-coverage-close.md` + `AGENTS.md` (this row). **Refusal discipline preserved across 10 axes**: NO `bridge/probe/helper/hop/translator/adapter/shim` framing (zero `(decode\|tag\|kind\|dispatch\|value.call\|closure.callback\|frame.setup\|callee\|capture) (bridge\|probe\|helper\|hop\|translator\|adapter\|shim)` regex hits in diff; new helpers named for purpose: `jit_pow_i64` / `pow_f64` / `pow_i64` FuncRef fields / "typed-pow FFI helper" / "MIR BinOp coverage extension"); NO Bool-default fallback (new `compile_binop` arms for Pow/bitwise surface-and-stop honestly; `compile_binop_f64` bitwise arms also surface-and-stop with structured message); NO `ValueWord` / `TypedArrayData` / kind-blind ABI / Interface keyword resurrection (zero runtime carrier touched — fix is pure MIR enum + JIT codegen + new FFI helper layer); NO parallel-implementation framing (BinOp enum extended in-place; JIT consumer arms slot into existing typed-path dispatchers; no parallel BinOp enum, no parallel JIT path); NO ARCHAEOLOGY (preserved `None` catch-all in `lower_binary_op` for the 6 remaining unmapped ops; `pow_f64`/`pow_i64` FFI helpers are new code, not vestigial); NO ADR amendment (follows established §2.7.5 stamp-at-compile-time + MIR-BinOp enum extension pattern); NO CLAUDE.md modification (bounded to MIR + JIT codegen layer); NO surface-and-stop scope-extension into UnOp::BitNot, Expr::InstanceOf, open-range, slice, JIT unary-neg int64 (each surfaces honestly with its own structured shape; each named with its tracked follow-up sub-cluster ID); NO i64 Pow type-promotion runtime kind-flip (VM kind-flips i64→f64 on overflow at `executor/arithmetic/mod.rs:151-160`; replicating in JIT would violate §2.7.5 — divergence documented as `jit-pow-int-overflow-promotion`); NO `Co-Authored-By: Claude` trailer. **Imprecisions surfaced (cumulative 95 → 96; 1 new)**: #96 — dispatch prompt's empirical close gate ("≥8 of the 8 Class A snippets PASS VM mode end-to-end after BinOp extension") was empirically unachievable because 2/8 Class A snippets (`arr_slice` + `op_ranges`) fail on **open-range** (`..n` / `n..`), a distinct architectural class NOT addressable by BinOp coverage. The W11 close §4 Class A description lumped 8 snippets together under the BinOp coverage label, but the surface tracebacks reveal the 2 open-range snippets fall through a different code path (`MakeRange: open-range bound on start side` at the VM bytecode dispatch — NOT the JIT `Rvalue::Aggregate` BinOp fallback). Resolution: the BinOp coverage fix landed cleanly with the 6/8 Class A snippets PASS preserved + the full 27-snippet matrix rose 12→21 VM (+9) and 12→16 JIT (+4); the 2 open-range residuals tracked as `W11-followup-open-range-iterator` per ADR-006 §2.7.23. (2) `crates/shape-vm/src/compiler/helpers.rs::infer_top_level_concrete_types_from_mir_with_resolvers` — added `copy_source` pre-pass (per-slot `Vec<Option<u16>>` map from `Assign(dst, Use(Move/Copy/MoveExplicit(Place::Local(src))))`); extended `infer_array_elem_from_operands` signature to accept `concrete_types` + `copy_source` so that `Move(temp)` operands in spread arrays (e.g. `[0, ...a, 4]`) can chase the temp back to the user-visible `a` slot via new `resolve_copy_chain` helper and project its `Array<T>` element type.
